@Article.id: @Article= Sheol is the Hebrew term for the underworld or the abode of the dead, mentioned numerous times throughout the Old Testament. It is often associated with darkness, stillness, and a place where both the righteous and the unrighteous go after death (Job.17.13; Psalm 89:48; Ecclesiastes 9:10). In some instances, Sheol is described as being located in the depths of the earth (Numbers 16:30, 33; Deu.32.22). ¶The concept of Sheol evolved throughout the Old Testament, with later texts suggesting a distinction between the fate of the righteous and the wicked (Psalm 49:14-15; Proverbs 15:24). However, the Old Testament does not provide a clear, consistent picture of the afterlife, and the idea of Sheol is often used as a metaphor for death and the grave (Psalm 6:5; 88:3; Isaiah 38:18). ¶In the New Testament, the Greek terms Hades (Luke 16:23; Act.2.27, 31; Revelation 1:18; 20:13-14), Gehenna (Matthew 5:22, 29-30; 10:28; Mrk.9.43, 45, 47; Luke 12:5; Jam.3.6), and Tartarus (2Pe.2.4) are used to refer to the realm of the dead or a place of punishment. These terms are often translated as "hell" in English Bibles, but their meanings are not always synonymous with the modern concept of hell as a place of eternal torment. ¶The biblical understanding of Sheol and the afterlife developed over time, with the New Testament providing a clearer picture of the distinction between the fate of the righteous and the wicked. As a Christian, it is essential to interpret the concept of Sheol in light of the fuller revelation of the New Testament, which emphasizes the hope of eternal life through faith in Jesus Christ (Jhn.3.16; 11:25-26; 1Co.15.50-57).

Adrammelech.name: Adrammelech

Ahab.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ahab.briefDescription: Son of Harum, from the tribe of Judah
Ahab.shortDescription: Aharhel was a son of Harum, a descendant of Judah.

Aharhel.name: Aharhel
Aharhel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Aharhel.briefDescription: Father of Eliphelet, one of David's mighty men
Aharhel.shortDescription: Ahasbai the Maachathite was the father of Eliphelet, one of David's mighty men.

Ahasbai.name: Ahasbai
Ahasbai.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Ahasbai.briefDescription: Persian Emperor (probably Xerxes I); husband of Esther
Ahasbai.shortDescription: Ahasuerus, Emperor of Persia (probably Xerxes I), chose Esther as his queen and issued decrees impacting the Jews.

Ahasuerus.name: Ahasuerus
Ahasuerus.description: Emperor Xerxes I, living at the time of Exile and Return
Ahasuerus.shortDescription: Ahaz was a descendant of Saul and Jonathan, from the tribe of Benjamin.

Ahaz.name: Ahaz
Ahaz.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ahaz.briefDescription: King of Israel, son of Ahab
Ahaz.shortDescription: Ahaziah, son of Ahab, reigned as king of Israel for two years.

Ahaziah.name: Ahaziah
Ahaziah.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ahaziah.briefDescription: Son of Abishur, from the tribe of Judah
Ahaziah.shortDescription: Ahban was the son of Abishur, a descendant of Judah.

Ahban.name: Ahban
Ahban.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ahban.briefDescription: Son of Shemer, from the tribe of Asher
Ahban.shortDescription: Aher was a son of Shemer, a descendant of Asher.

Aher.name: Aher
Aher.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Aher.briefDescription: Son of Abdiel, a Gadite chief
Aher.shortDescription: Ahi, son of Abdiel, was a chief of the Gadites.

Ahi.name: Ahi
Ahi.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ahi.briefDescription: Priest who sealed the covenant with Nehemiah
Ahi.shortDescription: Ahiah was a priest who sealed the covenant during Nehemiah's time.

Ahiah.name: Ahiah
Ahiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Ahiah.briefDescription: One of David's mighty men, son of Sharar
Ahiah.shortDescription: Ahiam, son of Sharar the Hararite, was one of David's mighty men.

Ahiam.name: Ahiam
Ahiam.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Ahiam.briefDescription: Son of Shemidah, from the tribe of Manasseh
Ahiam.shortDescription: Ahian was a son of Shemidah, a descendant of Manasseh.

Ahian.name: Ahian
Ahian.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ahian.briefDescription: Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag
Ahian.shortDescription: Ahiezer was a Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag.

Ahiezer.name: Ahiezer
Ahiezer.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ahiezer.briefDescription: Son of Ehud, from the tribe of Benjamin
Ahiezer.shortDescription: Ahihud was a son of Ehud, a descendant of Benjamin.

Ahihud.name: Ahihud
Ahihud.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ahihud.briefDescription: One of David's mighty men, the Pelonite
Ahihud.shortDescription: Ahijah the Pelonite was one of David's mighty men.

Ahijah.name: Ahijah
Ahijah.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ahijah.briefDescription: Son of Shaphan, protected Jeremiah from death
Ahijah.shortDescription: Ahikam, son of Shaphan, was an official during the reigns of Josiah and Jehoiakim, and he protected Jeremiah from death.

Ahikam.name: Ahikam
Ahikam.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ahikam.briefDescription: Father of Jehoshaphat and Baana, officials under David and Solomon
Ahikam.shortDescription: Ahilud was the father of Jehoshaphat, David's recorder, and Baana, one of Solomon's twelve district governors.

Ahilud.name: Ahilud
Ahilud.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ahilud.shortDescription: Ahimaaz, who was married to Solomon's daughter Basemath, served as the governor of Naphtali during Solomon's reign.

Ahimaaz.name: Ahimaaz
Ahimaaz.description: Priest living at the time of United Monarchy
Ahimaaz.shortDescription: Ahiman was a Levite who served as a gatekeeper during the reign of David.

Ahiman.name: Ahiman
Ahiman.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ahiman.briefDescription: High Priest at Nob who helped David, killed by Saul
Ahiman.shortDescription: Ahimelech, the High Priest at Nob, gave David consecrated bread and Goliath's sword but was later killed by Saul for aiding David.

Ahimelech.name: Ahimelech
Ahimelech.description: Priest living at the time of United Monarchy
Ahimelech.briefDescription: Levite descendant of Kohath, son of Elkanah
Ahimelech.shortDescription: Ahimoth, also known as Mahath, was a Levite descended from Kohath, the son of Elkanah.

Ahimoth.name: Ahimoth
Ahimoth.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ahimoth.briefDescription: Son of Iddo, a governor under Solomon
Ahimoth.shortDescription: Ahinadab, the son of Iddo, was one of Solomon's twelve governors, responsible for providing provisions for the king's household.

Ahinadab.name: Ahinadab
Ahinadab.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ahinadab.briefDescription: Wife of King Saul, daughter of Ahimaaz
Ahinadab.shortDescription: Ahinoam, the daughter of Ahimaaz, was one of King Saul's wives.

Ahinoam.name: Ahinoam
Ahinoam.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
Ahinoam.shortDescription: Ahio was a son of Elpaal and a descendant of Benjamin.

Ahio.name: Ahio
Ahio.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ahio.briefDescription: Leader of the tribe of Naphtali during the Exodus
Ahio.shortDescription: Ahira, son of Enan, was the leader of the tribe of Naphtali during the Israelites' journey through the wilderness.

Ahira.name: Ahira
Ahira.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ahira.briefDescription: Son of Benjamin, founder of the Ahiramite clan
Ahira.shortDescription: Ahiram, also known as Aharah or Ehi, was a son of Benjamin and the founder of the Ahiramite clan.

Ahiram.name: Ahiram
Ahiram.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ahiram.briefDescription: Father of Oholiab, a skilled artisan for the tabernacle
Ahiram.shortDescription: Ahisamach, from the tribe of Dan, was the father of Oholiab, a skilled artisan who helped construct the tabernacle.

Ahisamach.name: Ahisamach
Ahisamach.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ahisamach.shortDescription: Ahishahar was a son of Bilhan and a descendant of Benjamin.

Ahishahar.name: Ahishahar
Ahishahar.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ahishahar.shortDescription: Ahishar was in charge of King Solomon's palace.

Ahishar.name: Ahishar
Ahishar.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ahishar.briefDescription: David's counselor who joined Absalom's rebellion, later committed suicide
Ahishar.shortDescription: Ahithophel was a wise counselor to David who defected to Absalom's side during Absalom's rebellion, but later committed suicide when his advice was not followed.

Ahithophel.name: Ahithophel
Ahithophel.description: David's compatriot living at the time of United Monarchy
Ahithophel.briefDescription: Ancestor of Ezra the scribe, in the priestly line
Ahithophel.shortDescription: Ahitub, son of Amariah and father of Zadok, was an ancestor of Ezra the scribe in the priestly line.

Ahitub.name: Ahitub
Ahitub.description: Priest living at the time of United Monarchy
Ahitub.briefDescription: Father of Zabad, one of David's mighty men
Ahitub.shortDescription: Ahlai was the father of Zabad, who was one of David's mighty men.

Ahlai.name: Ahlai
Ahlai.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ahlai.briefDescription: Descendant of Benjamin, clan of David's mighty men
Ahlai.shortDescription: The Ahohites, descendants of Ahoah of Benjamin, were a clan that included some of David's mighty men, such as Eleazar and Zalmon.

Ahoah.name: Ahoah
Ahoah.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ahoah.shortDescription: Ahumai was a descendant of Judah through his grandson Reaiah.

Ahumai.name: Ahumai
Ahumai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ahumai.briefDescription: Son of Ashhur and Naarah, a Judahite
Ahumai.shortDescription: Ahuzzam was the son of Ashhur and his wife Naarah, from the tribe of Judah.

Ahuzzam.name: Ahuzzam
Ahuzzam.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ahuzzam.briefDescription: Friend and advisor of Abimelech, Philistine king of Gerar
Ahuzzam.shortDescription: Ahuzzath was a friend and advisor of Abimelech, the Philistine king of Gerar, who accompanied him when making a treaty with Isaac.

Ahuzzath.name: Ahuzzath
Ahuzzath.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Ahuzzath.shortDescription: Aiah was the father of Rizpah, who was a concubine of King Saul.

Aiah.name: Aiah
Aiah.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Aiah.briefDescription: Son of Ezer, a Horite chief in Seir
Aiah.shortDescription: Akan was the son of Ezer, a Horite chief who lived in the land of Seir.

Akan.name: Akan
Akan.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Akan.briefDescription: Descendant of King Jehoiachin of Judah
Akan.shortDescription: Akkub was a descendant of King Jehoiachin of Judah, listed in the genealogy of the royal line.

Akkub.name: Akkub
Akkub.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Akkub.shortDescription: Alemeth was a son of Becher and a descendant of Benjamin.

Alemeth.name: Alemeth
Alemeth.shortDescription: Alemeth, also known as Almon, was a Levitical city allotted to the tribe of Benjamin.

Alexander.name: Alexander
Alexander.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Alexander.shortDescription: Allon was the son of Jedaiah, a descendant of Simeon.

Allon.name: Allon

father.name: Word sometimes translated as a place
father.raw: Word sometimes translated as a place

Allon.briefDescription: A boundary marker for the territory of Naphtali.
Allon.shortDescription: Allon, meaning "oak," a place on the border of Naphtali's territory.

relations.father: Word sometimes translated as a place

Almodad.name: Almodad
Almodad.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Almodad.briefDescription: Father of apostles James and Matthew (Levi)
Almodad.shortDescription: Alphaeus was the father of two of Jesus' apostles, James and Matthew (also known as Levi).

Alphaeus.name: Alphaeus
Alphaeus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Alphaeus.shortDescription: Alvah was an Edomite chief, a descendant of Esau through his son Eliphaz.

Alvah.name: Alvah
Alvah.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Alvah.shortDescription: Alvan was a Horite chief, a descendant of Seir, who lived in the land of Edom before Esau's descendants.

Alvan.name: Alvan
Alvan.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Alvan.shortDescription: Amal was a son of Helem, a descendant of Asher, and is listed in the genealogy of the tribe of Asher.

Amal.name: Amal
Amal.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Amal.briefDescription: Grandson of Esau, ancestor of the Amalekites
Amal.shortDescription: Amalek was the son of Eliphaz and grandson of Esau, and he became the ancestor of the Amalekite people.

Amalek.name: Amalek
Amalek.briefDescription: A nomadic people, descendants of Esau, enemies of Israel.
Amalek.shortDescription: Amalek, a nomadic tribe descended from Esau, who were persistent enemies of Israel.

Amariah.name: Amariah
Amariah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Amariah.briefDescription: Ephraimite leader who opposed enslaving Judahite captives
Amariah.shortDescription: Amasa, along with other leaders of Ephraim, opposed the Israelite army's attempt to bring Judahite captives as slaves to Samaria.

Amasa.name: Amasa
Amasa.description: Ishmaelite living at the time of United Monarchy
Amasa.briefDescription: Chief of thirty who joined David
Amasa.shortDescription: Amasai, a chief of thirty mighty men, joined David at Ziklag while he was fleeing from Saul.

Amasai.name: Amasai
Amasai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Amasai.briefDescription: Commander who volunteered for God's service
Amasai.shortDescription: Amasiah, the son of Zichri, was a commander who willingly offered himself for the service of the Lord during the reign of King Jehoshaphat.

Amasiah.name: Amasiah
Amasiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Amasiah.shortDescription: Amaziah, the son of Joshah, was a descendant of Simeon.

Amaziah.name: Amaziah
Amaziah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Amaziah.shortDescription: The descendants of Ami (or Amon) were among the returned exiles who were servants of Solomon.

Ami.name: Ami
Ami.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Ami.shortDescription: Amittai was the father of Jonah, the prophet from Gath-hepher.

Amittai.name: Amittai
Amittai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Amittai.shortDescription: Ammiel was a Levite gatekeeper during the reign of King David.

Ammiel.name: Ammiel
Ammiel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ammiel.briefDescription: Descendant of Judah living in Jerusalem
Ammiel.shortDescription: Ammihud, a descendant of Judah, was among those who lived in Jerusalem after the exile.

Ammihud.name: Ammihud
Ammihud.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ammihud.briefDescription: Levite leader during the Ark's transfer
Ammihud.shortDescription: Amminadab was a Levite leader from the Uzzielite clan who participated in the transfer of the Ark of the Covenant to Jerusalem.

Amminadab.name: Amminadab
Amminadab.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Amminadab.briefDescription: Noble mentioned in a love song
Amminadab.shortDescription: Amminadib is mentioned in a poetic passage in the Song of Solomon, possibly referring to a noble or prince.

Amminadib.name: Amminadib
Amminadib.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Amminadib.briefDescription: Father of Ahiezer, leader of Dan
Amminadib.shortDescription: Ammishaddai was the father of Ahiezer, the leader of the tribe of Dan during the wilderness journey.

Ammishaddai.name: Ammishaddai
Ammishaddai.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ammishaddai.briefDescription: Son of Benaiah, one of David's mighty men
Ammishaddai.shortDescription: Ammizabad was the son of Benaiah, one of David's mighty men, and served as his father's second-in-command.

Ammizabad.name: Ammizabad
Ammizabad.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Ammizabad.briefDescription: Son of Shimon, descendant of Judah
Ammizabad.shortDescription: Amnon was a son of Shimon and a descendant of Judah.

Amnon.name: Amnon
Amnon.description: David's son living at the time of United Monarchy
Amnon.briefDescription: Priest who returned from Babylonian exile
Amnon.shortDescription: Amok was a priest who returned from the Babylonian exile and signed the covenant under Nehemiah.

Amok.name: Amok
Amok.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Amok.briefDescription: Governor of Samaria during Ahab's reign
Amok.shortDescription: Amon was the governor of Samaria during the reign of King Ahab of Israel.

Amon.name: Amon
Amon.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Amon.briefDescription: Egyptian god; city in Nahum, probably Thebes
Amon.shortDescription: Amon, mentioned in Jeremiah 46:25 and Nam.3.8, refers to the Egyptian god Amon and the city of Thebes, respectively.

Amorites.name: Amorites
Amorites.briefDescription: Prophet from Tekoa who prophesied to Israel
Amorites.shortDescription: Amos, a shepherd from Tekoa, called by God as a prophet to Israel.

Amos.name: Amos
Amos.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Amos.shortDescription: Amoz, the father of the prophet Isaiah.

Amoz.name: Amoz
Amoz.description: Isaiah's father living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Amoz.briefDescription: Believer greeted by Paul in Rome
Amoz.shortDescription: Ampliatus, a beloved fellow believer greeted by Paul in his letter to the Romans.

Ampliatus.name: Ampliatus
Ampliatus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Ampliatus.briefDescription: Father of Moses, Aaron, and Miriam
Ampliatus.shortDescription: Amram was the father of Moses, Aaron, and Miriam, and a member of the tribe of Levi.

Amram.name: Amram
Amram.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Amram.briefDescription: King of Shinar, ally of Chedorlaomer
Amram.shortDescription: Amraphel, king of Shinar, was one of the four kings who joined forces with Chedorlaomer in the battle against the five kings of the Plain.

Amraphel.name: Amraphel
Amraphel.description: King living at the time of the Patriarchs
Amraphel.shortDescription: Amzi, a descendant of Merari, was a Levite who served as a musician in the temple.

Amzi.name: Amzi
Amzi.description: Priest living at the time of Exile and Return
Amzi.briefDescription: Horite, father of Oholibamah, Esau's wife
Amzi.shortDescription: Anah was a Horite, the father of Oholibamah, one of Esau's wives, and the discoverer of hot springs in the wilderness.

Anah.name: Anah
Anah.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Anah.shortDescription: Anaiah was a priest who signed the covenant of faithfulness to God during Nehemiah's time.

Anaiah.name: Anaiah
Anaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Anaiah.briefDescription: Ancestor of giant people in Canaan
Anaiah.shortDescription: Anak was the ancestor of the Anakim, a race of giants who inhabited Canaan before the Israelite conquest.

Anak.name: Anak
Anak.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Anak.briefDescription: Descendants of Mizraim, son of Ham
Anak.shortDescription: The Anamim were a people group descended from Mizraim, the son of Ham, and are listed among the nations in the Table of Nations.

Anamim.name: Anamim
Anamim.shortDescription: Anammelech was an Assyrian deity worshiped by the Sepharvites, who were among the foreign peoples settled in Samaria by the Assyrian king.

Anammelech.name: Anammelech
Anammelech.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Anammelech.briefDescription: Signer of the covenant during Nehemiah's time
Anammelech.shortDescription: Anan was one of the leaders who signed the covenant during the time of Nehemiah.

Anan.name: Anan
Anan.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Anan.shortDescription: Anani was a descendant of King David, mentioned in the genealogy recorded in 1 Chronicles.

Anani.name: Anani
Anani.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Anani.briefDescription: Repairer of the wall in Jerusalem
Anani.shortDescription: Ananiah made repairs to a section of the wall near his house during the rebuilding of Jerusalem under Nehemiah's leadership.

Ananiah.name: Ananiah
Ananiah.briefDescription: A town inhabited by Benjamites after the Babylonian exile.
Ananiah.shortDescription: Ananiah, a town settled by the Benjamites after the exile.

Ananias.name: Ananias
Ananias.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Ananias.briefDescription: Father of Shamgar, a judge of Israel
Ananias.shortDescription: Anath was the father of Shamgar, one of the judges who delivered Israel from oppression.

Anath.name: Anath
Anath.briefDescription: Son of Becher, descendant of Benjamin
Anath.shortDescription: Anathoth was a son of Becher and a descendant of Benjamin, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Benjamin.

Anathoth.name: Anathoth
Anathoth.briefDescription: A Levitical city in Benjamin, home of the prophet Jeremiah.
Anathoth.shortDescription: Anathoth, a Levitical city in Benjamin, notable as the birthplace of Jeremiah.

Andrew.name: Andrew
Andrew.description: Apostle living at the time of the New Testament
Andrew.briefDescription: Fellow prisoner with Paul, outstanding among the apostles
Andrew.shortDescription: Andronicus, along with Junia, was a fellow prisoner with Paul and was noted as outstanding among the apostles.

Andronicus.name: Andronicus
Andronicus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Andronicus.shortDescription: Aner was an Amorite, one of the allies of Abram, who joined him in the battle against the kings who had taken Lot captive.

Aner.name: Aner
Aner.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Aner.briefDescription: Son of Shemida, descendant of Manasseh
Aner.shortDescription: Aniam was a son of Shemida and a descendant of Manasseh, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Manasseh.

Aniam.name: Aniam
Aniam.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Aniam.briefDescription: Prophetess who witnessed Jesus' dedication in the temple
Aniam.shortDescription: Anna, a prophetess and widow, witnessed the dedication of the infant Jesus in the temple and spoke about Him to all who were looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.

Anna.name: Anna
Anna.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Anna.briefDescription: High priest during Jesus' ministry and trial
Anna.shortDescription: Annas, a high priest, played a significant role in the events surrounding Jesus' arrest, trial, and crucifixion, as well as in the early persecution of the apostles.

Annas.name: Annas
Annas.description: High Priest living at the time of the New Testament
Annas.briefDescription: Son of Shashak, descendant of Benjamin
Annas.shortDescription: Anthothijah was a descendant of Benjamin, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Benjamin.

Anthothijah.name: Anthothijah
Anthothijah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Anthothijah.shortDescription: Antipas, described as Christ's faithful witness, was martyred in Pergamum, where Satan's throne was said to be.

Antipas.name: Antipas
Antipas.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Antipas.briefDescription: Son of Coz, descendant of Judah
Antipas.shortDescription: Anub was a descendant of Judah, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Judah.

Anub.name: Anub
Anub.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Anub.briefDescription: Approved in Christ, greeted by Paul
Anub.shortDescription: Apelles, described as "approved in Christ," was among those greeted by Paul in his letter to the Romans.

Apelles.name: Apelles
Apelles.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Apelles.briefDescription: Ancestor of King Saul, a Benjamite
Apelles.shortDescription: Aphiah, a Benjamite, was an ancestor of King Saul, the first king of Israel.

Aphiah.name: Aphiah
Aphiah.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Aphiah.briefDescription: Eloquent Jewish Christian from Alexandria, co-worker with Paul
Aphiah.shortDescription: Apollos, an eloquent and learned Jewish Christian from Alexandria, was an important figure in the early church who worked alongside Paul in spreading the gospel.

Apollos.name: Apollos
Apollos.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Apollos.briefDescription: Son of Nadab, descendant of Judah
Apollos.shortDescription: Appaim was a descendant of Judah, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Judah.

Appaim.name: Appaim
Appaim.briefDescription: Recipient of Paul's letter to Philemon
Appaim.shortDescription: Apphia, likely a member of Philemon's household, was one of the recipients of Paul's letter to Philemon.

Apphia.name: Apphia
Apphia.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Apphia.briefDescription: Jewish Christian tentmaker, co-worker with Paul
Apphia.shortDescription: Aquila, along with his wife Priscilla, was a Jewish Christian who worked as a tentmaker and played a significant role in the early church, partnering with Paul in his ministry.

Aquila.name: Aquila
Aquila.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Aquila.briefDescription: Son of Jether, descendant of Asher
Aquila.shortDescription: Ara was a descendant of Asher, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Asher.

Ara.name: Ara
Ara.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ara.shortDescription: Arad was a Benjamite, one of the sons of Beriah.

Arad.name: Arad
Arad.briefDescription: Arad, Canaanite city, attacked Israel, later inhabited by Kenites
Arad.shortDescription: Arad, a Canaanite city that attacked Israel during their wilderness journey, later inhabited by the Kenites.

Arah.name: Arah
Arah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Arah.briefDescription: Son of Shemer, descendant of Asher
Arah.shortDescription: Aram was a descendant of Asher, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Asher.

Aram.name: Aram
Aram.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Aram.briefDescription: Concubine of Manasseh; mother of Asriel
Aram.shortDescription: An Aramitess, who was a concubine of Manasseh, gave birth to Machir and Asriel..

Aramitess.name: Aramitess
Aramitess.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Aramitess.briefDescription: Son of Dishan, descendant of Seir the Horite
Aramitess.shortDescription: Aran was a descendant of Seir the Horite, mentioned in the genealogies of Esau's family and the Horite clans.

Aran.name: Aran
Aran.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Aran.briefDescription: Father of Anak, former name of Hebron
Aran.shortDescription: Arba was the father of Anak and the namesake of Kiriath-arba, which was the former name of Hebron.

Arba.name: Arba
Arba.briefDescription: Son of Herod the Great, Ethnarch of Judea
Arba.shortDescription: Archelaus, son of Herod the Great, Ethnarch over Judea, causing Joseph to fear returning there with Mary and the child Jesus.

Archelaus.name: Archelaus
Archelaus.description: Ethnarch living at the time of the New Testament
Archelaus.briefDescription: Fellow soldier and minister in Colossae
Archelaus.shortDescription: Archippus, addressed as a fellow soldier by Paul, was urged to complete the ministry he had received in the Lord.

Archippus.name: Archippus
Archippus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Archippus.briefDescription: Inhabitants of a region near Bethel
Archippus.shortDescription: The Archites were a people group mentioned in relation to the boundaries of Ephraim's territory and as the origin of Hushai, King David's friend and advisor.

Archites.name: Archites
Archites.briefDescription: Son of Benjamin, brother of Bela
Archites.shortDescription: Ard, a son of Benjamin, is listed among the descendants of Jacob who went to Egypt during the famine.

Ard.name: Ard
Ard.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ard.briefDescription: Son of Caleb, brother of Jether and Mered
Ard.shortDescription: Ardon was a son of Caleb, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Ardon.name: Ardon
Ardon.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ardon.briefDescription: Son of Gad, grandson of Jacob
Ardon.shortDescription: Areli, a son of Gad, is mentioned in the genealogy of Jacob and the census of the Israelites in the wilderness.

Areli.name: Areli
Areli.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Areli.briefDescription: King of the Nabataeans in Damascus
Areli.shortDescription: Aretas, the king of the Nabataeans, governed Damascus and tried to arrest Paul.

Aretas.name: Aretas
Aretas.description: King living at the time of the New Testament
Aretas.briefDescription: Officer of King Pekahiah of Israel
Aretas.shortDescription: Argob, along with Arieh, was with King Pekahiah when Pekah conspired against him and assassinated him.

Argob.name: Argob
Argob.briefDescription: A region in Bashan, known for its fortified cities.
Argob.shortDescription: Argob, a region in Bashan, conquered by the Israelites and known for its many fortified cities.

Aridai.name: Aridai
Aridai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Aridai.briefDescription: Son of Haman, enemy of the Jews
Aridai.shortDescription: Aridatha was one of the ten sons of Haman, who were killed and hanged after their father's downfall.

Aridatha.name: Aridatha
Aridatha.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Aridatha.briefDescription: Officer of King Pekahiah of Israel
Aridatha.shortDescription: Arieh, along with Argob, was with King Pekahiah when Pekah conspired against him and assassinated him.

Arieh.name: Arieh
Arieh.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Arieh.briefDescription: Two lion-like men of Moab killed by Benaiah
Arieh.shortDescription: Ariel refers to two lion-like men of Moab who were killed by Benaiah, one of David's mighty warriors.

Ariel.name: Ariel
Ariel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Ariel.shortDescription: Arioch, the captain of King Nebuchadnezzar's guard, was commanded to execute the wise men of Babylon but brought Daniel before the king to interpret his dream.

Arioch.name: Arioch
Arioch.description: King living at the time of the Patriarchs
Arioch.briefDescription: Son of Haman, enemy of the Jews
Arioch.shortDescription: Arisai was one of the ten sons of Haman, who were killed and hanged after their father's downfall.

Arisai.name: Arisai
Arisai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Arisai.briefDescription: Macedonian companion of Paul, fellow prisoner
Arisai.shortDescription: Aristarchus, a Macedonian from Thessalonica, was a faithful companion of Paul during his missionary journeys and imprisonment.

Aristarchus.name: Aristarchus
Aristarchus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Aristarchus.briefDescription: Believer in Rome, greeted by Paul
Aristarchus.shortDescription: Paul sends greetings to those who belong to the household of Aristobulus, suggesting that Aristobulus was a prominent believer in Rome.

Aristobulus.name: Aristobulus
Aristobulus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Aristobulus.briefDescription: Canaanite people group descended from Canaan
Aristobulus.shortDescription: The Arkites were one of the Canaanite tribes descended from Canaan, the son of Ham, and are mentioned in the Table of Nations.

Arkites.name: Arkites
Arkites.briefDescription: Son of Saul by Rizpah, killed by Gibeonites
Arkites.shortDescription: Armoni was a son of Saul by his concubine Rizpah, killed by the Gibeonites (2Sa.21.8).

Armoni.name: Armoni
Armoni.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Armoni.shortDescription: Arnan was a descendant of David's son Solomon (1Ch.3.21).

Arnan.name: Arnan
Arnan.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Arnan.briefDescription: Son of Gad and founder of the Arodite clan
Arnan.shortDescription: Arod, son of Gad, was the founder of the Arodite clan (Gen.46.16; Num.26.17).

Arod.name: Arod
Arod.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Arod.briefDescription: Son of Shem, ancestor of Abraham
Arod.shortDescription: Arpachshad, son of Shem, was born two years after the flood and was an ancestor of Abraham (Gen.10.22,24; 11:10-13; 1Ch.1.17-18,24; Luke 3:36).

Arpachshad.name: Arpachshad
Arpachshad.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Arpachshad.briefDescription: Persian king during Ezra and Nehemiah's time
Arpachshad.shortDescription: Artaxerxes was the Persian king who allowed Ezra and Nehemiah to return to Jerusalem (Ezra 7:1,11-12,21; Neh.2.1; 5:14).

Artaxerxes.name: Artaxerxes
Artaxerxes.description: Artaxerxes Longimanus, an emperor living at the time of Exile and Return
Artaxerxes.briefDescription: Companion of Paul, potential replacement for Titus
Artaxerxes.shortDescription: Artemas was a companion of Paul who could potentially replace Titus in Crete (Titus 3:12).

Artemas.name: Artemas
Artemas.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Artemas.briefDescription: Canaanite descendants of Canaan, son of Ham
Artemas.shortDescription: The Arvadites were a Canaanite People from Canaan, son of Ham (Gen.10.18; 1Ch.1.16).

Arvadites.name: Arvadites
Arvadites.briefDescription: Steward of King Elah's household in Tirzah
Arvadites.shortDescription: Arza was the steward of King Elah's household in Tirzah (1Ki.16.9).

Arza.name: Arza
Arza.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Arza.briefDescription: Levite gatekeeper who resettled in Jerusalem
Arza.shortDescription: Asa was a Levite gatekeeper who resettled in Jerusalem (1Ch.9.16).

Asa.name: Asa
Asa.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Asa.briefDescription: Levite sent by Jehoshaphat to teach the Law
Asa.shortDescription: Asahel was a Levite sent by Jehoshaphat to teach the Book of the Law in Judah (2Ch.17.8).

Asahel.name: Asahel
Asahel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Asahel.briefDescription: Levite leader who helped bring the ark to Jerusalem
Asahel.shortDescription: Asaiah was a Levite leader who assisted in bringing the ark of the covenant to Jerusalem during David's reign (1Ch.15.6, 11).

Asaiah.name: Asaiah
Asaiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Asaiah.briefDescription: Levite musician, psalmist, and ancestor of temple singers
Asaiah.shortDescription: Asaph was a Levite musician and psalmist during David's reign, and his descendants served as temple singers (1Ch.6.39; 15:17, 19; 16:5, 7, 37; 25:1, 2, 6, 9; 2Ch.5.12; 20:14; 29:13, 30; 35:15; Ezra 2:41; 3:10; Neh.7.44; 11:22; 12:35, 46; Psalms 50, 73-83).

Asaph.name: Asaph
Asaph.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Asaph.briefDescription: Son of Jehallelel, from the tribe of Judah
Asaph.shortDescription: Asarel was a son of Jehallelel, a descendant of Judah (1Ch.4.16).

Asarel.name: Asarel
Asarel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Asarel.briefDescription: Ancestor of exiles who returned to Jerusalem
Asarel.shortDescription: Asen was an ancestor of 223 individuals who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian captivity.

Asen.name: Asen
Asen.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Asen.briefDescription: Egyptian wife of Joseph; mother of Manasseh and Ephraim
Asen.shortDescription: Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera, priest of On, was given to Joseph as his wife by Pharaoh (Gen.41.45, 50; 46:20).

Asenath.name: Asenath
Asenath.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Asenath.briefDescription: Levite musician appointed by David to prophesy with lyres
Asenath.shortDescription: Asharelah (or Jesharelah) was a Levite musician appointed by David to prophesy with lyres (1Ch.25.2, 14).

Asharelah.name: Asharelah
Asharelah.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Asharelah.briefDescription: Son of Benjamin, founder of the Ashbelite clan
Asharelah.shortDescription: Ashbel was a son of Benjamin and the founder of the Ashbelite clan (Gen.46.21; Num.26.38; 1Ch.8.1).

Ashbel.name: Ashbel
Ashbel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ashbel.briefDescription: Son of Jacob and Zilpah, founder of the tribe of Asher

Asher.name: Asher
Asher.description: Jacob's son living at the time of the Patriarchs
Asher.shortDescription: Ashhur, a descendant of Judah through Hezron, was the  father (ie founder) of Tekoa (1Ch.2.24; 4:5).

Ashhur.name: Ashhur
Ashhur.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ashhur.briefDescription: Grandson of Japheth, son of Gomer
Ashhur.shortDescription: Ashkenaz was a grandson of Japheth and son of Gomer, mentioned in the Table of Nations (Gen.10.3; 1Ch.1.6).

Ashkenaz.name: Ashkenaz
Ashkenaz.briefDescription: A kingdom summoned to battle against Babylon.
Ashkenaz.shortDescription: Ashkenaz, a kingdom called to join the fight against Babylon in Jeremiah's prophecy.

Ashpenaz.name: Ashpenaz
Ashpenaz.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Ashpenaz.briefDescription: Son of Japhlet, descendant of Asher
Ashpenaz.shortDescription: Ashvath was a son of Japhlet and a descendant of Asher (1Ch.7.33).

Ashvath.name: Ashvath
Ashvath.shortDescription: Asiel was a descendant of Simeon mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe (1Ch.4.35).

Asiel.name: Asiel
Asiel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Asiel.briefDescription: Head of a family of temple servants who returned from exile
Asiel.shortDescription: Asnah was the head of a family of temple servants (Nethinim) who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian exile (Ezra 2:50).

Asnah.name: Asnah
Asnah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Asnah.briefDescription: One of the ten sons of Haman
Asnah.shortDescription: Aspatha was one of the ten sons of Haman, who were executed after their father's downfall (Est.9.7).

Aspatha.name: Aspatha
Aspatha.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Aspatha.briefDescription: Son of Gilead, descendant of Manasseh
Aspatha.shortDescription: Asriel was a son of Gilead and a descendant of Manasseh (1Ch.7.14).

Asriel.name: Asriel
Asriel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Asriel.briefDescription: Son of Shem; father of the Assyrians
Asriel.shortDescription: Asshur was a son of Shem and is considered the father of the Assyrians (Gen.10.22; 1Ch.1.17).

Asshur.name: Asshur
Asshur.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Asshur.shortDescription: Asshurim was a descendant of Abraham through his son Jokshan and grandson Dedan (Gen.25.3).

Asshurim.name: Asshurim
Asshurim.briefDescription: Levite ancestor of the prophet Samuel
Asshurim.shortDescription: Assir was a Levite ancestor of Samuel the prophet (1Ch.6.23, 37).

Assir.name: Assir
Assir.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Assir.briefDescription: Believer in Rome greeted by Paul
Assir.shortDescription: Asyncritus was a believer in Rome to whom Paul sent greetings (Rom.16.14).

Asyncritus.name: Asyncritus
Asyncritus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Asyncritus.briefDescription: Wife of Jerahmeel; mother of Onam
Asyncritus.shortDescription: Atarah was the wife of Jerahmeel and the mother of Onam, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah (1Ch.2.26).

Atarah.name: Atarah
Atarah.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
Atarah.briefDescription: Head of a family that returned from Babylonian exile
Atarah.shortDescription: Ater was the head of a family that returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian captivity (Ezr.2.16; Neh.7.21).

Ater.name: Ater
Ater.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Ater.briefDescription: Descendant of Judah who resettled in Jerusalem
Ater.shortDescription: Athaiah, a descendant of Judah, was one of the people who resettled in Jerusalem after the exile (Neh.11.4).

Athaiah.name: Athaiah
Athaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Athaiah.shortDescription: Athaliah was a descendant of Benjamin mentioned in the genealogy of Saul's family (1Ch.8.26).

Athaliah.name: Athaliah
Athaliah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Athaliah.briefDescription: One of those who agreed to put away foreign wives
Athaliah.shortDescription: Athlai was one of the men who agreed to put away their foreign wives during Ezra's reforms (Ezr.10.28).

Athlai.name: Athlai
Athlai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Athlai.briefDescription: One of David's mighty men, a Gadite warrior
Athlai.shortDescription: Attai the son of Zabad was one of David's mighty men, a Gadite warrior who joined him at Ziklag (1Ch.12.11).

Attai.name: Attai
Attai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Attai.briefDescription: Roman emperor during the birth of Jesus
Attai.shortDescription: Augustus, also known as Caesar, was the Roman emperor during the time of Jesus' birth and early life (Luk.2.1; 3:1; Act.25.21, 25; 27:1).

Augustus.name: Augustus
Augustus.description: Emperor living at the time of the New Testament
Augustus.briefDescription: Ancient people who inhabited the Gaza area
Augustus.shortDescription: The Avvim were an ancient people who inhabited the Gaza area before being destroyed by the Caphtorim (Deut 2:23; Josh 13:3).

Avvim.name: Avvim
Avvim.briefDescription: Town allotted to the tribe of Benjamin.
Avvim.shortDescription: Avvim was a town allotted to the tribe of Benjamin. (Joshua 18:23)

Azaliah.name: Azaliah
Azaliah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Azaliah.briefDescription: One of the Levites who sealed the covenant
Azaliah.shortDescription: Azaniah was one of the Levites who sealed the covenant during the time of Nehemiah (Neh.10.9).

Azaniah.name: Azaniah
Azaniah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Azaniah.briefDescription: One of the Danite warriors who joined David at Ziklag
Azaniah.shortDescription: Azarel was one of the Danite warriors who joined David at Ziklag (1Ch.12.6).

Azarel.name: Azarel
Azarel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Azarel.briefDescription: Descendant of Jerahmeel, son of Hezron
Azarel.shortDescription: Azariah was a descendant of Jerahmeel, the son of Hezron, from the tribe of Judah (1Ch.2.38, 39).

Azariah.name: Azariah
Azariah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Azariah.briefDescription: Son of King Jehoshaphat of Judah
Azariah.shortDescription: Azariahu was one of the sons of King Jehoshaphat of Judah (2Ch.21.2).

Azariahu.name: Azariahu
Azariahu.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Azariahu.briefDescription: Descendant of Reuben; father of Bela
Azariahu.shortDescription: Azaz, a descendant of Reuben, was the father of Bela (1Ch.5.8).

Azaz.name: Azaz
Azaz.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Azaz.shortDescription: Azaziah was a Levite musician who played the harp during the reign of King David (1Ch.15.21).

Azaziah.name: Azaziah
Azaziah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Azaziah.briefDescription: Father of Nehemiah, who repaired part of Jerusalem's wall
Azaziah.shortDescription: Azbuk was the father of Nehemiah, who repaired a section of Jerusalem's wall near the tombs of David (Neh.3.16).

Azbuk.name: Azbuk
Azbuk.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Azbuk.briefDescription: Descendant of Saul, had six sons
Azbuk.shortDescription: Azel, a descendant of Saul, had six sons: Azrikam, Bokeru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan (1Ch.8.37, 38; 9:43, 44).

Azel.name: Azel
Azel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Azel.briefDescription: Ancestor of exiles who returned to Jerusalem
Azel.shortDescription: Azgad was the ancestor of 1,222 (or 2,322) exiles who returned to Jerusalem (Ezr.2.12; 8:12; Neh.7.17).

Azgad.name: Azgad
Azgad.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Azgad.briefDescription: Descendant of Zattu, had married a foreign woman
Azgad.shortDescription: Aziza, a descendant of Zattu, was one of the men who had married foreign women during the time of Ezra (Ezr.10.27).

Aziza.name: Aziza
Aziza.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Aziza.briefDescription: Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag
Aziza.shortDescription: Azmaveth, a Benjamite warrior, was one of the men who joined David at Ziklag when he was fleeing from Saul (1Ch.12.3).

Azmaveth.name: Azmaveth
Azmaveth.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Azmaveth.briefDescription: Ancestor of Jesus Christ in the genealogy of Matthew
Azmaveth.shortDescription: Azor was an ancestor of Jesus Christ, mentioned in the genealogy of Matthew (Matt 1:13, 14).

Azor.name: Azor
Azor.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Azor.briefDescription: Ruler of the tribe of Naphtali under David
Azor.shortDescription: Azriel son of Jeremoth was the ruler of the tribe of Naphtali during the reign of King David (1Ch.27.19).

Azriel.name: Azriel
Azriel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Azriel.briefDescription: A descendant of David through Jehoiachin.
Azriel.shortDescription: Azrikam was a descendant of David through Jehoiachin.

Azrikam.name: Azrikam
Azrikam.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Azrikam.briefDescription: Wife of Caleb and mother of Jesher, Shobab, and Ardon.
Azrikam.shortDescription: Azubah was Caleb's wife and the mother of his sons Jesher, Shobab, and Ardon.

Azubah.name: Azubah
Azubah.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Azubah.briefDescription: Father of Paltiel, a leader from the tribe of Issachar.
Azubah.shortDescription: Azzan was the father of Paltiel, a leader from Issachar chosen to help divide Canaan.

Azzan.name: Azzan
Azzan.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Azzan.briefDescription: Father of the wicked prince Jaazaniah.
Azzan.shortDescription: Azzur was the father of Jaazaniah, one of the wicked princes Ezekiel saw in a vision.

Azzur.name: Azzur
Azzur.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Azzur.shortDescription: Baal was a descendant of Reuben, the firstborn son of Jacob.

Baal.name: Baal
Baal.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Baal.shortDescription: Baal-berith or El-berith, meaning "Lord of the Covenant" or "God of the Covenant," was a Canaanite deity worshipped by the Israelites in Shechem during the time of the judges (Judges 8:33; 9:4, 46).

Baal-hanan.name: Baal-hanan
Baal-hanan.description: King living at the time of the Patriarchs
Baal-hanan.briefDescription: King of the Ammonites during the Babylonian exile.
Baal-hanan.shortDescription: Baalis, the king of the Ammonites, sent Ishmael to assassinate Gedaliah the governor.

Baalis.name: Baalis
Baalis.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Baalis.briefDescription: A Philistine god worshiped at Ekron.
Baalis.shortDescription: Baal-zebub was the god of Ekron whom Ahaziah consulted about his illness.

Baal-zebub.name: Baal-zebub
Baal-zebub.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Baal-zebub.briefDescription: One of Solomon's twelve district governors.
Baal-zebub.shortDescription: Baana son of Ahilud was one of Solomon's twelve district governors.

Baana.name: Baana
Baana.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Baana.briefDescription: One of David's mighty warriors from Netophah.
Baana.shortDescription: Baanah the Netophathite was one of David's mighty warriors.

Baanah.name: Baanah
Baanah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Baanah.briefDescription: A wife of Shaharaim, a Benjamite.
Baanah.shortDescription: Baara was one of the wives of Shaharaim, a descendant of Benjamin.

Baara.name: Baara
Baara.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Baara.briefDescription: A Levite in the lineage of Gershom.
Baara.shortDescription: Baaseiah was a Levite descended from Gershom, the son of Levi.

Baaseiah.name: Baaseiah
Baaseiah.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Baaseiah.briefDescription: Third king of Israel who ruled for 24 years.
Baaseiah.shortDescription: Baasha, son of Ahijah, became king of Israel and reigned for 24 years.

Baasha.name: Baasha
Baasha.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Baasha.briefDescription: A Levite who lived in Jerusalem.
Baasha.shortDescription: Bakbakkar was a Levite who resided in Jerusalem.

Bakbakkar.name: Bakbakkar
Bakbakkar.description: Levite living at the time of Exile and Return
Bakbakkar.briefDescription: Ancestor of temple servants who returned from Babylonian exile.
Bakbakkar.shortDescription: Bakbuk was the ancestor of a family of temple servants who returned from exile.

Bakbuk.name: Bakbuk
Bakbuk.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bakbuk.briefDescription: A Levite who served in Jerusalem after the exile.
Bakbuk.shortDescription: Bakbukiah was a Levite who held leadership roles in Jerusalem after the exile.

Bakbukiah.name: Bakbukiah
Bakbukiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bakbukiah.briefDescription: A prophet hired to curse Israel but blessed them instead.
Bakbukiah.shortDescription: Balaam was a pagan prophet hired by Balak to curse Israel, but he blessed them instead.

Balaam.name: Balaam
Balaam.description: Prophet living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Balaam.shortDescription: Baladan was the father of Merodach-baladan, king of Babylon.

Baladan.name: Baladan
Baladan.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Baladan.briefDescription: King of Moab who hired Balaam to curse Israel.
Baladan.shortDescription: Balak, son of Zippor, was the king of Moab who tried to curse Israel through Balaam.

Balak.name: Balak
Balak.description: King living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Balak.briefDescription: A Levite musician in the time of David.
Balak.shortDescription: Bani was a Levite who served as a musician during David's reign.

Bani.name: Bani
Bani.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bani.briefDescription: A notorious prisoner released instead of Jesus.
Bani.shortDescription: Barabbas, a notorious prisoner, was released by Pilate instead of Jesus at the crowd's demand.

Barabbas.name: Barabbas
Barabbas.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Barabbas.briefDescription: The father of Elihu, one of Job's friends.
Barabbas.shortDescription: Barachel the Buzite was the father of Elihu, one of the men who spoke to Job.

Barachel.name: Barachel
Barachel.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Barachel.briefDescription: An Israelite commander who defeated Canaanite forces with Deborah.
Barachel.shortDescription: Barak, son of Abinoam, led the Israelites to victory against Canaanite forces under Deborah's leadership.

Barak.name: Barak
Barak.briefDescription: A descendant of David through the line of Solomon.
Barak.shortDescription: Bariah was a descendant of King David through Solomon's line.

Bariah.name: Bariah
Bariah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Bariah.briefDescription: A Jewish sorcerer who opposed Paul and Barnabas.
Bariah.shortDescription: Bar-Jesus, also known as Elymas, was a Jewish sorcerer who tried to hinder Paul and Barnabas' ministry.

Bar-jesus.name: Bar-jesus
Bar-jesus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Bar-jesus.briefDescription: Ancestor of a family of temple servants who returned from exile.
Bar-jesus.shortDescription: Barkos was the ancestor of a family of temple servants who returned from Babylonian captivity.

Barkos.name: Barkos
Barkos.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Barkos.briefDescription: A Levite from Cyprus who accompanied Paul on missionary journeys.
Barkos.shortDescription: Barnabas, also known as Joseph or Joses, was a Levite from Cyprus who played a significant role in the early church and accompanied Paul on missionary journeys.

Barnabas.name: Barnabas
Barnabas.description: Levite living at the time of the New Testament
Barnabas.briefDescription: One of the twelve apostles of Jesus Christ.
Barnabas.shortDescription: Bartholomew was one of the twelve apostles chosen by Jesus.

Bartholomew.name: Bartholomew
Bartholomew.description: Apostle living at the time of the New Testament
Bartholomew.briefDescription: A blind beggar healed by Jesus near Jericho.
Bartholomew.shortDescription: Bartimaeus, a blind beggar, was healed by Jesus as He was leaving Jericho.

Bartimaeus.name: Bartimaeus
Bartimaeus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Bartimaeus.briefDescription: Jeremiah's scribe who recorded and delivered his prophecies.
Bartimaeus.shortDescription: Baruch, son of Neriah, served as Jeremiah's scribe and faithfully recorded and delivered his prophecies.

Baruch.name: Baruch
Baruch.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Baruch.briefDescription: A Gileadite who provided for David during Absalom's rebellion.
Baruch.shortDescription: Barzillai the Gileadite supplied provisions to David during his flight from Absalom and was later honored by the king.

Barzillai.name: Barzillai
Barzillai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Barzillai.briefDescription: Wife of Ahimaaz, one of Solomon's district governors.
Barzillai.shortDescription: Basemath, daughter of Solomon, was married to Ahimaaz, one of Solomon's twelve district governors.

Basemath.name: Basemath
Basemath.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
Basemath.briefDescription: Wife of Uriah, later married David and bore Solomon.
Basemath.shortDescription: Bathsheba, the wife of Uriah, later became David's wife and the mother of Solomon.

Bathsheba.name: Bathsheba
Bathsheba.description: David's wife living at the time of United Monarchy
Bathsheba.briefDescription: Judah's Canaanite wife; mother of Er, Onan, and Shelah.
Bathsheba.shortDescription: Bath-shua, a Canaanite woman, was Judah's wife and the mother of his first three sons.

Bath-shua.name: Bath-shua
Bath-shua.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Bath-shua.briefDescription: A leader who helped repair Jerusalem's wall
Bath-shua.shortDescription: Bavvai, son of Henadad and ruler of half the district of Keilah, repaired a section of Jerusalem's wall.

Bavvai.name: Bavvai
Bavvai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bavvai.briefDescription: Ancestor of temple servants who returned from exile.
Bavvai.shortDescription: Bazluth (Bazlith) was the ancestor of a family of temple servants who returned from Babylonian captivity.

Bazluth.name: Bazluth
Bazluth.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bazluth.briefDescription: A Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag.
Bazluth.shortDescription: Bealiah, a Benjamite warrior, was one of the men who joined David at Ziklag.

Bealiah.name: Bealiah
Bealiah.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Bealiah.briefDescription: Head of a family that returned from Babylonian exile.
Bealiah.shortDescription: Bebai was the head of a family that returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian captivity.

Bebai.name: Bebai
Bebai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bebai.briefDescription: Son of Benjamin, listed among those who entered Egypt.
Bebai.shortDescription: Becher was a son of Benjamin and is listed among the family members who entered Egypt with Jacob.

Becher.name: Becher
Becher.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Becher.briefDescription: An ancestor of King Saul from the tribe of Benjamin.
Becher.shortDescription: Becorath was an ancestor of King Saul and a member of the tribe of Benjamin.

Becorath.name: Becorath
Becorath.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Becorath.briefDescription: Father of Hadad, an Edomite king.
Becorath.shortDescription: Bedad was the father of Hadad, one of the kings of Edom before Israel had a king.

Bedad.name: Bedad
Bedad.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Bedad.briefDescription: A descendant of Manasseh through Machir and Gilead.
Bedad.shortDescription: Bedan was a descendant of Manasseh, listed in the genealogy of Machir and Gilead.

Bedan.name: Bedan
Bedan.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Bedan.briefDescription: An Israelite who married a foreign woman during the exile.
Bedan.shortDescription: Bedeiah was one of the Israelites who had married foreign women during the exile and agreed to send them away.

Bedeiah.name: Bedeiah
Bedeiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bedeiah.briefDescription: A descendant of Asher through Zophah.
Bedeiah.shortDescription: Beera was a descendant of Asher, listed in the genealogy of Zophah.

Beera.name: Beera
Beera.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Beera.briefDescription: A Reubenite leader exiled by the Assyrian king Tiglath-Pileser.
Beera.shortDescription: Beerah, a leader from the tribe of Reuben, was taken into exile by Tiglath-Pileser, king of Assyria.

Beerah.name: Beerah
Beerah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Beerah.briefDescription: A Hittite, father of Esau's wife Judith.
Beerah.shortDescription: Beeri the Hittite was the father of Judith, one of Esau's wives.

Beeri.name: Beeri
Beeri.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Beeri.shortDescription: Bela, a descendant of Reuben, was the son of Azaz

Bela.name: Bela
Bela.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Bela.briefDescription: The last Emperor of Babylon, son of Nabonidus.
Bela.shortDescription: Belshazzar, the last Emperor of Babylon, is mentioned in the book of Daniel as the son of Nebuchadnezzar (though historically known as the son of Nabonidus).

Belshazzar.name: Belshazzar
Belshazzar.description: Emperor living at the time of Exile and Return
Belshazzar.briefDescription: A Levite appointed as a gatekeeper for the ark.
Belshazzar.shortDescription: Ben, a Levite, was appointed as one of the gatekeepers for the ark during its transfer to Jerusalem.

Ben.name: Ben
Ben.description: Singer living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ben.briefDescription: Solomon's son-in-law, an official in his administration.
Ben.shortDescription: Ben-abinadab, Solomon's son-in-law, was one of the twelve officials appointed over Israel to provide food for the king and his household.

Ben-abinadab.name: Ben-abinadab
Ben-abinadab.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ben-abinadab.briefDescription: A Levite musician during the transfer of the ark.
Ben-abinadab.shortDescription: Benaiah, a Levite, played the lyre and served as a gatekeeper during the transfer of the ark to Jerusalem.

Benaiah.name: Benaiah
Benaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Benaiah.briefDescription: Son of Lot's younger daughter; father of the Ammonites.
Benaiah.shortDescription: Ben-ammi was the son of Lot's younger daughter and the ancestor of the Ammonites.

Ben-ammi.name: Ben-ammi
Ben-ammi.description: Ammonite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Ben-ammi.briefDescription: One of Solomon's twelve district governors.
Ben-ammi.shortDescription: Ben-deker was one of the twelve district governors appointed by Solomon to provide provisions for the king and his household.

Ben-deker.name: Ben-deker
Ben-deker.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ben-deker.briefDescription: One of Solomon's twelve district governors.
Ben-deker.shortDescription: Ben-Geber was one of the twelve district governors appointed by Solomon, responsible for the region of Ramoth-gilead.

Ben-Geber.name: Ben-Geber
Ben-Geber.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ben-Geber.briefDescription: King of Aram, contemporary of Asa and Baasha.
Ben-Geber.shortDescription: Ben-hadad I, king of Aram, made a treaty with Asa of Judah and attacked Israel during the reign of Baasha.

Ben-hadad.name: Ben-hadad
Ben-hadad.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ben-hadad.shortDescription: Ben-hail, a Levite, was sent by Jehoshaphat to teach in Judah.

Ben-hail.name: Ben-hail
Ben-hail.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ben-hail.shortDescription: Ben-hanan was a descendant of Judah through Shimon.

Ben-hanan.name: Ben-hanan
Ben-hanan.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ben-hanan.shortDescription: Ben-hesed was one of Solomon's twelve officers over Israel.

Ben-hesed.name: Ben-hesed
Ben-hesed.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ben-hesed.shortDescription: Ben-hur was one of Solomon's twelve officers over Israel.

Ben-hur.name: Ben-hur
Ben-hur.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ben-hur.shortDescription: Beninu was a Levite who sealed the covenant in Nehemiah's time.

Beninu.name: Beninu
Beninu.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Beninu.briefDescription: Son of Bilhan, descendant of Benjamin
Beninu.shortDescription: This Benjamin was a son of Bilhan and a descendant of Benjamin.

Benjamin.name: Benjamin
Benjamin.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Benjamin.briefDescription: Levite from the family of Merari
Benjamin.shortDescription: Beno was a Levite from the family of Merari, son of Jaaziah.

Beno.name: Beno
Beno.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Beno.shortDescription: Ben-zoheth was a descendant of Judah through Ishi.

Ben-zoheth.name: Ben-zoheth
Ben-zoheth.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ben-zoheth.briefDescription: Father of Bela, an Edomite king
Ben-zoheth.shortDescription: Beor was the father of Bela, an early Edomite king.

Beor.name: Beor
Beor.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Beor.briefDescription: King of Sodom during Abraham's time
Beor.shortDescription: Bera was the king of Sodom who allied with other kings against Chedorlaomer.

Bera.name: Bera
Bera.description: King living at the time of the Patriarchs
Bera.briefDescription: Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag
Bera.shortDescription: Beracah, a Benjamite warrior, was one of the men who joined David at Ziklag.

Beracah.name: Beracah
Beracah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Beracah.shortDescription: Beraiah was a Benjamite, a descendant of Shimei.

Beraiah.name: Beraiah
Beraiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Beraiah.shortDescription: Berechiah was a descendant of King David through Zerubbabel.

Berechiah.name: Berechiah
Berechiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Berechiah.shortDescription: Bered was a descendant of Ephraim through Shuthelah.

Bered.name: Bered
Bered.briefDescription: Place near where Hagar encountered the Angel of the Lord.
Bered.shortDescription: Bered was a place near where Hagar encountered the Angel of the Lord when she fled from Sarah. (Genesis 16:14)

Beri.name: Beri
Beri.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Beri.briefDescription: Levite in the time of David
Beri.shortDescription: Beriah, a Levite during David's reign, had few sons.

Beriah.name: Beriah
Beriah.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Beriah.briefDescription: Jewish princess, sister of King Agrippa II
Beriah.shortDescription: Bernice, a Jewish princess and sister of King Agrippa II, heard Paul's defense.

Bernice.name: Bernice
Bernice.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Bernice.briefDescription: Ancestor of temple servants who returned from exile
Bernice.shortDescription: Besai was an ancestor of temple servants who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian captivity.

Besai.name: Besai
Besai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Besai.briefDescription: Father of Meshullam, a repairer of Jerusalem's gates
Besai.shortDescription: Besodeiah was the father of Meshullam, who repaired the Old Gate of Jerusalem.

Besodeiah.name: Besodeiah
Besodeiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Besodeiah.shortDescription: Beth-rapha was a descendant of Judah through Eshton.

Beth-rapha.name: Beth-rapha
Beth-rapha.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Beth-rapha.shortDescription: Bethuel, the son of Nahor and Milcah, was the father of Rebekah, Isaac's wife.

Bethuel.name: Bethuel
Bethuel.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Bethuel.briefDescription: Ancestor of exiles who returned to Jerusalem
Bethuel.shortDescription: Bezai was an ancestor of 323 individuals who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian captivity.

Bezai.name: Bezai
Bezai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bezai.briefDescription: Skilled craftsman who built the tabernacle
Bezai.shortDescription: Bezalel, son of Uri, was a skilled craftsman filled with God's Spirit to build the tabernacle and its furnishings.

Bezalel.name: Bezalel
Bezalel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bezalel.shortDescription: Bezer was a descendant of Asher through Helem.

Bezer.name: Bezer
Bezer.briefDescription: Levitical city of refuge in Reuben
Bezer.shortDescription: Bezer, also called Bozrah, was a Levitical city of refuge in the territory of Reuben.

Bichri.name: Bichri
Bichri.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Bichri.briefDescription: Officer of Jehu who witnessed Ahab's judgment
Bichri.shortDescription: Bidkar, an officer of Jehu, witnessed the LORD's judgment against Ahab and his family.

Bidkar.name: Bidkar
Bidkar.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Bidkar.shortDescription: Bigtha was one of the seven eunuchs who served King Ahasuerus.

Bigtha.name: Bigtha
Bigtha.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bigtha.briefDescription: Persian official who plotted against King Ahasuerus
Bigtha.shortDescription: Bigthan, a doorkeeper in Ahasuerus' court, plotted to assassinate the king but was exposed by Mordecai.

Bigthan.name: Bigthan
Bigthan.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bigthan.briefDescription: Ancestor of exiles who returned to Jerusalem
Bigthan.shortDescription: Bigvai was an ancestor of over 2,000 individuals who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian captivity.

Bigvai.name: Bigvai
Bigvai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bigvai.shortDescription: Bildad the Shuhite was one of Job's three friends who came to comfort him during his affliction.

Bildad.name: Bildad
Bildad.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Bildad.briefDescription: Head of a priestly division in David's time
Bildad.shortDescription: Bilgah was the head of the fifteenth division of priests during David's reign.

Bilgah.name: Bilgah
Bilgah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bilgah.briefDescription: Rachel's maidservant and mother of Dan and Naphtali
Bilgah.shortDescription: Bilhah, Rachel's maidservant, bore Jacob two sons, Dan and Naphtali, who became ancestors of Israelite tribes.

Bilhah.name: Bilhah
Bilhah.shortDescription: Bilhah was a town allotted to the tribe of Simeon in southern Judah.

Bilhan.name: Bilhan
Bilhan.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Bilhan.briefDescription: Leader who returned from Babylonian exile
Bilhan.shortDescription: Bilshan was one of the leaders who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian captivity.

Bilshan.name: Bilshan
Bilshan.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bilshan.briefDescription: Son of Japhlet from the tribe of Asher
Bilshan.shortDescription: Bimhal was a son of Japhlet, a descendant of Asher.

Bimhal.name: Bimhal
Bimhal.briefDescription: Descendant of King Saul and Jonathan
Bimhal.shortDescription: Binea was a descendant of King Saul and his son Jonathan.

Binea.name: Binea
Binea.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Binea.briefDescription: Israelite who divorced his foreign wife during Ezra's reforms
Binea.shortDescription: Binnui, one of the descendants of Pahath-moab, divorced his foreign wife during Ezra's reforms.

Binnui.name: Binnui
Binnui.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Binnui.briefDescription: King of Gomorrah during Abraham's time
Binnui.shortDescription: Birsha was the king of Gomorrah when Chedorlaomer and his allies invaded the region.

Birsha.name: Birsha
Birsha.description: King living at the time of the Patriarchs
Birsha.briefDescription: A son or town of Malchiel
Birsha.shortDescription: Birzaith was a town or Malchiel, descendant of Asher through his son Beriah.

Birzaith.name: Birzaith
Birzaith.description: Man or town at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Birzaith.briefDescription: Samaritan official who opposed the rebuilding of Jerusalem
Birzaith.shortDescription: Bishlam was a Samaritan official who, along with others, wrote to King Artaxerxes to oppose the rebuilding of Jerusalem.

Bishlam.name: Bishlam
Bishlam.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bishlam.briefDescription: Daughter of Pharaoh who married Mered of Judah
Bishlam.shortDescription: Bithiah, a daughter of Pharaoh, married Mered from the tribe of Judah.

Bithiah.name: Bithiah
Bithiah.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Bithiah.shortDescription: Biztha was one of the seven eunuchs who served King Ahasuerus.

Biztha.name: Biztha
Biztha.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Biztha.briefDescription: Herod's chamberlain who helped the people of Tyre and Sidon
Biztha.shortDescription: Blastus, Herod's chamberlain, was persuaded by the people of Tyre and Sidon to help them gain the king's favor.

Blastus.name: Blastus
Blastus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Blastus.briefDescription: Kinsman-redeemer who married Ruth, ancestor of David and Jesus
Blastus.shortDescription: Boaz, a wealthy Bethlehemite, married Ruth and became an ancestor of King David and Jesus Christ.

Boaz.name: Boaz
Boaz.briefDescription: One of two bronze pillars in Solomon's Temple
Boaz.shortDescription: Boaz was the name given to one of the two bronze pillars that stood at the entrance of Solomon's Temple (1Ki.7.21; 2Ch.3.17).

Bocheru.name: Bocheru
Bocheru.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Bocheru.briefDescription: Reubenite after whom a boundary stone was named
Bocheru.shortDescription: Bohan, son of Reuben, was the namesake of a boundary stone between the territories of Judah and Benjamin.

Bohan.name: Bohan
Bohan.briefDescription: High priest, son of Abishua and father of Uzzi
Bohan.shortDescription: Bukki, a high priest, was the son of Abishua and the father of Uzzi in the lineage of Aaron.

Bukki.name: Bukki
Bukki.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Bukki.briefDescription: Levite musician in David's time, son of Heman
Bukki.shortDescription: Bukkiah, a Levite and descendant of Heman, was a musician in the temple during David's reign.

Bukkiah.name: Bukkiah
Bukkiah.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Bukkiah.briefDescription: Son of Jerahmeel, descendant of Judah
Bukkiah.shortDescription: Bunah was a son of Jerahmeel and a descendant of Judah.

Bunah.name: Bunah
Bunah.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Bunah.briefDescription: Israelite leader who sealed the covenant during Nehemiah's time
Bunah.shortDescription: Bunni was one of the leaders of the people who sealed the covenant during the time of Nehemiah.

Bunni.name: Bunni
Bunni.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Bunni.briefDescription: Descendant of Gad, son of Abdiel
Bunni.shortDescription: Buz, a descendant of Gad, was the son of Abdiel and the father of Jahdo.

Buz.name: Buz
Buz.briefDescription: Arabian tribe, possibly descendants of Nahor
Buz.shortDescription: Buz was an Arabian tribe, possibly descended from Nahor, that was threatened with judgment.

Buzi.name: Buzi
Buzi.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Buzi.shortDescription: The Buzites were descendants of Buz, the son of Nahor, Abraham's brother.

Buzites.name: Buzites
Buzites.briefDescription: High priest during Jesus' ministry and trial
Buzites.shortDescription: Caiaphas, the high priest during Jesus' ministry, presided over Jesus' trial and played a key role in His crucifixion.

Caiaphas.name: Caiaphas
Caiaphas.description: High Priest living at the time of the New Testament
Caiaphas.briefDescription: Adam and Eve's firstborn son, killed his brother Abel
Caiaphas.shortDescription: Cain, the firstborn son of Adam and Eve, murdered his brother Abel and was cursed by God to be a wanderer.

Cain.name: Cain
Cain.description: Man living at the time before the Flood
Cain.briefDescription: Son of Arphaxad, ancestor of Jesus (Luke's genealogy)
Cain.shortDescription: Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, was an ancestor of Jesus Christ according to Luke's genealogy.

Cainan.name: Cainan
Cainan.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Cainan.briefDescription: Son of Zerah, grandson of Judah
Cainan.shortDescription: Calcol, a son of Zerah, was a grandson of Judah.

Calcol.name: Calcol
Calcol.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Calcol.briefDescription: Son of Hezron, descendant of Judah
Calcol.shortDescription: Caleb (Chelubai), the son of Hezron, was a descendant of Judah and the father of Hur.

Caleb.name: Caleb
Caleb.description: Joshua's compatriot living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness and Conquest
Caleb.briefDescription: Son of Ham; father of Sidon and Heth
Caleb.shortDescription: Canaan, the son of Ham, was the father of Sidon, Heth, and other Canaanite tribes.

Canaan.name: Canaan
Canaan.briefDescription: Ancient region; descendants of Ham; promised to Abraham's descendants
Canaan.shortDescription: Canaan was the ancient region between the Jordan River and the Mediterranean Sea, named after Noah's grandson and later promised to Abraham's descendants.

Canaanite_woman.name: Canaanite_woman
Canaanite_woman.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Canaanite_woman.briefDescription: Descendants of Mizraim who displaced the Avvim
Canaanite_woman.shortDescription: The Caphtorim, descendants of Mizraim, displaced the Avvim who lived in villages as far as Gaza.

Caphtorim.name: Caphtorim
Caphtorim.shortDescription: Carkas was one of the seven eunuchs who served King Ahasuerus.

Carkas.name: Carkas
Carkas.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Carkas.briefDescription: Son of Reuben; father of the Carmites
Carkas.shortDescription: Carmi, the son of Reuben, was the father of the Carmite clan.

Carmi.name: Carmi
Carmi.shortDescription: Carpus was a friend of Paul in Troas, with whom Paul left his cloak, books, and parchments.

Carpus.name: Carpus
Carpus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Carpus.shortDescription: Carshena was one of the seven princes of Persia and Media who had access to King Ahasuerus' presence.

Carshena.name: Carshena
Carshena.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Carshena.briefDescription: Descendants of Mizraim, ancestor of Philistines
Carshena.shortDescription: The Casluhim, descendants of Mizraim, were the ancestors of the Philistines.

Casluhim.name: Casluhim
Casluhim.briefDescription: King of Elam, led coalition against Sodom and Gomorrah
Casluhim.shortDescription: Chedorlaomer, king of Elam, led a coalition of kings against Sodom and Gomorrah and was later defeated by Abraham.

Chedorlaomer.name: Chedorlaomer
Chedorlaomer.description: King living at the time of the Patriarchs
Chedorlaomer.briefDescription: Israelite who married a foreign wife during Ezra's time
Chedorlaomer.shortDescription: Chelal was one of the Israelites who had married a foreign woman during the time of Ezra.

Chelal.name: Chelal
Chelal.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Chelal.briefDescription: Overseer of King David's farm workers
Chelal.shortDescription: Chelub the Ishmaelite was in charge of King David's farm workers.

Chelub.name: Chelub
Chelub.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Chelub.briefDescription: A man who married a foreign woman
Chelub.shortDescription: Cheluhi was among those who married foreign women during the Exile.

Cheluhi.name: Cheluhi
Cheluhi.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Cheluhi.briefDescription: Son of Bilhan, descendant of Benjamin
Cheluhi.shortDescription: Chenaanah was a son of Bilhan and descendant of Benjamin.

Chenaanah.name: Chenaanah
Chenaanah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Chenaanah.briefDescription: Levite who led the people in confession
Chenaanah.shortDescription: Chenani was a Levite who led the people in confession and worship.

Chenani.name: Chenani
Chenani.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Chenani.briefDescription: Levite leader of singing and administration under David
Chenani.shortDescription: Chenaniah was a Levite leader in charge of singing and administration during David's reign.

Chenaniah.name: Chenaniah
Chenaniah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Chenaniah.briefDescription: Son of Dishon, descendant of Esau
Chenaniah.shortDescription: Cheran was a son of Dishon and a descendant of Esau.

Cheran.name: Cheran
Cheran.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Cheran.briefDescription: Philistine tribe, often serving as royal guard
Cheran.shortDescription: The Cherethites were a Philistine tribe that often served as royal guards.

Cherethite.name: Cherethite
Cherethite.description: A military group in the Old Testament
Cherethite.shortDescription: Chesed was a son of Nahor, Abraham's brother, and Milcah.

Chesed.name: Chesed
Chesed.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Chesed.briefDescription: Son of David and Abigail the Carmelitess
Chesed.shortDescription: Chileab, also known as Daniel, was David's son by Abigail the Carmelitess.

Chileab.name: Chileab
Chileab.description: David's son living at the time of United Monarchy
Chileab.briefDescription: Son of Elimelech and Naomi; husband of Orpah
Chileab.shortDescription: Chilion was the son of Elimelech and Naomi, and the husband of Orpah.

Chilion.name: Chilion
Chilion.briefDescription: Barzillai's son, accompanied David to Jerusalem
Chilion.shortDescription: Chimham, son of Barzillai, accompanied David to Jerusalem after Absalom's rebellion.

Chimham.name: Chimham
Chimham.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Chimham.briefDescription: Father of Elidad, a leader from Benjamin
Chimham.shortDescription: Chislon was the father of Elidad, a leader from the tribe of Benjamin.

Chislon.name: Chislon
Chislon.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Chislon.briefDescription: Believer whose household reported divisions in Corinthian church
Chislon.shortDescription: Chloe's household informed Paul about divisions in the Corinthian church.

Chloe.name: Chloe
Chloe.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Chloe.briefDescription: Herod's steward, whose wife Joanna supported Jesus
