Chloe.shortDescription: Chuza, Herod's steward, was the husband of Joanna, who supported Jesus' ministry.

Chuza.name: Chuza
Chuza.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Chuza.briefDescription: Believer in Rome who sent greetings to Timothy
Chuza.shortDescription: Claudia, along with others, sent greetings to Timothy in Paul's letter.

Claudia.name: Claudia
Claudia.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Claudia.shortDescription: Claudius was the Roman emperor during part of Paul's ministry.

Claudius.name: Claudius
Claudius.description: Emperor living at the time of the New Testament
Claudius.briefDescription: Fellow laborer with Paul in the gospel
Claudius.shortDescription: Clement was a fellow laborer with Paul, whose name was in the Book of Life.

Clement.name: Clement
Clement.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Clement.briefDescription: Ancestor of Maaseiah, who settled in Jerusalem
Clement.shortDescription: Col-hozeh was an ancestor of Maaseiah, who settled in Jerusalem after the exile.

Col-hozeh.name: Col-hozeh
Col-hozeh.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Col-hozeh.briefDescription: Levite in charge of offerings during Hezekiah's reign
Col-hozeh.shortDescription: Conaniah, a Levite, was in charge of the offerings and distributions during Hezekiah's reign.

Conaniah.name: Conaniah
Conaniah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Conaniah.briefDescription: Centurion, first Gentile convert to Christianity
Conaniah.shortDescription: Cornelius, a centurion, was the first Gentile convert to Christianity through Peter's ministry.

Cornelius.name: Cornelius
Cornelius.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Cornelius.briefDescription: Ancestor of Jesus in Luke's genealogy
Cornelius.shortDescription: Cosam is listed as an ancestor of Jesus in Luke's genealogy.

Cosam.name: Cosam
Cosam.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Cosam.briefDescription: Midianite woman killed for immorality with an Israelite
Cosam.shortDescription: Cozbi, a Midianite woman, was killed along with an Israelite man for their immorality.

Cozbi.name: Cozbi
Cozbi.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Cozbi.briefDescription: Companion of Paul who went to Galatia
Cozbi.shortDescription: Crescens, a companion of Paul, went to Galatia during Paul's imprisonment.

Crescens.name: Crescens
Crescens.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Crescens.briefDescription: Synagogue leader in Corinth who believed in Jesus
Crescens.shortDescription: Crispus, the synagogue leader in Corinth, believed in Jesus and was baptized by Paul.

Crispus.name: Crispus
Crispus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Crispus.briefDescription: Son of Ham; father of Nimrod and other descendants
Crispus.shortDescription: Cush, a son of Ham, was the father of Nimrod and other descendants who became nations.

Cush.name: Cush
Cush.briefDescription: Ancient region in Africa; associated with Egypt and Babylon
Cush.shortDescription: Cush, often translated as Ethiopia, was an ancient region in Africa, mentioned in various contexts throughout the Bible.

Cushan-rishathaim.name: Cushan-rishathaim
Cushan-rishathaim.briefDescription: Ancestor of Jehudi, who read Jeremiah's scroll
Cushan-rishathaim.shortDescription: Cushi was the great grandfather of Jehudi, who was sent to fetch Baruch to read Jeremiah's scroll.

Cushi.name: Cushi
Cushi.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Cushi.briefDescription: Persian Emperor who decreed the rebuilding of the Temple
Cushi.shortDescription: Cyrus, Emperor of Persia, issued a decree allowing the Jews to return and rebuild the Temple in Jerusalem.

Cyrus.name: Cyrus
Cyrus.description: Emperor living at the time of Exile and Return
Cyrus.briefDescription: Son of Haman, enemy of the Jews
Cyrus.shortDescription: Dalphon was one of the ten sons of Haman who were killed and hanged.

Dalphon.name: Dalphon
Dalphon.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Dalphon.briefDescription: Woman who believed Paul's message in Athens
Dalphon.shortDescription: Damaris was a woman who believed Paul's message in Athens and joined him.

Damaris.name: Damaris
Damaris.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Damaris.briefDescription: Fifth son of Jacob, founder of Israelite tribe
Damaris.shortDescription: Dan was the fifth son of Jacob and the founder of the Israelite tribe bearing his name.

Dan.name: Dan
Dan.briefDescription: Israelite tribe; city in northern Israel; originally called Laish/Leshem.
Dan.shortDescription: Dan, an Israelite tribe and a city in northern Israel, formerly called Laish or Leshem.

Daniel.name: Daniel
Daniel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Daniel.briefDescription: Son of Zerah, grandson of Judah
Daniel.shortDescription: Dara was a son of Zerah and grandson of Judah.

Dara.name: Dara
Dara.briefDescription: Wise man surpassed by Solomon's wisdom
Dara.shortDescription: Darda was a wise man, but Solomon's wisdom surpassed his.

Darda.name: Darda
Darda.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Darda.briefDescription: Mede who became Emperor over Babylon
Darda.shortDescription: Darius the Mede received the Empire of Babylon and ruled over it.

Darius.name: Darius
Darius.description: Emperor living at the time of Exile and Return
Darius.briefDescription: Ancestor of a family of temple servants
Darius.shortDescription: Darkon was the ancestor of a family of temple servants who returned from the Babylonian exile.

Darkon.name: Darkon
Darkon.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Darkon.briefDescription: Reubenite who rebelled against Moses and Aaron
Darkon.shortDescription: Dathan, a Reubenite, joined Korah's rebellion against Moses and Aaron and was punished by God.

Dathan.name: Dathan
Dathan.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Dathan.briefDescription: The wife of Caiaphas the high priest.
Dathan.shortDescription: A reference to the unnamed wife of Caiaphas, the high priest who condemned Jesus.

daughter_of_Annas.name: daughter_of_Annas
daughter_of_Annas.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
daughter_of_Annas.shortDescription: The daughter of Machir married Hezron and gave birth to Segub.

daughter_of_Machir.name: daughter_of_Machir
daughter_of_Machir.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness - unnamed daughter of Machir
daughter_of_Machir.briefDescription: Married Jehohanan, son of Tobiah the Ammonite
daughter_of_Machir.shortDescription: The daughter of Meshullam was married to Jehohanan, the son of Tobiah the Ammonite.

daughter_of_Meshullam.name: daughter_of_Meshullam
daughter_of_Meshullam.description: Woman living at the time of Exile and Return - unnamed daughter of Meshullam
daughter_of_Meshullam.briefDescription: Daughter of Pharaoh - Wife of King Solomon
daughter_of_Meshullam.shortDescription: Solomon married the daughter of Pharaoh, forming a political alliance with Egypt.

daughter_of_Pharaoh.name: daughter_of_Pharaoh
daughter_of_Pharaoh.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness - unnamed daughter of Pharaoh
daughter_of_Pharaoh.briefDescription: Daughter of Putiel - Wife of Eleazar the priest
daughter_of_Pharaoh.shortDescription: The daughter of Putiel was the wife of Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest.

daughter_of_Putiel.name: daughter_of_Putiel
daughter_of_Putiel.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness - unnamed daughter of Putiel
daughter_of_Putiel.briefDescription: Married to a grandson of the high priest Eliashib
daughter_of_Putiel.shortDescription: The daughter of Sanballat the Horonite was married to a grandson of the high priest Eliashib.

daughter_of_Sanballat.name: daughter_of_Sanballat
daughter_of_Sanballat.description: Woman living at the time of Exile and Return - unnamed daughter of Sanballat
daughter_of_Sanballat.briefDescription: Married to one of Tobiah's sons
daughter_of_Sanballat.shortDescription: The daughter of Shecaniah was married to one of Tobiah the Ammonite's sons.

daughter_of_Shecaniah.name: daughter_of_Shecaniah
daughter_of_Shecaniah.description: Woman living at the time of Exile and Return - unnamed daughter of Shecaniah
daughter_of_Shecaniah.briefDescription: Elder daughter of Lot; mother of Moab
daughter_of_Shecaniah.shortDescription: Lot's unnamed elder daughter, who bore Moab after the destruction of Sodom.

daughter1_of_Lot.name: daughter1_of_Lot
daughter1_of_Lot.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs - unnamed older daughter of Lot
daughter1_of_Lot.briefDescription: Younger daughter of Lot; mother of Ben-ammi
daughter1_of_Lot.shortDescription: Lot's unnamed younger daughter, who bore Ben-ammi after the destruction of Sodom.

daughter2_of_Lot.name: daughter2_of_Lot
daughter2_of_Lot.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs - unnamed younger daughter of Lot
daughter2_of_Lot.briefDescription: Anointed king of Israel, ancestor of Jesus Christ
daughter2_of_Lot.shortDescription: David, son of Jesse, was the anointed king of Israel, a man after God's own heart, and an ancestor of Jesus Christ.

David.name: David
David.description: King living at the time of United Monarchy
David.briefDescription: Amorite king of Eglon, defeated by Joshua
David.shortDescription: Debir was the Amorite king of Eglon who joined a coalition against Joshua but was defeated.

Debir.name: Debir
Debir.briefDescription: A place on the northern border of Judah.
Debir.shortDescription: Debir, a place on the northern border of Judah, distinct from the city of Debir (Joshua 15:7).

Deborah.name: Deborah
Deborah.briefDescription: Grandson of Cush, son of Raamah
Deborah.shortDescription: Dedan was a grandson of Cush and a son of Raamah.

Dedan.name: Dedan
Dedan.briefDescription: An Arabian people and region, involved in trade.
Dedan.shortDescription: Dedan was an Arabian people and region, known for their involvement in trade.

Dehavite.name: Dehavite
Dehavite.description: Word sometimes translated as a name
Dehavite.briefDescription: Son of Abinadab, one of Solomon's twelve officers
Dehavite.shortDescription: Ben-Deker was one of Solomon's twelve officers in charge of providing food for the king and his household.

Deker.name: Deker
Deker.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Deker.briefDescription: Priest in charge of the twenty-third division
Deker.shortDescription: Delaiah was a priest appointed by David to be in charge of the twenty-third division of the priestly order.

Delaiah.name: Delaiah
Delaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Delaiah.shortDescription: Delilah was a Philistine woman who deceived Samson into revealing the secret of his strength, leading to his capture.

Delilah.name: Delilah
Delilah.briefDescription: Companion of Paul who later deserted him
Delilah.shortDescription: Demas was a companion of Paul who later deserted him because he loved the world.

Demas.name: Demas
Demas.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Demas.briefDescription: Silversmith who opposed Paul in Ephesus
Demas.shortDescription: Demetrius, a silversmith in Ephesus, stirred up opposition against Paul because his preaching threatened the business of making shrines of Artemis.

Demetrius.name: Demetrius
Demetrius.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Demetrius.briefDescription: Father of Eliasaph, leader of the tribe of Gad
Demetrius.shortDescription: Deuel, also called Reuel, was the father of Eliasaph, who was the leader of the tribe of Gad during the wilderness journey.

Deuel.name: Deuel
Deuel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Deuel.shortDescription: Diblaim was the father of Gomer, the woman whom the prophet Hosea married at God's command.

Diblaim.name: Diblaim
Diblaim.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Diblaim.briefDescription: Father of Shelomith, whose son blasphemed God's name
Diblaim.shortDescription: Dibri, of the tribe of Dan, was the father of Shelomith, whose son blasphemed the name of God and was stoned to death.

Dibri.name: Dibri
Dibri.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Dibri.shortDescription: Diklah was a descendant of Shem through Joktan, mentioned in the genealogies of Genesis and 1 Chronicles.

Diklah.name: Diklah
Diklah.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Diklah.shortDescription: Dinah, the daughter of Jacob and Leah, was raped by Shechem, leading to a violent confrontation between her brothers and the Shechemites.

Dinah.name: Dinah
Dinah.description: Jacob's daughter living at the time of the Patriarchs
Dinah.briefDescription: People group who opposed the rebuilding of Jerusalem
Dinah.shortDescription: The Dinaites were one of the groups who wrote to King Artaxerxes, opposing the rebuilding of Jerusalem during Ezra's time.

Dinaite.name: Dinaite
Dinaite.description: Word sometimes translated as a name
Dinaite.briefDescription: Areopagite who believed Paul's message in Athens
Dinaite.shortDescription: Dionysius the Areopagite was one of the people who believed Paul's message when he preached in Athens.

Dionysius.name: Dionysius
Dionysius.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Dionysius.shortDescription: Diotrephes was a church leader who loved to be first and refused to welcome John and his companions.

Diotrephes.name: Diotrephes
Diotrephes.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Diotrephes.shortDescription: Dishan was a descendant of Seir the Horite, mentioned in the genealogies of Esau's family.

Dishan.name: Dishan
Dishan.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Dishan.briefDescription: Descendant of Seir the Horite, son of Anah
Dishan.shortDescription: Dishon was a descendant of Seir the Horite and the son of Anah, mentioned in the genealogies of Esau's family.

Dishon.name: Dishon
Dishon.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Dishon.briefDescription: Descendants of Javan, son of Japheth
Dishon.shortDescription: The Dodanim were descendants of Javan, son of Japheth, mentioned in the Table of Nations.

Dodanim.name: Dodanim
Dodanim.briefDescription: Prophet who rebuked Jehoshaphat for his alliance with Ahaziah
Dodanim.shortDescription: Dodavahu, son of Eliezer of Mareshah, prophesied against Jehoshaphat for his alliance with Ahaziah, king of Israel.

Dodavahu.name: Dodavahu
Dodavahu.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Dodavahu.briefDescription: Father of Elhanan, one of David's mighty men
Dodavahu.shortDescription: Dodo the Bethlehemite was the father of Elhanan, one of David's mighty men.

Dodo.name: Dodo
Dodo.briefDescription: Edomite chief shepherd who betrayed David to Saul
Dodo.shortDescription: Doeg the Edomite, Saul's chief shepherd, witnessed David receiving help from Ahimelech and later betrayed him to Saul, resulting in the massacre of the priests at Nob.

Doeg.name: Doeg
Doeg.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Doeg.briefDescription: Jewish wife of Felix, the Roman governor
Doeg.shortDescription: Drusilla, the Jewish wife of Felix, the Roman governor, was present when Paul spoke to Felix about faith in Christ.

Drusilla.name: Drusilla
Drusilla.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Drusilla.briefDescription: Son of Ishmael and grandson of Abraham
Drusilla.shortDescription: Dumah was a son of Ishmael and grandson of Abraham, mentioned in the genealogies of Genesis and Chronicles.

Dumah.name: Dumah
Dumah.briefDescription: A city in the hill country of Judah.
Dumah.shortDescription: Dumah, a city located in the hill country of Judah (Joshua 15:52).

Ebal.name: Ebal
Ebal.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Ebal.briefDescription: Descendant of Adin who returned with Ezra
Ebal.shortDescription: Ebed, a descendant of Adin, was among those who returned to Jerusalem with Ezra during the second wave of the return from Babylonian exile.

Ebed.name: Ebed
Ebed.briefDescription: Ethiopian eunuch who rescued Jeremiah from a cistern
Ebed.shortDescription: Ebed-melech, an Ethiopian eunuch in King Zedekiah's service, rescued the prophet Jeremiah from a cistern and was promised safety by God for his faith.

Ebed-melech.name: Ebed-melech
Ebed-melech.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ebed-melech.briefDescription: A Gadite chief during the reign of Jotham
Ebed-melech.shortDescription: Eber was a Gadite chief during the reign of Jotham, king of Judah, mentioned in the genealogy of Gad.

Eber.name: Eber
Eber.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Eber.briefDescription: Levite descendant of Korah, ancestor of temple gatekeepers
Eber.shortDescription: Ebiasaph, also known as Abiasaph or Asaph, was a Levite descendant of Korah and an ancestor of temple gatekeepers.

Ebiasaph.name: Ebiasaph
Ebiasaph.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ebiasaph.briefDescription: Levite who assisted in Hezekiah's temple cleansing
Ebiasaph.shortDescription: Eden, a Levite from the family of Joah, assisted in the cleansing of the temple during Hezekiah's reign.

Eden.name: Eden
Eden.briefDescription: The perfect garden where God placed Adam and Eve.
Eden.shortDescription: Eden, the perfect garden created by God, where He placed Adam and Eve (Genesis 2:8, 10, 15; 3:23-24; 4:16; Isaiah 51:3; Ezk.28.13; 31:9, 16, 18; 36:35; Jol.2.3).

Eder.name: Eder
Eder.briefDescription: A town in the Negev region of Judah.
Eder.shortDescription: Eder, a town located in the Negev region of Judah (Joshua 15:21).

Eglah.name: Eglah
Eglah.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
Eglah.briefDescription: King of Moab who oppressed Israel
Eglah.shortDescription: Eglon, king of Moab, oppressed Israel for 18 years until Ehud, a left-handed Benjamite, assassinated him and delivered Israel.

Eglon.name: Eglon
Eglon.briefDescription: Amorite king of Lachish; city in Judah
Eglon.shortDescription: Eglon, an Amorite king of Lachish defeated by Joshua, also a city in Judah.

Egypt.name: Egypt
Egypt.briefDescription: Ancient nation; Israelites enslaved there; plagues and Exodus
Egypt.shortDescription: Egypt, a powerful nation where the Israelites were enslaved until God delivered them in the Exodus.

Ehud.name: Ehud
Ehud.briefDescription: Son of Ram, descendant of Judah
Ehud.shortDescription: Eker was a son of Ram and descendant of Judah.

Eker.name: Eker
Eker.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Eker.briefDescription: Son of Uzzi, an official under Solomon
Eker.shortDescription: Ela, the son of Uzzi, served as an official under King Solomon.

Ela.name: Ela
Ela.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ela.briefDescription: Son of Caleb; father of Kenaz
Ela.shortDescription: Elah was a son of Caleb and the father of Kenaz.

Elah.name: Elah
Elah.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Elah.briefDescription: A temple gatekeeper during David's reign
Elah.shortDescription: Elam was a temple gatekeeper during the reign of King David.

Elam.name: Elam
Elam.briefDescription: Ancient kingdom east of Babylon; ally and enemy of Israel
Elam.shortDescription: Elam, an ancient nation east of Babylon, at times an ally and at times an enemy of Israel.

Elasah.name: Elasah
Elasah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Elasah.briefDescription: Son of Midian, grandson of Abraham
Elasah.shortDescription: Eldaah was a son of Midian and grandson of Abraham.

Eldaah.name: Eldaah
Eldaah.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Eldaah.briefDescription: Prophet who prophesied in the Israelite camp
Eldaah.shortDescription: Eldad was a prophet who prophesied in the Israelite camp during the time of Moses.

Eldad.name: Eldad
Eldad.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Eldad.briefDescription: Ephraimite killed by the men of Gath
Eldad.shortDescription: Elead was an Ephraimite who was killed by the men of Gath.

Elead.name: Elead
Elead.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Elead.briefDescription: Son of Ephraim, ancestor of Joshua
Elead.shortDescription: Eleadah was a son of Ephraim and an ancestor of Joshua.

Eleadah.name: Eleadah
Eleadah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Eleadah.briefDescription: Son of Helez, a descendant of Judah
Eleadah.shortDescription: Eleasah was the son of Helez, a descendant of Judah.

Eleasah.name: Eleasah
Eleasah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Eleasah.briefDescription: Son of Mahli, had no sons, only daughters
Eleasah.shortDescription: Eleazar, son of Mahli, had no sons, only daughters.

Eleazar.name: Eleazar
Eleazar.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Eleazar.briefDescription: Warrior from Bethlehem who killed Goliath's brother Lahmi
Eleazar.shortDescription: Elhanan, a warrior from Bethlehem, killed Lahmi, the brother of Goliath the Gittite.

Elhanan.name: Elhanan
Elhanan.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Elhanan.briefDescription: High priest and judge of Israel at Shiloh
Elhanan.shortDescription: Eli was the high priest and judge of Israel who ministered at the tabernacle in Shiloh.

Eli.name: Eli
Eli.description: High Priest living at the time of United Monarchy
Eli.briefDescription: Gadite warrior who joined David at Ziklag
Eli.shortDescription: Eliab was a Gadite warrior who joined David at Ziklag.

Eliab.name: Eliab
Eliab.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Eliab.briefDescription: Father of Rezon, an adversary of Solomon
Eliab.shortDescription: Eliada was the father of Rezon, who became an adversary to King Solomon.

Eliada.name: Eliada
Eliada.description: David's son living at the time of United Monarchy
Eliada.briefDescription: Shaalbonite, one of David's mighty men
Eliada.shortDescription: Eliahba the Shaalbonite was one of David's mighty men.

Eliahba.name: Eliahba
Eliahba.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Eliahba.shortDescription: Eliakim was the palace administrator under King Hezekiah during the Assyrian invasion.

Eliakim.name: Eliakim
Eliakim.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Eliakim.briefDescription: Father of Bathsheba, wife of Uriah the Hittite
Eliakim.shortDescription: Eliam, also known as Ammiel, was the father of Bathsheba, who later became King David's wife.

Eliam.name: Eliam
Eliam.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Eliam.briefDescription: Son of Deuel, leader of the tribe of Gad
Eliam.shortDescription: Eliasaph, the son of Deuel, was the leader of the tribe of Gad during the wilderness journey.

Eliasaph.name: Eliasaph
Eliasaph.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Eliasaph.shortDescription: Eliashib was a priest who served during the reign of King David.

Eliashib.name: Eliashib
Eliashib.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Eliashib.briefDescription: Son of Heman, appointed as a musician in David's time
Eliashib.shortDescription: Eliathah, a son of Heman, was appointed as a musician during King David's reign.

Eliathah.name: Eliathah
Eliathah.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Eliathah.briefDescription: Son of Chislon, leader from the tribe of Benjamin
Eliathah.shortDescription: Elidad, the son of Chislon, was a leader from the tribe of Benjamin chosen to help divide the Promised Land.

Elidad.name: Elidad
Elidad.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Elidad.briefDescription: Son of Meshelemiah, a gatekeeper in David's time
Elidad.shortDescription: Eliehoenai, a son of Meshelemiah, was a gatekeeper during King David's reign.

Eliehoenai.name: Eliehoenai
Eliehoenai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Eliehoenai.briefDescription: The Mahavite, one of David's mighty men
Eliehoenai.shortDescription: Eliel the Mahavite was one of David's mighty men.

Eliel.name: Eliel
Eliel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Eliel.shortDescription: Elienai was a Benjamite, a descendant of Shimei.

Elienai.name: Elienai
Elienai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Elienai.briefDescription: A priest who blew trumpets before the ark.
Elienai.shortDescription: Eliezer was a priest who blew trumpets before the ark of God.

Eliezer.name: Eliezer
Eliezer.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Eliezer.shortDescription: Elihoreph was one of King Solomon's scribes.

Elihoreph.name: Elihoreph
Elihoreph.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Elihoreph.briefDescription: A Manassite who joined David at Ziklag.
Elihoreph.shortDescription: Elihu was one of the Manassites who joined David at Ziklag.

Elihu.name: Elihu
Elihu.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Elihu.shortDescription: Elijah was a Benjamite, one of the sons of Jeroham.

Elijah.name: Elijah
Elijah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Elijah.briefDescription: A Harodite, one of David's mighty men.
Elijah.shortDescription: Elika the Harodite was one of David's mighty men.

Elika.name: Elika
Elika.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Elika.shortDescription: Elimelech was Naomi's husband from Bethlehem who died in Moab.

Elimelech.name: Elimelech
Elimelech.briefDescription: A descendant of David through Jeconiah.
Elimelech.shortDescription: Elioenai was a descendant of David through Jeconiah, mentioned in the genealogy.

Elioenai.name: Elioenai
Elioenai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Elioenai.briefDescription: One of David's mighty men, son of Ur.
Elioenai.shortDescription: Eliphal, son of Ur, was one of David's mighty men.

Eliphal.name: Eliphal
Eliphal.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Eliphal.shortDescription: Eliphaz was the firstborn son of Esau and the father of several Edomite clans.

Eliphaz.name: Eliphaz
Eliphaz.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Eliphaz.briefDescription: A Levite musician during David's reign.
Eliphaz.shortDescription: Eliphelehu was a Levite musician who played the harp during the transfer of the ark to Jerusalem.

Eliphelehu.name: Eliphelehu
Eliphelehu.description: Singer living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Eliphelehu.briefDescription: A descendant of King Saul from the tribe of Benjamin.
Eliphelehu.shortDescription: Eliphelet was a descendant of King Saul from the tribe of Benjamin.

Eliphelet.name: Eliphelet
Eliphelet.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Eliphelet.shortDescription: Elisha was a prophet who performed many miracles and served as a spiritual leader in Israel after Elijah.

Elisha.name: Elisha
Elisha.description: Prophet living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Elisha.briefDescription: A son of Javan, who is grandson of Noah.
Elisha.shortDescription: Elishah was a son of Javan and great-grandson of Noah.

Elishah.name: Elishah
Elishah.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Elishah.briefDescription: A descendant of Jerahmeel from the tribe of Judah.
Elishah.shortDescription: Elishama was a descendant of Jerahmeel from the tribe of Judah.

Elishama.name: Elishama
Elishama.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Elishama.briefDescription: One of the captains who helped Jehoiada overthrow Athaliah.
Elishama.shortDescription: Elishaphat was one of the captains who helped Jehoiada the priest overthrow Queen Athaliah.

Elishaphat.name: Elishaphat
Elishaphat.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Elishaphat.shortDescription: Elisheba was the wife of Aaron and the daughter of Amminadab and sister of Nahshon.

Elisheba.name: Elisheba
Elisheba.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Elisheba.briefDescription: A son of David born in Jerusalem.
Elisheba.shortDescription: Elishua, also known as Elishama, was a son of David born to him in Jerusalem.

Elishua.name: Elishua
Elishua.description: David's son living at the time of United Monarchy
Elishua.shortDescription: Eliud was an ancestor of Jesus Christ, mentioned in the genealogy of Jesus in the Gospel of Matthew.

Eliud.name: Eliud
Eliud.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Eliud.briefDescription: The wife of Zechariah and mother of John the Baptist.
Eliud.shortDescription: Elizabeth was the wife of Zechariah, a priest, and the mother of John the Baptist.

Elizabeth.name: Elizabeth
Elizabeth.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Elizabeth.briefDescription: A Levite leader from the Kohathite clan.
Elizabeth.shortDescription: Elizaphan was a Levite leader from the Kohathite clan, responsible for various duties in the tabernacle and temple.

Elizaphan.name: Elizaphan
Elizaphan.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Elizaphan.briefDescription: A leader from the tribe of Reuben.
Elizaphan.shortDescription: Elizur, son of Shedeur, was a leader from the tribe of Reuben during the wilderness journey.

Elizur.name: Elizur
Elizur.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Elizur.briefDescription: A Korahite who joined David at Ziklag.
Elizur.shortDescription: Elkanah was a Korahite who joined David at Ziklag while he was fleeing from Saul.

Elkanah.name: Elkanah
Elkanah.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Elkanah.shortDescription: Elmadam was an ancestor of Jesus Christ, mentioned in the genealogy in Luke's Gospel.

Elmadam.name: Elmadam
Elmadam.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Elmadam.briefDescription: Father of Jeribai and Joshaviah, David's mighty men.
Elmadam.shortDescription: Elnaam was the father of Jeribai and Joshaviah, two of David's mighty men.

Elnaam.name: Elnaam
Elnaam.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Elnaam.briefDescription: Father of Nehushta, King Jehoiachin's mother.
Elnaam.shortDescription: Elnathan was the father of Nehushta, the mother of King Jehoiachin of Judah.

Elnathan.name: Elnathan
Elnathan.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Elnathan.shortDescription: Elon the Hittite was the father of Basemath, one of Esau's wives.

Elon.name: Elon
Elon.briefDescription: City in the territory of Dan
Elon.shortDescription: Elon, a city allotted to the tribe of Dan during the division of the Promised Land.

Elpaal.name: Elpaal
Elpaal.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Elpaal.briefDescription: A son of David born in Jerusalem.
Elpaal.shortDescription: Elpelet, also known as Eliphelet, was a son of David born in Jerusalem.

Elpelet.name: Elpelet
Elpelet.description: David's son living at the time of United Monarchy
Elpelet.briefDescription: A Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag.
Elpelet.shortDescription: Eluzai, a Benjamite warrior, was one of the mighty men who joined David at Ziklag.

Eluzai.name: Eluzai
Eluzai.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Eluzai.briefDescription: A Gadite warrior who joined David at Ziklag.
Eluzai.shortDescription: Elzabad, a Gadite warrior, was one of the mighty men who joined David at Ziklag.

Elzabad.name: Elzabad
Elzabad.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Elzabad.briefDescription: An ancient people group defeated by Chedorlaomer.
Elzabad.shortDescription: The Emim were a tall and numerous people who inhabited the region of Moab before the Moabites.

Emim.name: Emim
Emim.briefDescription: Father of Ahira, a leader of the tribe of Naphtali.
Emim.shortDescription: Enan was the father of Ahira, the leader of the tribe of Naphtali during the wilderness journey.

Enan.name: Enan
Enan.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Enan.briefDescription: Son of Cain, and a city named after him.
Enan.shortDescription: Enoch was the son of Cain, and Cain built a city and named it after him.

Enoch.name: Enoch
Enoch.description: Man living at the time before the Flood
Enoch.briefDescription: Son of Seth, during whose time people called on the Lord's name.
Enoch.shortDescription: Enosh was the son of Seth, and during his time people began to call upon the name of the Lord.

Enosh.name: Enosh
Enosh.description: Man living at the time before the Flood
Enosh.briefDescription: A believer in Asia and beloved by Paul.
Enosh.shortDescription: Epaenetus was the first convert to Christ in Asia and was beloved by Paul.

Epaenetus.name: Epaenetus
Epaenetus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Epaenetus.briefDescription: A faithful minister and fellow servant of Paul.
Epaenetus.shortDescription: Epaphras was a faithful minister of Christ and a fellow prisoner with Paul, who labored in prayer for the Colossians.

Epaphras.name: Epaphras
Epaphras.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Epaphras.briefDescription: A messenger and minister to Paul from Philippi.
Epaphras.shortDescription: Epaphroditus was a beloved brother, fellow worker, and fellow soldier who ministered to Paul's needs on behalf of the Philippians.

Epaphroditus.name: Epaphroditus
Epaphroditus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Epaphroditus.briefDescription: Caleb's concubine and mother of three sons.
Epaphroditus.shortDescription: Ephah was a concubine of Caleb who bore him three sons: Haran, Moza, and Gazez.

Ephah.name: Ephah
Ephah.briefDescription: Arabian tribe; known for their camels and dromedaries
Ephah.shortDescription: Ephah, an Arabian tribe known for their camels and dromedaries, mentioned in Isaiah's prophecy of nations coming to Jerusalem.

Ephai.name: Ephai
Ephai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ephai.briefDescription: Son of Ezrah, in the genealogy of Judah.
Ephai.shortDescription: Epher was one of the sons of Ezrah, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Epher.name: Epher
Epher.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Epher.briefDescription: Son of Zabad, in the genealogy of Judah.
Epher.shortDescription: Ephlal was the son of Zabad, who was a descendant of Judah through Jerahmeel.

Ephlal.name: Ephlal
Ephlal.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ephlal.briefDescription: A leader from the tribe of Manasseh.
Ephlal.shortDescription: Ephod son of Hanniel was appointed as the leader from the tribe of Manasseh to assist in dividing the land of Canaan.

Ephod.name: Ephod
Ephod.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ephod.briefDescription: Second son of Joseph, and a tribe of Israel.
Ephod.shortDescription: Ephraim was the second son of Joseph, and his descendants formed one of the twelve tribes of Israel, often playing a prominent role in the nation's history.

Ephraim.name: Ephraim
Ephraim.briefDescription: City near the Judean wilderness; Jesus withdrew there
Ephraim.shortDescription: Ephraim, a city near the Judean wilderness where Jesus withdrew after the raising of Lazarus.

Ephrathah.name: Ephrathah
Ephrathah.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ephrathah.briefDescription: A Hittite who sold a burial site to Abraham.
Ephrathah.shortDescription: Ephron the Hittite sold the cave of Machpelah to Abraham as a burial site for Sarah.

Ephron.name: Ephron
Ephron.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Ephron.briefDescription: Judah's grandson, ancestor of linen workers.
Ephron.shortDescription: Er was Judah's grandson, ancestor of linen workers at Beth-ashbea.

Er.name: Er
Er.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Er.briefDescription: Son of Shuthelah, grandson of Ephraim; head of clan.
Er.shortDescription: Eran was a son of Shuthelah and grandson of Ephraim, who became the head of the Eranite clan.

Eran.name: Eran
Eran.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Eran.briefDescription: Companion of Paul; served as city treasurer in Corinth.
Eran.shortDescription: Erastus was a companion of Paul who served as the city treasurer in Corinth.

Erastus.name: Erastus
Erastus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Erastus.briefDescription: Son of Gad; head of the Erite clan.
Erastus.shortDescription: Eri was a son of Gad and the head of the Erite clan.

Eri.name: Eri
Eri.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Eri.briefDescription: King of Assyria; son and successor of Sennacherib.
Eri.shortDescription: Esarhaddon was the king of Assyria, the son and successor of Sennacherib.

Esarhaddon.name: Esarhaddon
Esarhaddon.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Esarhaddon.briefDescription: Isaac's firstborn son; sold birthright to Jacob; became Edom.
Esarhaddon.shortDescription: Esau, also called Edom, was Isaac's firstborn son who sold his birthright to his twin brother Jacob and became the father of the Edomites.

Esau.name: Esau
Esau.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Esau.briefDescription: Son of Dishon, a Horite chief in Seir.
Esau.shortDescription: Eshban was a son of Dishon and a Horite chief who lived in the land of Seir.

Eshban.name: Eshban
Eshban.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Eshban.briefDescription: Amorite ally of Abram; valley named after him.
Eshban.shortDescription: Eshcol was an Amorite, an ally of Abram, and the valley of Eshcol was named after him.

Eshcol.name: Eshcol
Eshcol.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Eshcol.briefDescription: Son of Mehir; father of Beth-rapha, Paseah, and Tehinnah.
Eshcol.shortDescription: Eshton was the son of Mehir and the father of Beth-rapha, Paseah, and Tehinnah.

Eshton.name: Eshton
Eshton.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Eshton.shortDescription: Esli was an ancestor of Jesus Christ, mentioned in His genealogy.

Esli.name: Esli
Esli.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Esli.briefDescription: Jewish queen of Persia; saved her people from genocide.
Esli.shortDescription: Esther, also known as Hadassah, was a Jewish queen of Persia who saved her people from genocide.

Esther.name: Esther
Esther.description: Woman living at the time of Exile and Return
Esther.briefDescription: Son of Zerah; grandson of Judah.
Esther.shortDescription: Ethan was a son of Zerah and a grandson of Judah.

Ethan.name: Ethan
Ethan.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ethan.briefDescription: King of Sidon; father of Jezebel.
Ethan.shortDescription: Ethbaal was the king of Sidon and the father of Jezebel, who married King Ahab of Israel.

Ethbaal.name: Ethbaal
Ethbaal.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ethbaal.briefDescription: Son of Ashhur, the founder of Tekoa.
Ethbaal.shortDescription: Ethnan was a son of Ashhur (or 'Ashur'), the  father (ie founder) of Tekoa.

Ethnan.name: Ethnan
Ethnan.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ethnan.briefDescription: Greeted by Paul in his letter to Timothy.
Ethnan.shortDescription: Eubulus was a Christian who sent greetings to Timothy through Paul's letter.

Eubulus.name: Eubulus
Eubulus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Eubulus.briefDescription: Mother of Timothy; a Jewish believer.
Eubulus.shortDescription: Eunice was the mother of Timothy and a Jewish believer.

Eunice.name: Eunice
Eunice.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Eunice.briefDescription: Christian woman in Philippi; urged by Paul to reconcile.
Eunice.shortDescription: Euodia was a Christian woman in Philippi whom Paul urged to reconcile with Syntyche.

Euodia.name: Euodia
Euodia.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Euodia.briefDescription: Young man in Troas; fell from window; restored by Paul.
Euodia.shortDescription: Eutychus was a young man in Troas who fell from a window and was restored to life by Paul.

Eutychus.name: Eutychus
Eutychus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Eutychus.briefDescription: First woman; wife of Adam; tempted by the serpent.
Eutychus.shortDescription: Eve was the first woman, created as Adam's wife, who was tempted by the serpent and ate the forbidden fruit.

Eve.name: Eve
Eve.description: Woman living at the time before the Flood
Eve.briefDescription: Midianite king killed in battle against Israel.
Eve.shortDescription: Evi was one of the Midianite kings killed by the Israelites during the time of Moses.

Evi.name: Evi
Evi.description: King living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Evi.briefDescription: King of Babylon; released Jehoiachin from prison.
Evi.shortDescription: Evil-merodach was a king of Babylon who released Jehoiachin, the former king of Judah, from prison.

Evil-merodach.name: Evil-merodach
Evil-merodach.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Evil-merodach.briefDescription: Father of Naarai, one of David's mighty men.
Evil-merodach.shortDescription: Ezbai was the father of Naarai, who was one of David's mighty men.

Ezbai.name: Ezbai
Ezbai.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ezbai.briefDescription: Son of Bela, grandson of Benjamin.
Ezbai.shortDescription: Ezbon was a son of Bela and a grandson of Benjamin.

Ezbon.name: Ezbon
Ezbon.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ezbon.briefDescription: Prophet during Babylonian exile; author of the Book of Ezekiel.
Ezbon.shortDescription: Ezekiel was a prophet during the Babylonian exile who authored the Book of Ezekiel and served as a sign to the people of Israel.

Ezekiel.name: Ezekiel
Ezekiel.description: Prophet living at the time of Exile and Return
Ezekiel.briefDescription: Gadite warrior; joined David at Ziklag.
Ezekiel.shortDescription: Ezer was a Gadite warrior who joined David at Ziklag when he was fleeing from King Saul.

Ezer.name: Ezer
Ezer.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Ezer.briefDescription: Adino the Eznite; one of David's mighty men.
Ezer.shortDescription: Adino the Eznite, also known as Josheb-Basshebeth, was one of David's mighty men who wielded his spear against eight hundred men.

Eznites.name: Eznites
Eznites.briefDescription: Priest; scribe; led a group of exiles back to Jerusalem.
Eznites.shortDescription: Ezra was a priest and scribe who led a group of Jewish exiles back to Jerusalem and played a key role in the spiritual reformation of the people.

Ezra.name: Ezra
Ezra.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Ezra.briefDescription: Son of Chelub; mentioned in Judah's genealogy.
Ezra.shortDescription: Ezrah was a son of Chelub, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Ezrah.name: Ezrah
Ezrah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ezrah.shortDescription: Ezri was the overseer of King David's agricultural workers who tilled the soil.

Ezri.name: Ezri
Ezri.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ezri.briefDescription: A close relative of John Mark.
Ezri.shortDescription: A reference to the grandfather or other close relative of John Mark.

father_of_Barnabas.name: father_of_Barnabas
father_of_Barnabas.description: Levite living at the time of the New Testament - unnamed father of Barnabas
father_of_Barnabas.briefDescription: Father of Conaniah, a Levite in charge of offerings
father_of_Barnabas.shortDescription: The father of Conaniah, a Levite who supervised the offerings during King Hezekiah's reign.

father_of_Conaniah.name: father_of_Conaniah
father_of_Conaniah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy - unnamed father
father_of_Conaniah.briefDescription: The wife of Zechariah and mother of John the Baptist.
father_of_Conaniah.shortDescription: Elizabeth was the wife of Zechariah, a priest, and the mother of John the Baptist.

father_of_Elizabeth.name: father_of_Elizabeth
father_of_Elizabeth.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
father_of_Elizabeth.briefDescription: Father of Eunice - Grandfather of Timothy
father_of_Elizabeth.shortDescription: Eunice's father, unnamed in the Bible, was the maternal grandfather of Timothy.

father_of_Eunice.name: father_of_Eunice
father_of_Eunice.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament - unnamed father of Eunice
father_of_Eunice.shortDescription: The father of Hilkiah, who was the cousin of the prophet Jeremiah.

father_of_Hilkiah.name: father_of_Hilkiah
father_of_Hilkiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy - unnamed father
father_of_Hilkiah.shortDescription: An unnamed man, the father Naham and his sister Hodiah

father_of_Hodiah.name: father_of_Hodiah
father_of_Hodiah.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy - unnamed father
father_of_Hodiah.briefDescription: The father of Mary, Martha and Lazarus of Bethany.
father_of_Hodiah.shortDescription: The unnamed father of Mary, Martha and Lazarus, who lived in Bethany.

father_of_Lazarus.name: father_of_Lazarus
father_of_Lazarus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament - unnamed father
father_of_Lazarus.briefDescription: Father of Mamre - Amorite, ally of Abraham
father_of_Lazarus.shortDescription: Mamre's father, an Amorite, was an ally of Abraham during the battle against Chedorlaomer.

father_of_Mamre.name: father_of_Mamre
father_of_Mamre.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs - unnamed father
father_of_Mamre.briefDescription: Father of Nathan, one of David's mighty men
father_of_Mamre.shortDescription: The father of Nathan, one of King David's mighty men.

father_of_Nathan.name: father_of_Nathan
father_of_Nathan.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy - unnamed father
father_of_Nathan.briefDescription: Egyptian Pharaoh who gave his daughter to Hadad
father_of_Nathan.shortDescription: The father of Tahpenes, an Egyptian Pharaoh who gave his daughter in marriage to Hadad the Edomite.

father_of_Tahpenes.name: father_of_Tahpenes
father_of_Tahpenes.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy - unnamed father
father_of_Tahpenes.briefDescription: Father of Timothy - Greek man; husband of Eunice
father_of_Tahpenes.shortDescription: Timothy's father was a Greek man, and his mother was a believing Jewish woman named Eunice.

father_of_Timothy.name: father_of_Timothy
father_of_Timothy.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament - unnamed father of Timothy
father_of_Timothy.briefDescription: Roman governor of Judea; heard Paul's case.
father_of_Timothy.shortDescription: Felix was a Roman governor of Judea who heard the case against Paul and kept him in custody for two years.

Felix.name: Felix
Felix.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Felix.briefDescription: Roman governor of Judea; heard Paul's case; sent him to Rome.
Felix.shortDescription: Porcius Festus succeeded Felix as the Roman governor of Judea and heard Paul's case, ultimately sending him to Rome for trial.

Festus.name: Festus
Festus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Festus.briefDescription: Corinthian Christian; visited Paul in Ephesus.
Festus.shortDescription: Fortunatus was a Corinthian Christian who, along with Stephanas and Achaicus, visited Paul in Ephesus and refreshed his spirit.

Fortunatus.name: Fortunatus
Fortunatus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Fortunatus.briefDescription: Son of Ebed; led a revolt against Abimelech.
Fortunatus.shortDescription: Gaal, the son of Ebed, led a revolt against Abimelech, the ruler of Shechem, but was defeated and driven out of the city.

Gaal.name: Gaal
Gaal.briefDescription: Benjamite leader who settled in Jerusalem.
Gaal.shortDescription: Gabbai was a Benjamite leader who settled in Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Gabbai.name: Gabbai
Gabbai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Gabbai.briefDescription: Prophet during the reigns of David and Solomon.
Gabbai.shortDescription: Gad was a prophet who advised King David and wrote a book of his acts.

Gad.name: Gad
Gad.description: Jacob's son living at the time of the Patriarchs
Gad.briefDescription: Son of Susi; representative of Manasseh among the spies.
Gad.shortDescription: Gaddi, the son of Susi, was the representative of the tribe of Manasseh among the twelve spies sent to explore Canaan.

Gaddi.name: Gaddi
Gaddi.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Gaddi.briefDescription: Son of Sodi; representative of Zebulun among the spies.
Gaddi.shortDescription: Gaddiel, the son of Sodi, was the representative of the tribe of Zebulun among the twelve spies sent to explore Canaan.

Gaddiel.name: Gaddiel
Gaddiel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Gaddiel.briefDescription: Father of Menahem, king of Israel.
Gaddiel.shortDescription: Gadi was the father of Menahem, who became king of Israel after assassinating Shallum.

Gadi.name: Gadi
Gadi.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Gadi.shortDescription: Gaham was one of the sons born to Nahor, Abraham's brother, and his concubine Reumah.

Gaham.name: Gaham
Gaham.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Gaham.briefDescription: Ancestor of temple servants who returned from Babylonian exile.
Gaham.shortDescription: Gahar was an ancestor of a group of temple servants (Nethinim) who returned to Jerusalem from the Babylonian exile.

Gahar.name: Gahar
Gahar.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Gahar.briefDescription: Recipient of John's third epistle; commended for his faithfulness.
Gahar.shortDescription: Gaius, the recipient of John's third epistle, was commended for his faithfulness to the truth and his hospitality to traveling missionaries.

Gaius.name: Gaius
Gaius.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Gaius.briefDescription: Levite musician; descendant of Asaph; lived in Jerusalem.
Gaius.shortDescription: Galal, a Levite musician and descendant of Asaph, lived in Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Galal.name: Galal
Galal.description: Levite living at the time of Exile and Return
Galal.briefDescription: Roman proconsul of Achaia; refused to judge Paul.
Galal.shortDescription: Gallio, the Roman proconsul of Achaia, refused to judge Paul when the Jews brought charges against him in Corinth.

Gallio.name: Gallio
Gallio.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Gallio.briefDescription: Pharisee; teacher of the law; Paul's mentor.
Gallio.shortDescription: Gamaliel, a respected Pharisee and teacher of the law, was Paul's mentor and advised the Sanhedrin to be cautious in their treatment of the apostles.

Gamaliel.name: Gamaliel
Gamaliel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Gamaliel.briefDescription: Head of a priestly division in David's time.
Gamaliel.shortDescription: Gamul was the head of the twenty-second priestly division organized during King David's reign.

Gamul.name: Gamul
Gamul.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Gamul.briefDescription: One of David's mighty warriors; an Ithrite.
Gamul.shortDescription: Gareb the Ithrite was one of David's mighty warriors, known for his military prowess.

Gareb.name: Gareb
Gareb.shortDescription: Gareb, a hill mentioned in Jeremiah 31:39, was located near Jerusalem and was to be included within the city's future boundaries.

Garmites.name: Garmites
Garmites.briefDescription: Son of Eliphaz; grandson of Esau; Edomite chief.
Garmites.shortDescription: Gatam was a son of Eliphaz, grandson of Esau, and one of the chiefs of Edom.

Gatam.name: Gatam
Gatam.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Gatam.briefDescription: Son of Caleb and his concubine Ephah.
Gatam.shortDescription: Gazez was a son of Caleb and his concubine Ephah, born after Haran.

Gazez.name: Gazez
Gazez.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Gazez.briefDescription: Ancestor of temple servants who returned from Babylonian exile.
Gazez.shortDescription: Gazzam was an ancestor of a group of temple servants (Nethinim) who returned to Jerusalem from the Babylonian exile.

Gazzam.name: Gazzam
Gazzam.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Gazzam.briefDescription: Son of Uri; one of Solomon's twelve district governors.
Gazzam.shortDescription: Geber, the son of Uri, was one of the twelve district governors appointed by King Solomon to provide food for the royal household.

Geber.name: Geber
Geber.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Geber.briefDescription: Son of Jeduthun; a temple musician during David's reign.
Geber.shortDescription: Gedaliah, a son of Jeduthun, was one of the temple musicians appointed by King David to prophesy with lyres, harps, and cymbals.

Gedaliah.name: Gedaliah
Gedaliah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Gedaliah.briefDescription: Son of Abiel, the founder of Gibeon.
Gedaliah.shortDescription: Gedor was a son of Abiel (or 'Jeiel'), the  father (ie re-founder) of Gibeon, and an ancestor of King Saul.

Gedor.name: Gedor
Gedor.briefDescription: Town in Judah; home of Judahite clan members
Gedor.shortDescription: Gedor (or Geder), a town in the hill country of Judah, was the home of several members of Judahite clans.

Gehazi.name: Gehazi
Gehazi.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Gehazi.briefDescription: Father of Ammiel, the spy from the tribe of Dan.
Gehazi.shortDescription: Gemalli was the father of Ammiel, who represented the tribe of Dan as one of the twelve spies sent to explore Canaan.

Gemalli.name: Gemalli
Gemalli.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Gemalli.briefDescription: Son of Hilkiah; messenger sent by Zedekiah to Nebuchadnezzar.
Gemalli.shortDescription: Gemariah, the son of Hilkiah, was a messenger sent by King Zedekiah of Judah to deliver a letter to King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon.

Gemariah.name: Gemariah
Gemariah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Gemariah.briefDescription: Son of Hadad the Edomite; raised in Pharaoh's palace.
Gemariah.shortDescription: Genubath was the son of Hadad the Edomite and was raised in Pharaoh's palace alongside the Pharaoh's own children.

Genubath.name: Genubath
Genubath.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Genubath.briefDescription: Son of Bela, grandson of Benjamin.
Genubath.shortDescription: Gera was a son of Bela and a grandson of Benjamin.

Gera.name: Gera
Gera.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Gera.briefDescription: Son of Moses; father of Jonathan, a renegade priest.
Gera.shortDescription: Gershom was the firstborn son of Moses and Zipporah, and his descendant Jonathan served as a priest for the idolatrous Danites.

Gershom.name: Gershom
Gershom.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Gershom.briefDescription: Firstborn son of Levi; head of the Gershonite Levites.
Gershom.shortDescription: Gershon was the firstborn son of Levi and the ancestor of the Gershonite Levites, who were responsible for the tabernacle curtains, coverings, and related duties.

Gershon.name: Gershon
Gershon.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Gershon.briefDescription: Son of Jahdai, a descendant of Caleb.
Gershon.shortDescription: Geshan was a son of Jahdai, a wife of Caleb, from the tribe of Judah.

Geshan.name: Geshan
Geshan.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Geshan.briefDescription: Arab opponent of Nehemiah; tried to hinder wall rebuilding.
Geshan.shortDescription: Geshem the Arab, along with Sanballat and Tobiah, opposed Nehemiah's efforts to rebuild the walls of Jerusalem and tried to intimidate and discourage him.

Geshem.name: Geshem
Geshem.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Geshem.briefDescription: Son of Aram; grandson of Shem; part of Table of Nations.
Geshem.shortDescription: Gether was a son of Aram and a grandson of Shem, mentioned in the genealogies of the Table of Nations.

Gether.name: Gether
Gether.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Gether.briefDescription: Son of Machi; representative of Gad among the twelve spies.
Gether.shortDescription: Geuel, the son of Machi, was the representative of the tribe of Gad among the twelve spies sent by Moses to explore Canaan.

Geuel.name: Geuel
Geuel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Geuel.briefDescription: With Machbenah, daughters or towns of Sheva.
Geuel.shortDescription: Gibea, with Machbenah, daughters or cities founded by Sheva.

Gibea.name: Gibea
Gibea.description: Woman or town of the time of Divided Monarchy
Gibea.briefDescription: Son of Heman; a temple musician during David's reign.
Gibea.shortDescription: Giddalti was one of the sons of Heman, appointed by King David as a temple musician to prophesy with lyres, harps, and cymbals.

Giddalti.name: Giddalti
Giddalti.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Giddalti.briefDescription: Ancestor of temple servants who returned from Babylonian exile.
Giddalti.shortDescription: Giddel was an ancestor of a group of temple servants (Nethinim) who returned to Jerusalem from the Babylonian exile.

Giddel.name: Giddel
Giddel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Giddel.briefDescription: Judge of Israel; defeated Midianites; tested and reassured by God.
Giddel.shortDescription: Gideon, also called Jerubbaal or Jerubbesheth, was a judge of Israel who, with God's help, defeated the Midianites and delivered Israel from oppression.

Gideon.name: Gideon
Gideon.briefDescription: Leader of the tribe of Benjamin during the Exodus.
Gideon.shortDescription: Gideoni, the son of Abidan, was the leader of the tribe of Benjamin during the Israelites' journey through the wilderness.

Gideoni.name: Gideoni
Gideoni.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Gideoni.briefDescription: Priest and musician during the dedication of Jerusalem's wall.
Gideoni.shortDescription: Gilalai was a priest and musician who participated in the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem during Nehemiah's time.

Gilalai.name: Gilalai
Gilalai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Gilalai.briefDescription: Descendant of Manasseh; son of Michael; father of Jaroah.
Gilalai.shortDescription: Gilead, a descendant of Manasseh, was the son of Michael and the father of Jaroah, as recorded in the genealogy of the tribe of Gad.

Gilead.name: Gilead
Gilead.briefDescription: Mountainous region east of Jordan; tribal territory and personal name.
Gilead.shortDescription: Gilead refers to a mountainous region east of the Jordan River, as well as being a personal name for several individuals in the Old Testament.

Ginath.name: Ginath
Ginath.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ginath.briefDescription: A priest who sealed the covenant during Nehemiah's time.
Ginath.shortDescription: Ginnethon, a priest, was among those who sealed the covenant during Nehemiah's reforms.

Ginnethon.name: Ginnethon
Ginnethon.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Ginnethon.briefDescription: A Canaanite people group living in the Promised Land.
Ginnethon.shortDescription: The Girgashites were one of the Canaanite groups inhabiting the land promised to Abraham's descendants.

Girgashites.name: Girgashites
Girgashites.briefDescription: A people group David raided while living among the Philistines.
Girgashites.shortDescription: The Girzites were a people group David raided during his time living in Philistine territory.

Girzites.name: Girzites
Girzites.briefDescription: An overseer of the temple servants during Nehemiah's time.
Girzites.shortDescription: Gishpa was an overseer of the temple servants (Nethinim) during Nehemiah's time.

Gishpa.name: Gishpa
Gishpa.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Gishpa.briefDescription: A people group mentioned in the Old Testament.
Gishpa.shortDescription: The Gizonites are mentioned in 1Ch.11.34 without any additional context.

Gizonites.name: Gizonites
Gizonites.briefDescription: A descendant of Reuben, son of Joel.
Gizonites.shortDescription: Gog, a descendant of Reuben, is mentioned in the genealogy of Reuben in 1Ch.5.4.

Gog.name: Gog
Gog.briefDescription: A symbolic enemy of God's people in prophetic literature.
Gog.shortDescription: Gog is mentioned in the prophetic books of Ezekiel and Revelation as a symbolic enemy of God's people who will be defeated in the end times (Ezk.38.2, 3, 14, 16, 18; 39:1, 11; Revelation 20:8).

Goiim.name: Goiim
Goiim.briefDescription: A Philistine giant defeated by David.
Goiim.shortDescription: Goliath was the Philistine champion from Gath who was defeated by David in a battle.

Goliath.name: Goliath
Goliath.description: Giant living at the time of United Monarchy
Goliath.briefDescription: Son of Japheth; father of Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.
Goliath.shortDescription: Gomer, a son of Japheth, was the father of Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah, as listed in the Table of Nations.

Gomer.name: Gomer
Gomer.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Gomer.briefDescription: A descendant of Gad; father of Abdiel.
Gomer.shortDescription: Guni, a descendant of Gad, was the father of Abdiel, as mentioned in the genealogy of Gad.

Guni.name: Guni
Guni.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Guni.briefDescription: A descendant of Judah through Hezron.
Guni.shortDescription: Haahashtari was a descendant of Judah through Hezron, as listed in the genealogy in 1 Chronicles.

Haahashtari.name: Haahashtari
Haahashtari.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Haahashtari.briefDescription: A priest whose descendants were unable to prove their lineage.
Haahashtari.shortDescription: Habaiah was a priest whose descendants were excluded from the priesthood due to insufficient genealogical records.

Habaiah.name: Habaiah
Habaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Habaiah.briefDescription: A prophet who questioned God's justice.
Habaiah.shortDescription: Habakkuk, a prophet, wrote the book bearing his name, which records his dialogue with God about justice and faith.

Habakkuk.name: Habakkuk
Habakkuk.description: Prophet living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Habakkuk.briefDescription: Grandfather of Jaazaniah, a Rechabite leader.
Habakkuk.shortDescription: Habazziniah was the grandfather of Jaazaniah, a leader of the Rechabites during Jeremiah's time.

Habazziniah.name: Habazziniah
Habazziniah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Habazziniah.briefDescription: Father of Nehemiah, the governor of Judah.
Habazziniah.shortDescription: Hacaliah was the father of Nehemiah, who served as the governor of Judah during the Persian period.

Hacaliah.name: Hacaliah
Hacaliah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hacaliah.briefDescription: Father of Jehiel, one of David's family servants.
Hacaliah.shortDescription: Hachmoni was the father of Jehiel who, with Jonathan attended King David's sons.

Hachmoni.name: Hachmoni
Hachmoni.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hachmoni.briefDescription: A clan or family known for their mighty warriors.
Hachmoni.shortDescription: The Hachmonites, also known as the Tahchemonites, were a clan or family that produced some of David's mighty warriors.

Hachmonites.name: Hachmonites
Hachmonites.briefDescription: An Edomite prince who opposed Solomon.
Hachmonites.shortDescription: Hadad, an Edomite prince, fled to Egypt and later became an adversary to King Solomon.

Hadad.name: Hadad
Hadad.description: King living at the time of the Patriarchs
Hadad.briefDescription: King of Zobah, defeated by David.
Hadad.shortDescription: Hadadezer, king of Zobah, was defeated by King David in battle, and his allies, the Arameans, became subject to Israel.

Hadadezer.name: Hadadezer
Hadadezer.description: King living at the time of United Monarchy
Hadadezer.briefDescription: Father of Amasa, who opposed taking captives from Judah.
Hadadezer.shortDescription: Hadlai was the father of Amasa, who opposed taking captives from Judah during the reign of King Ahaz.

Hadlai.name: Hadlai
Hadlai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hadlai.briefDescription: Son of Joktan, a descendant of Shem.
Hadlai.shortDescription: Hadoram was a son of Joktan and a descendant of Shem, mentioned in the genealogies of Genesis and 1 Chronicles.

Hadoram.name: Hadoram
Hadoram.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Hadoram.briefDescription: A temple servant who returned from Babylonian exile.
Hadoram.shortDescription: Hagab was among the temple servants who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian captivity, as recorded in Ezra.

Hagab.name: Hagab
Hagab.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hagab.briefDescription: A temple servant who returned from Babylonian exile.
Hagab.shortDescription: Hagabah, also known as Hagaba, was among the temple servants who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian captivity.

Hagabah.name: Hagabah
Hagabah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hagabah.briefDescription: Sarah's Egyptian maidservant; mother of Ishmael.
Hagabah.shortDescription: Hagar, Sarah's Egyptian maidservant, bore Abraham a son named Ishmael and is also mentioned in Paul's allegory in Galatians.

Hagar.name: Hagar
Hagar.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Hagar.briefDescription: A prophet who encouraged the rebuilding of the temple.
Hagar.shortDescription: Haggai, a prophet during the post-exilic period, urged the people to rebuild the temple in Jerusalem.

Haggai.name: Haggai
Haggai.description: Prophet living at the time of Exile and Return
Haggai.briefDescription: A priest mentioned in Nehemiah's time.
Haggai.shortDescription: Haggedolim was a priest during the time of Nehemiah, mentioned in the list of priests and Levites.

Haggedolim.name: Haggedolim
Haggedolim.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Haggedolim.briefDescription: Son of Gad, grandson of Jacob.
Haggedolim.shortDescription: Haggi was a son of Gad and a grandson of Jacob, mentioned in the genealogies of Genesis and Numbers.

Haggi.name: Haggi
Haggi.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Haggi.shortDescription: Haggiah, a Levite descended from Merari, is mentioned in the genealogy of the Levites in 1 Chronicles.

Haggiah.name: Haggiah
Haggiah.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Haggiah.briefDescription: One of David's wives; mother of Adonijah.
Haggiah.shortDescription: Haggith was one of King David's wives and the mother of Adonijah, who attempted to claim the throne.

Haggith.name: Haggith
Haggith.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
Haggith.briefDescription: A Gadite, father of Mibhar, one of David's mighty men.
Haggith.shortDescription: Hagri was the father of Mibhar, one of David's mighty men from the tribe of Gad.

Hagri.name: Hagri
Hagri.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hagri.briefDescription: Father of Johanan, a leader who returned from Babylon.
Hagri.shortDescription: Hakkatan was the father of Johanan, a leader who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian exile.

Hakkatan.name: Hakkatan
Hakkatan.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hakkatan.briefDescription: A priest in the time of David.
Hakkatan.shortDescription: Hakkoz was a priest who served during the reign of King David, as mentioned in the priestly divisions.

Hakkoz.name: Hakkoz
Hakkoz.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hakkoz.briefDescription: A temple servant who returned from Babylonian exile.
Hakkoz.shortDescription: Hakupha was among the temple servants who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian captivity.

Hakupha.name: Hakupha
Hakupha.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hakupha.briefDescription: Father of Shallum, a leader who helped repair Jerusalem's wall.
Hakupha.shortDescription: Hallohesh was the father of Shallum, a leader who helped repair the wall of Jerusalem during Nehemiah's time.

Hallohesh.name: Hallohesh
Hallohesh.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hallohesh.briefDescription: One of Noah's three sons, father of Canaan.
Hallohesh.shortDescription: Ham, one of Noah's three sons, was the father of Canaan and the ancestor of various nations.

Ham.name: Ham
Ham.briefDescription: A place where Chedorlaomer defeated the Zuzim in battle.
Ham.shortDescription: Ham was a place where Chedorlaomer and his allies defeated the Zuzim in battle during the war of the kings (Genesis 14:5).

Haman.name: Haman
Haman.description: Persian living at the time of Exile and Return
Haman.briefDescription: A Canaanite people group descended from Canaan.
Haman.shortDescription: The Hamathites were a Canaanite people group descended from Canaan, son of Ham.

Hamathites.name: Hamathites
Hamathites.briefDescription: A Kenite clan, descendants of Salma.
Hamathites.shortDescription: Hammath was a Kenite clan, listed among the descendants of Salma, the  father (ie founder) of Bethlehem.

Hammath.name: Hammath
Hammath.briefDescription: A fortified city and Levitical town in Naphtali.
Hammath.shortDescription: Hammath, also known as Hammoth-dor or Hammon, was a fortified city in Naphtali allotted to the Levites (Joshua 19:35; 21:32; 19:28; 1Ch.6.76).

Hammedatha.name: Hammedatha
Hammedatha.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hammedatha.briefDescription: Father of Jerahmeel and Malchijah (or 'Malchiah'), officials during Jeremiah's time.
Hammedatha.shortDescription: Hammelech, meaning "the king," was the father of Jerahmeel and Malchijah (or 'Malchiah'), officials mentioned in the book of Jeremiah.

Hammelech.name: Hammelech
Hammelech.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hammelech.briefDescription: Sister of Gilead; mother of Ishhod, Abiezer, and Mahlah.
Hammelech.shortDescription: Hammolecheth, the sister of Gilead, was the mother of Ishhod, Abiezer, and Mahlah.

Hammolecheth.name: Hammolecheth
Hammolecheth.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Hammolecheth.briefDescription: Son of Mishma, a descendant of Simeon.
Hammolecheth.shortDescription: Hammuel, a descendant of Simeon, was the son of Mishma.

Hammuel.name: Hammuel
Hammuel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hammuel.briefDescription: A Hivite prince, father of Shechem.
Hammuel.shortDescription: Hamor, a Hivite prince, was the father and founder of Shechem and negotiated with Jacob's sons after Shechem's assault on Dinah.

Hamor.name: Hamor
Hamor.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Hamor.briefDescription: Son of Perez, grandson of Judah.
Hamor.shortDescription: Hamul was a son of Perez and a grandson of Judah, mentioned in the genealogies of Genesis, Numbers, and 1 Chronicles.

Hamul.name: Hamul
Hamul.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Hamul.briefDescription: Mother of Jehoahaz and Zedekiah, kings of Judah.
Hamul.shortDescription: Hamutal, the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah, was the mother of Jehoahaz and Zedekiah, two kings of Judah.

Hamutal.name: Hamutal
Hamutal.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hamutal.briefDescription: Jeremiah's cousin, from whom he bought a field.
Hamutal.shortDescription: Hanamel, Jeremiah's cousin, sold a field to the prophet as a sign of future restoration during the Babylonian siege.

Hanamel.name: Hanamel
Hanamel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hanamel.briefDescription: One of David's mighty warriors, son of Maacah.
Hanamel.shortDescription: Hanan, the son of Maacah, was one of King David's mighty warriors.

Hanan.name: Hanan
Hanan.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hanan.briefDescription: A musician in the temple during David's reign.
Hanan.shortDescription: Hanani was a temple musician during King David's reign, assigned to the twenty-fifth division.

Hanani.name: Hanani
Hanani.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hanani.briefDescription: Son of Heman, a musician in David's time
Hanani.shortDescription: Hananiah, a son of Heman, appointed as a musician during King David's reign.

Hananiah.name: Hananiah
Hananiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hananiah.briefDescription: The mother of the prophet Samuel.
Hananiah.shortDescription: Hannah, the wife of Elkanah, was the mother of the prophet Samuel, whom she dedicated to the Lord's service at a young age.

Hannah.name: Hannah
Hannah.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
Hannah.briefDescription: A descendant of Asher, son of Ulla.
Hannah.shortDescription: Hanniel, a descendant of Asher, was the son of Ulla, as mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Asher.

Hanniel.name: Hanniel
Hanniel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Hanniel.briefDescription: A son of Midian, grandson of Abraham.
Hanniel.shortDescription: Hanoch was one of the sons of Midian, a grandson of Abraham through his concubine Keturah.

Hanoch.name: Hanoch
Hanoch.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Hanoch.briefDescription: An Ammonite king who mistreated David's envoys.
Hanoch.shortDescription: Hanun, the Ammonite king, mistreated David's envoys, leading to a war between the Ammonites and Israel.

Hanun.name: Hanun
Hanun.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hanun.briefDescription: The head of the 18th priestly division.
Hanun.shortDescription: Happizzez was the head of the 18th division of priests during the time of David's organization of the priesthood.

Happizzez.name: Happizzez
Happizzez.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Happizzez.briefDescription: A descendant of Caleb, son of Caleb's concubine Ephah.
Happizzez.shortDescription: Haran was a descendant of Caleb, born to Caleb's concubine Ephah, along with his brothers Moza and Gazez.

Haran.name: Haran
Haran.briefDescription: A city in Mesopotamia, associated with Abraham and his family.
Haran.shortDescription: Haran was a city in Mesopotamia where Abraham and his family settled after leaving Ur, and from where Abraham later departed for Canaan (Genesis 11:31-32; 12:4-5; 27:43; 28:10; 29:4; Act.7.2, 4).

Hararites.name: Hararites
Hararites.briefDescription: A eunuch in King Ahasuerus' court.
Hararites.shortDescription: Harbona was one of the seven eunuchs who served King Ahasuerus, as mentioned in the book of Esther.

Harbona.name: Harbona
Harbona.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Harbona.briefDescription: A descendant of Caleb, son of Hur.
Harbona.shortDescription: Hareph, a descendant of Caleb, was the father of Beth-gader, as mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Hareph.name: Hareph
Hareph.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Hareph.briefDescription: A goldsmith whose son helped repair Jerusalem's wall.
Hareph.shortDescription: Harhaiah was a goldsmith whose son, Uzziel, was involved in repairing a section of Jerusalem's wall during Nehemiah's time.

Harhaiah.name: Harhaiah
Harhaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Harhaiah.shortDescription: Harhas or Hasrah was the father of Shallum's wife.

Harhas.name: Harhas
Harhas.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Harhas.briefDescription: Ancestor of temple servants who returned from exile.
Harhas.shortDescription: Harhur was an ancestor of temple servants who returned from Babylonian exile.

Harhur.name: Harhur
Harhur.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Harhur.briefDescription: A priestly division during David's reign.
Harhur.shortDescription: Harim was the name of a priestly division during King David's reign.

Harim.name: Harim
Harim.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Harim.briefDescription: One who sealed the covenant with Nehemiah.
Harim.shortDescription: Hariph was one of the individuals who sealed the covenant with Nehemiah.

Hariph.name: Hariph
Hariph.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hariph.shortDescription: Harnepher was a descendant of Asher, son of Zophah.

Harnepher.name: Harnepher
Harnepher.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Harnepher.briefDescription: Father of half the inhabitants of Manuhoth.
Harnepher.shortDescription: Haroeh or Reaiah was the father of half the inhabitants of Manuhoth.

Haroeh.name: Haroeh
Haroeh.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Haroeh.briefDescription: Ancestor of temple servants who returned from exile.
Haroeh.shortDescription: Harsha was an ancestor of temple servants who returned from Babylonian exile.

Harsha.name: Harsha
Harsha.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Harsha.shortDescription: Harum was a descendant of Judah, son of Coz.

Harum.name: Harum
Harum.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Harum.briefDescription: Father of Jedaiah, a repairer of Jerusalem's wall.
Harum.shortDescription: Harumaph was the father of Jedaiah, who repaired a section of Jerusalem's wall.

Harumaph.name: Harumaph
Harumaph.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Harumaph.briefDescription: A group of David's mighty men.
Harumaph.shortDescription: The Haruphites were a group of warriors among David's mighty men.

Haruphites.name: Haruphites
Haruphites.briefDescription: Father of Meshullemeth, wife of King Manasseh.
Haruphites.shortDescription: Haruz was the father of Meshullemeth, wife of King Manasseh of Judah.

Haruz.name: Haruz
Haruz.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Haruz.briefDescription: A descendant of King Jeconiah (Jehoiachin) of Judah.
Haruz.shortDescription: Hasadiah was a descendant of King Jeconiah (Jehoiachin) of Judah.

Hasadiah.name: Hasadiah
Hasadiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hasadiah.briefDescription: A musician in the temple, son of Jeduthun.
Hasadiah.shortDescription: Hashabiah was a musician in the temple, one of the sons of Jeduthun.

Hashabiah.name: Hashabiah
Hashabiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hashabiah.briefDescription: One who sealed the covenant with Nehemiah.
Hashabiah.shortDescription: Hashabnah was one of the individuals who sealed the covenant with Nehemiah.

Hashabnah.name: Hashabnah
Hashabnah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hashabnah.shortDescription: Hashabneiah repaired a section of Jerusalem's wall near his house.

Hashabneiah.name: Hashabneiah
Hashabneiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hashabneiah.briefDescription: A man who stood with Ezra during the reading of the Law.
Hashabneiah.shortDescription: Hashbaddanah was one of the men who stood with Ezra during the public reading of the Law.

Hashbaddanah.name: Hashbaddanah
Hashbaddanah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hashbaddanah.briefDescription: A descendant of King Jeconiah (Jehoiachin) of Judah.
Hashbaddanah.shortDescription: Hashubah was a descendant of King Jeconiah (Jehoiachin) of Judah.

Hashubah.name: Hashubah
Hashubah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hashubah.briefDescription: Ancestor of exiles who returned to Jerusalem.
Hashubah.shortDescription: Hashum was an ancestor of exiles who returned to Jerusalem from Babylon.

Hashum.name: Hashum
Hashum.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hashum.briefDescription: A leader from the tribe of Benjamin
Hashum.shortDescription: Hassenuah (also known as Hassenaah) was a leader from the tribe of Benjamin who lived in Jerusalem during the time of Nehemiah.

Hassenuah.name: Hassenuah
