Hassenuah.description: Man who lived in Senaah@Ezr.2.35-Neh before the time of Exile and Return
Hassenuah.briefDescription: A Levite who lived in Jerusalem after the exile.
Hassenuah.shortDescription: Hasshub was a Levite who lived in Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Hasshub.name: Hasshub
Hasshub.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hasshub.briefDescription: Ancestor of temple servants who returned from exile.
Hasshub.shortDescription: Hasupha was an ancestor of temple servants who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian exile.

Hasupha.name: Hasupha
Hasupha.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hasupha.briefDescription: A eunuch in the court of King Ahasuerus.
Hasupha.shortDescription: Hathach was a eunuch who served Queen Esther and acted as a messenger between her and Mordecai.

Hathach.name: Hathach
Hathach.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hathach.briefDescription: Son of Othniel, from the tribe of Judah.
Hathach.shortDescription: Hathath was a son of Othniel, the son of Kenaz, from the tribe of Judah.

Hathath.name: Hathath
Hathath.briefDescription: Ancestor of temple servants who returned from exile.
Hathath.shortDescription: Hatipha was an ancestor of temple servants who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian exile.

Hatipha.name: Hatipha
Hatipha.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hatipha.briefDescription: Ancestor of gatekeepers who returned from exile.
Hatipha.shortDescription: Hatita was an ancestor of gatekeepers who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian exile.

Hatita.name: Hatita
Hatita.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hatita.briefDescription: Ancestor of Solomon's servants who returned from exile.
Hatita.shortDescription: Hattil was an ancestor of Solomon's servants who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian exile.

Hattil.name: Hattil
Hattil.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hattil.briefDescription: A descendant of King Jeconiah (Jehoiachin) of Judah.
Hattil.shortDescription: Hattush was a descendant of King Jeconiah (Jehoiachin) of Judah.

Hattush.name: Hattush
Hattush.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hattush.briefDescription: A son of Joktan, descendant of Shem.
Hattush.shortDescription: Havilah was a son of Joktan, a descendant of Shem, mentioned in the Table of Nations.

Havilah.name: Havilah
Havilah.briefDescription: Region inhabited by Ishmaelites, raided by Saul
Havilah.shortDescription: Havilah was a region inhabited by the descendants of Ishmael (Gen.25.18), which was later raided by King Saul during his campaign against the Amalekites (1Sa.15.7).

Hazael.name: Hazael
Hazael.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hazael.briefDescription: Ancestor of Maaseiah, who lived in Jerusalem.
Hazael.shortDescription: Hazaiah was an ancestor of Maaseiah, a leader who lived in Jerusalem.

Hazaiah.name: Hazaiah
Hazaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hazaiah.briefDescription: A son of Joktan, descendant of Shem.
Hazaiah.shortDescription: Hazarmaveth was a son of Joktan, a descendant of Shem, mentioned in the Table of Nations.

Hazarmaveth.name: Hazarmaveth
Hazarmaveth.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Hazarmaveth.briefDescription: A Levite of the family of Shimei.
Hazarmaveth.shortDescription: Haziel was a Levite of the family of Shimei during the time of David.

Haziel.name: Haziel
Haziel.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Haziel.briefDescription: A son of Nahor, Abraham's brother.
Haziel.shortDescription: Hazo was a son of Nahor, the brother of Abraham.

Hazo.name: Hazo
Hazo.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Hazo.briefDescription: A woman mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.
Hazo.shortDescription: Hazzelelponi was a woman mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Hazzelelponi.name: Hazzelelponi
Hazzelelponi.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Hazzelelponi.briefDescription: Son of Mered, from the tribe of Judah.
Hazzelelponi.shortDescription: Heber was a son of Mered, from the tribe of Judah.

Heber.name: Heber
Heber.briefDescription: An ethnic group descended from Abraham.
Heber.shortDescription: The Hebrews were an ethnic group descended from Abraham, also known as Israelites or Jews.

Hebrews.name: Hebrews
Hebrews.briefDescription: Son of Mareshah, from the tribe of Judah.
Hebrews.shortDescription: Hebron was a son of Mareshah, a descendant of Caleb from the tribe of Judah.

Hebron.name: Hebron
Hebron.briefDescription: An important city in Judah; burial place of patriarchs.
Hebron.shortDescription: Hebron, also known as Kiriath-arba, was a significant city in Judah, associated with the patriarchs Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and later became a Levitical city and a city of refuge.

Hegai.name: Hegai
Hegai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hegai.briefDescription: One of the two wives of Ashhur.
Hegai.shortDescription: Helah was one of the two wives of Ashhur, the father of Tekoa, from the tribe of Judah.

Helah.name: Helah
Helah.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Helah.briefDescription: A returned exile who helped crown Joshua.
Helah.shortDescription: Heldai (or Helem), a returned exile, was one of those who provided silver and gold to make a crown for Joshua the high priest.

Heldai.name: Heldai
Heldai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Heldai.shortDescription: Heleb (also known as Heldai or Heled) was one of David's mighty warriors, a Netophathite.

Heleb.name: Heleb
Heleb.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Heleb.briefDescription: Son of Gilead, ancestor of the Helekites.
Heleb.shortDescription: Helek was a son of Gilead and ancestor of the Helekite clan in the tribe of Manasseh.

Helek.name: Helek
Helek.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Helek.briefDescription: A descendant of Jerahmeel from the tribe of Judah.
Helek.shortDescription: Helez was a descendant of Jerahmeel, son of Hezron, from the tribe of Judah.

Helez.name: Helez
Helez.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Helez.briefDescription: Father of Mary and maternal grandfather of Jesus.
Helez.shortDescription: Heli was the father of Mary and the maternal grandfather of Jesus.

Heli.name: Heli
Heli.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Heli.briefDescription: A priest during the time of Joiakim.
Heli.shortDescription: Helkai was a priest who served during the time of Joiakim the high priest.

Helkai.name: Helkai
Helkai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Helkai.briefDescription: Father of Eliab, a leader from the tribe of Zebulun.
Helkai.shortDescription: Helon was the father of Eliab, a leader from the tribe of Zebulun during the wilderness wanderings.

Helon.name: Helon
Helon.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Helon.briefDescription: A descendant of Seir the Horite.
Helon.shortDescription: Hemam was a descendant of Seir the Horite, mentioned in the genealogies of Esau.

Hemam.name: Hemam
Hemam.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Hemam.shortDescription: Heman was a grandson of Judah, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Heman.name: Heman
Heman.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Heman.briefDescription: A descendant of Seir the Horite.
Heman.shortDescription: Hemdan was a descendant of Seir the Horite, mentioned in the genealogies of Esau.

Hemdan.name: Hemdan
Hemdan.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Hemdan.briefDescription: A Levite whose sons assisted in rebuilding the temple.
Hemdan.shortDescription: Henadad was a Levite whose descendants helped rebuild the temple and signed the covenant during Nehemiah's time.

Henadad.name: Henadad
Henadad.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Henadad.briefDescription: A Mecherathite, one of David's mighty warriors.
Henadad.shortDescription: Hepher the Mecherathite was one of David's mighty warriors.

Hepher.name: Hepher
Hepher.briefDescription: A Canaanite city conquered by Joshua; later a Solomonic district.
Hepher.shortDescription: Hepher was a Canaanite city conquered by Joshua and later became part of a district under Solomon's rule.

Hephzibah.name: Hephzibah
Hephzibah.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hephzibah.briefDescription: A Levite who lived in Jerusalem.
Hephzibah.shortDescription: Heresh was a Levite who resided in Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Heresh.name: Heresh
Heresh.description: Levite living at the time of Exile and Return
Heresh.briefDescription: A Christian in Rome greeted by Paul.
Heresh.shortDescription: Hermas was a Christian in Rome to whom Paul sent greetings in his letter to the Romans.

Hermas.name: Hermas
Hermas.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Hermas.briefDescription: A Christian in Rome greeted by Paul.
Hermas.shortDescription: Hermes was a Christian in Rome to whom Paul sent greetings in his letter to the Romans.

Hermes.name: Hermes
Hermes.description: A male deity in the New Testament
Hermes.briefDescription: Greek god, associated with Paul and Barnabas
Hermes.shortDescription: In Act.14.12, the people of Lystra identified Paul as Hermes, the Greek god of messages and eloquence, because he was the chief speaker.

Hermogenes.name: Hermogenes
Hermogenes.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Hermogenes.briefDescription: A Tetrarch who persecuted the early church.
Hermogenes.shortDescription: Herod Agrippa I was a king who persecuted the early church, killing James and imprisoning Peter.

Herod.name: Herod
Herod.description: King Herod the Great: Ruler living at the time of the New Testament
Herod.briefDescription: Wife of Herod Antipas who had John beheaded.
Herod.shortDescription: Herodias was the wife of Herod Antipas, who had John the Baptist beheaded at her request.

Herodias.name: Herodias
Herodias.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Herodias.briefDescription: A relative of Paul in Rome.
Herodias.shortDescription: Herodion was a relative of Paul in Rome to whom Paul sent greetings.

Herodion.name: Herodion
Herodion.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Herodion.shortDescription: Hesed was one of the twelve district overseers who provided supplies for King Solomon and his household.

Hesed.name: Hesed
Hesed.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Hesed.briefDescription: Son of Canaan and ancestor of the Hittites.
Hesed.shortDescription: Heth was a son of Canaan and the ancestor of the Hittites, a prominent people group in the ancient Near East.

Heth.name: Heth
Heth.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Heth.shortDescription: Hezekiah was a king of Judah who instituted religious reforms and trusted in God during the Assyrian invasion.

Hezekiah.name: Hezekiah
Hezekiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hezekiah.briefDescription: Grandfather of Ben-Hadad, king of Aram.
Hezekiah.shortDescription: Hezion was the grandfather of Ben-Hadad, the king of Aram during the reign of Asa, king of Judah.

Hezion.name: Hezion
Hezion.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hezion.briefDescription: A priest in the time of David.
Hezion.shortDescription: Hezir was a priest who was assigned the 17th division of priestly service in the time of King David.

Hezir.name: Hezir
Hezir.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hezir.shortDescription: Hezro the Carmelite was one of David's mighty men, known for his valor and military prowess.

Hezro.name: Hezro
Hezro.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Hezro.briefDescription: Son of Perez, grandson of Judah, ancestor of David.
Hezro.shortDescription: Hezron was the son of Perez and grandson of Judah, and his descendants formed the Hezronite clan within the tribe of Judah.

Hezron.name: Hezron
Hezron.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Hezron.briefDescription: One of David's mighty men from Gaash.
Hezron.shortDescription: Hiddai (also called Hurai) from the brooks of Gaash was one of David's mighty men, known for his valor and military prowess.

Hiddai.name: Hiddai
Hiddai.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Hiddai.shortDescription: Hiel the Bethelite rebuilt Jericho during the reign of King Ahab, fulfilling Joshua's curse on the city.

Hiel.name: Hiel
Hiel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hiel.briefDescription: A Levite gatekeeper during David's reign.
Hiel.shortDescription: Hilkiah was a Levite who served as a gatekeeper in the tabernacle during the reign of King David.

Hilkiah.name: Hilkiah
Hilkiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hilkiah.shortDescription: Hillel the Pirathonite, a father of judge Abdon of Israel, led the nation for ten years.

Hillel.name: Hillel
Hillel.shortDescription: Hirah was an Adullamite who was a close friend of Judah, the son of Jacob.

Hirah.name: Hirah
Hirah.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Hirah.shortDescription: Hiram, a skilled craftsman from Tyre, was commissioned by Solomon to create the bronze furnishings for the temple.

Hiram.name: Hiram
Hiram.description: King living at the time of United Monarchy
Hiram.shortDescription: The Hivites were one of the Canaanite tribes inhabiting the land promised to the Israelites.

Hivites.name: Hivites
Hivites.shortDescription: Hizki was a descendant of Elpaal from the tribe of Benjamin.

Hizki.name: Hizki
Hizki.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hizki.briefDescription: A descendant of David through Jeconiah.
Hizki.shortDescription: Hizkiah was one of the descendants of King David through the line of Jeconiah.

Hizkiah.name: Hizkiah
Hizkiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hizkiah.shortDescription: Hobab, the son of Jethro (or 'Reuel') the Midianite and Moses' brother-in-law, was invited by Moses to join the Israelites on their journey to the Promised Land.

Hobab.name: Hobab
Hobab.description: Midianite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Hobab.shortDescription: Hod was a descendant of Asher, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Asher.

Hod.name: Hod
Hod.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hod.briefDescription: A descendant of David through Jeconiah.
Hod.shortDescription: Hodaviah (also known as Hodaiah) was one of the descendants of King David through the line of Jeconiah.

Hodaviah.name: Hodaviah
Hodaviah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hodaviah.shortDescription: Hodesh was one of the wives of Shaharaim, a Benjamite, and the mother of several of his children.

Hodesh.name: Hodesh
Hodesh.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hodesh.briefDescription: Wife of a Judahite named Ishbar.
Hodesh.shortDescription: Hodiah was the wife of Ishbar, a descendant of Judah, and the mother two clans.

Hodiah.name: Hodiah
Hodiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hodiah.briefDescription: Daughter of Zelophehad, of the tribe of Manasseh.
Hodiah.shortDescription: Hoglah was one of the five daughters of Zelophehad who received an inheritance in the Promised Land due to their father's lack of male heirs.

Hoglah.name: Hoglah
Hoglah.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Hoglah.briefDescription: King of Hebron, defeated by Joshua.
Hoglah.shortDescription: Hoham, the Amorite king of Hebron, was one of the five kings who formed an alliance against Joshua and the Israelites, but was ultimately defeated.

Hoham.name: Hoham
Hoham.briefDescription: A corrupt priest, son of Eli.
Hoham.shortDescription: Hophni, along with his brother Phinehas, was a corrupt priest who served under his father Eli at the tabernacle in Shiloh.

Hophni.name: Hophni
Hophni.description: Priest living at the time of United Monarchy
Hophni.shortDescription: Hophra, also known as Pharaoh Apries, was the king of Egypt during the time of the prophet Jeremiah and the Babylonian invasion of Judah.

Hophra.name: Hophra
Hophra.description: Pharaoh living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hophra.briefDescription: King of Gezer, defeated by Joshua.
Hophra.shortDescription: Horam, the king of Gezer, came to help Lachish but was defeated by Joshua and the Israelites.

Horam.name: Horam
Horam.shortDescription: The Horites were a people group who inhabited the region of Seir before the Edomites.

Hori.name: Hori
Hori.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Hori.briefDescription: A people group opposed to Nehemiah's work.
Hori.shortDescription: The Horonites were a people group who opposed Nehemiah's efforts to rebuild the walls of Jerusalem.

Horonites.name: Horonites
Horonites.briefDescription: A Levite gatekeeper during David's reign.
Horonites.shortDescription: Hosah was a Levite who served as a gatekeeper at the tabernacle during the reign of King David.

Hosah.name: Hosah
Hosah.shortDescription: Hosah, a Levitical city on the border of Asher (Joshua 19:29).

Hosea.name: Hosea
Hosea.description: Prophet living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hosea.briefDescription: A military leader during Jeremiah's time.
Hosea.shortDescription: Hoshaiah, the son of Maaseiah, was one of the military leaders who sought Jeremiah's counsel after the fall of Jerusalem but later disobeyed his prophetic warning.

Hoshaiah.name: Hoshaiah
Hoshaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hoshaiah.briefDescription: A descendant of King Jeconiah of Judah.
Hoshaiah.shortDescription: Hoshama was a descendant of Jeconiah, the exiled king of Judah, according to the genealogy in 1 Chronicles.

Hoshama.name: Hoshama
Hoshama.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hoshama.briefDescription: Leader of the half-tribe of Ephraim.
Hoshama.shortDescription: Hoshea, the son of Azaziah, was the leader of the half-tribe of Ephraim during King David's reign.

Hoshea.name: Hoshea
Hoshea.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Hoshea.briefDescription: One of David's mighty warriors from Aroer.
Hoshea.shortDescription: Hotham the Aroerite was listed among David's mighty warriors known for their military exploits.

Hotham.name: Hotham
Hotham.briefDescription: A son of Heman, a musician in David's court.
Hotham.shortDescription: Hothir was one of the fourteen sons of Heman, appointed by King David to lead worship in the temple.

Hothir.name: Hothir
Hothir.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hothir.briefDescription: A huge man from Gath, killed by David's warriors.
Hothir.shortDescription: A huge man from Gath, possibly a descendant of the Rephaim, was killed by David's warriors during a battle with the Philistines.

huge_man.name: huge_man
huge_man.description: Giant living at the time of United Monarchy
huge_man.briefDescription: A son of Aram, grandson of Shem.
huge_man.shortDescription: Hul was one of the sons of Aram and a grandson of Shem, mentioned in the genealogies of Genesis and 1 Chronicles.

Hul.name: Hul
Hul.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Hul.briefDescription: A prophetess during the reign of King Josiah.
Hul.shortDescription: Huldah was a prophetess who was consulted by King Josiah's officials regarding the authenticity of the Book of the Law discovered during temple repairs.

Huldah.name: Huldah
Huldah.description: Prophetess living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Huldah.shortDescription: Huppah was a priest who was assigned the thirteenth division of priestly service during King David's reign.

Huppah.name: Huppah
Huppah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Huppah.shortDescription: Huppim is mentioned in the genealogy of Manasseh as a descendant of Ir or as a son of Machir.

Huppim.name: Huppim
Huppim.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Huppim.briefDescription: One of Solomon's twelve district governors.
Huppim.shortDescription: Hur was one of the twelve district governors appointed by King Solomon to oversee the provision of food for the royal household.

Hur.name: Hur
Hur.description: King living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Hur.shortDescription: Huri was a descendant of Gad, mentioned in the genealogy of the Gadites.

Huri.name: Huri
Huri.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Huri.briefDescription: Husband of Hammolecheth - Father of Ishhod, Abiezer, and Mahlah
Huri.shortDescription: Hammolecheth's husband, unnamed in the Bible, was the father of Ishhod, Abiezer, and Mahlah.

husband_of_Hammolecheth.name: husband_of_Hammolecheth
husband_of_Hammolecheth.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness - unnamed husband of Hammolecheth
husband_of_Hammolecheth.briefDescription: Husband of Mary - John Mark's father
husband_of_Hammolecheth.shortDescription: Mary's husband, unnamed in the Bible, was the father of John Mark.

husband_of_Mary.name: husband_of_Mary
husband_of_Mary.description: Levite living at the time of the New Testament  - unnamed husband of Mary
husband_of_Mary.briefDescription: Husband of Matred - Mezahab, grandfather of Mehetabel
husband_of_Mary.shortDescription: Matred's husband was Mezahab, the grandfather of Mehetabel, who was the wife of an Edomite king.

husband_of_Matred.name: husband_of_Matred
husband_of_Matred.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs - unnamed husband of Matred
husband_of_Matred.briefDescription: Husband of Shimeath - Father of King Joash's assassins
husband_of_Matred.shortDescription: Shimeath's husband, unnamed in the Bible, was the father of Jozachar, one of the assassins of King Joash.

husband_of_Shimeath.name: husband_of_Shimeath
husband_of_Shimeath.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy - unnamed husband of Shimeath
husband_of_Shimeath.briefDescription: Husband of Zeruiah; father of Abishai and Joab
husband_of_Shimeath.shortDescription: The unnamed husband of Zeruiah, who was the father of David's mighty men, Abishai and Joab.

husband_of_Zeruiah.name: husband_of_Zeruiah
husband_of_Zeruiah.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
husband_of_Zeruiah.briefDescription: A son or town founded by Ezer.
husband_of_Zeruiah.shortDescription: Hushah was either a descendant of Judah or a town founded by Ezer in the tribe of.

Hushah.name: Hushah
Hushah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Hushah.briefDescription: David's friend and advisor who helped him during Absalom's rebellion.
Hushah.shortDescription: Hushai the Arkite was a loyal friend and advisor to King David, who helped him during Absalom's rebellion by countering the advice of Ahithophel.

Hushai.name: Hushai
Hushai.description: David's compatriot living at the time of United Monarchy
Hushai.shortDescription: Husham was a king of Edom who reigned before the Israelites had kings, according to the genealogies in Genesis and 1 Chronicles.

Husham.name: Husham
Husham.description: King living at the time of the Patriarchs
Husham.briefDescription: A clan or family known for their warriors.
Husham.shortDescription: The Hushathites were a clan or family that produced several of David's mighty warriors, including Sibbecai and Mebunnai.

Hushathites.name: Hushathites
Hushathites.briefDescription: A son of Aher, from the tribe of Dan.
Hushathites.shortDescription: Hushim, a son of Aher, is mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Dan in 1 Chronicles.

Hushim.name: Hushim
Hushim.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Hushim.briefDescription: A man who rejected faith and good conscience.
Hushim.shortDescription: Hymenaeus was a man who had rejected the faith and was handed over to Satan by Paul so that he might learn not to blaspheme.

Hymenaeus.name: Hymenaeus
Hymenaeus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Hymenaeus.briefDescription: One of King David's sons born in Jerusalem.
Hymenaeus.shortDescription: Ibhar was one of the sons born to David in Jerusalem, as recorded in 2 Samuel and 1 Chronicles.

Ibhar.name: Ibhar
Ibhar.description: David's son living at the time of United Monarchy
Ibhar.briefDescription: A Benjamite who lived in Jerusalem.
Ibhar.shortDescription: Ibneiah, a descendant of Benjamin, was one of the Israelites who resettled in Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Ibneiah.name: Ibneiah
Ibneiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Ibneiah.briefDescription: A Benjamite who lived in Jerusalem.
Ibneiah.shortDescription: Ibnijah, a descendant of Benjamin, was one of the Israelites who resettled in Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Ibnijah.name: Ibnijah
Ibnijah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Ibnijah.briefDescription: A Levite of the family of Merari.
Ibnijah.shortDescription: Ibri was a Levite of the family of Merari, mentioned in the context of the divisions of the Levitical priests during the reign of King David.

Ibri.name: Ibri
Ibri.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ibri.shortDescription: Ibsam was a descendant of Issachar, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Issachar in 1 Chronicles.

Ibsam.name: Ibsam
Ibsam.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ibsam.briefDescription: A judge of Israel from Bethlehem.
Ibsam.shortDescription: Ibzan was a judge of Israel who led the nation for seven years, had thirty sons and thirty daughters, and was buried in Bethlehem.

Ibzan.name: Ibzan
Ibzan.briefDescription: Son of Phinehas, born when the ark was captured.
Ibzan.shortDescription: Ichabod was the son of Phinehas, born when the ark of God was captured by the Philistines and named to reflect the departure of glory from Israel.

Ichabod.name: Ichabod
Ichabod.description: Levite living at the time of United Monarchy
Ichabod.shortDescription: Idbash was a descendant of Judah, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Judah in 1 Chronicles.

Idbash.name: Idbash
Idbash.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Idbash.briefDescription: Zechariah's son, a leader of Manasseh
Idbash.shortDescription: Iddo, the son of Zechariah, was a leader of the half-tribe of Manasseh during King David's reign.

Iddo.name: Iddo
Iddo.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Iddo.shortDescription: Igal was a descendant of King David, mentioned in the genealogy of David's descendants through his son Jeconiah.

Igal.name: Igal
Igal.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Igal.briefDescription: A man of God during Jeremiah's time.
Igal.shortDescription: Igdaliah, a man of God, was associated with a chamber in the house of the Lord during the time of the prophet Jeremiah.

Igdaliah.name: Igdaliah
Igdaliah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Igdaliah.briefDescription: Father of Ira, one of David's mighty warriors.
Igdaliah.shortDescription: Ikkesh the Tekoite was the father of Ira, one of King David's mighty warriors and a leader in his army.

Ikkesh.name: Ikkesh
Ikkesh.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Ikkesh.shortDescription: Imlah was the father of Micaiah, a prophet who spoke the truth to King Ahab of Israel, despite the king's displeasure.

Imlah.name: Imlah
Imlah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Imlah.briefDescription: A priestly division and its descendants.
Imlah.shortDescription: Immer was the name of a priestly division during the time of David, and his descendants served as priests in the post-exilic period.

Immer.name: Immer
Immer.briefDescription: A place from which exiles returned to Jerusalem
Immer.shortDescription: Immer, a place from which exiles returned to Jerusalem (Ezra 2:59; Nehemiah 7:61).

Imna.name: Imna
Imna.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Imna.shortDescription: Imnah, a Levite, was appointed as an overseer of the freewill offerings during the religious reforms of King Hezekiah.

Imnah.name: Imnah
Imnah.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Imnah.shortDescription: Imrah was a descendant of Asher, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Asher in 1 Chronicles.

Imrah.name: Imrah
Imrah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Imrah.briefDescription: A descendant of Judah who lived in Jerusalem.
Imrah.shortDescription: Imri, a descendant of Perez from the tribe of Judah, was among those who settled in Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Imri.name: Imri
Imri.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Imri.shortDescription: Iphdeiah was a descendant of Benjamin through Shashak.

Iphdeiah.name: Iphdeiah
Iphdeiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Iphdeiah.briefDescription: Son of Bela, descendant of Benjamin.
Iphdeiah.shortDescription: Ir was a son of Bela and descendant of Benjamin.

Ir.name: Ir
Ir.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Ir.shortDescription: Ira the Jairite was a priest under King David.

Ira.name: Ira
Ira.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ira.briefDescription: Descendant of Cain; father of Mehujael.
Ira.shortDescription: Irad was a descendant of Cain and the father of Mehujael.

Irad.name: Irad
Irad.description: Man living at the time before the Flood
Irad.shortDescription: Iram was an Edomite chief, a descendant of Esau.

Iram.name: Iram
Iram.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Iram.briefDescription: Son of Bela, grandson of Benjamin.
Iram.shortDescription: Iri was a son of Bela and grandson of Benjamin.

Iri.name: Iri
Iri.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Iri.briefDescription: Captain who arrested Jeremiah for desertion.
Iri.shortDescription: Irijah, a captain, arrested Jeremiah on suspicion of desertion to the Babylonians.

Irijah.name: Irijah
Irijah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Irijah.briefDescription: Son of Caleb, brother of Elah and Naam.
Irijah.shortDescription: Iru was a son of Caleb, along with his brothers Elah and Naam.

Iru.name: Iru
Iru.briefDescription: Son of Abraham; father of Jacob and Esau.
Iru.shortDescription: Isaac was the son of Abraham and Sarah, and the father of Jacob and Esau. God made a covenant with him, renewing the promises given to Abraham.

Isaac.name: Isaac
Isaac.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Isaac.briefDescription: Prophet during reigns of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, Hezekiah.
Isaac.shortDescription: Isaiah was a prophet in Judah during the reigns of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah. His prophecies are recorded in the biblical book bearing his name.

Isaiah.name: Isaiah
Isaiah.description: Prophet living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Isaiah.briefDescription: Daughter of Haran, Milcah's sister, Lot's sister.
Isaiah.shortDescription: Iscah was the daughter of Haran and sister of Milcah and Lot.

Iscah.name: Iscah
Iscah.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Iscah.briefDescription: Son of Mered in the genealogy of Judah
Iscah.shortDescription: Ishbah's sons were the  father (ie founder) of Eshtemoa and Keilah, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah..

Ishbah.name: Ishbah
Ishbah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ishbah.shortDescription: Ishbak was a son of Abraham and his concubine Keturah.

Ishbak.name: Ishbak
Ishbak.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Ishbak.briefDescription: Philistine giant who fought against David's men.
Ishbak.shortDescription: Ishbi-benob, a Philistine giant, was killed by David's warrior Abishai when he threatened David's life.

Ishbi-benob.name: Ishbi-benob
Ishbi-benob.description: Giant living at the time of United Monarchy
Ishbi-benob.briefDescription: Son of Saul, king over Israel.
Ishbi-benob.shortDescription: Ish-bosheth (also called Eshbaal), Saul's son, was made king over Israel by Abner but was ultimately assassinated, leading to David's rule over all Israel.

Ish-bosheth.name: Ish-bosheth
Ish-bosheth.description: Saul's son living at the time of United Monarchy
Ish-bosheth.shortDescription: Ishhod was the son of Hammoleketh, who was the sister of Manasseh.

Ishhod.name: Ishhod
Ishhod.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ishhod.briefDescription: Son of Appaim, descendant of Judah.
Ishhod.shortDescription: Ishi was a descendant of Judah through Appaim.

Ishi.name: Ishi
Ishi.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ishi.briefDescription: Son of Hur, descendant of Judah.
Ishi.shortDescription: Ishma was a son of Hur, a descendant of Judah.

Ishma.name: Ishma
Ishma.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ishma.briefDescription: Descendant of Benjamin through Saul's line.
Ishma.shortDescription: Ishmael was a descendant of Benjamin through King Saul's line.

Ishmael.name: Ishmael
Ishmael.description: Abraham's son living at the time of the Patriarchs
Ishmael.briefDescription: Gibeonite warrior who joined David at Ziklag.
Ishmael.shortDescription: Ishmaiah the Gibeonite was a mighty warrior among the Thirty who joined David at Ziklag.

Ishmaiah.name: Ishmaiah
Ishmaiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ishmaiah.shortDescription: Ishmerai was a descendant of Benjamin through Elpaal.

Ishmerai.name: Ishmerai
Ishmerai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ishmerai.shortDescription: Ishpah was a descendant of Benjamin through Beriah.

Ishpah.name: Ishpah
Ishpah.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ishpah.shortDescription: Ishpan was a descendant of Benjamin through Shashak.

Ishpan.name: Ishpan
Ishpan.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ishpan.briefDescription: Son of Asher, grandson of Jacob.
Ishpan.shortDescription: Ishvah was a son of Asher and grandson of Jacob.

Ishvah.name: Ishvah
Ishvah.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ishvah.briefDescription: Son of Asher, grandson of Jacob.
Ishvah.shortDescription: Ishvi (or Isui) was a son of Asher and grandson of Jacob.

Ishvi.name: Ishvi
Ishvi.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ishvi.briefDescription: Overseer under King Hezekiah during temple restoration.
Ishvi.shortDescription: Ismachiah was one of the overseers appointed by King Hezekiah during the restoration of the temple and its services.

Ismachiah.name: Ismachiah
Ismachiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ismachiah.briefDescription: Overseer under King Hezekiah during temple restoration.
Ismachiah.shortDescription: Ismachiah was one of the overseers appointed by King Hezekiah during the restoration of the temple and its services.

Israel.name: Israel
Israel.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Israel.briefDescription: Patriarch, son of Isaac, father of twelve tribes
Israel.shortDescription: Israel, also named Jacob, was the son of Isaac, the father of the twelve tribes of Israel, and a key patriarch of the Jewish people.

Issachar.name: Issachar
Issachar.description: Jacob's son living at the time of the Patriarchs
Issachar.briefDescription: Korahite warrior who joined David at Ziklag.
Issachar.shortDescription: Isshiah the Korahite was one of the warriors who joined David at Ziklag.

Isshiah.name: Isshiah
Isshiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Isshiah.briefDescription: Descendant of Harim; agreed to put away foreign wife.
Isshiah.shortDescription: Isshijah, a descendant of Harim, was among those who agreed to put away their foreign wives during Ezra's reforms.

Isshijah.name: Isshijah
Isshijah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Isshijah.briefDescription: Youngest son of Aaron; served as priest.
Isshijah.shortDescription: Ithamar was the fourth and youngest son of Aaron, who served as a priest alongside his brothers Nadab, Abihu, and Eleazar.

Ithamar.name: Ithamar
Ithamar.description: Priest living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ithamar.briefDescription: Descendant of Benjamin living in Jerusalem.
Ithamar.shortDescription: Ithiel, a descendant of Benjamin, was among those who settled in Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Ithiel.name: Ithiel
Ithiel.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ithiel.shortDescription: Ithmah the Moabite was one of the mighty warriors in David's army.

Ithmah.name: Ithmah
Ithmah.description: Moabite warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ithmah.briefDescription: Descendant of Asher, son of Zophah.
Ithmah.shortDescription: Ithran (or Jether) was a descendant of Asher, the son of Zophah.

Ithran.name: Ithran
Ithran.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Ithran.briefDescription: Son of David, born in Hebron to Eglah.
Ithran.shortDescription: Ithream was a son of David, born to him in Hebron by his wife Eglah.

Ithream.name: Ithream
Ithream.description: David's son living at the time of United Monarchy
Ithream.briefDescription: Clan descended from Kiriath-jearim; two of David's warriors.
Ithream.shortDescription: The Ithrites were a clan descended from Kiriath-jearim (<ref="1Ch.2.53">1Ch.2.53</ref>), and two of David's mighty warriors, Ira and Gareb, were Ithrites.

Ithrites.name: Ithrites
Ithrites.briefDescription: Gittite commander who remained loyal to David.
Ithrites.shortDescription: Ittai the Gittite was a commander who remained loyal to David during Absalom's rebellion.

Ittai.name: Ittai
Ittai.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Ittai.briefDescription: Son of Asher listed in the genealogy in Chronicles
Ittai.shortDescription: Izhar was a descendant of Asher mentioned among the genealogies of the twelve tribes of Israel.

Izhar.name: Izhar
Izhar.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Izhar.shortDescription: Izliah was a Benjamite, one of the sons of Elpaal.

Izliah.name: Izliah
Izliah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Izliah.shortDescription: Izrahiah, a son of Uzzi, was a descendant of Issachar and a clan head.

Izrahiah.name: Izrahiah
Izrahiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Izrahiah.briefDescription: Descendant of Parosh; agreed to put away foreign wife.
Izrahiah.shortDescription: Izziah, a descendant of Parosh, was among those who agreed to put away their foreign wives during Ezra's reforms.

Izziah.name: Izziah
Izziah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Izziah.shortDescription: Jaakobah was a leader from the tribe of Simeon during the reign of King Hezekiah.

Jaakobah.name: Jaakobah
Jaakobah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jaakobah.briefDescription: Returned exile; descendant of Solomon's servants.
Jaakobah.shortDescription: Jaalah was among the descendants of Solomon's servants who returned to Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Jaalah.name: Jaalah
Jaalah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jaalah.briefDescription: Father of Elhanan, who killed Goliath's brother.
Jaalah.shortDescription: Jaare-oregim (or Jair) was the father of Elhanan, who killed Lahmi, the brother of Goliath the Gittite.

Jaare-oregim.name: Jaare-oregim
Jaare-oregim.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Jaare-oregim.shortDescription: Jaareshiah was a descendant of Benjamin through Jeroham.

Jaareshiah.name: Jaareshiah
Jaareshiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jaareshiah.briefDescription: One of David's mighty warriors, the son of Abner.
Jaareshiah.shortDescription: Jaasiel, the son of Abner, was one of David's mighty warriors.

Jaasiel.name: Jaasiel
Jaasiel.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Jaasiel.briefDescription: Descendant of Bani; agreed to put away foreign wife.
Jaasiel.shortDescription: Jaasu, a descendant of Bani, was among those who agreed to put away their foreign wives during Ezra's reforms.

Jaasu.name: Jaasu
Jaasu.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jaasu.briefDescription: Leader among the twenty-five men in Ezekiel's vision.
Jaasu.shortDescription: Jaazaniah, the son of Azzur, was one of the twenty-five men in Ezekiel's vision who were devising iniquity and giving wicked counsel.

Jaazaniah.name: Jaazaniah
Jaazaniah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jaazaniah.briefDescription: Levite, descendant of Merari during David's reign.
Jaazaniah.shortDescription: Jaaziah was a Levite, a descendant of Merari through his son Beno, who served during King David's reign.

Jaaziah.name: Jaaziah
Jaaziah.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jaaziah.shortDescription: Jaaziel (or Aziel) was a Levite appointed as a musician to play the bronze cymbals during the bringing of the ark to Jerusalem in David's reign.

Jaaziel.name: Jaaziel
Jaaziel.description: Singer living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jaaziel.briefDescription: Son of Lamech; father of nomadic herdsmen.
Jaaziel.shortDescription: Jabal, the son of Lamech and Adah, was the ancestor of those who dwell in tents and have livestock.

Jabal.name: Jabal
Jabal.description: Man living at the time before the Flood
Jabal.briefDescription: Assassinated Zechariah; reigned as king of Israel for a month.
Jabal.shortDescription: Jabesh, the son of Shallum, conspired against and assassinated Zechariah, becoming king of Israel, but reigned for only one month before being killed by Menahem.

Jabesh.name: Jabesh
Jabesh.briefDescription: A town in Gilead, loyal to Saul
Jabesh.shortDescription: Jabesh-gilead, a town in Gilead known for its loyalty to King Saul.

Jabez.name: Jabez
Jabez.briefDescription: A place associated with the families of scribes
Jabez.shortDescription: Jabez, a place associated with the families of scribes (1Ch.2.55).

Jabin.name: Jabin
Jabin.briefDescription: Gadite leader during the reign of Jotham.
Jabin.shortDescription: Jacan was a leader from the tribe of Gad during the reign of Jotham, king of Judah.

Jacan.name: Jacan
Jacan.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jacan.briefDescription: Head of a priestly division during David's reign.
Jacan.shortDescription: Jachin was the head of the twenty-first priestly division organized by King David.

Jachin.name: Jachin
Jachin.briefDescription: One of two bronze pillars in Solomon's Temple
Jachin.shortDescription: Jachin was the name given to one of the two bronze pillars that stood at the entrance of Solomon's Temple (1Ki.7.21; 2Ch.3.17).

Jacob.name: Jacob
Jacob.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jacob.briefDescription: Son of Onam and brother of Shammai.
Jacob.shortDescription: Jada was the son of Onam and the brother of Shammai, from the tribe of Judah.

Jada.name: Jada
Jada.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jada.briefDescription: Israelite who agreed to put away his foreign wife.
Jada.shortDescription: Jaddai was among the Israelites who agreed to put away their foreign wives during Ezra's reforms.

Jaddai.name: Jaddai
Jaddai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jaddai.briefDescription: One of the leaders who sealed Nehemiah's covenant.
Jaddai.shortDescription: Jaddua was one of the leaders who sealed the covenant made by the people of Israel during Nehemiah's time.

Jaddua.name: Jaddua
Jaddua.description: Priest living at the time of Exile and Return
Jaddua.briefDescription: Meronothite who helped repair Jerusalem's walls under Nehemiah.
Jaddua.shortDescription: Jadon the Meronothite was one of the people who helped repair the walls of Jerusalem during Nehemiah's time.

Jadon.name: Jadon
Jadon.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jadon.briefDescription: Wife of Heber; killed Sisera, the Canaanite commander.
Jadon.shortDescription: Jael, the wife of Heber the Kenite, killed Sisera, the commander of the Canaanite army, by driving a tent peg through his temple.

Jael.name: Jael
Jael.briefDescription: Levite, descendant of Shimei, son of Gershon.
Jael.shortDescription: Jahath was a Levite, a descendant of Shimei, and a grandson of Gershon.

Jahath.name: Jahath
Jahath.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jahath.briefDescription: Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag.
Jahath.shortDescription: Jahaziel, a Benjamite warrior, was one of the mighty men who joined David at Ziklag while he was hiding from Saul.

Jahaziel.name: Jahaziel
Jahaziel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jahaziel.briefDescription: Descendant of Caleb, son of Jephunneh.
Jahaziel.shortDescription: Jahdai was one of the descendants of Caleb, the son of Jephunneh.

Jahdai.name: Jahdai
Jahdai.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jahdai.briefDescription: Mighty warrior and head of a father's house in Manasseh.
Jahdai.shortDescription: Jahdiel was a mighty warrior and head of a father's house in the half-tribe of Manasseh, east of the Jordan.

Jahdiel.name: Jahdiel
Jahdiel.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jahdiel.shortDescription: Jahdo, a son of Buz, was a descendant of Gad and a clan head.

Jahdo.name: Jahdo
Jahdo.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jahdo.briefDescription: Son of Zebulun, grandson of Jacob, and a clan leader.
Jahdo.shortDescription: Jahleel, son of Zebulun, was a grandson of Jacob and a clan leader.

Jahleel.name: Jahleel
Jahleel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jahleel.shortDescription: Jahmai, a son of Tola, was a descendant of Issachar and a clan head.

Jahmai.name: Jahmai
Jahmai.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jahmai.briefDescription: Son of Naphtali, grandson of Jacob, and a clan leader.
Jahmai.shortDescription: Jahzeel (or Jahziel), son of Naphtali, was a grandson of Jacob and a clan leader.

Jahzeel.name: Jahzeel
Jahzeel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jahzeel.briefDescription: Opposed Ezra's command for Israelites to divorce foreign wives.
Jahzeel.shortDescription: Jahzeiah, along with three others, opposed Ezra's order for Israelites to divorce their foreign wives.

Jahzeiah.name: Jahzeiah
Jahzeiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jahzeiah.shortDescription: Jahzerah was a priest who lived in Jerusalem after the exile.

Jahzerah.name: Jahzerah
Jahzerah.description: Levite living at the time of Exile and Return
Jahzerah.shortDescription: Jair was the father of Mordecai, a Benjamite mentioned in the book of Esther.

Jair.name: Jair
Jair.briefDescription: An Israelite who possessed towns in Gilead
Jair.shortDescription: Jair, an Israelite who possessed towns in Gilead (Joshua 13:30).

Jairus.name: Jairus
Jairus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Jairus.briefDescription: Father of Agur, the author of Proverbs 30
Jairus.shortDescription: Jakeh was the father of Agur, the author of Proverbs 30.

Jakeh.name: Jakeh
Jakeh.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Jakeh.shortDescription: Jakim was the head of the 12th priestly division during David's reign.

Jakim.name: Jakim
Jakim.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jakim.shortDescription: Jalam was a son of Esau and Oholibamah, and a chief of Edom.

Jalam.name: Jalam
Jalam.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Jalam.briefDescription: Son of Ezrah, from the tribe of Judah
Jalam.shortDescription: Jalon was a son of Ezrah, from the tribe of Judah.

Jalon.name: Jalon
Jalon.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jalon.shortDescription: Jambres was an Egyptian magician who, along with Jannes, opposed Moses.

Jambres.name: Jambres
Jambres.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Jambres.shortDescription: James, father of Judas who was also know as Thaddaeus, one of the Twelve Apostles chosen by Jesus.

James.name: James
James.description: Apostle living at the time of the New Testament
James.briefDescription: Son of Ram, descendant of Judah
James.shortDescription: Jamin was a son of Ram and a descendant of Judah.

Jamin.name: Jamin
Jamin.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jamin.shortDescription: Jamlech was a leader from the tribe of Simeon during King Hezekiah's reign.

Jamlech.name: Jamlech
Jamlech.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jamlech.briefDescription: Gadite leader who settled in Bashan
Jamlech.shortDescription: Janai was a leader from the tribe of Gad who settled in the region of Bashan.

Janai.name: Janai
Janai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Janai.briefDescription: Ancestor of Jesus in Luke's genealogy
Janai.shortDescription: Jannai was an ancestor of Jesus mentioned in Luke's genealogy.

Jannai.name: Jannai
Jannai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jannai.shortDescription: Jannes was an Egyptian magician who, along with Jambres, opposed Moses.

Jannes.name: Jannes
Jannes.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Jannes.briefDescription: Son of Noah, ancestor of Indo-European peoples
Jannes.shortDescription: Japheth was one of Noah's three sons and the ancestor of the Indo-European peoples.

Japheth.name: Japheth
Japheth.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Japheth.briefDescription: Son of David, born in Jerusalem
Japheth.shortDescription: Japhia was one of King David's sons, born to him in Jerusalem.

Japhia.name: Japhia
Japhia.briefDescription: A town on the border of Zebulun
Japhia.shortDescription: Japhia, a town on the border of Zebulun's territory (Joshua 19:12).

Japhlet.name: Japhlet
Japhlet.briefDescription: Ancestor of Noah; father of Enoch
Japhlet.shortDescription: Jared was an ancestor of Noah and the father of Enoch, who walked with God.

Jared.name: Jared
Jared.description: Man living at the time before the Flood
Jared.briefDescription: Egyptian servant who married Sheshan's daughter
Jared.shortDescription: Jarha was an Egyptian servant who married the daughter of Sheshan, a descendant of Judah.

Jarha.name: Jarha
Jarha.description: Egyptian living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jarha.briefDescription: Priest who married a foreign woman
Jarha.shortDescription: Jarib was a priest who had married a foreign woman during the time of Ezra.

Jarib.name: Jarib
Jarib.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jarib.shortDescription: Jaroah was a Gadite, the son of Gilead, mentioned in a genealogy.

Jaroah.name: Jaroah
Jaroah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jaroah.briefDescription: Ancient Hebrew book mentioned in the Old Testament
Jaroah.shortDescription: Jashar was an ancient Hebrew book mentioned in the Old Testament, but not included in the biblical canon.

Jashar.name: Jashar
Jashar.briefDescription: Father of some of David's mighty men
Jashar.shortDescription: Jashen, also known as Hashem, was the father of some of David's mighty men.

Jashen.name: Jashen
Jashen.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Jashen.briefDescription: Korahite who joined David at Ziklag
Jashen.shortDescription: Jashobeam was a Korahite who joined David at Ziklag when he was fleeing from Saul.

Jashobeam.name: Jashobeam
Jashobeam.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jashobeam.briefDescription: Israelite who married a foreign woman during Ezra's time
Jashobeam.shortDescription: Jashub was an Israelite who had married a foreign woman during the time of Ezra.

Jashub.name: Jashub
Jashub.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jashub.briefDescription: Paul's host in Thessalonica; relative of Paul
Jashub.shortDescription: Jason was Paul's host in Thessalonica and a relative of Paul mentioned in his letter to the Romans.

Jason.name: Jason
Jason.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Jason.shortDescription: Jathniel was a Levite who served as a gatekeeper during the reign of King David.

Jathniel.name: Jathniel
Jathniel.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jathniel.briefDescription: Son of Japheth; ancestor of Greek people
Jathniel.shortDescription: Javan, the ancestor of the Greek people, was a son of Japheth and grandson of Noah.

Javan.name: Javan
Javan.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Javan.briefDescription: Hagrite in charge of David's flocks
Javan.shortDescription: Jaziz the Hagrite was in charge of King David's flocks.

Jaziz.name: Jaziz
Jaziz.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jaziz.briefDescription: Levite, son of Zerah, ancestor of Asaph
Jaziz.shortDescription: Jeatherai, also known as Ethni, was a Levite, the son of Zerah, and an ancestor of Asaph.

Jeatherai.name: Jeatherai
Jeatherai.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jeatherai.briefDescription: Witness to Isaiah's prophecy about Maher-shalal-hash-baz
Jeatherai.shortDescription: Jeberechiah was a witness to Isaiah's prophecy concerning his son Maher-shalal-hash-baz.

Jeberechiah.name: Jeberechiah
Jeberechiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jeberechiah.briefDescription: Canaanite tribe inhabiting Jerusalem before David's conquest
Jeberechiah.shortDescription: The Jebusites were a Canaanite tribe that inhabited Jerusalem before King David conquered the city.

Jebusites.name: Jebusites
Jebusites.briefDescription: Mother of King Uzziah (Azariah) of Judah
Jebusites.shortDescription: Jecoliah was the mother of King Uzziah (also known as Azariah) of Judah.

Jecoliah.name: Jecoliah
Jecoliah.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jecoliah.briefDescription: Head of a priestly division during David's reign
Jecoliah.shortDescription: Jedaiah was the head of the second priestly division during King David's reign and his descendants returned from the Babylonian exile.

Jedaiah.name: Jedaiah
Jedaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jedaiah.briefDescription: One of David's mighty men from the tribe of Manasseh
Jedaiah.shortDescription: Jediael the son of Shimri was one of David's mighty men from the tribe of Manasseh.

Jediael.name: Jediael
Jediael.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jediael.briefDescription: Mother of King Josiah of Judah
Jediael.shortDescription: Jedidah was the mother of King Josiah of Judah and the daughter of Adaiah of Bozkath.

Jedidah.name: Jedidah
Jedidah.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jedidah.briefDescription: Levitical musician and singer during David's reign
Jedidah.shortDescription: Jeduthun, also known as Ethan, was a Levitical musician and singer during the reign of King David.

Jeduthun.name: Jeduthun
Jeduthun.description: Levite living at the time of Exile and Return
Jeduthun.briefDescription: Son of Elah, descendant of Judah
Jeduthun.shortDescription: Jehallelel was a son of Elah and a descendant of Judah.

Jehallelel.name: Jehallelel
Jehallelel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehallelel.briefDescription: Levite descendant of Amram during David's reign
Jehallelel.shortDescription: Jehdeiah was a Levite descendant of Amram who served during the reign of King David.

Jehdeiah.name: Jehdeiah
Jehdeiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehdeiah.briefDescription: Head of the 20th priestly division under David
Jehdeiah.shortDescription: Jehezkel was the head of the 20th priestly division during the reign of King David.

Jehezkel.name: Jehezkel
Jehezkel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehezkel.briefDescription: Levitical musician during the reign of King David
Jehezkel.shortDescription: Jehiel was a Levitical musician who played the harp and lyre during the reign of King David.

Jehiel.name: Jehiel
Jehiel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jehiel.briefDescription: Ephraimite leader who opposed taking Judean captives
Jehiel.shortDescription: Jehizkiah, an Ephraimite leader, opposed taking Judean captives during the reign of King Ahaz of Judah.

Jehizkiah.name: Jehizkiah
Jehizkiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehizkiah.shortDescription: Jehoaddah, also known as Jarah, was a descendant of King Saul mentioned in the genealogies of Benjamin.

Jehoaddah.name: Jehoaddah
Jehoaddah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehoaddah.briefDescription: Mother of King Amaziah of Judah
Jehoaddah.shortDescription: Jehoaddan was the mother of King Amaziah of Judah and was from Jerusalem.

Jehoaddan.name: Jehoaddan
Jehoaddan.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehoaddan.briefDescription: King of Israel, son of Jehu
Jehoaddan.shortDescription: Jehoahaz, the son of Jehu, was king of Israel and reigned for seventeen years.

Jehoahaz.name: Jehoahaz
Jehoahaz.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehoahaz.briefDescription: Levite gatekeeper during the reign of David
Jehoahaz.shortDescription: Jehohanan was a Levite gatekeeper during the reign of King David.

Jehohanan.name: Jehohanan
Jehohanan.description: Ammonite living at the time of Exile and Return
Jehohanan.briefDescription: King of Judah, son of Jehoiakim
Jehohanan.shortDescription: Jehoiachin, also known as Jeconiah, Coniah, or Shallum, was a king of Judah who reigned for three months before being exiled to Babylon.

Jehoiachin.name: Jehoiachin
Jehoiachin.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehoiachin.briefDescription: Counselor to King David after Ahithophel
Jehoiachin.shortDescription: Jehoiada, the son of Benaiah, succeeded Ahithophel as King David's counselor.

Jehoiada.name: Jehoiada
Jehoiada.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehoiada.briefDescription: King of Judah, son of Josiah
Jehoiada.shortDescription: Jehoiakim, also known as Eliakim, was a king of Judah who reigned for eleven years before being succeeded by his son Jehoiachin.

Jehoiakim.name: Jehoiakim
Jehoiakim.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehoiakim.briefDescription: Head of a priestly division during David's reign
Jehoiakim.shortDescription: Jehoiarib was the head of the first priestly division organized by King David.

Jehoiarib.name: Jehoiarib
Jehoiarib.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehoiarib.briefDescription: Levite sent by Jehoshaphat to teach the Law
Jehoiarib.shortDescription: Jehonathan was a Levite sent by King Jehoshaphat to teach the Book of the Law in the cities of Judah.

Jehonathan.name: Jehonathan
Jehonathan.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jehonathan.briefDescription: Son of Jehoshaphat, king of Judah.
Jehonathan.shortDescription: Jehoram, also known as Joram, was the son of Jehoshaphat and king of Judah.

Jehoram.name: Jehoram
Jehoram.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehoram.briefDescription: Son of Asa, king of Judah.
Jehoram.shortDescription: Jehoshaphat was the son of Asa and became a powerful and righteous king of Judah.

Jehoshaphat.name: Jehoshaphat
Jehoshaphat.description: David's compatriot living at the time of United Monarchy
Jehoshaphat.briefDescription: Daughter of Jehoram, saved Joash from Athaliah's massacre.
Jehoshaphat.shortDescription: Jehosheba, also known as Jehoshabeath, was the daughter of King Jehoram and the wife of Jehoiada the priest; she saved the infant Joash from Athaliah's massacre of the royal family.

Jehosheba.name: Jehosheba
Jehosheba.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehosheba.briefDescription: Son of Obed-edom, a gatekeeper for the ark.
Jehosheba.shortDescription: Jehozabad was one of the sons of Obed-edom who served as a gatekeeper for the ark of the covenant.

Jehozabad.name: Jehozabad
Jehozabad.description: Moabite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehozabad.briefDescription: Father of Joshua the high priest during the exile.
Jehozabad.shortDescription: Jehozadak (or Jozadak) was the father of Joshua, the high priest who returned from the Babylonian exile and helped rebuild the temple.

Jehozadak.name: Jehozadak
Jehozadak.description: High Priest living at the time of Exile and Return
Jehozadak.briefDescription: A Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag.
Jehozadak.shortDescription: Jehu was one of the Benjamite archers and slingers who joined David at Ziklag while he was hiding from Saul.

Jehu.name: Jehu
Jehu.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehu.shortDescription: Jehubbah was a descendant of Asher mentioned in the genealogy recorded in 1 Chronicles.

Jehubbah.name: Jehubbah
Jehubbah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehubbah.briefDescription: An official during Zedekiah's reign, opposed Jeremiah.
Jehubbah.shortDescription: Jehucal (or Jucal) was an official during King Zedekiah's reign who opposed the prophet Jeremiah and sought his punishment.

Jehucal.name: Jehucal
Jehucal.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehucal.briefDescription: An official who read Jeremiah's scroll to Jehoiakim.
Jehucal.shortDescription: Jehudi was an official during King Jehoiakim's reign who read Jeremiah's scroll to the king, who then burned it piece by piece.

Jehudi.name: Jehudi
Jehudi.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehudi.briefDescription: Wife of Mered; mother of Jered, Heber, and Jekuthiel.
Jehudi.shortDescription: Jehudijah (or the Judahite) was the wife of Mered and the mother of Jered, Heber, and Jekuthiel, as recorded in the genealogy of Judah.

Jehudijah.name: Jehudijah
Jehudijah.briefDescription: A Levite who helped cleanse the temple under Hezekiah.
Jehudijah.shortDescription: Jehuel was a Levite from the family of Heman who participated in the cleansing of the temple during King Hezekiah's reign.

Jehuel.name: Jehuel
Jehuel.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jehuel.briefDescription: One of David's mighty men from Mahavite.
Jehuel.shortDescription: Jeiel the Mahavite was listed among David's mighty men in 1 Chronicles.

Jeiel.name: Jeiel
Jeiel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jeiel.shortDescription: Jekameam was a Levite, the son of Hebron, mentioned in the genealogies of the Levites in 1 Chronicles.

Jekameam.name: Jekameam
Jekameam.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jekameam.briefDescription: Son of Shallum, a descendant of Judah.
Jekameam.shortDescription: Jekamiah was a descendant of Judah through Shallum, as recorded in the genealogy in 1 Chronicles.

Jekamiah.name: Jekamiah
Jekamiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jekamiah.briefDescription: Son of Mered, founder of Zanoah.
Jekamiah.shortDescription: Jekuthiel was the son of Mered and the  father (ie founder) of Zanoah, as recorded in the genealogy of Judah in 1 Chronicles.

Jekuthiel.name: Jekuthiel
Jekuthiel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jekuthiel.briefDescription: One of Job's daughters born after his restoration.
Jekuthiel.shortDescription: Jemimah was the first of three daughters born to Job after God restored his fortunes, as recorded in the book of Job.

Jemimah.name: Jemimah
Jemimah.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Jemimah.briefDescription: Son of Simeon, also known as Nemuel.
Jemimah.shortDescription: Jemuel (or Nemuel) was a son of Simeon and a founder of the Nemuelite clan, as recorded in Genesis, Exodus, Numbers, and 1 Chronicles.

Jemuel.name: Jemuel
Jemuel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jemuel.briefDescription: A judge of Israel, known for his tragic vow.
Jemuel.shortDescription: Jephthah was a judge of Israel who delivered the Israelites from the Ammonites but made a tragic vow that resulted in the sacrifice of his daughter.

Jephthah.name: Jephthah
Jephthah.shortDescription: Jephunneh is mentioned in the genealogy of Asher in 1 Chronicles.

Jephunneh.name: Jephunneh
Jephunneh.description: Caleb's father living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jephunneh.briefDescription: A son of Joktan, descendant of Shem.
Jephunneh.shortDescription: Jerah was one of the sons of Joktan, a descendant of Shem, as recorded in the Table of Nations in Genesis and 1 Chronicles.

Jerah.name: Jerah
Jerah.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Jerah.briefDescription: A Levite of the Merari clan.
Jerah.shortDescription: Jerahmeel was a Levite of the family of Merari, as recorded in the genealogy in 1 Chronicles.

Jerahmeel.name: Jerahmeel
Jerahmeel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jerahmeel.briefDescription: Son of Mered, founder of Gedor.
Jerahmeel.shortDescription: Jered was the son of Mered and the  father (ie founder) of Gedor, as recorded in the genealogy of Judah in 1 Chronicles.

Jered.name: Jered
Jered.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jered.briefDescription: A descendant of Hashum who married a foreign wife.
Jered.shortDescription: Jeremai, a descendant of Hashum, was among those who married foreign women during the time of Ezra and agreed to send them away.

Jeremai.name: Jeremai
Jeremai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jeremai.briefDescription: A Gadite warrior who joined David at Ziklag.
Jeremai.shortDescription: Jeremiah was one of the Gadite warriors who joined David at Ziklag when he was fleeing from King Saul.

Jeremiah.name: Jeremiah
Jeremiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jeremiah.shortDescription: Jeremoth was a son of Becher and a member of the tribe of Benjamin, as mentioned in the genealogy in 1 Chronicles.

Jeremoth.name: Jeremoth
Jeremoth.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jeremoth.briefDescription: A Levite, the firstborn son of Hebron.
Jeremoth.shortDescription: Jeriah was a Levite, the firstborn son of Hebron, and an ancestor of the Hebronites who served in the tabernacle during David's reign.

Jeriah.name: Jeriah
Jeriah.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jeriah.shortDescription: Jeribai was listed among David's mighty men, a group of skilled warriors who fought alongside him.

Jeribai.name: Jeribai
Jeribai.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jeribai.briefDescription: A son of Tola, from the tribe of Issachar.
Jeribai.shortDescription: Jeriel was one of the sons of Tola and a leader in the tribe of Issachar, as mentioned in the genealogy in 1 Chronicles.

Jeriel.name: Jeriel
Jeriel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jeriel.briefDescription: A Levite leader during David's reign.
Jeriel.shortDescription: Jerijah was a Levite leader from the family of Hebron who served during the reign of King David.

Jerijah.name: Jerijah
Jerijah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jerijah.briefDescription: One of David's warriors from the tribe of Benjamin.
Jerijah.shortDescription: Jerimoth was a Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag when he was fleeing from King Saul.

Jerimoth.name: Jerimoth
Jerimoth.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jerimoth.briefDescription: Wife of Caleb; mother of unnamed children.
Jerimoth.shortDescription: Jerioth was one of the wives of Caleb, and she bore him some unnamed children, as mentioned in the genealogy of Judah in 1 Chronicles.

Jerioth.name: Jerioth
Jerioth.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jerioth.briefDescription: First king of the northern kingdom of Israel.
Jerioth.shortDescription: Jeroboam I was the first king of the northern kingdom of Israel after the split of the united monarchy following Solomon's reign.

Jeroboam.name: Jeroboam
Jeroboam.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jeroboam.briefDescription: A Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag.
Jeroboam.shortDescription: Jeroham was a Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag when he was fleeing from King Saul.

Jeroham.name: Jeroham
Jeroham.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jeroham.shortDescription: The unnamed wives David took in Jerusalem, who bore him more sons and daughters.

Jerusalem_wives.name: Jerusalem_wives
Jerusalem_wives.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
Jerusalem_wives.briefDescription: Mother of King Jotham of Judah.
Jerusalem_wives.shortDescription: Jerusha was the mother of King Jotham of Judah and the daughter of Zadok.

Jerusha.name: Jerusha
Jerusha.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jerusha.briefDescription: A Levite, son of Jeduthun the musician.
Jerusha.shortDescription: Jeshaiah was a Levite, the son of Jeduthun, and a musician appointed by King David to serve in the tabernacle choir.

Jeshaiah.name: Jeshaiah
Jeshaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jeshaiah.briefDescription: Head of the fourteenth priestly division.
Jeshaiah.shortDescription: Jeshebeab was the head of the fourteenth division of priests during the time of David.

Jeshebeab.name: Jeshebeab
Jeshebeab.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jeshebeab.briefDescription: A son of Caleb and Azubah.
Jeshebeab.shortDescription: Jesher was one of the sons of Caleb and his wife Azubah, as mentioned in the genealogy of Judah in 1 Chronicles.

Jesher.name: Jesher
Jesher.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jesher.shortDescription: Jeshishai was an ancestor of the Gadite leaders who lived in Bashan, as mentioned in the genealogy of Gad in 1 Chronicles.

Jeshishai.name: Jeshishai
Jeshishai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jeshishai.briefDescription: A leader from the tribe of Simeon.
Jeshishai.shortDescription: Jeshohaiah was a leader from the tribe of Simeon during the time of King Hezekiah.

Jeshohaiah.name: Jeshohaiah
Jeshohaiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jeshohaiah.briefDescription: Head of a priestly family that returned from exile.
Jeshohaiah.shortDescription: Jeshua was the head of a priestly family that returned to Jerusalem from the Babylonian exile.

Jeshua.name: Jeshua
Jeshua.briefDescription: A town in Judah inhabited after the Babylonian exile
Jeshua.shortDescription: Jeshua, a town in Judah inhabited by the people of Judah after the Babylonian exile (Nehemiah 11:26).

Jesimiel.name: Jesimiel
Jesimiel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jesimiel.shortDescription: Jesse was the father of King David and an ancestor of Jesus Christ.

Jesse.name: Jesse
Jesse.description: David's father living at the time of United Monarchy
Jesse.shortDescription: Jesse was the father of King David and an ancestor of Jesus Christ.

Jesus.name: Jesus
Jesus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Jesus.briefDescription: The Son of God and Savior of the world.
Jesus.shortDescription: Jesus Christ, the central figure of Christianity, is the Son of God who became incarnate, died for the sins of humanity, and rose from the dead, providing salvation for all who believe in Him.

Jether.name: Jether
Jether.shortDescription: Jetheth, one of the chiefs of Edom.

Jetheth.name: Jetheth
Jetheth.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Jetheth.shortDescription: Jethro, also known as Reuel, was Moses' father-in-law and a priest of Midian.

Jethro.name: Jethro
Jethro.description: Midianite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jethro.shortDescription: Jetur, one of the twelve sons of Ishmael.

Jetur.name: Jetur
Jetur.briefDescription: A descendant of Ishmael and a region in Syria
Jetur.shortDescription: Jetur, a descendant of Ishmael, also a region (Ituraea) mentioned in the New Testament.

Jeuel.name: Jeuel
Jeuel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jeuel.briefDescription: A Levite, son of Shimei or Shubael
Jeuel.shortDescription: Jeush, a Levite, was a son of Shimei (or Shubael) during David's reign.

Jeush.name: Jeush
Jeush.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Jeush.briefDescription: A descendant of Shaharaim from the tribe of Benjamin
Jeush.shortDescription: Jeuz was a Benjamite, born to Shaharaim in the country of Moab.

Jeuz.name: Jeuz
Jeuz.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jeuz.briefDescription: A leader of the Judean forces
Jeuz.shortDescription: Jezaniah, also known as Azariah, was a leader of the Judean forces after the fall of Jerusalem.

Jezaniah.name: Jezaniah
Jezaniah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jezaniah.briefDescription: Queen of Israel; wife of King Ahab
Jezaniah.shortDescription: Queen Jezebel, the Phoenician wife of King Ahab, promoted Baal worship and persecuted the prophets of God.

Jezebel.name: Jezebel
Jezebel.description: Queen living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jezebel.briefDescription: Son of Naphtali, founder of the Jezerite clan
Jezebel.shortDescription: Jezer, a son of Naphtali, was the ancestor of the Jezerite clan.

Jezer.name: Jezer
Jezer.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jezer.briefDescription: A Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag
Jezer.shortDescription: Jeziel, a Benjamite, was one of the warriors who joined David at Ziklag.

Jeziel.name: Jeziel
Jeziel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jeziel.briefDescription: A leader of the singers at the wall dedication
Jeziel.shortDescription: Jezrahiah was one of the leaders of the singers during the dedication of Jerusalem's wall.

Jezrahiah.name: Jezrahiah
Jezrahiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jezrahiah.briefDescription: A son of Hur in the genealogy of Judah
Jezrahiah.shortDescription: Jezreel was a son of Hur, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Jezreel.name: Jezreel
Jezreel.briefDescription: A city in Issachar, associated with Ahab and Jezebel
Jezreel.shortDescription: Jezreel, a city in Issachar, known for its association with King Ahab and Queen Jezebel.

Jidlaph.name: Jidlaph
Jidlaph.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Jidlaph.briefDescription: Son of Seraiah, founder of Ge-harashim
Jidlaph.shortDescription: Joab, the son of Seraiah, was the  father (ie founder) of Ge-harashim.

Joab.name: Joab
Joab.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Joab.briefDescription: A son of Obed-edom, a gatekeeper of the temple
Joab.shortDescription: Joah, a son of Obed-edom, was one of the gatekeepers assigned to guard the temple.

Joah.name: Joah
Joah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Joah.briefDescription: Father of Joah, King Josiah's secretary
Joah.shortDescription: Joahaz was the father of Joah, who served as King Josiah's secretary during the temple repairs.

Joahaz.name: Joahaz
Joahaz.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Joahaz.shortDescription: Joanan is listed as an ancestor of Jesus in the genealogy recorded by Luke.

Joanan.name: Joanan
Joanan.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Joanan.briefDescription: A follower of Jesus who supported His ministry
Joanan.shortDescription: Joanna, the wife of Chuza, was one of the women who followed and supported Jesus during His earthly ministry.

Joanna.name: Joanna
Joanna.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Joanna.briefDescription: A Benjaminite warrior who joined David at Ziklag
Joanna.shortDescription: Joash, the son of Shemaah the Gibeathite, was a Benjaminite warrior who joined David at Ziklag.

Joash.name: Joash
Joash.briefDescription: A righteous man who endured great suffering
Joash.shortDescription: Job, a blameless and upright man from Uz, endured severe trials and maintained his faith in God.

Job.name: Job
Job.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Job.briefDescription: A son of Elpaal, descendant of Benjamin
Job.shortDescription: Jobab, a descendant of Benjamin, was one of the sons of Elpaal.

Jobab.name: Jobab
Jobab.briefDescription: Mother of Moses, Aaron, and Miriam
Jobab.shortDescription: Jochebed, the daughter of Levi, married her nephew Amram and became the mother of Moses, Aaron, and Miriam.

Jochebed.name: Jochebed
Jochebed.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jochebed.shortDescription: Joda is listed as an ancestor of Jesus in the genealogy recorded by Luke.

Joda.name: Joda
Joda.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Joda.briefDescription: A Benjaminite who resettled in Jerusalem
Joda.shortDescription: Joed, a Benjaminite, was among those who resettled in Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Joed.name: Joed
Joed.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Joed.briefDescription: The brother of Nathan, one of David's mighty men
Joed.shortDescription: Joel, the brother of Nathan, was one of the mighty men who fought valiantly for King David.

Joel.name: Joel
Joel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Joel.briefDescription: A Benjaminite warrior who joined David at Ziklag
Joel.shortDescription: Joelah, a Benjaminite warrior, was among those who joined David at Ziklag while he was fleeing from Saul.

Joelah.name: Joelah
Joelah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Joelah.briefDescription: A Benjaminite warrior who joined David at Ziklag
Joelah.shortDescription: Joezer, a Korahite warrior, was among the Benjaminites who joined David at Ziklag while he was fleeing from Saul.

Joezer.name: Joezer
Joezer.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Joezer.briefDescription: A leader from the tribe of Dan
Joezer.shortDescription: Jogli, the son of Bukki, was a leader from the tribe of Dan appointed to help divide the Promised Land.

Jogli.name: Jogli
Jogli.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jogli.briefDescription: One of David's mighty warriors from the Tizite clan
Jogli.shortDescription: Joha the Tizite was one of David's mighty warriors, known for his bravery and military skill.

Joha.name: Joha
Joha.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Joha.briefDescription: A Gadite warrior who joined David at Ziklag
Joha.shortDescription: Johanan, a Gadite warrior, was one of the men who joined David at Ziklag and helped him fight against raiding bands.

Johanan.name: Johanan
Johanan.description: Priest living at the time of Exile and Return
Johanan.briefDescription: A member of the high priest's family
Johanan.shortDescription: John, a member of the high priest's family, was present during Peter and John's questioning before the Sanhedrin.

John.name: John
John.description: Apostle living at the time of the New Testament
John.briefDescription: A high priest, son of Eliashib
John.shortDescription: Joiada served as a high priest during the Persian period, succeeding his father Eliashib.

Joiada.name: Joiada
Joiada.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Joiada.briefDescription: A high priest, son of Jeshua
Joiada.shortDescription: Joiakim served as a high priest during the early Persian period, succeeding his father Jeshua.

Joiakim.name: Joiakim
Joiakim.description: Priest living at the time of Exile and Return
Joiakim.briefDescription: A priestly family that returned from Babylonian exile
Joiakim.shortDescription: Joiarib (or Jehoiarib) was a priestly family that returned from the Babylonian exile and settled in Jerusalem.

Joiarib.name: Joiarib
Joiarib.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Joiarib.briefDescription: A descendant of Judah through Shelah
Joiarib.shortDescription: Jokim was a descendant of Judah through Shelah, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Jokim.name: Jokim
Jokim.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Jokim.briefDescription: A son of Abraham and Keturah
