Jokim.shortDescription: Jokshan was one of the sons born to Abraham and his wife Keturah after the death of Sarah.

Jokshan.name: Jokshan
Jokshan.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Jokshan.briefDescription: Son of Eber and ancestor of Arabian tribes
Jokshan.shortDescription: Joktan, a descendant of Shem through Eber, was the ancestor of several Arabian tribes.

Joktan.name: Joktan
Joktan.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Joktan.briefDescription: Son of Rechab, who promoted Rechabite lifestyle
Joktan.shortDescription: Jonadab, the son of Rechab, instructed his descendants to abstain from wine, live in tents, and follow a nomadic lifestyle, which they faithfully observed.

Jonadab.name: Jonadab
Jonadab.description: David's nephew living at the time of United Monarchy
Jonadab.briefDescription: Prophet swallowed by fish, preached to Nineveh
Jonadab.shortDescription: Jonah, a prophet from Gath-hepher, was sent to preach to Nineveh.

Jonah.name: Jonah
Jonah.description: Prophet living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jonah.shortDescription: Jonam, son of Eliakim, was an ancestor of Jesus Christ.

Jonam.name: Jonam
Jonam.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jonam.briefDescription: Son of Jada, descendant of Judah
Jonam.shortDescription: One of the descendants of Judah, Jonathan was the son of Jada.

Jonathan.name: Jonathan
Jonathan.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jonathan.briefDescription: Ancestor of exiles who returned to Jerusalem
Jonathan.shortDescription: The descendants of Jorah (or Hariph) were among the exiles who returned to Jerusalem from Babylon.

Jorah.name: Jorah
Jorah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jorah.briefDescription: A chief of the half-tribe of Manasseh
Jorah.shortDescription: Jorai was a clan chief of the half-tribe of Manasseh during the reign of King Jotham of Judah.

Jorai.name: Jorai
Jorai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jorai.briefDescription: A Levite during the reign of King David
Jorai.shortDescription: Joram was a Levite who served during the reign of King David.

Joram.name: Joram
Joram.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Joram.shortDescription: Jorim is listed as one of the ancestors of Jesus Christ in the genealogy recorded by Luke.

Jorim.name: Jorim
Jorim.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jorim.briefDescription: A son or town founded by Raham
Jorim.shortDescription: Jorkeam was a descendant of Caleb or a town founded by Raham in the tribe of Judah.

Jorkeam.name: Jorkeam
Jorkeam.description: Man or town at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jorkeam.shortDescription: Josech (or Joseph) is listed as one of the ancestors of Jesus Christ in the genealogy recorded by Luke.

Josech.name: Josech
Josech.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Josech.briefDescription: Son of Asaph, a musician in David's time
Josech.shortDescription: Joseph, a son of Asaph, was a musician who served during King David's reign.

Joseph.name: Joseph
Joseph.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Joseph.briefDescription: Brother of Jesus and son of Mary
Joseph.shortDescription: Joses (or Joseph) was one of Jesus' brothers and a son of Mary.

Joses.name: Joses
Joses.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Joses.briefDescription: A Simeonite leader during Hezekiah's reign
Joses.shortDescription: Joshah was one of the leaders from the tribe of Simeon during the reign of King Hezekiah.

Joshah.name: Joshah
Joshah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Joshah.briefDescription: One of David's mighty warriors from Mithan
Joshah.shortDescription: Joshaphat the Mithnite was listed among David's mighty warriors.

Joshaphat.name: Joshaphat
Joshaphat.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Joshaphat.briefDescription: One of David's mighty warriors from Eliahba's sons
Joshaphat.shortDescription: Joshaviah, a son of Elnaam, was listed among David's mighty warriors.

Joshaviah.name: Joshaviah
Joshaviah.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Joshaviah.briefDescription: A son of Heman, a musician in David's time
Joshaviah.shortDescription: Joshbekashah, one of Heman's sons, was a musician appointed by King David to lead worship.

Joshbekashah.name: Joshbekashah
Joshbekashah.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Joshbekashah.briefDescription: A Simeonite leader during Hezekiah's reign
Joshbekashah.shortDescription: Joshibiah was one of the leaders from the tribe of Simeon during the reign of King Hezekiah.

Joshibiah.name: Joshibiah
Joshibiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Joshibiah.briefDescription: Owner of a field in Beth-shemesh
Joshibiah.shortDescription: The ark of the covenant was placed on a rock in the field of Joshua of Beth-shemesh.

Joshua.name: Joshua
Joshua.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Joshua.briefDescription: King of Judah known for his religious reforms
Joshua.shortDescription: Josiah was a king of Judah who instituted significant religious reforms and repaired the temple.

Josiah.name: Josiah
Josiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Josiah.briefDescription: Father of Shelomith, a returning exile
Josiah.shortDescription: Josiphiah was the father of Shelomith, who was among the exiles who returned with Ezra to Jerusalem.

Josiphiah.name: Josiphiah
Josiphiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Josiphiah.briefDescription: A son of Jahdai, a Calebite
Josiphiah.shortDescription: Jotham was a son of Jahdai, who was a wife of Caleb.

Jotham.name: Jotham
Jotham.briefDescription: A Manassite leader who joined David at Ziklag
Jotham.shortDescription: One of two men called Jozabad who, along with other Manassite leaders, joined David at Ziklag when he was returning to fight against the Philistines.

Jozabad.name: Jozabad
Jozabad.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jozabad.briefDescription: One of the servants who killed King Joash
Jozabad.shortDescription: Jozacar (or Zabad), along with Jehozabad, was one of the servants who conspired against and killed King Joash of Judah.

Jozacar.name: Jozacar
Jozacar.description: Ammonite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jozacar.briefDescription: A priest whose descendants married foreign women
Jozacar.shortDescription: Among the descendants of Jozadak, some priests were found to have married foreign women during the time of Ezra.

Jozadak.name: Jozadak
Jozadak.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Jozadak.briefDescription: Father of musicians who play the lyre and pipe
Jozadak.shortDescription: Jubal was the father of all who play the lyre and pipe.

Jubal.name: Jubal
Jubal.description: Man living at the time before the Flood
Jubal.briefDescription: A Levite who married a foreign woman
Jubal.shortDescription: Judah was one of the Levites who had married foreign women during the time of Ezra and agreed to put them away.

Judah.name: Judah
Judah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Judah.briefDescription: A prophet and leader in the Jerusalem church
Judah.shortDescription: Judas, also known as Barsabbas, was a prophet and respected leader in the Jerusalem church who accompanied Paul and Barnabas to Antioch.

Judas.name: Judas
Judas.description: Apostle living at the time of the New Testament
Judas.briefDescription: A brother of Jesus and author of the epistle
Judas.shortDescription: Jude, a brother of Jesus, was the author of the Epistle of Jude and is mentioned in the lists of Jesus' siblings.

Jude.name: Jude
Jude.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Jude.briefDescription: A Hittite woman who married Esau
Jude.shortDescription: Judith, the daughter of Beeri the Hittite, was one of Esau's wives, causing grief to Isaac and Rebekah.

Judith.name: Judith
Judith.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Judith.briefDescription: A Christian woman greeted by Paul in Romans
Judith.shortDescription: Julia, along with Philologus and Nereus, was greeted by Paul in his letter to the Romans.

Julia.name: Julia
Julia.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Julia.briefDescription: A centurion of the Augustan Cohort
Julia.shortDescription: Julius, a centurion of the Augustan Cohort, was responsible for escorting Paul to Rome and showed him kindness during the journey.

Julius.name: Julius
Julius.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Julius.briefDescription: A prominent apostle and relative of Paul
Julius.shortDescription: Junia, along with Andronicus, was recognized as a prominent apostle and a relative of Paul who had been imprisoned with him.

Junia.name: Junia
Junia.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Junia.shortDescription: Jushab-hesed was one of the sons of Zerubbabel, a descendant of King Jehoiachin of Judah.

Jushab-hesed.name: Jushab-hesed
Jushab-hesed.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Jushab-hesed.briefDescription: Titius Justus, a Gentile believer who hosted Paul in Corinth
Jushab-hesed.shortDescription: Titius Justus, a Gentile worshiper of God, hosted Paul in Corinth after he left the synagogue.

Justus.name: Justus
Justus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Justus.briefDescription: A Levite who returned from Babylonian exile
Justus.shortDescription: Kadmiel was a Levite who returned from Babylonian exile and played a significant role in the rebuilding of the temple and the spiritual renewal of the people.

Kadmiel.name: Kadmiel
Kadmiel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Kadmiel.shortDescription: The Kadmonites were one of the Canaanite people groups living in the land promised to Abraham's descendants.

Kadmonites.name: Kadmonites
Kadmonites.briefDescription: A priest during the time of Joiakim
Kadmonites.shortDescription: Kallai was a priest who served during the time of Joiakim, the son of Jeshua, after the Babylonian exile.

Kallai.name: Kallai
Kallai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Kallai.shortDescription: Kareah was the father of Johanan and Jonathan, two military leaders who joined Gedaliah after the fall of Jerusalem to the Babylonians.

Kareah.name: Kareah
Kareah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Kareah.briefDescription: A son of Ishmael and a nomadic tribe
Kareah.shortDescription: Kedar was a son of Ishmael and the eponymous ancestor of a nomadic Arab tribe known for their tents and flocks.

Kedar.name: Kedar
Kedar.description: Arabian living at the time of the Patriarchs
Kedar.briefDescription: A son of Ishmael; Nodab, a Hagrite tribe
Kedar.shortDescription: Kedemah was a son of Ishmael, while the Nodab were a Hagrite tribe defeated by the Reubenites, Gadites, and half-tribe of Manasseh.

Kedemah.name: Kedemah
Kedemah.description: Arabian living at the time of the Patriarchs
Kedemah.briefDescription: A Levite who married a foreign woman
Kedemah.shortDescription: Kelaiah, also known as Kelita, was a Levite who had married a foreign woman and pledged to put her away during Ezra's reform.

Kelaiah.name: Kelaiah
Kelaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Kelaiah.briefDescription: A Levite who taught the Law and sealed the covenant
Kelaiah.shortDescription: Kelita was a Levite who helped explain the Law to the people and sealed the covenant during the time of Nehemiah.

Kelita.name: Kelita
Kelita.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Kelita.briefDescription: A Levite leader during David's reign
Kelita.shortDescription: Kemuel, the son of Hebron, was the leader of the Levites during the reign of King David.

Kemuel.name: Kemuel
Kemuel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Kemuel.briefDescription: Son of Enosh, descendant of Seth
Kemuel.shortDescription: Kenan (Cainan), the son of Enosh, was a descendant of Seth and an ancestor of Noah.

Kenan.name: Kenan
Kenan.description: Man living at the time before the Flood
Kenan.shortDescription: Kenaz was a descendant of Caleb, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Judah.

Kenaz.name: Kenaz
Kenaz.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Kenaz.briefDescription: A nomadic tribe friendly to the Israelites
Kenaz.shortDescription: The Kenites were a nomadic tribe who lived in the region of Canaan and were generally friendly to the Israelites, often providing them with assistance and support.

Kenite.name: Kenite
Kenite.briefDescription: A Canaanite tribe in the Promised Land
Kenite.shortDescription: The Kenizzites were one of the Canaanite tribes inhabiting the land God promised to give to Abraham's descendants.

Kenizzites.name: Kenizzites
Kenizzites.briefDescription: Job's third daughter born after his restoration
Kenizzites.shortDescription: Keren-happuch was the name of Job's third daughter, born to him after God restored his fortunes.

Keren-happuch.name: Keren-happuch
Keren-happuch.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Keren-happuch.briefDescription: A family of temple servants who returned from exile
Keren-happuch.shortDescription: Keros was the name of a family of temple servants (Nethinim) who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian exile.

Keros.name: Keros
Keros.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Keros.shortDescription: Keturah was the concubine Abraham took after the death of Sarah, and she bore him six sons.

Keturah.name: Keturah
Keturah.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Keturah.briefDescription: Job's second daughter born after his restoration
Keturah.shortDescription: Keziah was the name of Job's second daughter, born to him after God restored his fortunes.

Keziah.name: Keziah
Keziah.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Keziah.briefDescription: A Levite from the Merari clan
Keziah.shortDescription: Kish was a Levite from the clan of Merari, whose descendants were assigned duties in the tabernacle service.

Kish.name: Kish
Kish.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Kish.briefDescription: A Levite and father of Jeduthun (or 'Ethan')
Kish.shortDescription: Kishi, also known as Kushaiah, was a Levite from the clan of Merari and the father of Jeduthun (or 'Ethan'), a prominent musician in King David's court.

Kishi.name: Kishi
Kishi.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Kishi.briefDescription: Descendants of Javan, son of Japheth
Kishi.shortDescription: Kittim, a descendant of Japheth, son of Noah.

Kittim.name: Kittim
Kittim.briefDescription: Son of Levi; Levite clan ancestor
Kittim.shortDescription: Kohath, son of Levi, was the ancestor of Moses and Aaron.

Kohath.name: Kohath
Kohath.description: Levi's son living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Kohath.shortDescription: Kolaiah, a false prophet among the exiles in Babylon.

Kolaiah.name: Kolaiah
Kolaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Kolaiah.briefDescription: Son of Hebron, descendant of Caleb
Kolaiah.shortDescription: Korah, son of Hebron, from the tribe of Judah.

Korah.name: Korah
Korah.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Korah.shortDescription: Kore, a Levite gatekeeper, descendant of Korah

Kore.name: Kore
Kore.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Kore.shortDescription: Koz, a descendant of Judah through Ashhur.

Koz.name: Koz
Koz.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Koz.shortDescription: Laadah, a son of Shelah and descendant of Judah.

Laadah.name: Laadah
Laadah.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Laadah.briefDescription: Brother of Rebekah; father of Leah and Rachel
Laadah.shortDescription: Laban, the brother of Rebekah and father of Jacob's wives, Leah and Rachel.

Laban.name: Laban
Laban.briefDescription: A place Moses spoke to Israel
Laban.shortDescription: Laban, a place where Moses spoke to the Israelites (Deu.1.1).

Ladan.name: Ladan
Ladan.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ladan.briefDescription: Father of Eliasaph, a Gershonite Levite
Ladan.shortDescription: Lael, the father of Eliasaph, a leader of the Gershonite Levites.

Lael.name: Lael
Lael.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Lael.shortDescription: Lahad, a descendant of Judah through Jahath.

Lahad.name: Lahad
Lahad.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Lahad.shortDescription: Lahmi, the brother of Goliath, was killed by Elhanan the Bethlehemite.

Lahmi.name: Lahmi
Lahmi.description: Giant living at the time of United Monarchy
Lahmi.shortDescription: Laish, the father of Palti (or 'Paltiel'), to whom Saul gave his daughter Michal in marriage.

Laish.name: Laish
Laish.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Laish.briefDescription: Descendant of Cain; first polygamist mentioned in the Bible
Laish.shortDescription: Lamech, a descendant of Cain, was the first polygamist mentioned in the Bible.

Lamech.name: Lamech
Lamech.description: Man living at the time before the Flood
Lamech.briefDescription: Husband of Deborah the prophetess and judge
Lamech.shortDescription: Lappidoth, the husband of Deborah, a prophetess and judge in Israel.

Lappidoth.name: Lappidoth
Lappidoth.briefDescription: Brother of Mary and Martha; raised from the dead
Lappidoth.shortDescription: Lazarus, the brother of Mary and Martha, was raised from the dead by Jesus (Jhn.11.1-44; 12.1-2,9-11).

Lazarus.name: Lazarus
Lazarus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Lazarus.briefDescription: Wife of Jacob; mother of six sons and Dinah
Lazarus.shortDescription: Leah, the first wife of Jacob and mother of six of his sons and his daughter Dinah.

Leah.name: Leah
Leah.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Leah.briefDescription: Ancestor of temple servants who returned from Babylonian exile
Leah.shortDescription: Lebanah or Lebana, whose descendants were among the temple servants who returned from the Babylonian exile.

Lebanah.name: Lebanah
Lebanah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Lebanah.shortDescription: Lecah, a descendant of Judah through Er.

Lecah.name: Lecah
Lecah.description: Man or town at the time of Divided Monarchy
Lecah.briefDescription: Son of Mizraim; progenitor of a people group
Lecah.shortDescription: Lehabim, a son of Mizraim and progenitor of a people group.

Lehabim.name: Lehabim
Lehabim.briefDescription: King mentioned in Proverbs; his mother taught him wisdom
Lehabim.shortDescription: Lemuel, a king whose wise sayings are recorded in Proverbs 31, as taught by his mother.

Lemuel.name: Lemuel
Lemuel.description: King living at the time of United Monarchy
Lemuel.briefDescription: Son of Dedan, descendant of Abraham and Keturah
Lemuel.shortDescription: Letushim, a son of Dedan and descendant of Abraham and Keturah.

Letushim.name: Letushim
Letushim.briefDescription: Son of Dedan, descendant of Abraham and Keturah
Letushim.shortDescription: Leummim, a son of Dedan and descendant of Abraham and Keturah.

Leummim.name: Leummim
Leummim.briefDescription: Son of Dedan, descendant of Abraham and Keturah
Leummim.shortDescription: Leummim, a son of Dedan and descendant of Abraham and Keturah.

Levi.name: Levi
Levi.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Levi.briefDescription: Grandson of Merari; grandson of Levi
Levi.shortDescription: Libni, a grandson of Merari and grandson of Levi.

Libni.name: Libni
Libni.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Libni.shortDescription: Likhi, a son of Shemida and descendant of Manasseh.

Likhi.name: Likhi
Likhi.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Likhi.briefDescription: Christian in Rome; greeted by Paul
Likhi.shortDescription: Linus, a Christian in Rome to whom Paul sent greetings.

Linus.name: Linus
Linus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Linus.briefDescription: Symbolic name of Hosea's son; represents God's rejection of Israel
Linus.shortDescription: Lo-ammi, meaning "not my people," was the symbolic name given to Hosea's son, representing God's rejection of Israel.

Lo-ammi.name: Lo-ammi
Lo-ammi.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Lo-ammi.briefDescription: Grandmother of Timothy; commended for her faith
Lo-ammi.shortDescription: Lois, the grandmother of Timothy, was commended by Paul for her sincere faith.

Lois.name: Lois
Lois.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Lois.briefDescription: Symbolic name of Hosea's daughter; represents God's withdrawal of mercy
Lois.shortDescription: Lo-ruhamah, meaning "not pitied," was the symbolic name given to Hosea's daughter, representing God's withdrawal of mercy from Israel.

Lo-ruhamah.name: Lo-ruhamah
Lo-ruhamah.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Lo-ruhamah.briefDescription: Abraham's nephew; rescued from Sodom's destruction
Lo-ruhamah.shortDescription: Lot, the nephew of Abraham, was rescued from Sodom before its destruction.

Lot.name: Lot
Lot.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Lot.briefDescription: Son of Seir the Horite; chief of Edom
Lot.shortDescription: Lotan, a son of Seir the Horite and a chief of Edom.

Lotan.name: Lotan
Lotan.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Lotan.briefDescription: Prophet and teacher in the church at Antioch.
Lotan.shortDescription: Lucius of Cyrene was a prophet and teacher in the church at Antioch who was involved in sending Paul and Barnabas on their first missionary journey.

Lucius.name: Lucius
Lucius.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Lucius.briefDescription: Son of Shem; progenitor of the Lydians
Lucius.shortDescription: Lud, a son of Shem and progenitor of the Lydians.

Lud.name: Lud
Lud.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Lud.briefDescription: Son of Mizraim; progenitor of a people group
Lud.shortDescription: Ludim, a son of Mizraim and progenitor of a people group.

Ludim.name: Ludim
Ludim.briefDescription: Physician; companion of Paul; author of Luke and Acts
Ludim.shortDescription: Luke, a physician and companion of Paul, was the author of the Gospel of Luke and the Book of Acts.

Luke.name: Luke
Luke.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Luke.briefDescription: Seller of purple goods; converted by Paul in Philippi
Luke.shortDescription: Lydia, a seller of purple goods from Thyatira, was converted by Paul in Philippi and hosted the church in her home.

Lydia.name: Lydia
Lydia.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Lydia.briefDescription: Tetrarch of Abilene during John the Baptist's ministry
Lydia.shortDescription: Lysanias, the tetrarch of Abilene, ruled during the time of John the Baptist's ministry (Luk.3.1).

Lysanias.name: Lysanias
Lysanias.description: Tetrarch of Abilene living at the time of the New Testament
Lysanias.briefDescription: Roman commander who rescued Paul from a Jewish mob
Lysanias.shortDescription: Claudius Lysias, the Roman commander in Jerusalem, rescued Paul from a Jewish mob and sent him to Felix.

Lysias.name: Lysias
Lysias.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Lysias.briefDescription: Father of Hanan, one of David's mighty men
Lysias.shortDescription: Maacah was the father of Hanan, one of David's mighty men.

Maacah.name: Maacah
Maacah.shortDescription: Maacah (Maacath), a region near Geshur (Deu.3.14; Joshua 12:5).

Maacathites.name: Maacathites
Maacathites.briefDescription: One of the men who married foreign women during Ezra's time
Maacathites.shortDescription: Maadai was one of the men who had married foreign women during the time of Ezra.

Maadai.name: Maadai
Maadai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Maadai.briefDescription: Levite musician who participated in the dedication of Jerusalem's wall
Maadai.shortDescription: Maai, a Levite musician, participated in the dedication of Jerusalem's wall during the time of Nehemiah.

Maai.name: Maai
Maai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Maai.briefDescription: Priest during the time of David and Nehemiah
Maai.shortDescription: Maasai, also known as Amashsai, was a priest who served during the time of  Nehemiah.

Maasai.name: Maasai
Maasai.description: Levite living at the time of Exile and Return
Maasai.briefDescription: Levite gatekeeper during the reign of David
Maasai.shortDescription: Maaseiah, a Levite gatekeeper, served during the reign of King David.

Maaseiah.name: Maaseiah
Maaseiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Maaseiah.shortDescription: Maath is mentioned in the genealogy of Jesus Christ as recorded in the Gospel of Luke.

Maath.name: Maath
Maath.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Maath.briefDescription: Son of Ram, from the tribe of Judah
Maath.shortDescription: Maaz was a son of Ram, a descendant of Judah.

Maaz.name: Maaz
Maaz.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Maaz.briefDescription: Priest who served in the 24th division
Maaz.shortDescription: Maaziah was the head of the 24th priestly division during the time of David.

Maaziah.name: Maaziah
Maaziah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Maaziah.briefDescription: Gadite warrior who joined David at Ziklag
Maaziah.shortDescription: Machbannai, a Gadite warrior, was one of the men who joined David at Ziklag.

Machbannai.name: Machbannai
Machbannai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Machbannai.briefDescription: Daughter or town founded by Sheva
Machbannai.shortDescription: Machbenah was a descendant or town of Caleb, the son of Hezron, from the tribe of Judah.

Machbenah.name: Machbenah
Machbenah.description: Woman or town of at the time of Divided Monarchy
Machbenah.briefDescription: Father of Geuel, from the tribe of Gad
Machbenah.shortDescription: Machi was the father of Geuel, a scout from the tribe of Gad sent to explore Canaan.

Machi.name: Machi
Machi.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Machi.shortDescription: Machir, the son of Ammiel, hosted Mephibosheth, Jonathan's son, in Lo-debar and later provided supplies to David during Absalom's rebellion.

Machir.name: Machir
Machir.description: Manasseh's son living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Machir.briefDescription: One of those who married foreign women
Machir.shortDescription: Machnadebai was among those who had married foreign women during the time of Ezra.

Machnadebai.name: Machnadebai
Machnadebai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Machnadebai.briefDescription: Son of Japheth; progenitor of the Medes
Machnadebai.shortDescription: Madai, a son of Japheth, is considered the ancestor of the Medes.

Madai.name: Madai
Madai.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Madai.briefDescription: Descendant of Caleb, son of Hezron
Madai.shortDescription: Madmannah was a descendant of Caleb, the son of Hezron, from the tribe of Judah.

Madmannah.name: Madmannah
Madmannah.briefDescription: A town in the Negev of Judah
Madmannah.shortDescription: Madmannah (Beth-marcaboth), a town in the Negev region of Judah (Joshua 15:31; 19:5; 1Ch.4.31).

Magdiel.name: Magdiel
Magdiel.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Magdiel.briefDescription: Son of Japheth; progenitor of a people group
Magdiel.shortDescription: Magog, a son of Japheth, is considered the ancestor of a people group mentioned in Ezekiel's prophecy.

Magog.name: Magog
Magog.briefDescription: A nation that will attack Israel in the future
Magog.shortDescription: Magog, a nation that will join forces with Gog to attack Israel in the future, as prophesied in Ezekiel and Revelation (Ezk.38.2; 39:6; Revelation 20:8).

Magpiash.name: Magpiash
Magpiash.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Magpiash.briefDescription: Descendant of Seth; ancestor of Noah
Magpiash.shortDescription: Mahalalel was a descendant of Seth and an ancestor of Noah, living before the flood.

Mahalalel.name: Mahalalel
Mahalalel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Mahalalel.shortDescription: Mahalath was one of King Rehoboam's wives, a granddaughter of David through his son Jerimoth.

Mahalath.name: Mahalath
Mahalath.briefDescription: A musical or literary term in the Psalms
Mahalath.shortDescription: Mahalath, mentioned in the superscriptions of Psalms 53 and 88, is a musical or literary term of uncertain meaning.

Maharai.name: Maharai
Maharai.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Maharai.briefDescription: Levite who helped purify the temple under Hezekiah
Maharai.shortDescription: Mahath, a Levite, participated in the purification of the temple during the reign of King Hezekiah.

Mahath.name: Mahath
Mahath.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Mahath.briefDescription: Some of David's mighty men, possibly from Mahavim.
Mahath.shortDescription: The Mahavites were represented among David's mighty men.

Mahavites.name: Mahavites
Mahavites.briefDescription: Son of Heman, a musician in David's time.
Mahavites.shortDescription: Mahazioth, a son of Heman, served as a musician during David's reign.

Mahazioth.name: Mahazioth
Mahazioth.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Mahazioth.briefDescription: Symbolic name given to Isaiah's son, meaning "swift is booty, speedy is prey."
Mahazioth.shortDescription: Maher-shalal-hash-baz, meaning "swift is booty, speedy is prey," was the symbolic name given to Isaiah's son.

Maher-shalal-hash-baz.name: Maher-shalal-hash-baz
Maher-shalal-hash-baz.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Maher-shalal-hash-baz.shortDescription: Mahlah was the daughter of Zelophehad's sister.

Mahlah.name: Mahlah
Mahlah.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Mahlah.briefDescription: Son of Mushi, grandson of Merari.
Mahlah.shortDescription: Mahli, a son of Mushi and grandson of Merari, was a Levite during the time of David.

Mahli.name: Mahli
Mahli.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Mahli.briefDescription: Husband of Ruth, son of Elimelech and Naomi.
Mahli.shortDescription: Mahlon, the son of Elimelech and Naomi, married Ruth but died in Moab.

Mahlon.name: Mahlon
Mahlon.briefDescription: Father of wise men surpassed in wisdom by Solomon.
Mahlon.shortDescription: Mahol was the father of wise men whom Solomon surpassed in wisdom.

Mahol.name: Mahol
Mahol.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Mahol.shortDescription: Mahseiah was the grandfather of Baruch, Jeremiah's scribe.

Mahseiah.name: Mahseiah
Mahseiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Mahseiah.briefDescription: Prophet who wrote the book bearing his name.
Mahseiah.shortDescription: Malachi, a prophet, received the word of the Lord to Israel.

Malachi.name: Malachi
Malachi.description: Prophet living at the time of Exile and Return
Malachi.shortDescription: Malcam was a son of Shaharaim, a Benjamite, born in Moab.

Malcam.name: Malcam
Malcam.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Malcam.briefDescription: Son of Pashur, involved in plot against Jeremiah.
Malcam.shortDescription: Malchiah, son of Pashur, was involved in a plot against Jeremiah.

Malchiah.name: Malchiah
Malchiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Malchiah.briefDescription: Son of Beriah, grandson of Asher.
Malchiah.shortDescription: Malchiel, son of Beriah and grandson of Asher, was the ancestor of the Malchielites.

Malchiel.name: Malchiel
Malchiel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Malchiel.briefDescription: Head of the fifth priestly division in David's time.
Malchiel.shortDescription: Malchijah was the head of the fifth priestly division during David's reign.

Malchijah.name: Malchijah
Malchijah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Malchijah.briefDescription: Descendant of King Jeconiah (Jehoiachin) of Judah.
Malchijah.shortDescription: Malchiram was a descendant of King Jeconiah (Jehoiachin) of Judah.

Malchiram.name: Malchiram
Malchiram.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Malchiram.briefDescription: Son of King Saul, killed in battle against the Philistines.
Malchiram.shortDescription: Malchi-shua, a son of King Saul, was killed in battle against the Philistines.

Malchi-shua.name: Malchi-shua
Malchi-shua.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Malchi-shua.briefDescription: High priest's servant whose ear was cut off by Peter.
Malchi-shua.shortDescription: Malchus, the high priest's servant, had his ear cut off by Peter during Jesus' arrest.

Malchus.name: Malchus
Malchus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Malchus.briefDescription: Son of Heman, a musician in David's court.
Malchus.shortDescription: Mallothi, a son of Heman, was a musician in David's court.

Mallothi.name: Mallothi
Mallothi.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Mallothi.briefDescription: Son of Hashabiah, a Merarite Levite.
Mallothi.shortDescription: Malluch, a son of Hashabiah, was a Merarite Levite.

Malluch.name: Malluch
Malluch.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Malluch.shortDescription: Mamre was an Amorite and an ally of Abraham.

Mamre.name: Mamre
Mamre.briefDescription: A place near Hebron; associated with Abraham
Mamre.shortDescription: Mamre, a place near Hebron where Abraham lived and built an altar to the Lord (Genesis 13:18; 18:1).

Manaen.name: Manaen
Manaen.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Manaen.shortDescription: Manahath was a son of Shobal, a Horite.

Manahath.name: Manahath
Manahath.briefDescription: A place where Benjamites lived after the exile
Manahath.shortDescription: Manahath (Nohah), a place where some Benjamites lived after returning from the Babylonian exile (1Ch.8.6).

Manasseh.name: Manasseh
Manasseh.description: Joseph's son living at the time of the Patriarchs
Manasseh.briefDescription: Father of Samson, from the tribe of Dan.
Manasseh.shortDescription: Manoah, Samson's father from the tribe of Dan, was visited by the Angel of the Lord who announced Samson's birth.

Manoah.name: Manoah
Manoah.description: Samson's father living before Israel's Monarchy
Manoah.briefDescription: Father of Achish, king of Gath.
Manoah.shortDescription: Maoch (or Maacah) was the father of Achish, king of Gath, to whom David fled.

Maoch.name: Maoch
Maoch.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Maoch.briefDescription: Son of Shammai, a descendant of Caleb.
Maoch.shortDescription: Maon, a son of Shammai, was a descendant of Caleb.

Maon.name: Maon
Maon.briefDescription: A city in Judah; home of Nabal and Abigail
Maon.shortDescription: Maon, a city in the hill country of Judah (Joshua 15:55), home of Nabal and Abigail (1Sa.25.2).

Mareshah.name: Mareshah
Mareshah.briefDescription: A town in Judah; fortified by Rehoboam
Mareshah.shortDescription: Mareshah, a town in the Shephelah of Judah (Joshua 15:44), fortified by Rehoboam (2Ch.11.8) and the site of Asa's victory over Zerah the Ethiopian (2Ch.14.9-10).

Mark.name: Mark
Mark.description: Levite living at the time of the New Testament
Mark.briefDescription: One of the seven princes of Persia and Media.
Mark.shortDescription: Marsena was one of the seven princes of Persia and Media who advised King Ahasuerus.

Marsena.name: Marsena
Marsena.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Marsena.briefDescription: Sister of Mary and Lazarus, hosted Jesus.
Marsena.shortDescription: Martha, the sister of Mary and Lazarus, hosted Jesus in her home.

Martha.name: Martha
Martha.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Martha.briefDescription: Mother of John Mark; hosted prayer meeting.
Martha.shortDescription: Mary, the mother of John Mark, hosted a prayer meeting in her house.

Mary.name: Mary
Mary.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Mary.briefDescription: Follower of Jesus, first witness of His resurrection.
Mary.shortDescription: Mary Magdalene, a follower of Jesus, was the first to witness His resurrection.

Mary_Magdalene.name: Mary_Magdalene
Mary_Magdalene.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Mary_Magdalene.briefDescription: Son of Aram, grandson of Shem.
Mary_Magdalene.shortDescription: Mash (or Meshech), a son of Aram, was a grandson of Shem.

Mash.name: Mash
Mash.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Mash.briefDescription: Son of Ishmael, grandson of Abraham.
Mash.shortDescription: Massa, a son of Ishmael, was a grandson of Abraham.

Massa.name: Massa
Massa.description: Arabian living at the time of the Patriarchs
Massa.briefDescription: Mother of Mehetabel, wife of Hadar, king of Edom.
Massa.shortDescription: Matred was the mother of Mehetabel, who was the wife of Hadar, king of Edom.

Matred.name: Matred
Matred.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Matred.briefDescription: Saul's ancestral clan from the tribe of Benjamin.
Matred.shortDescription: The Matrite (or Matri) clan was Saul's ancestral clan from the tribe of Benjamin.

Matri.name: Matri
Matri.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Matri.briefDescription: Priest of Baal, killed during Jehoiada's reforms.
Matri.shortDescription: Mattan, a priest of Baal, was killed during Jehoiada's religious reforms.

Mattan.name: Mattan
Mattan.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Mattan.briefDescription: Son of Heman, a musician in David's court.
Mattan.shortDescription: Mattaniah, a son of Heman, was a musician in King David's court.

Mattaniah.name: Mattaniah
Mattaniah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Mattaniah.shortDescription: Mattatha was an ancestor of Jesus Christ, mentioned in His genealogy.

Mattatha.name: Mattatha
Mattatha.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Mattatha.shortDescription: Mattathias was an ancestor of Jesus Christ, mentioned in His genealogy.

Mattathias.name: Mattathias
Mattathias.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Mattathias.briefDescription: Descendant of Hashum, who divorced his foreign wife.
Mattathias.shortDescription: Mattattah, a descendant of Hashum, divorced his foreign wife during Ezra's reforms.

Mattattah.name: Mattattah
Mattattah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Mattattah.briefDescription: Descendant of Hashum, who divorced his foreign wife.
Mattattah.shortDescription: Mattenai, a descendant of Hashum, divorced his foreign wife during Ezra's reforms.

Mattenai.name: Mattenai
Mattenai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Mattenai.briefDescription: Ancestor of Joseph, the husband of Mary.
Mattenai.shortDescription: This is one of the ancestors of Joseph, the husband of Mary, the mother of Jesus.

Matthan.name: Matthan
Matthan.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Matthan.shortDescription: Matthat was an ancestor of Jesus Christ, mentioned in His genealogy.

Matthat.name: Matthat
Matthat.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Matthat.briefDescription: Apostle, tax collector, author of the Gospel of Matthew.
Matthat.shortDescription: Matthew, also known as Levi, was a tax collector who became one of Jesus' twelve apostles and authored the Gospel of Matthew.

Matthew.name: Matthew
Matthew.description: Apostle living at the time of the New Testament
Matthew.briefDescription: Apostle chosen to replace Judas Iscariot.
Matthew.shortDescription: Matthias was chosen by lot to replace Judas Iscariot as one of the twelve apostles.

Matthias.name: Matthias
Matthias.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Matthias.briefDescription: Levite musician during the time of David.
Matthias.shortDescription: Mattithiah, a Levite, served as a musician during the time of King David.

Mattithiah.name: Mattithiah
Mattithiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Mattithiah.briefDescription: Clan or inhabitants of an unknown place called Mecherah.
Mattithiah.shortDescription: The Mecherathites were a clan or inhabitants of an unknown place called Mecherah.

Mecherathites.name: Mecherathites
Mecherathites.briefDescription: Eldad's companion who prophesied in the Israelite camp.
Mecherathites.shortDescription: Medad, along with Eldad, prophesied in the Israelite camp, though they had not gone to the tent of meeting.

Medad.name: Medad
Medad.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Medad.shortDescription: Medan was a son of Abraham and his concubine Keturah.

Medan.name: Medan
Medan.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Medan.briefDescription: Wife of Hadar, king of Edom.
Medan.shortDescription: Mehetabel was the wife of Hadar, a king of Edom.

Mehetabel.name: Mehetabel
Mehetabel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Mehetabel.briefDescription: Ancestor of temple servants who returned from Babylonian exile.
Mehetabel.shortDescription: Mehida was an ancestor of temple servants (Nethinim) who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian captivity.

Mehida.name: Mehida
Mehida.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Mehida.shortDescription: Mehir was the son of Chelub.

Mehir.name: Mehir
Mehir.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Mehir.briefDescription: People from Abel-meholah, including Adriel and Barzillai.
Mehir.shortDescription: The Meholathites were people from Abel-meholah, including Adriel, who married Saul's daughter Merab, and Barzillai, whose son married Saul's daughter Michal.

Meholathites.name: Meholathites
Meholathites.briefDescription: Descendant of Cain; father of Methushael.
Meholathites.shortDescription: Mehujael was a descendant of Cain and the father of Methushael.

Mehujael.name: Mehujael
Mehujael.description: Man living at the time before the Flood
Mehujael.briefDescription: One of King Ahasuerus' seven eunuchs.
Mehujael.shortDescription: Mehuman was one of the seven eunuchs who served King Ahasuerus.

Mehuman.name: Mehuman
Mehuman.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Mehuman.briefDescription: Gibeonite who helped repair Jerusalem's wall under Nehemiah.
Mehuman.shortDescription: Melatiah the Gibeonite helped repair a section of Jerusalem's wall during Nehemiah's rebuilding project.

Melatiah.name: Melatiah
Melatiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Melatiah.briefDescription: An ancestor of Jesus in Luke's genealogy.
Melatiah.shortDescription: Melchi, mentioned in Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

Melchi.name: Melchi
Melchi.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Melchi.briefDescription: King of Salem and priest of God Most High.
Melchi.shortDescription: Melchizedek, king of Salem and priest of God Most High, who blessed Abraham.

Melchizedek.name: Melchizedek
Melchizedek.description: Priest living at the time of the Patriarchs
Melchizedek.briefDescription: An ancestor of Jesus in Luke's genealogy.
Melchizedek.shortDescription: Melea, mentioned in Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

Melea.name: Melea
Melea.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Melea.shortDescription: Melech, a descendant of King Saul mentioned in the genealogies of Benjamin.

Melech.name: Melech
Melech.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Melech.briefDescription: Babylonian official in charge of Daniel and his friends.
Melech.shortDescription: Melzar, the steward appointed by the chief eunuch to oversee Daniel and his companions.

Melzar.name: Melzar
Melzar.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Melzar.briefDescription: One of the seven princes of Persia and Media.
Melzar.shortDescription: Memucan, a wise prince who advised King Ahasuerus to depose Queen Vashti.

Memucan.name: Memucan
Memucan.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Memucan.briefDescription: King of Israel who reigned for ten years.
Memucan.shortDescription: Menahem, king of Israel, known for his cruelty and for paying tribute to Assyria.

Menahem.name: Menahem
Menahem.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Menahem.briefDescription: An ancestor of Jesus in Luke's genealogy.
Menahem.shortDescription: Menna, mentioned in Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

Menna.name: Menna
Menna.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Menna.shortDescription: Menuhoth, a clan descended from Caleb, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Menuhoth.name: Menuhoth
Menuhoth.briefDescription: Son of Othniel and father of Ophrah.
Menuhoth.shortDescription: Meonothai, son of Othniel, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Meonothai.name: Meonothai
Meonothai.briefDescription: Son of Saul and Rizpah, given to the Gibeonites.
Meonothai.shortDescription: Mephibosheth, son of Saul and Rizpah, was given to the Gibeonites for execution.

Mephibosheth.name: Mephibosheth
Mephibosheth.description: Jonathan's son living at the time of United Monarchy
Mephibosheth.shortDescription: Merab, the eldest daughter of King Saul, was promised to David but given to Adriel.

Merab.name: Merab
Merab.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
Merab.briefDescription: A priest during the high priesthood of Joiakim.
Merab.shortDescription: Meraiah, a priest who served during the time of Joiakim the high priest.

Meraiah.name: Meraiah
Meraiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Meraiah.briefDescription: A priest in the lineage of Eleazar.
Meraiah.shortDescription: Meraioth, a priest in the lineage of Eleazar, mentioned in genealogies.

Meraioth.name: Meraioth
Meraioth.description: Priest living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Meraioth.briefDescription: The third son of Levi and founder of the Merarites.
Meraioth.shortDescription: Merari, the third son of Levi, was the founder of the Merarite clan of Levites.

Merari.name: Merari
Merari.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Merari.briefDescription: Son of Ezrah from the tribe of Judah.
Merari.shortDescription: Mered, a son of Ezrah, married Bithiah, the daughter of Pharaoh.

Mered.name: Mered
Mered.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Mered.briefDescription: An Israelite who married a foreign woman.
Mered.shortDescription: Meremoth, an Israelite who married a foreign woman during the Babylonian exile.

Meremoth.name: Meremoth
Meremoth.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Meremoth.briefDescription: One of the seven princes of Persia and Media.
Meremoth.shortDescription: Meres, one of the seven princes of Persia and Media who advised King Ahasuerus.

Meres.name: Meres
Meres.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Meres.briefDescription: King of Babylon who sent envoys to Hezekiah.
Meres.shortDescription: Merodach-baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and a gift to Hezekiah, king of Judah.

Merodach-baladan.name: Merodach-baladan
Merodach-baladan.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Merodach-baladan.briefDescription: Son of Caleb and founder of Ziph.
Merodach-baladan.shortDescription: Mesha, a son of Caleb, was the  father (ie founder) of Ziph.

Mesha.name: Mesha
Mesha.briefDescription: A place marking the Joktanite territory
Mesha.shortDescription: Mesha, a place mentioned as the limit of the territory settled by the descendants of Joktan (Genesis 10:30).

Meshech.name: Meshech
Meshech.briefDescription: A nation, descendant of Japheth; associated with Tubal
Meshech.shortDescription: Meshech, a nation descended from Japheth (Genesis 10:2), often mentioned together with Tubal as trading partners of Tyre (Ezk.27.13) and allies of Gog (Ezk.38.2, 3; 39:1).

Meshelemiah.name: Meshelemiah
Meshelemiah.description: Levite living at the time of Exile and Return
Meshelemiah.briefDescription: A leader who sealed the covenant with Nehemiah.
Meshelemiah.shortDescription: Meshezabel, one of the leaders who sealed the covenant with Nehemiah.

Meshezabel.name: Meshezabel
Meshezabel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Meshezabel.briefDescription: A priest who settled in Jerusalem after the exile.
Meshezabel.shortDescription: Meshillemith (also called Meshillemoth), a priest who settled in Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Meshillemith.name: Meshillemith
Meshillemith.description: Levite living at the time of Exile and Return
Meshillemith.briefDescription: An Ephraimite leader during the reign of Pekah.
Meshillemith.shortDescription: Meshillemoth, an Ephraimite leader who opposed the enslavement of Judean captives.

Meshillemoth.name: Meshillemoth
Meshillemoth.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Meshillemoth.briefDescription: A Simeonite leader during Hezekiah's reign.
Meshillemoth.shortDescription: Meshobab, a Simeonite leader during the reign of King Hezekiah.

Meshobab.name: Meshobab
Meshobab.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Meshobab.briefDescription: Son of Zerubbabel, a descendant of David.
Meshobab.shortDescription: Meshullam, a son of Zerubbabel, mentioned in the genealogy of David's descendants.

Meshullam.name: Meshullam
Meshullam.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Meshullam.briefDescription: Mother of Amon, king of Judah.
Meshullam.shortDescription: Meshullemeth, the mother of Amon, king of Judah, was from Jotbah.

Meshullemeth.name: Meshullemeth
Meshullemeth.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Meshullemeth.briefDescription: The oldest person mentioned in the Bible.
Meshullemeth.shortDescription: Methuselah, the son of Enoch, was the oldest person mentioned in the Bible, living 969 years.

Methuselah.name: Methuselah
Methuselah.description: Man living at the time before the Flood
Methuselah.briefDescription: A descendant of Cain; father of Lamech.
Methuselah.shortDescription: Methushael, a descendant of Cain, was the father of Lamech.

Methushael.name: Methushael
Methushael.description: Man living at the time before the Flood
Methushael.briefDescription: A group of temple servants who returned from exile.
Methushael.shortDescription: The Meunim were a group of temple servants who returned to Jerusalem from the Babylonian exile.

Meunim.name: Meunim
Meunim.briefDescription: A people group who were enemies of Israel.
Meunim.shortDescription: The Meunites were a people group who lived near Israel and were enemies of God's people.

Meunites.name: Meunites
Meunites.briefDescription: Grandfather of Mehetabel, wife of Edomite king Hadar.
Meunites.shortDescription: Mezahab was the grandfather of Mehetabel, who was the wife of Hadar, an Edomite king.

Mezahab.name: Mezahab
Mezahab.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Mezahab.briefDescription: The people group of Jaasiel, one of David's warriors.
Mezahab.shortDescription: The Mezobaites were the people group of Jaasiel, one of David's mighty warriors.

Mezobaites.name: Mezobaites
Mezobaites.briefDescription: One of David's mighty warriors, son of Hagri.
Mezobaites.shortDescription: Mibhar, the son of Hagri, was one of David's mighty warriors.

Mibhar.name: Mibhar
Mibhar.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Mibhar.shortDescription: Mibsam, a descendant of Simeon, was the son of Mishma.

Mibsam.name: Mibsam
Mibsam.description: Arabian living at the time of the Patriarchs
Mibsam.briefDescription: A chief of Edom, descendant of Esau.
Mibsam.shortDescription: Mibzar was one of the chiefs of Edom, descended from Esau.

Mibzar.name: Mibzar
Mibzar.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Mibzar.briefDescription: A Levite who lived in Jerusalem after the exile.
Mibzar.shortDescription: Mica (or Micah), a Levite, lived in Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Mica.name: Mica
Mica.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Mica.briefDescription: A Levite, son of Uzziel, during David's reign.
Mica.shortDescription: Micah, a Levite and son of Uzziel, served during the reign of King David.

Micah.name: Micah
Micah.description: Prophet living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Micah.briefDescription: A prophet who foretold King Ahab's death.
Micah.shortDescription: Micaiah (or Micahu), a prophet, foretold the death of King Ahab of Israel in battle against the Arameans.

Micaiah.name: Micaiah
Micaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Micaiah.briefDescription: A Manassite who joined David at Ziklag.
Micaiah.shortDescription: Michael, a Manassite, was one of the men who joined David at Ziklag when he was fleeing from King Saul.

Michael.name: Michael
Michael.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Michael.briefDescription: Daughter of Saul; wife of David.
Michael.shortDescription: Michal, the younger daughter of King Saul, became David's wife and helped him escape from Saul's attempts to kill him.

Michal.name: Michal
Michal.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
Michal.briefDescription: A Benjamite who lived in Jerusalem.
Michal.shortDescription: Michri, a Benjamite, is mentioned in the list of the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

Michri.name: Michri
Michri.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Michri.briefDescription: Son of Abraham and Keturah; father of the Midianites.
Michri.shortDescription: Midian, a son of Abraham and Keturah, was the ancestor of the Midianite people.

Midian.name: Midian
Midian.briefDescription: A nation descended from Abraham; oppressed Israel in the time of the judges
Midian.shortDescription: Midian, a nation descended from Abraham through Keturah (Genesis 25:2), which oppressed Israel during the time of the judges (Judges 6-8) and was later defeated by Gideon (Judges 7-8).

Mijamin.name: Mijamin
Mijamin.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Mijamin.briefDescription: A military commander during David's reign.
Mijamin.shortDescription: Mikloth was the leader of the second division of David's army, serving during the second month of each year.

Mikloth.name: Mikloth
Mikloth.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Mikloth.briefDescription: A Levite musician during David's reign.
Mikloth.shortDescription: Mikneiah, a Levite, was one of the musicians appointed by David to play the harp during the procession of the ark to Jerusalem.

Mikneiah.name: Mikneiah
Mikneiah.description: Singer living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Mikneiah.briefDescription: A priest who participated in the dedication of Jerusalem's wall.
Mikneiah.shortDescription: Milalai, a priest, was among the musicians who participated in the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem during the time of Nehemiah.

Milalai.name: Milalai
Milalai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Milalai.shortDescription: Milcah, the daughter of Haran and wife of Nahor, was the mother of Bethuel and the grandmother of Rebekah.

Milcah.name: Milcah
Milcah.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Milcah.shortDescription: Miniamin, a Levite, assisted in distributing the offerings and tithes to his fellow Levites during the reign of King Hezekiah.

Miniamin.name: Miniamin
Miniamin.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Miniamin.briefDescription: A descendant of Judah, mentioned in a genealogy.
Miniamin.shortDescription: Miriam, a descendant of Judah, is mentioned in the genealogy of Ezrah.

Miriam.name: Miriam
Miriam.description: Moses' sister living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Miriam.briefDescription: A Benjamite mentioned in a genealogy.
Miriam.shortDescription: Mirmah, a Benjamite, is listed in the genealogy of Shaharaim.

Mirmah.name: Mirmah
Mirmah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Mirmah.briefDescription: A Jewish exile in Babylon, friend of Daniel.
Mirmah.shortDescription: Mishael, also known as Meshach, was one of the four young Jewish men chosen to serve in Nebuchadnezzar's court and was a companion of Daniel during the Babylonian exile.

Mishael.name: Mishael
Mishael.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Mishael.shortDescription: Misham, a Benjamite, was one of the sons of Elpaal mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Benjamin.

Misham.name: Misham
Misham.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Misham.shortDescription: Mishma, a descendant of Simeon, was the son of Mibsam and the father of Hammuel.

Mishma.name: Mishma
Mishma.description: Arabian living at the time of the Patriarchs
Mishma.briefDescription: A Gadite warrior who joined David at Ziklag.
Mishma.shortDescription: Mishmannah, a Gadite, was one of the warriors who joined David at Ziklag when he was fleeing from Saul.

Mishmannah.name: Mishmannah
Mishmannah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Mishmannah.briefDescription: A clan descended from Shobal, of the tribe of Judah.
Mishmannah.shortDescription: The Mishraites were a clan descended from Shobal, who was a descendant of Judah through Caleb.

Mishraites.name: Mishraites
Mishraites.briefDescription: One of the returnees from Babylonian exile.
Mishraites.shortDescription: Mispar, also known as Mispereth, was among the first group of exiles who returned to Jerusalem from Babylon under the leadership of Zerubbabel.

Mispar.name: Mispar
Mispar.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Mispar.briefDescription: The people of an unknown place or family.
Mispar.shortDescription: The Mithnites are mentioned in the context of Joshaphat the Mithnite, one of David's mighty warriors, but no further information about them is provided.

Mithnites.name: Mithnites
Mithnites.briefDescription: The treasurer of King Cyrus of Persia.
Mithnites.shortDescription: Mithredath, the treasurer of King Cyrus of Persia, was responsible for overseeing the return of the temple articles to Sheshbazzar, the prince of Judah.

Mithredath.name: Mithredath
Mithredath.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Mithredath.briefDescription: A grandson of Esau and a chief of Edom.
Mithredath.shortDescription: Mizzah was a grandson of Esau and one of the chiefs of Edom listed in the genealogies of Genesis and 1 Chronicles.

Mizzah.name: Mizzah
Mizzah.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Mizzah.briefDescription: A Cypriot disciple who hosted Paul in Jerusalem.
Mizzah.shortDescription: Mnason, an early disciple from Cyprus, provided lodging for Paul and his companions when they arrived in Jerusalem.

Mnason.name: Mnason
Mnason.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Mnason.briefDescription: Son of Lot, born from an incestuous relationship.
Mnason.shortDescription: Moab, the son of Lot by his eldest daughter, was the ancestor of the Moabite people.

Moab.name: Moab
Moab.briefDescription: Ancient kingdom east of the Dead Sea in Jordan
Moab.shortDescription: Moab was an ancient kingdom located east of the Dead Sea in present-day Jordan, often in conflict with Israel.

Molid.name: Molid
Molid.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Molid.briefDescription: Esther's cousin, a Jewish leader in the Persian court.
Molid.shortDescription: Mordecai, Esther's cousin and guardian, played a crucial role in saving the Jews from Haman's plot during the reign of King Ahasuerus (Xerxes) of Persia.

Mordecai.name: Mordecai
Mordecai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Mordecai.briefDescription: Prophet who led Israel out of Egypt
Mordecai.shortDescription: Moses was a prophet whom God used to lead Israel out of slavery in Egypt and give them the law.

Moses.name: Moses
Moses.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Moses.briefDescription: Mother-in-law of Peter, healed by Jesus
Moses.shortDescription: Peter's unnamed mother-in-law, whom Jesus healed of a fever.

motherInLaw_of_Peter.name: motherInLaw_of_Peter
motherInLaw_of_Peter.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
motherInLaw_of_Peter.briefDescription: Son of Caleb by his concubine Ephah
motherInLaw_of_Peter.shortDescription: Moza was a son of Caleb, the renowned spy, by his concubine Ephah.

Moza.name: Moza
Moza.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Moza.shortDescription: Muppim, also known as Shephupham, Shephuphan, or Shuphamite, was a son of Benjamin mentioned in the genealogies of Genesis, Numbers, and 1 Chronicles.

Muppim.name: Muppim
Muppim.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Muppim.briefDescription: A Levite, son of Merari, and head of a clan.
Muppim.shortDescription: Mushi was a son of Merari and a grandson of Levi, who became the head of the Mushite clan within the Levitical tribe.

Mushi.name: Mushi
Mushi.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Mushi.briefDescription: Son of Caleb, from the tribe of Judah.
Mushi.shortDescription: Naam was a son of Caleb, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Judah in 1 Chronicles.

Naam.name: Naam
Naam.briefDescription: An Ammonite; mother of King Rehoboam of Judah.
Naam.shortDescription: Naamah, an Ammonite woman, was the mother of Rehoboam, who succeeded his father Solomon as king of Judah.

Naamah.name: Naamah
Naamah.briefDescription: A town in the Judean lowlands
Naamah.shortDescription: Naamah was a town in the Judean lowlands, mentioned in the tribal allotment of Judah.

Naaman.name: Naaman
Naaman.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Naaman.briefDescription: The people group of Zophar, Job's friend.
Naaman.shortDescription: The Naamathites were the people group to which Zophar, one of Job's three friends, belonged.

Naamathites.name: Naamathites
Naamathites.briefDescription: Wife of Ashhur, the founder of Tekoa.
Naamathites.shortDescription: Naarah was one of the two wives of Ashhur, the  father (ie founder) of Tekoa, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Naarah.name: Naarah
Naarah.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Naarah.briefDescription: One of David's mighty warriors, son of Ezbai.
Naarah.shortDescription: Naarai, the son of Ezbai, was one of the mighty warriors who fought alongside King David.

Naarai.name: Naarai
Naarai.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Naarai.briefDescription: Wealthy Carmelite; husband of Abigail. He was known for foolishness.
Naarai.shortDescription: Nabal, a wealthy man from Carmel, was known for his foolishness and mistreatment of David, which nearly led to his demise.

Nabal.name: Nabal
Nabal.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Nabal.briefDescription: Jezreelite vineyard owner, unjustly killed by Ahab and Jezebel.
Nabal.shortDescription: Naboth, a Jezreelite, was unjustly killed by King Ahab and Queen Jezebel for refusing to sell his ancestral vineyard.

Naboth.name: Naboth
Naboth.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Naboth.briefDescription: Threshing floor where Uzzah touched the ark.
Naboth.shortDescription: Nacon, also known as Chidon, was the owner of the threshing floor where Uzzah touched the ark of God and was struck down.

Nacon.name: Nacon
Nacon.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Nacon.briefDescription: Son of Shammai, from the tribe of Judah.
Nacon.shortDescription: Nadab was a son of Shammai, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Judah.

Nadab.name: Nadab
Nadab.description: Priest living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Nadab.shortDescription: Naggai is mentioned in the genealogy of Jesus Christ as recorded in the Gospel of Luke.

Naggai.name: Naggai
Naggai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Naggai.briefDescription: Brother of Hodiah who married Ishbar.
Naggai.shortDescription: Naham, the brother of Hodiah whose son founded Keilah the Garmite city.

Naham.name: Naham
Naham.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Naham.briefDescription: One who returned from Babylonian exile with Zerubbabel.
Naham.shortDescription: Nahamani was among those who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian captivity with Zerubbabel.

Nahamani.name: Nahamani
Nahamani.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Nahamani.briefDescription: One of David's mighty men, Joab's armor-bearer.
Nahamani.shortDescription: Naharai the Beerothite, Joab's armor-bearer, was one of David's mighty men.

Naharai.name: Naharai
Naharai.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Naharai.briefDescription: Ammonite king who besieged Jabesh Gilead.
Naharai.shortDescription: Nahash, the king of the Ammonites, besieged Jabesh Gilead and threatened to gouge out the right eyes of its inhabitants.

Nahash.name: Nahash
Nahash.description: King living at the time of United Monarchy
Nahash.briefDescription: A Levite during the reign of King Hezekiah.
Nahash.shortDescription: Nahath was one of the Levites appointed by King Hezekiah to oversee the distribution of offerings and tithes.

Nahath.name: Nahath
Nahath.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Nahath.briefDescription: The spy from the tribe of Naphtali.
Nahath.shortDescription: Nahbi, the son of Vophsi, was the representative from the tribe of Naphtali among the twelve spies sent to explore Canaan.

Nahbi.name: Nahbi
Nahbi.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Nahbi.briefDescription: Son of Serug; father of Terah, Abraham's father.
Nahbi.shortDescription: Nahor, the son of Serug and father of Terah, was Abraham's grandfather.

Nahor.name: Nahor
Nahor.description: Abraham's brother living at the time of the Patriarchs
Nahor.briefDescription: Son of Amminadab, leader of Judah, ancestor of David.
Nahor.shortDescription: Nahshon, the son of Amminadab, was a leader of the tribe of Judah during the Exodus and an ancestor of King David and Jesus Christ.

Nahshon.name: Nahshon
Nahshon.shortDescription: Nahum is mentioned in the genealogy of Jesus Christ as recorded in the Gospel of Luke.

Nahum.name: Nahum
Nahum.description: Prophet living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Nahum.shortDescription: Naomi, also known as Mara, was an Israelite woman who, after losing her husband and sons, returned from Moab to Bethlehem with her daughter-in-law Ruth.

Naomi.name: Naomi
Naomi.briefDescription: Son of Ishmael, defeated by Reubenites, Gadites, and Manassites.
Naomi.shortDescription: Naphish, a son of Ishmael, was among the Hagrite tribes defeated by the Reubenites, Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh.

Naphish.name: Naphish
Naphish.description: Arabian living at the time of the Patriarchs
Naphish.briefDescription: Son of Jacob, founder of the tribe of Naphtali.
Naphish.shortDescription: Naphtali, the sixth son of Jacob and second son of Bilhah, was the founder of the Israelite tribe that bore his name.

Naphtali.name: Naphtali
Naphtali.description: Jacob's son living at the time of the Patriarchs
Naphtali.briefDescription: Descendants of Mizraim, son of Ham
Naphtali.shortDescription: The Naphtuhim were descendants of Mizraim, son of Ham, who settled in northern Egypt.

Naphtuhim.name: Naphtuhim
Naphtuhim.briefDescription: Christian in Rome with a household
Naphtuhim.shortDescription: Narcissus was likely a wealthy Christian in Rome with a household of believers.

Narcissus.name: Narcissus
Narcissus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Narcissus.briefDescription: Son of Attai, from the tribe of Judah.
Narcissus.shortDescription: Nathan, the son of Attai, is mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Judah.

Nathan.name: Nathan
Nathan.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Nathan.briefDescription: Disciple of Jesus, from Cana in Galilee.
Nathan.shortDescription: Nathanael, also known as Bartholomew, was one of Jesus' twelve disciples, who was initially skeptical but later confessed Jesus as the Son of God.

Nathanael.name: Nathanael
Nathanael.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Nathanael.briefDescription: A court official during King Josiah's reign.
Nathanael.shortDescription: Nathan-melech was a court official who had living quarters near the entrance of the temple during King Josiah's reign.

Nathan-melech.name: Nathan-melech
Nathan-melech.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Nathan-melech.briefDescription: Descendant of David, son of Shemaiah.
Nathan-melech.shortDescription: Neariah, a descendant of King David, was the son of Shemaiah and had three sons: Elioenai, Hizkiah, and Azrikam.

Neariah.name: Neariah
Neariah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Neariah.briefDescription: A leader who sealed the covenant with Nehemiah.
Neariah.shortDescription: Nebai was one of the leaders who sealed the covenant with Nehemiah to follow God's law.

Nebai.name: Nebai
Nebai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Nebai.briefDescription: Firstborn son of Ishmael; father of a tribe.
Nebai.shortDescription: Nebaioth, the firstborn son of Ishmael, was the ancestor of a tribe known for their flocks and mentioned in Isaiah's prophecy of future blessing.

Nebaioth.name: Nebaioth
Nebaioth.description: Arabian living at the time of the Patriarchs
Nebaioth.briefDescription: Father of Jeroboam, the first king of Israel.
Nebaioth.shortDescription: Nebat was the father of Jeroboam I, who became the first king of the northern kingdom of Israel after the division of the monarchy.

Nebat.name: Nebat
Nebat.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Nebat.briefDescription: Emperor of Babylon who conquered Judah
Nebat.shortDescription: Nebuchadnezzar II was the powerful Emperor of Babylon who destroyed Jerusalem and exiled the Jews.

Nebuchadnezzar.name: Nebuchadnezzar
Nebuchadnezzar.description: Emperor living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Nebuchadnezzar.shortDescription: Nebu-sar-sekim was a high official in Nebuchadnezzar's army when Jerusalem fell.

Nebu-sar-sekim.name: Nebu-sar-sekim
Nebu-sar-sekim.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Nebu-sar-sekim.briefDescription: Babylonian official during the fall of Jerusalem.
Nebu-sar-sekim.shortDescription: Nebushazban, a Babylonian official, was among those who took charge of Jeremiah after the fall of Jerusalem.

Nebushazban.name: Nebushazban
Nebushazban.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Nebushazban.briefDescription: Babylonian captain of the guard during Jerusalem's fall.
Nebushazban.shortDescription: Nebuzaradan, the captain of the Babylonian guard, oversaw the destruction of Jerusalem, the exile of its inhabitants, and the release of Jeremiah.

Nebuzaradan.name: Nebuzaradan
Nebuzaradan.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Nebuzaradan.briefDescription: Egyptian Pharaoh during the reigns of Josiah and Jehoiakim.
Nebuzaradan.shortDescription: Pharaoh Neco II, the ruler of Egypt, opposed King Josiah of Judah, installed Jehoiakim as a vassal king, and was later defeated by Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon.

Neco.name: Neco
Neco.description: Pharaoh living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Neco.briefDescription: Descendant of King Jeconiah of Judah
Neco.shortDescription: Nedabiah was a descendant of Jeconiah, the exiled king of Judah.

Nedabiah.name: Nedabiah
Nedabiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Nedabiah.briefDescription: Hometown of the false prophet Shemaiah
Nedabiah.shortDescription: Nehelam was the hometown of Shemaiah, a false prophet who opposed Jeremiah.

Nehelamite.name: Nehelamite
Nehelamite.briefDescription: A leader who returned from Babylon to Jerusalem.
Nehelamite.shortDescription: Nehemiah was among the leaders who returned with Zerubbabel from Babylonian exile to Jerusalem.

Nehemiah.name: Nehemiah
Nehemiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Nehemiah.briefDescription: Mother of King Jehoiachin of Judah.
Nehemiah.shortDescription: Nehushta, the daughter of Elnathan of Jerusalem, was the mother of King Jehoiachin of Judah.

Nehushta.name: Nehushta
Nehushta.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Nehushta.briefDescription: A family of temple servants who returned from exile.
Nehushta.shortDescription: The descendants of Nekoda were among the temple servants who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian captivity.

Nekoda.name: Nekoda
Nekoda.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Nekoda.briefDescription: Son of Eliab, from the tribe of Reuben.
Nekoda.shortDescription: Nemuel was a son of Eliab and a member of the tribe of Reuben.

Nemuel.name: Nemuel
Nemuel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Nemuel.briefDescription: Son of King David, born in Jerusalem.
Nemuel.shortDescription: Nepheg was one of the sons of King David, born to him in Jerusalem.

Nepheg.name: Nepheg
Nepheg.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Nepheg.briefDescription: Mysterious giants mentioned in the pre-flood and post-flood eras.
Nepheg.shortDescription: The Nephilim were a race of giants mentioned in the context of the sons of God marrying human women before the flood and later encountered by the Israelite spies in Canaan.

Nephilim.name: Nephilim
Nephilim.briefDescription: Family of temple servants who returned from exile.
Nephilim.shortDescription: The Nephushesim (or Nephisim) were a family of temple servants who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian captivity.

Nephushesim.name: Nephushesim
Nephushesim.briefDescription: Father of Abner, King Saul's commander.
Nephushesim.shortDescription: Ner was the father of Abner, the commander of King Saul's army, and a member of Saul's family.

Ner.name: Ner
Ner.description: Saul's uncle living at the time of United Monarchy
Ner.briefDescription: A Christian in Rome greeted by Paul.
Ner.shortDescription: Nereus was a Christian in Rome to whom Paul sent greetings in his letter to the Romans.

Nereus.name: Nereus
Nereus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Nereus.briefDescription: Future Babylonian king at Jerusalem's fall
Nereus.shortDescription: Nergal-sar-ezer was a Babylonian official present at the capture of Jerusalem in 587 BC and probably became king Neriglissar.

Nergal-sar-ezer.name: Nergal-sar-ezer
Nergal-sar-ezer.description: Babylonian official of Samgar living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Nergal-sar-ezer.shortDescription: Neri was an ancestor of Jesus, mentioned in Luke's genealogy.

Neri.name: Neri
Neri.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Neri.shortDescription: Neriah was the father of Baruch, Jeremiah's scribe.

Neriah.name: Neriah
Neriah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Neriah.shortDescription: Nero was likely the Roman emperor during Paul's final imprisonment.

Nero.name: Nero
Nero.description: Emperor living at the time of the New Testament
Nero.shortDescription: Nethanel was a priest who blew trumpets before the ark of God.

Nethanel.name: Nethanel
Nethanel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Nethanel.briefDescription: Musician in David's time, descended from Asaph
Nethanel.shortDescription: Nethaniah was a musician in David's time, brother of Asharelah (or 'Asarelah').

Nethaniah.name: Nethaniah
Nethaniah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Nethaniah.shortDescription: Neziah was the ancestor of a family of temple servants who returned from the Babylonian exile.

Neziah.name: Neziah
Neziah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Neziah.shortDescription: Nicanor was one of the seven men chosen to assist the apostles in the early Jerusalem church.

Nicanor.name: Nicanor
Nicanor.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Nicanor.shortDescription: Nicodemus was a Pharisee and a member of the Jewish ruling council who had several encounters with Jesus.

Nicodemus.name: Nicodemus
Nicodemus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Nicodemus.shortDescription: Nicolaus was a proselyte from Antioch chosen as one of the seven deacons in the early church.

Nicolaus.name: Nicolaus
Nicolaus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Nicolaus.shortDescription: Nimrod was a mighty hunter and ruler who built several notable cities, including Babel and Nineveh.

Nimrod.name: Nimrod
Nimrod.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Nimrod.shortDescription: Nimshi was the grandfather of Jehu, the king of Israel who overthrew the dynasty of Ahab.

Nimshi.name: Nimshi
Nimshi.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Nimshi.shortDescription: Noadiah was a Levite entrusted with the oversight of the treasuries during the time of Ezra.

Noadiah.name: Noadiah
Noadiah.description: Woman living at the time of Exile and Return
Noadiah.briefDescription: Righteous man, built ark, survived flood
Noadiah.shortDescription: Noah was a righteous man who built the ark and survived the great flood with his family.

Noah.name: Noah
Noah.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Noah.shortDescription: Nobah was an Israelite who captured the town of Kenath and renamed it after himself.

Nobah.name: Nobah
Nobah.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Nobah.shortDescription: Nogah was one of King David's sons born in Jerusalem.

Nogah.name: Nogah
Nogah.description: David's son living at the time of United Monarchy
Nogah.shortDescription: Nun was the father of Joshua, Moses' assistant and successor as leader of Israel.

Nun.name: Nun
Nun.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Nun.briefDescription: Hosted a church in her house
Nun.shortDescription: Nympha was a woman who hosted a church in her house in Laodicea.

Nympha.name: Nympha
Nympha.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Nympha.shortDescription: Obadiah was a Gadite warrior who joined David at Ziklag when he was fleeing from Saul.

Obadiah.name: Obadiah
Obadiah.description: Prophet living at the time of Exile and Return
Obadiah.shortDescription: Obal was one of the sons of Joktan, a descendant of Shem, mentioned in the Table of Nations.

Obal.name: Obal
Obal.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Obal.shortDescription: Obed was an Ishmaelite warrior who fought in David's army.

Obed.name: Obed
Obed.shortDescription: Obed-edom was a Gittite in whose house the Ark of the Covenant stayed for three months, bringing blessings to his household.

Obed-edom.name: Obed-edom
Obed-edom.description: Singer living at the time of United Monarchy
Obed-edom.shortDescription: Obil the Ishmaelite was in charge of King David's camels.

Obil.name: Obil
Obil.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Obil.shortDescription: Ochran was the father of Pagiel, a leader from the tribe of Asher during the Exodus.

Ochran.name: Ochran
Ochran.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ochran.shortDescription: Oded was a prophet and the father of Azariah, who prophesied during the reign of King Asa of Judah.

Oded.name: Oded
Oded.description: Prophet living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Oded.briefDescription: King of Bashan, defeated by Israel
Oded.shortDescription: Og was the Amorite king of Bashan who was defeated by the Israelites during their journey to the Promised Land.

Og.name: Og
Og.description: King living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Og.shortDescription: Ohad was one of the sons of Simeon, as listed in the genealogies.

Ohad.name: Ohad
Ohad.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ohad.shortDescription: Ohel was a descendant of Judah through the line of Zerubbabel.

Ohel.name: Ohel
Ohel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
