Ohel.shortDescription: Oholah was a symbolic name given to Samaria, representing the northern kingdom of Israel in Ezekiel's prophecy.

Oholah.name: Oholah
Oholah.description: Woman living at the time of Exile and Return
Oholah.shortDescription: Oholiab was a skilled craftsman from the tribe of Dan who was appointed to work on the construction of the tabernacle.

Oholiab.name: Oholiab
Oholiab.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Oholiab.shortDescription: Oholibah was a symbolic name given to Jerusalem, representing the southern kingdom of Judah in Ezekiel's prophecy.

Oholibah.name: Oholibah
Oholibah.description: Woman living at the time of Exile and Return
Oholibah.shortDescription: Oholibamah was one of Esau's wives, a descendant of Seir the Horite.

Oholibamah.name: Oholibamah
Oholibamah.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Oholibamah.shortDescription: Olympas was a believer in Rome to whom Paul sent greetings.

Olympas.name: Olympas
Olympas.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Olympas.briefDescription: Son of Eliphaz, grandson of Esau
Olympas.shortDescription: Omar was a son of Eliphaz and a grandson of Esau, mentioned in the genealogies of Edom.

Omar.name: Omar
Omar.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Omar.briefDescription: Chief of the tribe of Issachar
Omar.shortDescription: Omri was the chief of the tribe of Issachar during the reign of King David.

Omri.name: Omri
Omri.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Omri.shortDescription: On was a Reubenite who joined Korah in his rebellion against Moses and Aaron.

On.name: On
On.briefDescription: An ancient Egyptian city, mentioned in relation to Joseph
On.shortDescription: On, also known as Heliopolis, was an ancient Egyptian city mentioned in the Bible in connection with Joseph's life and prophetic references to Egypt.

Onam.name: Onam
Onam.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Onam.shortDescription: Onan was the second son of Judah, who was punished by God for his wicked actions.

Onan.name: Onan
Onan.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Onan.shortDescription: Onesimus was a runaway slave who became a Christian through Paul's ministry and was the subject of Paul's letter to Philemon.

Onesimus.name: Onesimus
Onesimus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Onesimus.shortDescription: Onesiphorus was a friend of Paul who often refreshed him and was not ashamed of his imprisonment.

Onesiphorus.name: Onesiphorus
Onesiphorus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Onesiphorus.shortDescription: Ophir was a son of Joktan, a descendant of Shem, mentioned in the Table of Nations.

Ophir.name: Ophir
Ophir.briefDescription: A region known for its gold
Ophir.shortDescription: Ophir was a region famous for its high-quality gold, which was imported to Israel during the reigns of David and Solomon.

Ophrah.name: Ophrah
Ophrah.briefDescription: Gideon's hometown in Manasseh; a town in Issachar
Ophrah.shortDescription: Ophrah, also known as Hapharaim, was Gideon's hometown in Manasseh and the site of important events during his time as a judge, as well as a town allotted to the tribe of Issachar.

Oreb.name: Oreb
Oreb.shortDescription: Oren was a son of Jerahmeel, a descendant of Judah.

Oren.name: Oren
Oren.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Oren.briefDescription: Jebusite, sold threshing floor to David
Oren.shortDescription: Ornan (Araunah) was a Jebusite who sold his threshing floor to King David, which later became the site of Solomon's temple.

Ornan.name: Ornan
Ornan.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ornan.shortDescription: Orpah was Naomi's daughter-in-law who stayed in Moab after the death of her husband, while Ruth accompanied Naomi to Bethlehem.

Orpah.name: Orpah
Orpah.briefDescription: Assyrian king who settled people in Samaria
Orpah.shortDescription: Osnappar (probably Ashurbanipal) was an Assyrian king who brought people from various regions and settled them in Samaria.

Osnappar.name: Osnappar
Osnappar.description: King living at the time of Exile and Return
Osnappar.briefDescription: Son of Shemaiah, gatekeeper of the ark
Osnappar.shortDescription: Othni was a son of Shemaiah and served as a gatekeeper for the ark of the covenant.

Othni.name: Othni
Othni.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Othni.briefDescription: First judge of Israel, Caleb's nephew
Othni.shortDescription: Othniel, Caleb's nephew, was the first judge of Israel who delivered the people from Cushan-Rishathaim.

Othniel.name: Othniel
Othniel.shortDescription: Ozem was one of David's brothers, a son of Jesse.

Ozem.name: Ozem
Ozem.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ozem.shortDescription: Paarai the Arbite was one of David's mighty men, known for his military prowess.

Paarai.name: Paarai
Paarai.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Paarai.shortDescription: Padon was an ancestor of a group of temple servants who returned from the Babylonian exile.

Padon.name: Padon
Padon.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Padon.briefDescription: Leader of the tribe of Asher
Padon.shortDescription: Pagiel, son of Ochran, was the leader of the tribe of Asher during the wilderness wanderings.

Pagiel.name: Pagiel
Pagiel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Pagiel.briefDescription: Group of returnees from Babylonian exile
Pagiel.shortDescription: Pahath-moab was a group of people who returned to Jerusalem from the Babylonian exile, some of whom married foreign women.

Pahath-moab.name: Pahath-moab
Pahath-moab.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Pahath-moab.briefDescription: Son of Uzai, repaired Jerusalem's wall
Pahath-moab.shortDescription: Palal, son of Uzai, repaired a section of Jerusalem's wall near the Parbar during Nehemiah's time.

Palal.name: Palal
Palal.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Palal.briefDescription: Son of Reuben, ancestor of the Palluites
Palal.shortDescription: Pallu was a son of Reuben and the ancestor of the Palluite clan within the tribe of Reuben.

Pallu.name: Pallu
Pallu.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Pallu.shortDescription: Palti (Paltiel), son of Laish, was Michal's husband from Gallim before she was taken back by David.

Palti.name: Palti
Palti.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Palti.briefDescription: Leader from Issachar, helped divide Canaan
Palti.shortDescription: Paltiel, son of Azzan, was the leader from the tribe of Issachar who helped divide the land of Canaan.

Paltiel.name: Paltiel
Paltiel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Paltiel.briefDescription: Son of Haman, killed by the Jews
Paltiel.shortDescription: Parmashta was one of Haman's ten sons killed by the Jews in Susa during the events of Purim.

Parmashta.name: Parmashta
Parmashta.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Parmashta.shortDescription: Parmenas was one of the seven men chosen by the apostles to serve as deacons in the early church.

Parmenas.name: Parmenas
Parmenas.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Parmenas.briefDescription: Father of Elizaphan, leader from Zebulun
Parmenas.shortDescription: Parnach was the father of Elizaphan, the leader from the tribe of Zebulun who helped divide the land of Canaan.

Parnach.name: Parnach
Parnach.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Parnach.briefDescription: Family that returned from Babylonian exile
Parnach.shortDescription: Parosh was the head of a family that returned to Jerusalem from the Babylonian exile, some of whom married foreign women.

Parosh.name: Parosh
Parosh.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Parosh.briefDescription: Son of Haman killed by the Jews
Parosh.shortDescription: Parshandatha, one of the ten sons of Haman killed by the Jews.

Parshandatha.name: Parshandatha
Parshandatha.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Parshandatha.shortDescription: Parthians, one of the people groups present in Jerusalem at Pentecost.

Parthians.name: Parthians
Parthians.briefDescription: Father of one of Solomon's district governors
Parthians.shortDescription: Paruah, the father of Jehoshaphat, who was one of Solomon's twelve district governors.

Paruah.name: Paruah
Paruah.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Paruah.briefDescription: Son of Japhlet from the tribe of Asher
Paruah.shortDescription: Pasach, a son of Japhlet, who was a descendant of Asher.

Pasach.name: Pasach
Pasach.briefDescription: Son of Eshton from the tribe of Judah
Pasach.shortDescription: Paseah, a son of Eshton, who was from the tribe of Judah.

Paseah.name: Paseah
Paseah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Paseah.briefDescription: Head of a priestly family that returned from exile
Paseah.shortDescription: Pashhur, the ancestor of a priestly family that returned from Babylonian exile.

Pashhur.name: Pashhur
Pashhur.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Pashhur.briefDescription: Descendants of Mizraim, son of Ham
Pashhur.shortDescription: Pathrusim, a People from Mizraim, son of Ham, according to Genesis 10:14.

Pathrusim.name: Pathrusim
Pathrusim.briefDescription: Believer in Rome greeted by Paul
Pathrusim.shortDescription: Patrobas, a believer in Rome to whom Paul sent greetings.

Patrobas.name: Patrobas
Patrobas.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Patrobas.briefDescription: Apostle to the Gentiles, author of many NT epistles
Patrobas.shortDescription: Paul, also known as Saul, the apostle to the Gentiles and author of many New Testament epistles.

Paul.name: Paul
Paul.description: Apostle living at the time of the New Testament
Paul.briefDescription: Leader from Naphtali appointed to divide Canaan
Paul.shortDescription: Pedahel, son of Ammihud, the leader from Naphtali appointed to help divide Canaan.

Pedahel.name: Pedahel
Pedahel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Pedahel.briefDescription: Father of Gamaliel, leader from Manasseh
Pedahel.shortDescription: Pedahzur, father of Gamaliel, the leader from the tribe of Manasseh during the wilderness wanderings.

Pedahzur.name: Pedahzur
Pedahzur.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Pedahzur.briefDescription: Officer over the half-tribe of Manasseh
Pedahzur.shortDescription: Pedaiah, son of Parosh, an officer over the half-tribe of Manasseh in King David's administration.

Pedaiah.name: Pedaiah
Pedaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Pedaiah.briefDescription: King of Israel who reigned for 20 years
Pedaiah.shortDescription: Pekah, son of Remaliah, king of Israel who reigned for 20 years.

Pekah.name: Pekah
Pekah.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Pekah.briefDescription: King of Israel who reigned for 2 years
Pekah.shortDescription: Pekahiah, son of Menahem, king of Israel who reigned for 2 years before being assassinated.

Pekahiah.name: Pekahiah
Pekahiah.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Pekahiah.shortDescription: Pelaiah, a descendant of King David, mentioned in the genealogy of 1 Chronicles 3.

Pelaiah.name: Pelaiah
Pelaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Pelaiah.briefDescription: Priest in Jerusalem during Nehemiah's time
Pelaiah.shortDescription: Pelaliah, a priest who lived in Jerusalem during Nehemiah's time.

Pelaliah.name: Pelaliah
Pelaliah.description: Levite living at the time of Exile and Return
Pelaliah.shortDescription: Pelatiah, a descendant of King Solomon mentioned in the genealogy of 1 Chronicles 3.

Pelatiah.name: Pelatiah
Pelatiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Pelatiah.briefDescription: Descendant of Shem, ancestor of Abraham
Pelatiah.shortDescription: Peleg, a descendant of Shem and ancestor of Abraham, during whose time the earth was divided.

Peleg.name: Peleg
Peleg.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Peleg.briefDescription: Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag
Peleg.shortDescription: Pelet, a Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag, along with his brother Berachah.

Pelet.name: Pelet
Pelet.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Pelet.briefDescription: Son of Jonathan, descendant of Jerahmeel
Pelet.shortDescription: Peleth, a son of Jonathan and descendant of Jerahmeel, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Peleth.name: Peleth
Peleth.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Peleth.briefDescription: Elite military unit loyal to King David
Peleth.shortDescription: The Pelethites, along with the Cherethites, were an elite military unit loyal to King David.

Pelethites.name: Pelethites
Pelethites.briefDescription: Designation for two of David's mighty warriors
Pelethites.shortDescription: Pelonite or Paltite, a designation for two of David's mighty warriors, Helez and Ahijah.

Pelonites.name: Pelonites
Pelonites.description: Warriors from or of Pelon or Palet(?)
Pelonites.shortDescription: Peninnah, one of Elkanah's two wives and the rival of Hannah, Samuel's mother.

Peninnah.name: Peninnah
Peninnah.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
Peninnah.briefDescription: Son of Hur, descendant of Judah
Peninnah.shortDescription: Penuel, a son of Hur and descendant of Judah, mentioned in the genealogy of 1 Chronicles 4.

Penuel.name: Penuel
Penuel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Penuel.briefDescription: Son of Machir, grandson of Manasseh
Penuel.shortDescription: Peresh, a son of Machir and grandson of Manasseh, mentioned in the genealogy of 1 Chronicles 7.

Peresh.name: Peresh
Peresh.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Peresh.briefDescription: Son of Judah, ancestor of David and Jesus
Peresh.shortDescription: Perez, son of Judah and Tamar, was an ancestor of King David and Jesus Christ.

Perez.name: Perez
Perez.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Perez.briefDescription: Canaanite people group living in the land of Canaan
Perez.shortDescription: The Perizzites, a Canaanite people group that inhabited the land of Canaan before the Israelite conquest.

Perizzites.name: Perizzites
Perizzites.briefDescription: Female Christian in Rome greeted by Paul
Perizzites.shortDescription: Persis, a beloved female Christian in Rome whom Paul greeted in his letter to the Romans.

Persis.name: Persis
Persis.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Persis.briefDescription: Ancestor of a family of Solomon's servants
Persis.shortDescription: Peruda or Perida, the ancestor of a family of Solomon's servants who returned from the Babylonian exile.

Peruda.name: Peruda
Peruda.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Peruda.briefDescription: Ancestor of a family of Solomon's servants
Peruda.shortDescription: Peruda or Perida, the ancestor of a family of Solomon's servants who returned from the Babylonian exile.

Peter.name: Peter
Peter.description: Apostle living at the time of the New Testament
Peter.shortDescription: Peter, also known as Simon, Cephas, or Simon Bar-Jonah, was one of the twelve apostles of Jesus and a prominent leader in the early Christian church.

Pethahiah.name: Pethahiah
Pethahiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Pethahiah.shortDescription: Pethuel, the father of the prophet Joel, as mentioned in Jol.1.1.

Pethuel.name: Pethuel
Pethuel.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Pethuel.briefDescription: Son of Obed-Edom, a temple gatekeeper
Pethuel.shortDescription: Peullethai, the eighth son of Obed-Edom, a gatekeeper in the temple during the time of David.

Peullethai.name: Peullethai
Peullethai.description: Levite living at the time of United Monarchy
Peullethai.shortDescription: Phanuel, the father of Anna, a prophetess who witnessed the infant Jesus at the temple.

Phanuel.name: Phanuel
Phanuel.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Phanuel.briefDescription: Pharaoh and father-in-law of Mered, a descendant of Judah
Phanuel.shortDescription: Pharaoh, the Egyptian ruler whose daughter Bithiah married Mered, a descendant of Judah.

Pharaoh.name: Pharaoh
Pharaoh.description: Pharaoh living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Pharaoh.shortDescription: Phicol, the commander of the army of Abimelech, the Philistine king of Gerar, who accompanied Abimelech in making treaties with Abraham and Isaac.

Phicol.name: Phicol
Phicol.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Phicol.briefDescription: Recipient of Paul's letter, Colossian slave owner
Phicol.shortDescription: Philemon, a Christian from Colossae to whom Paul wrote a letter appealing for the reconciliation with his runaway slave, Onesimus.

Philemon.name: Philemon
Philemon.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Philemon.shortDescription: Philetus, along with Hymenaeus, was a false teacher who had strayed from the truth and taught that the resurrection had already taken place.

Philetus.name: Philetus
Philetus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Philetus.briefDescription: One of the seven deacons, evangelized in Samaria
Philetus.shortDescription: Philip, one of the seven deacons chosen by the early church in Jerusalem, who preached the gospel in Samaria and baptized an Ethiopian eunuch.

Philip.name: Philip
Philip.description: Tetrarch living at the time of the New Testament
Philip.briefDescription: Descendants of Mizraim, son of Ham
Philip.shortDescription: The Philistines, a people group descended from Mizraim, the son of Ham, according to the Table of Nations in Genesis and 1 Chronicles.

Philistine.name: Philistine
Philistine.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Philistine.briefDescription: Believer in Rome greeted by Paul
Philistine.shortDescription: Philologus, a Christian in Rome to whom Paul sent greetings in his letter to the Romans.

Philologus.name: Philologus
Philologus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Philologus.briefDescription: Wicked son of Eli the priest
Philologus.shortDescription: Phinehas, the son of Eli the priest, was known for his wickedness and died in battle against the Philistines along with his brother Hophni.

Phinehas.name: Phinehas
Phinehas.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Phinehas.briefDescription: Believer in Rome greeted by Paul
Phinehas.shortDescription: Phlegon, a Christian in Rome to whom Paul sent greetings in his letter to the Romans.

Phlegon.name: Phlegon
Phlegon.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Phlegon.briefDescription: Deacon of the church in Cenchreae
Phlegon.shortDescription: Phoebe, a deacon of the church in Cenchreae, whom Paul commended to the believers in Rome.

Phoebe.name: Phoebe
Phoebe.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Phoebe.briefDescription: Believer who deserted Paul in Asia
Phoebe.shortDescription: Phygelus, along with Hermogenes, was mentioned by Paul as having deserted him during his ministry in Asia.

Phygelus.name: Phygelus
Phygelus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Phygelus.briefDescription: Roman governor who presided over Jesus' trial
Phygelus.shortDescription: Pontius Pilate, the Roman governor of Judea who presided over the trial of Jesus and ultimately ordered his crucifixion.

Pilate.name: Pilate
Pilate.description: Ruler living at the time of the New Testament
Pilate.shortDescription: Pildash, one of the sons of Nahor, Abraham's brother, and Milcah.

Pildash.name: Pildash
Pildash.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Pildash.shortDescription: Pilha, one of the leaders of the people who signed the covenant during Nehemiah's time.

Pilha.name: Pilha
Pilha.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Pilha.shortDescription: Piltai, the head of the priestly family of Moadiah during the time of Joiakim, the high priest.

Piltai.name: Piltai
Piltai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Piltai.briefDescription: Edomite clan chief descended from Esau
Piltai.shortDescription: Pinon, one of the chiefs of Edom, descended from Esau, mentioned in the genealogies of Genesis and 1 Chronicles.

Pinon.name: Pinon
Pinon.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Pinon.shortDescription: Piram, the Amorite king of Jarmuth, who joined a coalition against Joshua and the Israelites.

Piram.name: Piram
Piram.shortDescription: Pispa, a descendant of Asher, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Asher in 1 Chronicles.

Pispa.name: Pispa
Pispa.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Pispa.shortDescription: Pithon, a descendant of King Saul, mentioned in the genealogies of the tribe of Benjamin in 1 Chronicles.

Pithon.name: Pithon
Pithon.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Pithon.shortDescription: Pochereth-hazzebaim, a family listed among the descendants of Solomon's servants who returned from the Babylonian exile.

Pochereth-hazzebaim.name: Pochereth-hazzebaim
Pochereth-hazzebaim.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Pochereth-hazzebaim.briefDescription: Son of Haman, enemy of the Jews
Pochereth-hazzebaim.shortDescription: Poratha, one of the ten sons of Haman, who was hanged along with his brothers after their father's downfall.

Poratha.name: Poratha
Poratha.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Poratha.briefDescription: Egyptian official who bought Joseph as a slave
Poratha.shortDescription: Potiphar, the Egyptian officer who purchased Joseph as a slave and later imprisoned him after false accusations by Potiphar's wife.

Potiphar.name: Potiphar
Potiphar.description: Egyptian living at the time of the Patriarchs
Potiphar.shortDescription: Potiphera, the priest of On (Heliopolis), whose daughter Asenath was given to Joseph as a wife by Pharaoh.

Potiphera.name: Potiphera
Potiphera.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Potiphera.briefDescription: Early Christian teacher; wife of Aquila
Potiphera.shortDescription: Priscilla, also known as Prisca, was a Jewish Christian and the wife of Aquila, who worked with Paul in his ministry and hosted a church in their home.

Priscilla.name: Priscilla
Priscilla.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Priscilla.briefDescription: One of the seven deacons in Jerusalem
Priscilla.shortDescription: Prochorus, one of the seven men chosen by the early church in Jerusalem to oversee the daily distribution of food to widows.

Prochorus.name: Prochorus
Prochorus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Prochorus.shortDescription: Puah, one of the two Hebrew midwives, along with Shiphrah, who defied Pharaoh's order to kill male Hebrew babies.

Puah.name: Puah
Puah.briefDescription: Chief official of Malta who hosted Paul
Puah.shortDescription: Publius, the chief official of Malta, who hosted Paul and his companions for three days after their shipwreck on the island.

Publius.name: Publius
Publius.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Publius.briefDescription: Christian in Rome who greeted Timothy
Publius.shortDescription: Pudens, a Christian in Rome who sent greetings to Timothy through Paul's letter.

Pudens.name: Pudens
Pudens.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Pudens.briefDescription: Gideon's servant who accompanied him to the Midianite camp
Pudens.shortDescription: Purah was Gideon's servant who went with him to spy on the Midianite camp.

Purah.name: Purah
Purah.briefDescription: Son of Ham, grandson of Noah
Purah.shortDescription: Put was a son of Ham and grandson of Noah.

Put.name: Put
Put.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Put.briefDescription: A clan from the tribe of Judah
Put.shortDescription: The Puthites were a clan from the tribe of Judah.

Puthites.name: Puthites
Puthites.briefDescription: Father-in-law of Eleazar, son of Aaron
Puthites.shortDescription: Putiel was the father-in-law of Eleazar, son of Aaron.

Putiel.name: Putiel
Putiel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Putiel.briefDescription: A Berean who accompanied Paul to Asia
Putiel.shortDescription: Pyrrhus was a Berean who accompanied Paul on his journey to Asia.

Pyrrhus.name: Pyrrhus
Pyrrhus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Pyrrhus.briefDescription: A Christian brother in Corinth who sent greetings to Rome
Pyrrhus.shortDescription: Quartus, a Christian brother in Corinth, sent greetings to the believers in Rome through Paul's letter.

Quartus.name: Quartus
Quartus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Quartus.briefDescription: A queen who visited Solomon and praised his wisdom
Quartus.shortDescription: The Queen of Sheba visited King Solomon, tested him with questions, and praised his wisdom and prosperity.

Queen_of_Sheba.name: Queen_of_Sheba
Queen_of_Sheba.description: Queen living at the time of United Monarchy
Queen_of_Sheba.briefDescription: Governor of Syria during a census mentioned in Luke
Queen_of_Sheba.shortDescription: Quirinius was the governor of Syria when a census was taken, as mentioned in Luke's Gospel.

Quirinius.name: Quirinius
Quirinius.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Quirinius.briefDescription: Son of Cush, grandson of Ham, great-grandson of Noah
Quirinius.shortDescription: Raamah was a son of Cush, grandson of Ham, and great-grandson of Noah, mentioned in the Table of Nations.

Raamah.name: Raamah
Raamah.briefDescription: A trading partner of Tyre, possibly in Arabia
Raamah.shortDescription: Raamah was a place, possibly located in Arabia, that traded with the city of Tyre, offering spices, precious stones, and gold.

Rab-mag.name: Rab-mag
Rab-mag.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Rab-mag.briefDescription: A Babylonian official sent by King Sennacherib to Jerusalem
Rab-mag.shortDescription: Rab-saris was one of the Babylonian officials sent by King Sennacherib to Jerusalem during his campaign against Judah.

Rab-saris.name: Rab-saris
Rab-saris.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Rab-saris.briefDescription: A Babylonian official who taunted Hezekiah and Jerusalem's people
Rab-saris.shortDescription: Rabshakeh was a Babylonian official who tried to intimidate Hezekiah and the people of Jerusalem into surrendering.

Rabshakeh.name: Rabshakeh
Rabshakeh.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Rabshakeh.briefDescription: Wife of Jacob; mother of Joseph and Benjamin
Rabshakeh.shortDescription: Rachel was the beloved wife of Jacob, and the mother of Joseph and Benjamin.

Rachel.name: Rachel
Rachel.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Rachel.briefDescription: One of David's brothers, son of Jesse
Rachel.shortDescription: Raddai was one of David's brothers, a son of Jesse.

Raddai.name: Raddai
Raddai.description: David's brother living at the time of United Monarchy
Raddai.briefDescription: A Canaanite woman who helped the Israelite spies in Jericho
Raddai.shortDescription: Rahab, a Canaanite woman from Jericho, hid the Israelite spies and was spared during the city's destruction.

Rahab.name: Rahab
Rahab.briefDescription: A poetic name for Egypt, representing a mythical sea monster
Rahab.shortDescription: Rahab is a poetic name for Egypt, often represented as a mythical sea monster that God defeated, symbolizing His power over the nations (Psalm 89:10; Isaiah 51:9).

Raham.name: Raham
Raham.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Raham.briefDescription: Son of Sheresh, of the tribe of Manasseh
Raham.shortDescription: Rakem was a son of Sheresh, from the tribe of Manasseh.

Rakem.name: Rakem
Rakem.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Rakem.briefDescription: Firstborn son of Jerahmeel, of the tribe of Judah
Rakem.shortDescription: Ram was the firstborn son of Jerahmeel, from the tribe of Judah.

Ram.name: Ram
Ram.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ram.briefDescription: People from Ramah who tended King David's vineyards
Ram.shortDescription: The Ramathites were people from Ramah who were in charge of King David's vineyards.

Ramathites.name: Ramathites
Ramathites.briefDescription: One of the men who married foreign women
Ramathites.shortDescription: Ramiah was one of the men who had married foreign women during the time of Ezra.

Ramiah.name: Ramiah
Ramiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Ramiah.briefDescription: Ancestor of Philistine giants; his descendants fought against David's warriors.
Ramiah.shortDescription: Rapha was the ancestor of several Philistine giants who fought against David and his mighty warriors.

Rapha.name: Rapha
Rapha.description: Giant living at the time of United Monarchy
Rapha.briefDescription: Son of Binea, a descendant of King Saul
Rapha.shortDescription: Raphah (or Rephaiah) was a son of Binea and a descendant of King Saul.

Raphah.name: Raphah
Raphah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Raphah.briefDescription: Father of Palti, a spy sent by Moses
Raphah.shortDescription: Raphu was the father of Palti, one of the twelve spies sent by Moses to explore Canaan.

Raphu.name: Raphu
Raphu.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Raphu.shortDescription: Reaiah was a Reubenite, the son of Micah, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Reuben.

Reaiah.name: Reaiah
Reaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Reaiah.briefDescription: A Midianite king killed by the Israelites
Reaiah.shortDescription: Reba was one of the five Midianite kings killed by the Israelites during the time of Moses.

Reba.name: Reba
Reba.description: King living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Reba.briefDescription: Wife of Isaac; mother of Jacob and Esau
Reba.shortDescription: Rebekah was the wife of Isaac and the mother of Jacob and Esau.

Rebekah.name: Rebekah
Rebekah.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Rebekah.briefDescription: Ancestor of the Rechabites, who were faithful to God
Rebekah.shortDescription: Rechab was the ancestor of the Rechabites, a nomadic tribe known for their faithfulness to God's commands.

Rechab.name: Rechab
Rechab.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Rechab.briefDescription: One of the leaders who returned with Zerubbabel
Rechab.shortDescription: Reelaiah (or Raamiah) was one of the leaders who returned to Jerusalem with Zerubbabel after the Babylonian exile.

Reelaiah.name: Reelaiah
Reelaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Reelaiah.briefDescription: Son of Jahdai, of the tribe of Judah
Reelaiah.shortDescription: Regem was a son of Jahdai, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Judah.

Regem.name: Regem
Regem.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Regem.briefDescription: A messenger sent to inquire of the Lord
Regem.shortDescription: Regem-melech was one of the messengers sent to the house of God to inquire of the Lord.

Regem-melech.name: Regem-melech
Regem-melech.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Regem-melech.briefDescription: Son of Eliezer, grandson of Moses, a Levite
Regem-melech.shortDescription: Rehabiah was the son of Eliezer and grandson of Moses, a Levite with many descendants.

Rehabiah.name: Rehabiah
Rehabiah.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Rehabiah.briefDescription: Father of Hadadezer, king of Zobah
Rehabiah.shortDescription: Rehob was the father of Hadadezer, the king of Zobah, who was defeated by King David.

Rehob.name: Rehob
Rehob.shortDescription: Rehob was a Levitical city within the territory of Asher (Jos. 19:30; 21:31; 1 Ch. 6:75).

Rehoboam.name: Rehoboam
Rehoboam.description: King living at the time of United Monarchyand Divided Monarchy
Rehoboam.briefDescription: One of the leaders who returned with Zerubbabel
Rehoboam.shortDescription: Rehum (or Harim or Nehum) was one of the leaders who returned to Jerusalem with Zerubbabel after the Babylonian exile.

Rehum.name: Rehum
Rehum.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Rehum.briefDescription: A supporter of Solomon during Adonijah's rebellion
Rehum.shortDescription: Rei was one of the people who remained loyal to King Solomon during Adonijah's attempt to seize the throne.

Rei.name: Rei
Rei.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Rei.briefDescription: Son of Hebron, of the tribe of Judah
Rei.shortDescription: Rekem was a son of Hebron, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Judah.

Rekem.name: Rekem
Rekem.shortDescription: Rekem was a city allotted to the tribe of Benjamin (Jos. 18:27).

Remaliah.name: Remaliah
Remaliah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Remaliah.briefDescription: A gatekeeper of the temple during David's reign
Remaliah.shortDescription: Rephael was one of the gatekeepers of the temple during the reign of King David.

Rephael.name: Rephael
Rephael.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Rephael.briefDescription: Son of Beriah, of the tribe of Asher
Rephael.shortDescription: Rephah was a son of Beriah, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Asher.

Rephah.name: Rephah
Rephah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Rephah.shortDescription: Rephaiah was a descendant of King David, mentioned in the genealogy of David's descendants.

Rephaiah.name: Rephaiah
Rephaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Rephaiah.briefDescription: A race of giants who inhabited Canaan
Rephaiah.shortDescription: The Rephaim, also known as Zamzummin, were a race of giants who inhabited parts of Canaan before the Israelite conquest.

Rephaim.name: Rephaim
Rephaim.briefDescription: Son of Beriah, of the tribe of Asher
Rephaim.shortDescription: Resheph was a son of Beriah, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Asher.

Resheph.name: Resheph
Resheph.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Resheph.briefDescription: Son of Peleg, ancestor of Abraham
Resheph.shortDescription: Reu was the son of Peleg and an ancestor of Abraham, living to the age of 239 years.

Reu.name: Reu
Reu.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Reu.briefDescription: Firstborn son of Jacob and Leah
Reu.shortDescription: Reuben was the firstborn son of Jacob and Leah, and his descendants formed one of the twelve tribes of Israel.

Reuben.name: Reuben
Reuben.description: Jacob's son living at the time of the Patriarchs
Reuben.shortDescription: Reuel was a Benjamite mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Benjamin.

Reuel.name: Reuel
Reuel.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Reuel.shortDescription: Reumah was the concubine of Nahor, Abraham's brother, and she bore him four sons.

Reumah.name: Reumah
Reumah.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Reumah.briefDescription: King of Aram (Syria) during the reign of Ahaz
Reumah.shortDescription: Rezin was the king of Aram (Syria) who allied with Pekah, king of Israel, against Ahaz, king of Judah.

Rezin.name: Rezin
Rezin.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Rezin.briefDescription: Son of Eliada, adversary of Solomon
Rezin.shortDescription: Rezon, son of Eliada, was an adversary of Solomon who reigned over Aram (Syria) in Damascus.

Rezon.name: Rezon
Rezon.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Rezon.briefDescription: Son of Zerubbabel, ancestor of Jesus
Rezon.shortDescription: Rhesa was the son of Zerubbabel and an ancestor of Jesus Christ, according to Luke's genealogy.

Rhesa.name: Rhesa
Rhesa.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Rhesa.briefDescription: Servant girl in the house of Mary
Rhesa.shortDescription: Rhoda was a servant girl in the house of Mary, the mother of John Mark, who recognized Peter's voice at the door.

Rhoda.name: Rhoda
Rhoda.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Rhoda.briefDescription: Father of Ittai, one of David's mighty men
Rhoda.shortDescription: Ribai was the father of Ittai the Gibeathite, one of David's mighty men.

Ribai.name: Ribai
Ribai.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Ribai.briefDescription: Father of Baanah and Rechab, murderers of Ish-bosheth
Ribai.shortDescription: Rimmon was a Beerothite and the father of Rechab and Baanah, who murdered Ish-bosheth, Saul's son.

Rimmon.name: Rimmon
Rimmon.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Rimmon.briefDescription: A Syrian deity worshipped by Naaman
Rimmon.shortDescription: Rimmon was a Syrian deity worshipped by Naaman, who asked for God's pardon for bowing down in Rimmon's temple (2Ki.5.18).

Rinnah.name: Rinnah
Rinnah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Rinnah.briefDescription: Son of Gomer, grandson of Japheth
Rinnah.shortDescription: Riphath was a son of Gomer and grandson of Japheth, mentioned in the Table of Nations.

Riphath.name: Riphath
Riphath.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Riphath.briefDescription: Son of Ulla, of the tribe of Asher
Riphath.shortDescription: Rizia was a son of Ulla, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Asher.

Rizia.name: Rizia
Rizia.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Rizia.briefDescription: Concubine of Saul; mother of Armoni and Mephibosheth
Rizia.shortDescription: Rizpah was a concubine of King Saul and the mother of Armoni and Mephibosheth, who were executed by the Gibeonites.

Rizpah.name: Rizpah
Rizpah.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
Rizpah.briefDescription: Son of Shomer, of the tribe of Asher
Rizpah.shortDescription: Rohgah was a son of Shomer (or 'Shamer'), mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Asher.

Rohgah.name: Rohgah
Rohgah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Rohgah.briefDescription: Son of Heman, a Levitical singer
Rohgah.shortDescription: Romamti-ezer was a son of Heman and a Levitical singer during the reign of King David.

Romamti-ezer.name: Romamti-ezer
Romamti-ezer.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Romamti-ezer.briefDescription: Son of Benjamin, grandson of Jacob
Romamti-ezer.shortDescription: Rosh (or Rapha) was a son of Benjamin and grandson of Jacob, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Benjamin.

Rosh.name: Rosh
Rosh.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Rosh.briefDescription: Son of Simon of Cyrene, who carried Jesus' cross
Rosh.shortDescription: Rufus was the son of Simon of Cyrene, who was compelled to carry Jesus' cross on the way to His crucifixion.

Rufus.name: Rufus
Rufus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Rufus.briefDescription: Moabite woman, ancestor of David and Jesus
Rufus.shortDescription: Ruth was a Moabite woman who married Boaz and became an ancestor of King David and Jesus Christ.

Ruth.name: Ruth
Ruth.briefDescription: Son of Cush, grandson of Ham
Ruth.shortDescription: Sabtah (or Sabta) was a son of Cush and a grandson of Ham, mentioned in the Table of Nations.

Sabtah.name: Sabtah
Sabtah.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Sabtah.briefDescription: Son of Cush, grandson of Ham
Sabtah.shortDescription: Sabteca was a son of Cush and a grandson of Ham, mentioned in the Table of Nations.

Sabteca.name: Sabteca
Sabteca.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Sabteca.briefDescription: Son of Obed-edom, a Levitical gatekeeper
Sabteca.shortDescription: Sachar was a son of Obed-edom and a Levitical gatekeeper during the reign of King David.

Sachar.name: Sachar
Sachar.description: Levite living at the time of United Monarchy
Sachar.shortDescription: Sachia was a son of Shaharaim, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Benjamin.

Sachia.name: Sachia
Sachia.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Sachia.briefDescription: Benjamite who settled in Jerusalem after the exile
Sachia.shortDescription: Sallai was a Benjamite who settled in Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile, as recorded in Nehemiah 11.

Sallai.name: Sallai
Sallai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Sallai.shortDescription: Sallu was a Benjamite who lived in Jerusalem, as recorded in the genealogy of 1 Chronicles 9.

Sallu.name: Sallu
Sallu.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Sallu.briefDescription: Son of Hur, founder of Bethlehem
Sallu.shortDescription: Salma was the son of Hur and the  father (ie founder) of Bethlehem, as mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Salma.name: Salma
Salma.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Salma.briefDescription: Son of Nahshon; husband of Rahab; ancestor of David
Salma.shortDescription: Salmon (or Sala) was the son of Nahshon and husband of Rahab - an ancestor of King David and Jesus Christ.

Salmon.name: Salmon
Salmon.briefDescription: Danced for Herod, requested John the Baptist's execution
Salmon.shortDescription: Salome, the daughter of Herodias, danced before Herod and requested the execution of John the Baptist.

Salome.name: Salome
Salome.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Salome.briefDescription: Father of Zimri, a Simeonite prince
Salome.shortDescription: Salu was the father of Zimri, a Simeonite prince who was killed by Phinehas for his involvement with a Midianite woman.

Salu.name: Salu
Salu.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Salu.briefDescription: Babylonian official present at the fall of Jerusalem
Salu.shortDescription: Samgar was a Babylonian official present at the fall of Jerusalem, as recorded in the book of Jeremiah.

Samgar.name: Samgar
Samgar.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Samgar.briefDescription: King of Edom before Israel had kings
Samgar.shortDescription: Samlah was a king of Edom who reigned before Israel had kings, as mentioned in the genealogies of Esau.

Samlah.name: Samlah
Samlah.description: King living at the time of the Patriarchs
Samlah.briefDescription: Israelite judge with great strength from God
Samlah.shortDescription: Samson was an Israelite judge known for his God-given strength, which he used to fight against the Philistines.

Samson.name: Samson
Samson.briefDescription: Prophet and last judge of Israel before the kings
Samson.shortDescription: Samuel was a prophet and the last judge of Israel before the establishment of the monarchy, who anointed both Saul and David as kings.

Samuel.name: Samuel
Samuel.description: Prophet living at the time of United Monarchy
Samuel.briefDescription: Governor of Samaria, opposed Nehemiah's rebuilding of Jerusalem
Samuel.shortDescription: Sanballat was the governor of Samaria who opposed Nehemiah's efforts to rebuild the walls of Jerusalem.

Sanballat.name: Sanballat
Sanballat.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Sanballat.briefDescription: Philistine giant killed by Sibbechai the Hushathite
Sanballat.shortDescription: Saph (or Sippai) was a Philistine giant who was killed by Sibbechai the Hushathite during a battle with the Philistines.

Saph.name: Saph
Saph.description: Giant living at the time of United Monarchy
Saph.briefDescription: Wife of Ananias, lied to the apostles, died
Saph.shortDescription: Sapphira, along with her husband Ananias, lied to the apostles about the proceeds from a property sale and died as a result.

Sapphira.name: Sapphira
Sapphira.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Sapphira.briefDescription: Wife of Abraham; mother of Isaac
Sapphira.shortDescription: Sarah (or Sarai) was the wife of Abraham and the mother of Isaac, through whom God fulfilled His promise to make Abraham a great nation.

Sarah.name: Sarah
Sarah.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Sarah.briefDescription: Son of Shelah, descendant of Judah
Sarah.shortDescription: Saraph was a son of Shelah and a descendant of Judah, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Saraph.name: Saraph
Saraph.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Saraph.briefDescription: First king of Israel, anointed by Samuel
Saraph.shortDescription: Saul was the first king of Israel, anointed by the prophet Samuel, who initially followed God but later disobeyed and was rejected as king.

Saul.name: Saul
Saul.description: King living at the time of United Monarchy
Saul.briefDescription: Jewish chief priest whose seven sons attempted to exorcise demons
Saul.shortDescription: Sceva was a Jewish chief priest whose seven sons attempted to exorcise demons in the name of Jesus, as mentioned in the book of Acts.

Sceva.name: Sceva
Sceva.description: Priest living at the time of the New Testament
Sceva.briefDescription: Nomadic people group mentioned by Paul
Sceva.shortDescription: The Scythians were a nomadic people group known for their barbaric reputation.

Scythians.name: Scythians
Scythians.briefDescription: Son of Cush, brother of Raamah
Scythians.shortDescription: Seba was a son of Cush and brother of Raamah.

Seba.name: Seba
Seba.briefDescription: Kingdom mentioned in prophetic and poetic texts
Seba.shortDescription: Seba was a kingdom mentioned in prophetic and poetic texts, often in the context of bringing gifts to God (Psa. 72:10; Isa. 43:3).

Secundus.name: Secundus
Secundus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Secundus.briefDescription: Son of Hezron; father of Jair
Secundus.shortDescription: Segub, son of Hezron, was the father of Jair, who had twenty-three cities in Gilead.

Segub.name: Segub
Segub.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Segub.briefDescription: Horite chief, ancestor of the Horites of Seir
Segub.shortDescription: Seir was a Horite chief and an ancestor of the Horites who inhabited the land of Seir.

Seir.name: Seir
Seir.briefDescription: Region inhabited by Edomites, descendants of Esau
Seir.shortDescription: Seir was a mountainous region inhabited by the Edomites, the descendants of Esau (Gen. 32:3; 36:8; Num. 24:18; Deut. 2:4; Josh. 24:4).

Seled.name: Seled
Seled.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Seled.briefDescription: Son of Shemaiah, gatekeeper of the temple
Seled.shortDescription: Semachiah, a son of Shemaiah, served as a gatekeeper in the temple during the time of David.

Semachiah.name: Semachiah
Semachiah.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Semachiah.shortDescription: Semein is mentioned in the genealogy of Jesus Christ, as recorded in Luke's Gospel.

Semein.name: Semein
Semein.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Semein.shortDescription: Sennacherib and Sargon were Assyrian kings who led military campaigns against Judah during the reigns of King Hezekiah and King Ahaz, respectively.

Sennacherib.name: Sennacherib
Sennacherib.description: Emperor living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Sennacherib.briefDescription: Priest during David's reign, head of the fourth division
Sennacherib.shortDescription: Seorim was a priest who served during David's reign and was the head of the fourth priestly division.

Seorim.name: Seorim
Seorim.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Seorim.briefDescription: Daughter of Asher, sister of Asher's sons
Seorim.shortDescription: Serah was the daughter of Asher and the sister of Asher's sons, as mentioned in the genealogies of the tribe of Asher.

Serah.name: Serah
Serah.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Serah.briefDescription: Son of Kenaz; father of Joab
Serah.shortDescription: Seraiah, the son of Kenaz, was the father of Joab, who was the father of the Valley of Craftsmen.

Seraiah.name: Seraiah
Seraiah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Seraiah.briefDescription: Son of Zebulun, ancestor of the Seredites
Seraiah.shortDescription: Sered was a son of Zebulun and the ancestor of the Seredite clan.

Sered.name: Sered
Sered.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Sered.briefDescription: Proconsul of Cyprus, converted under Paul's ministry
Sered.shortDescription: Sergius Paulus, the proconsul of Cyprus, was an intelligent man who sought to hear the word of God and believed after witnessing Paul's confrontation with Elymas the sorcerer.

Sergius_Paulus.name: Sergius_Paulus
Sergius_Paulus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Sergius_Paulus.briefDescription: Son of Reu; father of Nahor, ancestor of Abraham
Sergius_Paulus.shortDescription: Serug was the son of Reu, the father of Nahor, and an ancestor of Abraham, living to the age of 230 years.

Serug.name: Serug
Serug.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Serug.briefDescription: Third son of Adam and Eve, ancestor of righteous line
Serug.shortDescription: Seth was the third son of Adam and Eve, born after the death of Abel, and became the ancestor of the righteous line of humanity.

Seth.name: Seth
Seth.description: Man living at the time before the Flood
Seth.briefDescription: Son of Michael, spy from the tribe of Asher
Seth.shortDescription: Sethur, the son of Michael, was one of the twelve spies sent by Moses to scout out the land of Canaan, representing the tribe of Asher.

Sethur.name: Sethur
Sethur.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Sethur.shortDescription: Shaaph, a son of Jahdai, was a descendant of Caleb.

Shaaph.name: Shaaph
Shaaph.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shaaph.briefDescription: Eunuch in charge of King Ahasuerus' concubines
Shaaph.shortDescription: Shaashgaz was the eunuch appointed by King Ahasuerus to be in charge of the concubines in the royal harem.

Shaashgaz.name: Shaashgaz
Shaashgaz.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shaashgaz.briefDescription: Levite who opposed Ezra's marriage reforms
Shaashgaz.shortDescription: Shabbethai, a Levite, opposed Ezra's efforts to address the issue of intermarriage among the returned exiles.

Shabbethai.name: Shabbethai
Shabbethai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shabbethai.briefDescription: Benjamite who had wives Hushim and Baara
Shabbethai.shortDescription: Shaharaim, a Benjamite, had two wives, Hushim and Baara, and he sent them away.

Shaharaim.name: Shaharaim
Shaharaim.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shaharaim.briefDescription: Son of Sismai, descendant of Judah
Shaharaim.shortDescription: Shallum, the son of Sismai, was a descendant of Judah through the line of Jerahmeel.

Shallum.name: Shallum
Shallum.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shallum.briefDescription: Nethinim ancestor whose descendants returned from Babylonian exile
Shallum.shortDescription: Shalmai (or Shamlai) was an ancestor of the Nethinim, and his descendants were among those who returned to Jerusalem from the Babylonian exile.

Shalmai.name: Shalmai
Shalmai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shalmai.briefDescription: Assyrian king who conquered Samaria and exiled Israelites
Shalmai.shortDescription: Shalmaneser, king of Assyria, besieged Samaria and conquered the northern kingdom of Israel, exiling its inhabitants and ending the kingdom's existence.

Shalmaneser.name: Shalmaneser
Shalmaneser.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shalmaneser.briefDescription: Mighty man in David's army, a Harodite
Shalmaneser.shortDescription: Shama, the son of Hotham the Aroerite, was one of the mighty men in David's army.

Shama.name: Shama
Shama.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shama.briefDescription: Judge of Israel who killed 600 Philistines
Shama.shortDescription: Shamgar, the son of Anath, was a judge of Israel who delivered the nation by killing 600 Philistines with an oxgoad.

Shamgar.name: Shamgar
Shamgar.briefDescription: Fifth division commander in David's army
Shamgar.shortDescription: Shamhuth the Izrahite was the commander of the fifth division in David's army, serving during the fifth month.

Shamhuth.name: Shamhuth
Shamhuth.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shamhuth.shortDescription: Shamir (or Shamur) was a Levite descended from Uzziel, the son of Kohath.

Shamir.name: Shamir
Shamir.briefDescription: Town in the hill country of Judah
Shamir.shortDescription: Shamir was a town in the hill country of Judah (Josh. 15:48).

Shamma.name: Shamma
Shamma.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shamma.briefDescription: Mighty man in David's army, defended a lentil field
Shamma.shortDescription: Shammah, one of David's mighty men, single-handedly defended a lentil field against the Philistines, resulting in a great victory for Israel.

Shammah.name: Shammah
Shammah.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Shammah.briefDescription: Son of Onam; father of Nadab and Abishur
Shammah.shortDescription: Shammai, the son of Onam, was the father of Nadab and Abishur, who were descendants of Jerahmeel, the son of Hezron.

Shammai.name: Shammai
Shammai.briefDescription: Son of David born in Jerusalem
Shammai.shortDescription: Shammua (or Shimea) was one of the sons of David born to him in Jerusalem.

Shammua.name: Shammua
Shammua.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shammua.shortDescription: Shamsherai was a Benjamite descendant who lived in Jerusalem during the time of the genealogical records in 1 Chronicles.

Shamsherai.name: Shamsherai
Shamsherai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shamsherai.briefDescription: Gadite leader during the reign of Jotham
Shamsherai.shortDescription: Shapham was a leader of the Gadites who lived in Gilead during the reign of Jotham, king of Judah.

Shapham.name: Shapham
Shapham.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shapham.briefDescription: Scribe during the reign of King Josiah
Shapham.shortDescription: Shaphan, the scribe, played a significant role in the religious reforms of King Josiah and the events surrounding the fall of Jerusalem to the Babylonians.

Shaphan.name: Shaphan
Shaphan.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shaphan.briefDescription: Overseer of David's herds in the valleys
Shaphan.shortDescription: Shaphat, the son of Adlai, was appointed by King David as the overseer of his herds that grazed in the valleys.

Shaphat.name: Shaphat
Shaphat.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shaphat.briefDescription: Israelite who divorced his foreign wife
Shaphat.shortDescription: Sharai was one of the Israelites who had married a foreign woman and agreed to divorce her as part of Ezra's religious reforms.

Sharai.name: Sharai
Sharai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Sharai.briefDescription: Father of Ahiam, one of David's mighty men
Sharai.shortDescription: Sharar (or Sachar) was the father of Ahiam, who was one of David's mighty men known for his bravery and military prowess.

Sharar.name: Sharar
Sharar.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Sharar.briefDescription: Assyrian prince who assassinated his father Sennacherib
Sharar.shortDescription: Sharezer, along with his brother Adrammelech, assassinated their father, King Sennacherib of Assyria, and fled to the land of Ararat.

Sharezer.name: Sharezer
Sharezer.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Sharezer.briefDescription: Israelite who divorced his foreign wife
Sharezer.shortDescription: Shashai was one of the Israelites who had married a foreign woman and agreed to divorce her as part of Ezra's religious reforms.

Shashai.name: Shashai
Shashai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shashai.briefDescription: Benjamite leader and descendant of Elpaal
Shashai.shortDescription: Shashak, a Benjamite leader, was a descendant of Elpaal and is mentioned in the genealogical records of 1 Chronicles.

Shashak.name: Shashak
Shashak.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shashak.shortDescription: Shaul (or Joel) was a Levite descended from Kohath, as listed in the genealogies of 1 Chronicles.

Shaul.name: Shaul
Shaul.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shaul.briefDescription: Israelite who divorced his foreign wife
Shaul.shortDescription: Sheal was one of the Israelites who had married a foreign woman and agreed to divorce her as part of Ezra's religious reforms.

Sheal.name: Sheal
Sheal.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Sheal.briefDescription: Grandfather of Zerubbabel, descendant of David
Sheal.shortDescription: Shealtiel, the grandfather of Zerubbabel, was a descendant of David and played a significant role in the rebuilding of the temple after the Babylonian exile.

Shealtiel.name: Shealtiel
Shealtiel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shealtiel.shortDescription: Sheariah was a Benjamite, the son of Azel, and is mentioned in the genealogical records of 1 Chronicles.

Sheariah.name: Sheariah
Sheariah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Sheariah.shortDescription: Shear-jashub, meaning "a remnant shall return," was the son of the prophet Isaiah and accompanied his father to meet King Ahaz.

Shear-jashub.name: Shear-jashub
Shear-jashub.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shear-jashub.briefDescription: Gadite leader during the reign of Jotham
Shear-jashub.shortDescription: Sheba was one of the Gadite leaders who lived in Bashan during the reign of Jotham, king of Judah.

Sheba.name: Sheba
Sheba.briefDescription: Kingdom known for wealth, trade, and the Queen of Sheba
Sheba.shortDescription: Sheba, also referred to as the South, was a kingdom known for its wealth, trade, and the visit of its queen to King Solomon (1 Ki. 10:1-13; 2 Chr. 9:1-12; Ps. 72:10, 15; Isa. 60:6; Jer. 6:20; Ezek. 27:22-23; 38:13; Luke 11:31).

Shebaniah.name: Shebaniah
Shebaniah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shebaniah.briefDescription: Son of Caleb and his concubine Maacah
Shebaniah.shortDescription: Sheber was one of the sons born to Caleb and his concubine Maacah, as mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Sheber.name: Sheber
Sheber.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Sheber.briefDescription: Steward of the palace during Hezekiah's reign
Sheber.shortDescription: Shebna was a high-ranking official in King Hezekiah's court, serving as the steward of the palace, but was later demoted and replaced by Eliakim.

Shebna.name: Shebna
Shebna.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shebna.briefDescription: Levite descendant of Gershom, in charge of treasuries
Shebna.shortDescription: Shebuel (or Shubael), a descendant of Gershom, was a Levite appointed by King David to be in charge of the treasuries of the house of God.

Shebuel.name: Shebuel
Shebuel.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shebuel.shortDescription: Shecaniah, a Levite priest during the reign of King David.

Shecaniah.name: Shecaniah
Shecaniah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shecaniah.shortDescription: Shechem, a son of Shemida, from the tribe of Manasseh.

Shechem.name: Shechem
Shechem.briefDescription: City in Canaan, later allotted to Ephraim
Shechem.shortDescription: Shechem was a prominent city in Canaan, later allotted to the tribe of Ephraim, and the site of important events in Israel's history (Gen. 12:6; 33:18-20; Josh. 20:7; 24:1, 25-26; Judg. 9; 1 Ki. 12:1, 25; Ps. 60:6; 108:7; Act.7.16).

Shedeur.name: Shedeur
Shedeur.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shedeur.briefDescription: Ephraim's granddaughter who built three towns
Shedeur.shortDescription: Sheerah, granddaughter of Ephraim, who built Beth-horon and Uzzen-sheerah.

Sheerah.name: Sheerah
Sheerah.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Sheerah.shortDescription: Shehariah, a descendant of Benjamin in the genealogy of Saul.

Shehariah.name: Shehariah
Shehariah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shehariah.briefDescription: Son of Arpachshad and ancestor of Abraham
Shehariah.shortDescription: Shelah, son of Arpachshad and ancestor of Abraham in Luke's genealogy.

Shelah.name: Shelah
Shelah.shortDescription: Shelah, also called Shiloah, the King's Pool, or Siloam, was a pool in Jerusalem fed by the Gihon Spring via Hezekiah's Tunnel (Neh. 3:15; Isa. 8:6; Neh. 2:14; Luke 13:4; Jhn.9.7, 11).

Shelemiah.name: Shelemiah
Shelemiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shelemiah.briefDescription: Son of Joktan, descendant of Shem
Shelemiah.shortDescription: Sheleph, a son of Joktan and descendant of Shem.

Sheleph.name: Sheleph
Sheleph.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Sheleph.shortDescription: Shelesh, a son of Hotham (or 'Helem'), from the tribe of Asher.

Shelesh.name: Shelesh
Shelesh.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shelesh.briefDescription: Father of Ahihud, a leader from Asher
Shelesh.shortDescription: Shelomi, the father of Ahihud, a leader from the tribe of Asher.

Shelomi.name: Shelomi
Shelomi.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shelomi.shortDescription: Shelomith (or Shelomoth), a Levite of the family of Izhar.

Shelomith.name: Shelomith
Shelomith.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shelomith.shortDescription: Shelomoth (or Shelomith), Levites of the family of Shimei, son of Gershon.

Shelomoth.name: Shelomoth
Shelomoth.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shelomoth.briefDescription: Leader of the tribe of Simeon
Shelomoth.shortDescription: Shelumiel, the son of Zurishaddai, leader of the tribe of Simeon during the wilderness wanderings.

Shelumiel.name: Shelumiel
Shelumiel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shelumiel.briefDescription: Noah's son, ancestor of Semitic peoples
Shelumiel.shortDescription: Shem, one of Noah's three sons and the ancestor of the Semitic peoples.

Shem.name: Shem
Shem.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Shem.shortDescription: Shema, a son of Hebron and descendant of Judah.

Shema.name: Shema
Shema.briefDescription: Town in the Negev region of Judah
Shema.shortDescription: Shema was a town in the Negev region of Judah (Josh. 15:26).

Shemaah.name: Shemaah
Shemaah.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shemaah.shortDescription: Shemaiah, a Levite of the sons of Elizaphan, who helped bring the ark to Jerusalem.

Shemaiah.name: Shemaiah
Shemaiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shemaiah.shortDescription: Shemariah, a Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag while he was hiding from Saul.

Shemariah.name: Shemariah
Shemariah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shemariah.shortDescription: Shemeber, the king of Zeboiim, who joined the battle against Chedorlaomer and his allies.

Shemeber.name: Shemeber
Shemeber.description: King living at the time of the Patriarchs
Shemeber.shortDescription: Shemed, a son of Elpaal, who built Ono and Lod with its towns.

Shemed.name: Shemed
Shemed.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shemed.briefDescription: Levite of the family of Merari
Shemed.shortDescription: Shemer, a Levite of the family of Merari, an ancestor of Ethan the singer.

Shemer.name: Shemer
Shemer.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shemer.briefDescription: Son of Gilead, great-grandson of Manasseh
Shemer.shortDescription: Shemida, a son of Gilead and great-grandson of Manasseh, who was the ancestor of the Shemidaite clan.

Shemida.name: Shemida
Shemida.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shemida.shortDescription: Shemiramoth, a Levite appointed by David to play the harp and lyres in the procession of the ark.

Shemiramoth.name: Shemiramoth
Shemiramoth.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shemiramoth.briefDescription: Son of Tola, a leader of Issachar
Shemiramoth.shortDescription: Shemuel, a son of Tola and a leader of the tribe of Issachar during David's reign.

Shemuel.name: Shemuel
Shemuel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shemuel.briefDescription: Descendant of King Jeconiah (Jehoiachin) of Judah
Shemuel.shortDescription: Shenazzar, a descendant of King Jeconiah (Jehoiachin) of Judah, born during the Babylonian captivity.

Shenazzar.name: Shenazzar
Shenazzar.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shenazzar.shortDescription: Shephatiah, a Benjamite warrior who joined David at Ziklag while he was hiding from Saul.

Shephatiah.name: Shephatiah
Shephatiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shephatiah.shortDescription: Shepho, a Horite chief, the son of Shobal, who lived in the land of Seir.

Shepho.name: Shepho
Shepho.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Shepho.briefDescription: Levite who assisted Ezra and Nehemiah
Shepho.shortDescription: Sherebiah, a Levite who played a significant role in assisting Ezra and Nehemiah in their respective missions.

Sherebiah.name: Sherebiah
Sherebiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Sherebiah.shortDescription: Sheresh, a son of Machir and Maacah, mentioned in the genealogy of Manasseh.

Sheresh.name: Sheresh
Sheresh.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Sheresh.shortDescription: Sheshai, one of the Anakite giants, was defeated by Caleb during the conquest of Canaan.

Sheshai.name: Sheshai
Sheshai.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Sheshai.briefDescription: Jerahmeelite whose daughter married his Egyptian servant
Sheshai.shortDescription: Sheshan, a Jerahmeelite who gave his daughter in marriage to his Egyptian servant, Jarha.

Sheshan.name: Sheshan
Sheshan.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Sheshan.briefDescription: Persian noble in King Ahasuerus's court
Sheshan.shortDescription: Shethar, one of the seven Persian nobles who had access to King Ahasuerus's presence.

Shethar.name: Shethar
Shethar.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shethar.briefDescription: Persian official who questioned the Jews' temple rebuilding
Shethar.shortDescription: Shethar-bozenai, a Persian official who questioned the legitimacy of the Jews' efforts to rebuild the temple in Jerusalem.

Shethar-bozenai.name: Shethar-bozenai
Shethar-bozenai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shethar-bozenai.shortDescription: Sheva, a son of Caleb's concubine Maacah, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Sheva.name: Sheva
Sheva.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Sheva.shortDescription: Shilhi, the father of Azubah, who was the mother of King Jehoshaphat of Judah.

Shilhi.name: Shilhi
Shilhi.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shilhi.briefDescription: Son of Naphtali, ancestor of the Shillemites
Shilhi.shortDescription: Shillem (or Shallum), a son of Naphtali and ancestor of the Shillemite clan.

Shillem.name: Shillem
Shillem.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shillem.briefDescription: A name in Jacob's blessing on Judah
Shillem.shortDescription: Shiloh, a term used in Jacob's prophetic blessing on Judah, possibly referring to a future ruler or a place of rest.

Shiloh.name: Shiloh
Shiloh.briefDescription: Israelite sanctuary and assembly point during the judges' period
Shiloh.shortDescription: Shiloh was an important Israelite sanctuary and assembly point during the period of the judges, where the tabernacle was set up and the land was allotted to the tribes (Josh. 18:1, 8-10; 19:51; 21:2; 22:9, 12; Judg. 18:31; 21:12, 19, 21; 1 Sam. 1:3, 9, 24; 2:14; 3:21; 4:3, 4, 12; 14:3; 1 Ki. 2:27; 14:2, 4; Ps. 78:60; Jer. 7:12, 14; 26:6, 9; 41:5).

Shiloni.name: Shiloni
Shiloni.briefDescription: Son of Zophah, a descendant of Asher.
Shiloni.shortDescription: Shilshah was a son of Zophah and a descendant of Asher.

Shilshah.name: Shilshah
Shilshah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shilshah.briefDescription: Levite of the family of Merari during David's reign.
Shilshah.shortDescription: Shimea was a Levite of the family of Merari who served during King David's reign.

Shimea.name: Shimea
Shimea.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shimea.briefDescription: Son of Mikloth and nephew of Ner in Benjamin's genealogy.
Shimea.shortDescription: Shimeah (or Shimeam) was a Benjamite, son of Mikloth and nephew of Ner.

Shimeah.name: Shimeah
Shimeah.description: David's brother living at the time of United Monarchy
Shimeah.briefDescription: Mother of Jozacar, one of King Joash's assassins.
Shimeah.shortDescription: Shimeath was an Ammonitess, the mother of Jozacar (or 'Jozachar'), one of the assassins of King Joash of Judah.

Shimeath.name: Shimeath
Shimeath.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shimeath.briefDescription: Overseer of King David's vineyards from Ramah.
Shimeath.shortDescription: Shimei from Ramah was the overseer of King David's vineyards.

Shimei.name: Shimei
Shimei.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shimei.briefDescription: Israelite who married a foreign wife during the exile.
Shimei.shortDescription: Shimeon was an Israelite who had married a foreign wife during the Babylonian exile and agreed to send her away.

Shimeon.name: Shimeon
Shimeon.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shimeon.briefDescription: Son of Judah and descendant of Caleb.
Shimeon.shortDescription: Shimon was a son of Judah and a descendant of Caleb, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Shimon.name: Shimon
Shimon.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shimon.briefDescription: Benjamite descendant of Shimei, son of Elpaal.
Shimon.shortDescription: Shimrath was a Benjamite descendant of Shimei, the son of Elpaal.

Shimrath.name: Shimrath
Shimrath.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shimrath.briefDescription: One of David's mighty warriors from the tribe of Benjamin.
Shimrath.shortDescription: Shimri was one of David's mighty warriors from the tribe of Benjamin.

Shimri.name: Shimri
Shimri.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shimri.briefDescription: Son of Issachar and progenitor of the Shimronites.
Shimri.shortDescription: Shimron was a son of Issachar and the progenitor of the Shimronite clan within the tribe of Issachar.

Shimron.name: Shimron
Shimron.briefDescription: Canaanite city conquered by Joshua, allotted to Zebulun
Shimron.shortDescription: Shimron, also called Shimron-meron, was a Canaanite city conquered by Joshua (Josh. 11:1; 12:20) and later allotted to the tribe of Zebulun (Josh. 19:15).

Shimshai.name: Shimshai
Shimshai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shimshai.briefDescription: King of Admah who rebelled against Chedorlaomer.
Shimshai.shortDescription: Shinab was the king of Admah who, along with other kings, rebelled against Chedorlaomer, king of Elam.

Shinab.name: Shinab
Shinab.description: King living at the time of the Patriarchs
Shinab.briefDescription: Simeonite descendant of Ziza in Hezekiah's time.
Shinab.shortDescription: Shiphi was a Simeonite descendant of Ziza who lived during the reign of King Hezekiah.

Shiphi.name: Shiphi
Shiphi.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shiphi.briefDescription: People from Siphmoth who oversaw David's vineyards.
Shiphi.shortDescription: The Shiphmites were people from Siphmoth who were in charge of King David's vineyards.

Shiphmites.name: Shiphmites
Shiphmites.briefDescription: Hebrew midwife who feared God and disobeyed Pharaoh.
Shiphmites.shortDescription: Shiphrah, along with Puah, was a Hebrew midwife who feared God and disobeyed Pharaoh's command to kill male babies.

Shiphrah.name: Shiphrah
Shiphrah.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shiphrah.briefDescription: Father of Kemuel, a leader from the tribe of Ephraim.
Shiphrah.shortDescription: Shiphtan was the father of Kemuel, a leader from the tribe of Ephraim appointed to help divide the Promised Land.

Shiphtan.name: Shiphtan
Shiphtan.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shiphtan.briefDescription: Egyptian Pharaoh who invaded Judah during Rehoboam's reign.
Shiphtan.shortDescription: Shishak was an Egyptian Pharaoh who invaded Judah during the reign of King Rehoboam and captured fortified cities.

Shishak.name: Shishak
Shishak.description: Pharaoh living at the time of United Monarchy
Shishak.briefDescription: Sharonite in charge of King David's herds in Sharon.
Shishak.shortDescription: Shitrai was a Sharonite who was in charge of King David's herds that grazed in the plain of Sharon.

Shitrai.name: Shitrai
Shitrai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shitrai.briefDescription: Reubenite father of Adina, one of David's mighty warriors.
Shitrai.shortDescription: Shiza was a Reubenite, the father of Adina, who was one of David's mighty warriors.

Shiza.name: Shiza
Shiza.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shiza.shortDescription: Shobab was a son of Caleb and his wife Azubah.

Shobab.name: Shobab
Shobab.description: David's son living at the time of United Monarchy
Shobab.briefDescription: Commander of Hadadezer's army, killed by David's men.
Shobab.shortDescription: Shobach (or Shophach) was the commander of Hadadezer's army, who was killed by David's men during a battle.

Shobach.name: Shobach
Shobach.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Shobach.briefDescription: Ancestor of a family of gatekeepers who returned from exile.
Shobach.shortDescription: Shobai was the ancestor of a family of gatekeepers who returned to Jerusalem from the Babylonian exile.

Shobai.name: Shobai
Shobai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shobai.briefDescription: Son of Hur, founder of Kiriath-jearim, Judahite descendant.
Shobai.shortDescription: Shobal was a Judahite, the son of Hur and the father (ie founder) of Kiriath-jearim.

Shobal.name: Shobal
Shobal.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Shobal.shortDescription: Shobek was a Levite who signed the covenant led by Nehemiah to follow God's law.

Shobek.name: Shobek
Shobek.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Shobek.briefDescription: Ammonite who provided supplies to David at Mahanaim.
Shobek.shortDescription: Shobi was an Ammonite who brought supplies to David at Mahanaim when he was fleeing from his son Absalom.

Shobi.name: Shobi
Shobi.description: Ammonite living at the time of United Monarchy
Shobi.briefDescription: Merarite Levite of the family of Jaaziah.
Shobi.shortDescription: Shoham was a Merarite Levite belonging to the family of Jaaziah during the organization of the Levitical service.

Shoham.name: Shoham
Shoham.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shoham.shortDescription: Shomer (or Shemer) was an Asherite descendant, the son of Heber.

Shomer.name: Shomer
Shomer.description: Moabite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shomer.shortDescription: Shua was an Asherite descendant, the daughter of Heber.

Shua.name: Shua
Shua.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Shua.briefDescription: Son of Abraham and Keturah, ancestor of a tribe.
Shua.shortDescription: Shuah was a son of Abraham and Keturah, and his descendants formed a tribe.

Shuah.name: Shuah
Shuah.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Shuah.shortDescription: Shual (or Ulla) was an Asherite descendant, the son of Zophah.

Shual.name: Shual
Shual.briefDescription: Region raided by Philistines during Saul's reign
Shual.shortDescription: Shual was a region raided by Philistine raiders during Saul's reign, along with Ophrah and the land of Shaalim (1 Sam. 13:17).

Shuhah.name: Shuhah
Shuhah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Shuhah.briefDescription: People group associated with Bildad, Job's friend.
Shuhah.shortDescription: The Shuhites were a people group to which Bildad, one of Job's friends, belonged.

Shuhites.name: Shuhites
Shuhites.briefDescription: A group or place mentioned in Song of Solomon.
Shuhites.shortDescription: The Shulammites are mentioned in the Song of Solomon, possibly referring to a group of people or a place associated with the Shulammite woman.

Shulammites.name: Shulammites
Shulammites.briefDescription: A family of Kirjath-jearim, descendants of Judah.
Shulammites.shortDescription: The Shumathites were a family from Kirjath-jearim, descendants of Judah through Shobal.

Shumathites.name: Shumathites
Shumathites.briefDescription: People from Shunem; includes Abishag and the Shunammite woman
Shumathites.shortDescription: Shunammites, inhabitants of Shunem, including Abishag, David's nurse, and the Shunammite woman, Elisha's benefactor.

Shunammites.name: Shunammites
Shunammites.briefDescription: Son of Gad, ancestor of the Shunite clan.
Shunammites.shortDescription: Shuni was a son of Gad and the ancestor of the Shunite clan within the tribe of Gad.

Shuni.name: Shuni
Shuni.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shuni.shortDescription: Shuppim was a Levite who served as a gatekeeper during the reign of King David.

Shuppim.name: Shuppim
Shuppim.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shuppim.shortDescription: Shuthelah was an Ephraimite descendant, the son of Zabad, mentioned in the genealogy of Ephraim.

Shuthelah.name: Shuthelah
Shuthelah.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Shuthelah.briefDescription: Ancestor of a family of temple servants who returned from exile.
Shuthelah.shortDescription: Siaha (or Sia) was the ancestor of a family of temple servants who returned to Jerusalem from the Babylonian exile.

Siaha.name: Siaha
Siaha.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Siaha.briefDescription: One of David's mighty men, a Hushathite.
Siaha.shortDescription: Sibbecai (or Mebunnai) was one of David's mighty men, a Hushathite who fought valiantly in David's battles.

Sibbecai.name: Sibbecai
Sibbecai.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Sibbecai.briefDescription: Firstborn son of Canaan, ancestor of the Sidonians.
Sibbecai.shortDescription: Sidon was the firstborn son of Canaan and the ancestor of the Sidonian people.

Sidon.name: Sidon
Sidon.shortDescription: Sidon, also called Great Sidon, was a prominent Phoenician city known for its trade and culture, and was the hometown of Jezebel, wife of King Ahab (1 Ki. 16:31). It is mentioned in various contexts throughout the Bible, including the Old Testament (Gen. 10:19; Josh. 11:8; Judg. 10:6; Isa. 23:2-4; Jer. 27:3; Ezek. 28:21-22) and the New Testament (Matt. 11:21-22; Luke 4:26; Act.27.3).

Sihon.name: Sihon
Sihon.description: King living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Sihon.briefDescription: Prominent member of the Jerusalem church, companion of Paul.
Sihon.shortDescription: Silas (or Silvanus) was a prominent member of the Jerusalem church who accompanied Paul on his second missionary journey and co-wrote some of his epistles.

Silas.name: Silas
Silas.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Silas.briefDescription: Prophet and teacher in the church at Antioch.
Silas.shortDescription: Simeon, also called Niger, was a prophet and teacher in the church at Antioch who was involved in sending Paul and Barnabas on their first missionary journey.

Simeon.name: Simeon
Simeon.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
