Simeon.briefDescription: A tanner in Joppa who hosted Peter.
Simeon.shortDescription: Simon was a tanner in Joppa who hosted the apostle Peter in his home.

Simon.name: Simon
Simon.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Simon.briefDescription: Canaanite people group descended from Canaan.
Simon.shortDescription: The Sinites were a Canaanite people group descended from Canaan, the son of Ham.

Sinites.name: Sinites
Sinites.briefDescription: Ancestor of a family of temple servants who returned from exile.
Sinites.shortDescription: Sisera was the ancestor of a family of temple servants who returned to Jerusalem from the Babylonian exile.

Sisera.name: Sisera
Sisera.description: Deborah's adversary living before Israel's Monarchy
Sisera.shortDescription: Sismai was a descendant of Judah through Jerahmeel, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Sismai.name: Sismai
Sismai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Sismai.briefDescription: Egyptian queen, given in marriage to Hadad
Sismai.shortDescription: The sister of Tahpenes, an Egyptian queen, was given in marriage to Hadad the Edomite by Pharaoh.

sister_of_Tahpenes.name: sister_of_Tahpenes
sister_of_Tahpenes.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
sister_of_Tahpenes.briefDescription: Son of Uzziel, a Levite of the Kohathite clan.
sister_of_Tahpenes.shortDescription: Sithri was a son of Uzziel, a Levite of the Kohathite clan, mentioned in the genealogy of the Levites.

Sithri.name: Sithri
Sithri.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Sithri.briefDescription: Egyptian pharaoh to whom King Hoshea sent messengers.
Sithri.shortDescription: So was an Egyptian pharaoh to whom King Hoshea of Israel sent messengers, likely seeking an alliance against Assyria.

So.name: So
So.description: Pharaoh living at the time of Divided Monarchy
So.briefDescription: Son of Heber, from the tribe of Judah.
So.shortDescription: Soco was a son of Heber, from the tribe of Judah, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Soco.name: Soco
Soco.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Soco.briefDescription: Father of the Zebulunite spy Gaddiel.
Soco.shortDescription: Sodi was the father of Gaddiel, the man chosen from the tribe of Zebulun to spy out the land of Canaan.

Sodi.name: Sodi
Sodi.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Sodi.briefDescription: Son of David, king of Israel, known for his wisdom.
Sodi.shortDescription: Solomon, also called Jedidiah, was the son of David and Bathsheba who succeeded his father as king of Israel and was renowned for his wisdom, wealth, and the construction of the First Temple in Jerusalem.

Solomon.name: Solomon
Solomon.description: King living at the time of United Monarchy
Solomon.briefDescription: Son of Jashen - One of David's mighty men
Solomon.shortDescription: The son of Jashen, unnamed in the Bible, was one of David's mighty men.

son_of_Jashen.name: son_of_Jashen
son_of_Jashen.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
son_of_Jashen.briefDescription: Grandson of Eliashib, married Sanballat's daughter
son_of_Jashen.shortDescription: The son of Joiada, a grandson of the high priest Eliashib, married the daughter of Sanballat the Horonite.

son_of_Joiada.name: son_of_Joiada
son_of_Joiada.description: Priest living at the time of Exile and Return
son_of_Joiada.briefDescription: Companion of Paul from Berea, greeted the Roman church.
son_of_Joiada.shortDescription: Sopater (or Sosipater) was a companion of Paul from Berea who accompanied him on his journey to Jerusalem and later sent greetings to the church in Rome.

Sopater.name: Sopater
Sopater.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Sopater.briefDescription: Ancestor of a family of Solomon's servants who returned from exile.
Sopater.shortDescription: Sophereth (or Hassophereth) was the ancestor of a family of Solomon's servants who were among the exiles that returned to Jerusalem from Babylon.

Sophereth.name: Sophereth
Sophereth.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Sophereth.briefDescription: Synagogue ruler in Corinth, co-sender of 1 Corinthians.
Sophereth.shortDescription: Sosthenes was a synagogue ruler in Corinth who was beaten by a mob and later became a co-sender of Paul's first letter to the Corinthians.

Sosthenes.name: Sosthenes
Sosthenes.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Sosthenes.briefDescription: Ancestor of a family of Solomon's servants who returned from exile.
Sosthenes.shortDescription: Sotai was the ancestor of a family of Solomon's servants who were among the exiles that returned to Jerusalem from Babylon.

Sotai.name: Sotai
Sotai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Sotai.briefDescription: Believer in Rome, greeted by Paul as a beloved friend.
Sotai.shortDescription: Stachys was a believer in Rome whom Paul greeted as his beloved friend.

Stachys.name: Stachys
Stachys.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Stachys.briefDescription: Corinthian believer, baptized by Paul, sent to visit him.
Stachys.shortDescription: Stephanas was a believer from Corinth who, along with his household, was baptized by Paul and later visited Paul to provide support and encouragement.

Stephanas.name: Stephanas
Stephanas.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Stephanas.briefDescription: One of seven deacons, full of faith, performed miracles, martyred.
Stephanas.shortDescription: Stephen, one of the seven deacons chosen by the early church, was full of faith and the Holy Spirit, performed miracles, and became the first Christian martyr after his powerful testimony before the Sanhedrin.

Stephen.name: Stephen
Stephen.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Stephen.briefDescription: Son of Zophah, from the tribe of Asher
Stephen.shortDescription: Suah, a descendant of Asher mentioned in a genealogy.

Suah.name: Suah
Suah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Suah.briefDescription: African people who invaded Judah under Shishak
Suah.shortDescription: The Sukkiim were an African people who joined Shishak's invasion of Judah.

Sukkiim.name: Sukkiim
Sukkiim.briefDescription: Woman who helped support Jesus and his disciples
Sukkiim.shortDescription: Susanna was one of the women who provided for Jesus out of her resources.

Susanna.name: Susanna
Susanna.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Susanna.briefDescription: Father of the Manassite spy Gaddi
Susanna.shortDescription: Susi was the father of Gaddi, the spy from the tribe of Manasseh.

Susi.name: Susi
Susi.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Susi.briefDescription: Woman in the Philippian church whom Paul exhorted
Susi.shortDescription: Syntyche was a prominent woman in Philippi whom Paul urged to reconcile with Euodia.

Syntyche.name: Syntyche
Syntyche.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Syntyche.briefDescription: Family of temple servants who returned from exile
Syntyche.shortDescription: The Tabbaoth were a family of temple servants who returned to Judah from Babylonian exile.

Tabbaoth.name: Tabbaoth
Tabbaoth.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Tabbaoth.briefDescription: Man whom Syria and Ephraim wanted to make king of Judah
Tabbaoth.shortDescription: Tabeal was the man Syria and Ephraim conspired to make king of Judah in place of Ahaz.

Tabeal.name: Tabeal
Tabeal.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Tabeal.briefDescription: Persian official involved in accusing the Jews to Artaxerxes
Tabeal.shortDescription: Tabeel was a Persian official who joined in writing an accusation against the Jews to Artaxerxes.

Tabeel.name: Tabeel
Tabeel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Tabeel.briefDescription: Charitable woman in Joppa whom Peter raised from the dead
Tabeel.shortDescription: Tabitha, also called Dorcas, was a beloved disciple in Joppa known for her charitable works, whom Peter raised from the dead.

Tabitha.name: Tabitha
Tabitha.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Tabitha.briefDescription: Father of Ben-hadad, king of Syria
Tabitha.shortDescription: Tabrimmon was the father of Ben-hadad, the king of Syria who was an ally of Asa king of Judah.

Tabrimmon.name: Tabrimmon
Tabrimmon.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Tabrimmon.briefDescription: Son of Telah, from the tribe of Ephraim
Tabrimmon.shortDescription: Tahan was a descendant of Ephraim, the son of Telah and father of Ladan.

Tahan.name: Tahan
Tahan.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Tahan.shortDescription: Tahash was a son of Abraham's brother Nahor and his concubine Reumah.

Tahash.name: Tahash
Tahash.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Tahash.briefDescription: Levite of the Kohathite clan, son of Assir
Tahash.shortDescription: Tahath was a Levite of the Kohathite clan, the son of Assir and father of Uriel.

Tahath.name: Tahath
Tahath.briefDescription: Israelite campsite during the wilderness journey
Tahath.shortDescription: Tahath, a place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness journey (Num. 33:26-27).

Tahpenes.name: Tahpenes
Tahpenes.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
Tahpenes.briefDescription: King of Geshur; father of David's wife Maacah
Tahpenes.shortDescription: Talmai was the king of Geshur and father of Maacah, one of David's wives and mother of Absalom.

Talmai.name: Talmai
Talmai.description: King living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Talmai.briefDescription: Levite gatekeeper who returned from Babylonian exile
Talmai.shortDescription: Talmon was a Levite gatekeeper who returned from Babylon and served at the temple.

Talmon.name: Talmon
Talmon.description: Levite living at the time of Exile and Return
Talmon.briefDescription: David's daughter, raped by her half-brother Amnon
Talmon.shortDescription: Tamar was David's daughter who was raped by her half-brother Amnon, leading to tragic consequences.

Tamar.name: Tamar
Tamar.briefDescription: Landmark on the southeastern border of Israel
Tamar.shortDescription: Tamar, a place mentioned as a landmark on the southeastern border of the Promised Land (Ezek. 47:18-19; 48:28).

Tanhumeth.name: Tanhumeth
Tanhumeth.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Tanhumeth.briefDescription: Daughter of Solomon; wife of Ben-abinadab
Tanhumeth.shortDescription: Taphath was a daughter of Solomon who married Ben-abinadab, one of Solomon's district governors.

Taphath.name: Taphath
Taphath.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
Taphath.briefDescription: Son of Hebron from the tribe of Judah
Taphath.shortDescription: Tappuah was a son of Hebron, a descendant of Judah.

Tappuah.name: Tappuah
Tappuah.shortDescription: Tappuah, a town in the Shephelah (lowlands) of Judah (Josh. 15:34).

Tarea.name: Tarea
Tarea.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Tarea.briefDescription: Son of Bilhan from the tribe of Benjamin
Tarea.shortDescription: Tarshish was a son of Bilhan, a descendant of Benjamin.

Tarshish.name: Tarshish
Tarshish.briefDescription: Distant maritime city known for its wealth and trade
Tarshish.shortDescription: Tarshish, a distant maritime city known for its ships, wealth, and trade, possibly located in southern Spain (1 Kgs. 10:22; Isa. 23:1; Jonah 1:3).

Tartan.name: Tartan
Tartan.briefDescription: Persian governor who questioned the Jews' authority to rebuild
Tartan.shortDescription: Tattenai was the Persian governor of the province Beyond the River who questioned the Jews' authority to rebuild the temple.

Tattenai.name: Tattenai
Tattenai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Tattenai.shortDescription: Tebah was a son of Abraham's brother Nahor and his concubine Reumah.

Tebah.name: Tebah
Tebah.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Tebah.shortDescription: Tebaliah was a Levite gatekeeper, the third son of Hosah.

Tebaliah.name: Tebaliah
Tebaliah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Tebaliah.briefDescription: Father of Ir-nahash from the tribe of Judah
Tebaliah.shortDescription: Tehinnah was the father of Ir-nahash and a descendant of Chelub from the tribe of Judah.

Tehinnah.name: Tehinnah
Tehinnah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Tehinnah.briefDescription: Son of Resheph from the tribe of Ephraim
Tehinnah.shortDescription: Telah was a descendant of Ephraim, the son of Resheph and father of Tahan.

Telah.name: Telah
Telah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Telah.briefDescription: Temple servant who married a foreign wife
Telah.shortDescription: Telem was a temple servant who married a foreign wife during the Exile and later divorced her.

Telem.name: Telem
Telem.briefDescription: Town in the Negev region of Judah
Telem.shortDescription: Telem, a town in the Negev region of Judah, allotted to the tribe of Judah (Josh. 15:24).

Tema.name: Tema
Tema.briefDescription: Arabian city and tribe known for trade
Tema.shortDescription: Tema, an Arabian city and tribe known for its caravans and trade, mentioned in prophetic and poetic texts (Job.6.19; Isa. 21:14; Jer. 25:23).

Temah.name: Temah
Temah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Temah.briefDescription: Grandson of Esau, associated with an Edomite clan
Temah.shortDescription: Teman was a grandson of Esau and is associated with an important Edomite clan known for its wisdom.

Teman.name: Teman
Teman.briefDescription: Region and town in Edom, known for wisdom
Teman.shortDescription: Teman, a region and town in Edom, named after Esau's grandson, known for its wisdom (Jer. 49:7; Amo.1.12; Obad. 1:9).

Temeni.name: Temeni
Temeni.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Temeni.briefDescription: Abraham's father who settled in Haran
Temeni.shortDescription: Terah was the father of Abraham, Nahor, and Haran, who left Ur to settle in Haran.

Terah.name: Terah
Terah.briefDescription: Israelite campsite during the wilderness journey
Terah.shortDescription: Terah, a place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness journey (Num. 33:27-28).

Teresh.name: Teresh
Teresh.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Teresh.briefDescription: Paul's scribe who wrote the Epistle to the Romans
Teresh.shortDescription: Tertius served as Paul's scribe, writing down the Epistle to the Romans.

Tertius.name: Tertius
Tertius.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Tertius.briefDescription: Lawyer who accused Paul before Governor Felix
Tertius.shortDescription: Tertullus was a lawyer hired by the Jewish authorities to accuse Paul before the Roman governor Felix.

Tertullus.name: Tertullus
Tertullus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Tertullus.briefDescription: Recipient of Luke's Gospel and Acts
Tertullus.shortDescription: Theophilus was the recipient of Luke's Gospel and the Book of Acts, likely a Christian of high social standing.

Theophilus.name: Theophilus
Theophilus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Theophilus.briefDescription: Jewish rebel leader killed by Roman authorities
Theophilus.shortDescription: Theudas was a Jewish rebel leader who was killed, and his followers scattered, by the Roman authorities.

Theudas.name: Theudas
Theudas.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Theudas.briefDescription: One of Jesus' twelve disciples, known for doubting
Theudas.shortDescription: Thomas, also called Didymus, was one of Jesus' twelve disciples, known for doubting Jesus' resurrection until he saw him in person.

Thomas.name: Thomas
Thomas.description: Apostle living at the time of the New Testament
Thomas.briefDescription: Roman emperor during Jesus' ministry and death
Thomas.shortDescription: Tiberius was the Roman emperor during Jesus' ministry and crucifixion, mentioned in several Gospel accounts.

Tiberius.name: Tiberius
Tiberius.description: Emperor living at the time of the New Testament
Tiberius.briefDescription: Rival to Omri for the throne of Israel
Tiberius.shortDescription: Tibni was a rival to Omri for the throne of Israel, leading to a divided kingdom until his death.

Tibni.name: Tibni
Tibni.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Tibni.briefDescription: King of Goiim, ally of Chedorlaomer
Tibni.shortDescription: Tidal, king of Goiim, was one of the four kings allied with Chedorlaomer in the battle against the five kings of the plain.

Tidal.name: Tidal
Tidal.description: King living at the time of the Patriarchs
Tidal.briefDescription: Assyrian king who conquered parts of Israel
Tidal.shortDescription: Tiglath-pileser, also known as Pul, was an Assyrian king who conquered parts of Israel and received tribute from King Menahem.

Tiglath-pileser.name: Tiglath-pileser
Tiglath-pileser.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Tiglath-pileser.briefDescription: Father of Shallum, husband of Huldah the prophetess
Tiglath-pileser.shortDescription: Tikvah, also known as Tokhath, was the father of Shallum and the father-in-law of Huldah the prophetess.

Tikvah.name: Tikvah
Tikvah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Tikvah.briefDescription: Son of Shimon from the tribe of Judah
Tikvah.shortDescription: Tilon was a son of Shimon, a descendant of Judah.

Tilon.name: Tilon
Tilon.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Tilon.briefDescription: Father of Bartimaeus, a blind man healed by Jesus
Tilon.shortDescription: Timaeus was the father of Bartimaeus, a blind beggar who was healed by Jesus near Jericho.

Timaeus.name: Timaeus
Timaeus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Timaeus.briefDescription: Concubine of Eliphaz; mother of Amalek
Timaeus.shortDescription: Timna was a concubine of Eliphaz, son of Esau, and the mother of Amalek.

Timna.name: Timna
Timna.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Timna.briefDescription: One of the seven chosen to serve the church
Timna.shortDescription: Timon was one of the seven men chosen by the apostles to oversee the daily distribution of food to widows in the early church.

Timon.name: Timon
Timon.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Timon.shortDescription: Timothy was a young disciple and co-worker of Paul, who accompanied him on his missionary journeys and later served as a pastor.

Timothy.name: Timothy
Timothy.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Timothy.briefDescription: Son of Japheth, grandson of Noah
Timothy.shortDescription: Tiras was a son of Japheth and grandson of Noah, listed among the nations descended from Noah after the flood.

Tiras.name: Tiras
Tiras.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Tiras.briefDescription: King of Cush (Ethiopia) who opposed Sennacherib
Tiras.shortDescription: Tirhakah, the king of Cush (Ethiopia), opposed Sennacherib's invasion of Judah during the reign of King Hezekiah.

Tirhakah.name: Tirhakah
Tirhakah.description: King living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Tirhakah.briefDescription: Son of Caleb by his concubine Maacah
Tirhakah.shortDescription: Tirhanah was a son of Caleb by his concubine Maacah, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Tirhanah.name: Tirhanah
Tirhanah.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Tirhanah.briefDescription: Son of Jehallelel from the tribe of Judah
Tirhanah.shortDescription: Tiria was a son of Jehallelel, a descendant of Judah, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah.

Tiria.name: Tiria
Tiria.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Tiria.briefDescription: Daughter of Zelophehad, granted inheritance rights
Tiria.shortDescription: Tirzah was one of the five daughters of Zelophehad who were granted inheritance rights in the absence of male heirs.

Tirzah.name: Tirzah
Tirzah.briefDescription: Canaanite city that became the capital of Israel
Tirzah.shortDescription: Tirzah, a Canaanite city captured by Joshua, later served as the capital of the northern kingdom of Israel before Samaria (Josh. 12:24; 1 Kgs. 14:17; 15:21, 33; 16:6-23).

Titus.name: Titus
Titus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Titus.briefDescription: Clan from the tribe of Manasseh
Titus.shortDescription: The Tizites were a clan from the tribe of Manasseh, mentioned among the mighty warriors who joined David at Ziklag.

Tizites.name: Tizites
Tizites.briefDescription: Levite sent by Jehoshaphat to teach the Law
Tizites.shortDescription: Tobadonijah was a Levite sent by King Jehoshaphat to teach the Book of the Law in the cities of Judah.

Tobadonijah.name: Tobadonijah
Tobadonijah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Tobadonijah.briefDescription: Ancestor of returned exiles unable to prove lineage
Tobadonijah.shortDescription: Tobiah was an ancestor of some returned exiles who were unable to prove their Israelite lineage and were excluded from the priesthood.

Tobiah.name: Tobiah
Tobiah.description: Nehemiah's adversary living at the time of Exile and Return
Tobiah.briefDescription: Levite sent by Jehoshaphat to teach the Law
Tobiah.shortDescription: Tobijah was a Levite sent by King Jehoshaphat to teach the Book of the Law in the cities of Judah.

Tobijah.name: Tobijah
Tobijah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Tobijah.briefDescription: Son of Gomer, grandson of Japheth
Tobijah.shortDescription: Togarmah was a son of Gomer and grandson of Japheth, mentioned in the Table of Nations.

Togarmah.name: Togarmah
Togarmah.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Togarmah.shortDescription: Tohu, also known as Nahath or Toah, was an ancestor of the prophet Samuel, mentioned in his genealogy.

Tohu.name: Tohu
Tohu.description: Levite living at the time of United Monarchy
Tohu.briefDescription: King of Hamath who sent his son to David
Tohu.shortDescription: Toi, also known as Tou, was the king of Hamath who sent his son Joram to congratulate David on his victory over Hadadezer.

Toi.name: Toi
Toi.description: King living at the time of United Monarchy
Toi.briefDescription: Son of Issachar, ancestor of the Tolaites
Toi.shortDescription: Tola was a son of Issachar and the ancestor of the Tolaite clan.

Tola.name: Tola
Tola.briefDescription: Gentile Christian from Ephesus, companion of Paul
Tola.shortDescription: Trophimus was a Gentile Christian from Ephesus who accompanied Paul on his missionary journeys and was falsely accused of defiling the temple in Jerusalem.

Trophimus.name: Trophimus
Trophimus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Trophimus.briefDescription: Christian woman in Rome greeted by Paul
Trophimus.shortDescription: Tryphaena was a Christian woman in Rome to whom Paul sent greetings in his letter to the Romans.

Tryphaena.name: Tryphaena
Tryphaena.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Tryphaena.briefDescription: Christian woman in Rome greeted by Paul
Tryphaena.shortDescription: Tryphosa was a Christian woman in Rome to whom Paul sent greetings in his letter to the Romans.

Tryphosa.name: Tryphosa
Tryphosa.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
Tryphosa.briefDescription: Son of Japheth, grandson of Noah
Tryphosa.shortDescription: Tubal was a son of Japheth and grandson of Noah, mentioned in the Table of Nations.

Tubal.name: Tubal
Tubal.briefDescription: A nation, descendant of Japheth, known for trading
Tubal.shortDescription: Tubal, a nation descended from Japheth, known for trading in slaves and bronze, and allied with Gog in the prophecy of Ezekiel (Gen. 10:2; Isa. 66:19; Ezek. 27:13; 38:2-3; 39:1).

Tubal-cain.name: Tubal-cain
Tubal-cain.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Tubal-cain.briefDescription: Faithful companion and messenger of Paul
Tubal-cain.shortDescription: Tychicus was a faithful companion of Paul who served as his messenger to the churches in Asia Minor.

Tychicus.name: Tychicus
Tychicus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Tychicus.briefDescription: Ephesian teacher who hosted Paul's discussions
Tychicus.shortDescription: Tyrannus was a teacher in Ephesus who allowed Paul to use his lecture hall for daily discussions about the gospel.

Tyrannus.name: Tyrannus
Tyrannus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Tyrannus.briefDescription: Recipient of Agur's wisdom in Proverbs 30
Tyrannus.shortDescription: Ucal is mentioned in Proverbs 30:1 as one of the recipients of the wise sayings of Agur son of Jakeh.

Ucal.name: Ucal
Ucal.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Ucal.briefDescription: Israelite who agreed to put away his foreign wife
Ucal.shortDescription: Uel was one of the Israelites who agreed to put away his foreign wife during the time of Ezra's reforms.

Uel.name: Uel
Uel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Uel.briefDescription: Son of Sheresh, great-grandson of Manasseh
Uel.shortDescription: Ulam was a son of Sheresh and great-grandson of Manasseh, mentioned in the genealogy of the tribe of Manasseh.

Ulam.name: Ulam
Ulam.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ulam.shortDescription: Unni was a Levite musician who played the harp and participated in the procession when the ark was brought to Jerusalem during David's reign.

Unni.name: Unni
Unni.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Unni.briefDescription: Father of one of David's mighty men
Unni.shortDescription: Ur was the father of Eliphal, one of David's mighty men.

Ur.name: Ur
Ur.briefDescription: The hometown of Abraham in Mesopotamia
Ur.shortDescription: Ur of the Chaldeans, the hometown of Abraham in Mesopotamia, from where God called him to go to the land of Canaan (Gen. 11:28, 31; 15:7; Neh. 9:7).

Urbanus.name: Urbanus
Urbanus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Urbanus.briefDescription: Father of Geber, one of Solomon's officers
Urbanus.shortDescription: Uri was the father of Geber, one of the twelve officers appointed by Solomon to provide for the king and his household.

Uri.name: Uri
Uri.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Uri.briefDescription: Priest during the reign of King Ahaz
Uri.shortDescription: Uriah was a priest during the reign of King Ahaz who followed the king's instructions in building a new altar based on a pagan model from Damascus.

Uriah.name: Uriah
Uriah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Uriah.briefDescription: Kohathite leader who helped bring the ark to Jerusalem
Uriah.shortDescription: Uriel, a Kohathite leader, assisted in bringing the ark to Jerusalem.

Uriel.name: Uriel
Uriel.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Uriel.briefDescription: Judahite who resettled in Jerusalem after the exile
Uriel.shortDescription: Uthai, a descendant of Perez, resettled in Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Uthai.name: Uthai
Uthai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Uthai.briefDescription: Son of Aram, grandson of Shem
Uthai.shortDescription: Uz, son of Aram and grandson of Shem, was an early Semitic patriarch.

Uz.name: Uz
Uz.briefDescription: A region associated with Job, Edom, and Arabia
Uz.shortDescription: Uz, a region mentioned in the Bible as the homeland of Job, possibly located in Edom or Arabia (Job.1.1; Jer. 25:20; Lam. 4:21).

Uzai.name: Uzai
Uzai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Uzai.briefDescription: Son of Joktan, descendant of Shem
Uzai.shortDescription: Uzal, a descendant of Shem, was one of Joktan's sons.

Uzal.name: Uzal
Uzal.briefDescription: A place mentioned in Ezekiel's prophecy
Uzal.shortDescription: Uzal, a place mentioned in Ezekiel's prophecy, possibly referring to a region in Arabia known for its high-quality swords (Ezek. 27:19).

Uzza.name: Uzza
Uzza.briefDescription: A garden where Manasseh and Amon were buried
Uzza.shortDescription: Uzza, a garden in Jerusalem where Manasseh and his son Amon, kings of Judah, were buried (2 Kgs. 21:18, 26).

Uzzah.name: Uzzah
Uzzah.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Uzzah.briefDescription: High priest in the line of Eleazar
Uzzah.shortDescription: Uzzi was a high priest in the lineage of Eleazar, son of Aaron.

Uzzi.name: Uzzi
Uzzi.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Uzzi.briefDescription: Ashterathite, one of David's mighty warriors
Uzzi.shortDescription: Uzzia the Ashterathite was one of David's mighty warriors.

Uzzia.name: Uzzia
Uzzia.description: Warrior living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Uzzia.shortDescription: Uzziah was in charge of King David's storehouses.

Uzziah.name: Uzziah
Uzziah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Uzziah.briefDescription: Levite musician in the time of David
Uzziah.shortDescription: Uzziel (Azarel), a Levite musician, served during David's reign.

Uzziel.name: Uzziel
Uzziel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Uzziel.briefDescription: Son of Haman, enemy of the Jews
Uzziel.shortDescription: Vaizatha was one of the ten sons of Haman, the enemy of the Jews.

Vaizatha.name: Vaizatha
Vaizatha.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Vaizatha.briefDescription: Israelite who married a foreign wife
Vaizatha.shortDescription: Vaniah was an Israelite who had married a foreign wife during the Exile.

Vaniah.name: Vaniah
Vaniah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Vaniah.briefDescription: Queen of Persia, deposed by King Ahasuerus (Xerxes)
Vaniah.shortDescription: Vashti, queen of Persia, was deposed by King Ahasuerus for disobeying his command.

Vashti.name: Vashti
Vashti.description: Woman living at the time of Exile and Return
Vashti.briefDescription: Father of Nahbi, the Naphtalite spy
Vashti.shortDescription: Vophsi was the father of Nahbi, the man chosen to represent the tribe of Naphtali as a spy.

Vophsi.name: Vophsi
Vophsi.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Vophsi.shortDescription: The wife of Heli was sister of Elizather who mothered John the Baptist.

wife_of_Heli.name: wife_of_Heli
wife_of_Heli.description: Woman living at the time of the New Testament
wife_of_Heli.briefDescription: Horite chief, descendant of Seir the Horite
wife_of_Heli.shortDescription: Zaavan, a Horite chief, was a descendant of Seir the Horite.

Zaavan.name: Zaavan
Zaavan.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Zaavan.shortDescription: Zabad, the son of Ahlai, was one of David's mighty warriors.

Zabad.name: Zabad
Zabad.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Zabad.briefDescription: Israelite who married a foreign wife
Zabad.shortDescription: Zabbai, of the sons of Bebai, was an Israelite who had married a foreign wife during the Exile.

Zabbai.name: Zabbai
Zabbai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Zabbai.briefDescription: Overseer of King David's wine cellars
Zabbai.shortDescription: Zabdi the Shiphmite was in charge of King David's wine cellars.

Zabdi.name: Zabdi
Zabdi.briefDescription: Father of Jashobeam, commander of David's army
Zabdi.shortDescription: Zabdiel was the father of Jashobeam, a commander in King David's army.

Zabdiel.name: Zabdiel
Zabdiel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Zabdiel.briefDescription: Son of Nathan, priest and king's friend
Zabdiel.shortDescription: Zabud, son of Nathan, served as a priest and was a close friend of King Solomon.

Zabud.name: Zabud
Zabud.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Zabud.shortDescription: Zaccai was the ancestor of a family that returned to Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Zaccai.name: Zaccai
Zaccai.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Zaccai.briefDescription: Tax collector who hosted Jesus in Jericho
Zaccai.shortDescription: Zacchaeus, a wealthy tax collector, hosted Jesus in his home and experienced a transformative encounter.

Zacchaeus.name: Zacchaeus
Zacchaeus.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Zacchaeus.shortDescription: Zaccur was a Merarite Levite, one of the sons of Jaaziah.

Zaccur.name: Zaccur
Zaccur.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Zaccur.briefDescription: Postexilic high priest, descendant of Zadok
Zaccur.shortDescription: Zadok was a high priest who served in Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Zadok.name: Zadok
Zadok.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Zadok.shortDescription: Zaham was one of the sons born to King Rehoboam of Judah.

Zaham.name: Zaham
Zaham.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Zaham.briefDescription: Father of Hanun, who repaired Jerusalem's wall
Zaham.shortDescription: Zalaph was the father of Hanun, who repaired a section of Jerusalem's wall.

Zalaph.name: Zalaph
Zalaph.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Zalaph.shortDescription: Zalmon, also known as Ilai, was one of King David's mighty men, hailing from Ahlai.

Zalmon.name: Zalmon
Zalmon.description: Warrior living at the time of United Monarchy
Zalmon.shortDescription: Zalmunna was a Midianite king who was defeated and killed by Gideon during the time of the judges.

Zalmunna.name: Zalmunna
Zalmunna.shortDescription: Zattu was the ancestor of a family that returned to Jerusalem after the Babylonian exile.

Zattu.name: Zattu
Zattu.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Zattu.briefDescription: Son of Jonathan, descendant of Jerahmeel
Zattu.shortDescription: Zaza was a son of Jonathan and a descendant of Jerahmeel from the tribe of Judah.

Zaza.name: Zaza
Zaza.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Zaza.shortDescription: Zebadiah was a Gadite warrior who joined David's forces at Ziklag.

Zebadiah.name: Zebadiah
Zebadiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Zebadiah.shortDescription: Zebah was a Midianite king who, along with Zalmunna, was defeated and killed by Gideon during the time of the judges.

Zebah.name: Zebah
Zebah.briefDescription: Father of the apostles James and John
Zebah.shortDescription: Zebedee was the father of James and John, two of Jesus' twelve apostles, and was a fisherman by trade.

Zebedee.name: Zebedee
Zebedee.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Zebedee.briefDescription: Mother of King Jehoiakim of Judah
Zebedee.shortDescription: Zebidah, daughter of Pedaiah of Rumah, was the mother of Jehoiakim, king of Judah.

Zebidah.name: Zebidah
Zebidah.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Zebidah.briefDescription: Israelite who married a foreign woman
Zebidah.shortDescription: Zebina was one of the Israelites who had married foreign women during the time of Ezra.

Zebina.name: Zebina
Zebina.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Zebina.shortDescription: Zebul was the governor of Shechem who served under Abimelech, the son of Gideon, during his reign as king.

Zebul.name: Zebul
Zebul.briefDescription: Israelite tribe, son of Jacob and Leah
Zebul.shortDescription: Zebulun was the tenth son of Jacob, the sixth and last of Leah, and his descendants formed one of the twelve tribes of Israel.

Zebulun.name: Zebulun
Zebulun.description: Jacob's son living at the time of the Patriarchs
Zebulun.shortDescription: Zechariah, son of Isshiah, was a Levite mentioned in the genealogical records of the Levites during the reign of David.

Zechariah.name: Zechariah
Zechariah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Zechariah.briefDescription: Son of Abiel, founder of Gibeon
Zechariah.shortDescription: Zecher (or Zechariah), a son of Abiel (or 'Jeiel'), the father (and founder) of Gibeon.

Zecher.name: Zecher
Zecher.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Zecher.briefDescription: Son of Jeconiah, king of Judah
Zecher.shortDescription: Zedekiah, a son of Jeconiah (also known as Jehoiachin), king of Judah.

Zedekiah.name: Zedekiah
Zedekiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Zedekiah.shortDescription: Zeeb, a prince of Midian, was captured and killed by the Ephraimites during Gideon's victory over the Midianites.

Zeeb.name: Zeeb
Zeeb.briefDescription: Ammonite, one of David's mighty men
Zeeb.shortDescription: Zelek the Ammonite was one of David's mighty men.

Zelek.name: Zelek
Zelek.description: Warrior Ammonite living at the time of United Monarchy
Zelek.briefDescription: Israelite whose daughters received his inheritance
Zelek.shortDescription: Zelophehad, a descendant of Manasseh, had no sons, so his daughters appealed for and received his inheritance.

Zelophehad.name: Zelophehad
Zelophehad.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Zelophehad.shortDescription: The Zemarites were a Canaanite people group mentioned in the Table of Nations.

Zemarites.name: Zemarites
Zemarites.shortDescription: Zemirah was a son of Becher and grandson of Benjamin.

Zemirah.name: Zemirah
Zemirah.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Zemirah.shortDescription: Zenas, a lawyer, was commended by Paul and was to be sent on his journey with Apollos.

Zenas.name: Zenas
Zenas.description: Man living at the time of the New Testament
Zenas.briefDescription: Priest during the reign of Zedekiah
Zenas.shortDescription: Zephaniah, a priest during King Zedekiah's reign, was taken captive to Babylon.

Zephaniah.name: Zephaniah
Zephaniah.description: Prophet living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Zephaniah.briefDescription: Son of Eliphaz, grandson of Esau
Zephaniah.shortDescription: Zepho, a son of Eliphaz and grandson of Esau, was a chief of Edom.

Zepho.name: Zepho
Zepho.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Zepho.shortDescription: Zerah, a Levite of the Gershonite clan, was the son of Iddo.

Zerah.name: Zerah
Zerah.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Zerah.shortDescription: Zerahiah (or Ahitub), a priest, was an ancestor of Ezra the scribe.

Zerahiah.name: Zerahiah
Zerahiah.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Zerahiah.briefDescription: Wife of Haman, enemy of the Jews
Zerahiah.shortDescription: Zeresh, the wife of Haman, advised him to have Mordecai hanged on a gallows.

Zeresh.name: Zeresh
Zeresh.description: Woman living at the time of Exile and Return
Zeresh.briefDescription: Son of Ashhur, founder of Tekoa
Zeresh.shortDescription: Zereth, a son of Ashhur (or 'Ashur') and Helah, was a Judahite.

Zereth.name: Zereth
Zereth.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Zereth.briefDescription: Son of Jeduthun, a temple musician
Zereth.shortDescription: Zeri (or Izri), a son of Jeduthun, was a temple musician during the reign of David.

Zeri.name: Zeri
Zeri.description: Levite living at the time of United Monarchy
Zeri.shortDescription: Zeror, a Benjamite, was an ancestor of King Saul.

Zeror.name: Zeror
Zeror.description: Man living at the time of United Monarchy
Zeror.briefDescription: Mother of Jeroboam, first king of Israel
Zeror.shortDescription: Zeruah was the mother of Jeroboam, who became the first king of the northern kingdom of Israel.

Zeruah.name: Zeruah
Zeruah.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
Zeruah.briefDescription: Leader of the first Jewish exiles from Babylon
Zeruah.shortDescription: Zerubbabel (or Sheshbazzar), a descendant of David, led the first group of Jewish exiles back to Jerusalem from Babylon and oversaw the rebuilding of the temple.

Zerubbabel.name: Zerubbabel
Zerubbabel.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Zerubbabel.briefDescription: Sister of David; mother of Joab, Abishai, and Asahel
Zerubbabel.shortDescription: Zeruiah, David's sister, was the mother of Joab, Abishai, and Asahel, who were among David's mighty men and military leaders.

Zeruiah.name: Zeruiah
Zeruiah.description: Woman living at the time of United Monarchy
Zeruiah.briefDescription: Son of Laadan, a Gershonite Levite
Zeruiah.shortDescription: Zetham, a son of Laadan, was a Gershonite Levite during the time of David.

Zetham.name: Zetham
Zetham.description: Levite living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Zetham.shortDescription: Zethan was a son of Bilhan and a descendant of Benjamin.

Zethan.name: Zethan
Zethan.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Zethan.briefDescription: One of the seven eunuchs of King Ahasuerus
Zethan.shortDescription: Zethar was one of the seven eunuchs who served King Ahasuerus of Persia.

Zethar.name: Zethar
Zethar.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Zethar.briefDescription: Gadite clan leader during the reign of Jotham
Zethar.shortDescription: Zia was a Gadite clan leader during the reigns of Jotham, king of Judah, and Jeroboam, king of Israel.

Zia.name: Zia
Zia.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Zia.briefDescription: Servant of Saul's household, later served David
Zia.shortDescription: Ziba, a servant of Saul's household, became a servant of David and played a role in the conflict between David and Mephibosheth.

Ziba.name: Ziba
Ziba.description: David's compatriot living at the time of United Monarchy
Ziba.briefDescription: Hivite grandfather of Esau's wife Oholibamah
Ziba.shortDescription: Zibeon, the Hivite grandfather of Esau's wife Oholibamah.

Zibeon.name: Zibeon
Zibeon.description: Edomite living at the time of the Patriarchs
Zibeon.shortDescription: Zibia was a son of Shaharaim, a Benjamite, born to his wife Hodesh in the country of Moab.

Zibia.name: Zibia
Zibia.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Zibia.briefDescription: Mother of King Joash (or 'Jehoash') of Judah from Beersheba
Zibia.shortDescription: Zibiah, from Beersheba, was the mother of King Joash (or 'Jehoash') of Judah.

Zibiah.name: Zibiah
Zibiah.description: Woman living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Zibiah.shortDescription: Zichri, a Levite descendant of Moses, was a treasurer in the temple during the time of David.

Zichri.name: Zichri
Zichri.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Zichri.briefDescription: Family of temple servants who returned from exile
Zichri.shortDescription: Ziha was a family of temple servants (Nethinim) who returned to Jerusalem from Babylonian captivity.

Ziha.name: Ziha
Ziha.description: Man living at the time of Exile and Return
Ziha.briefDescription: Wife of Lamech; mother of Tubal-Cain and Naamah
Ziha.shortDescription: Zillah, one of Lamech's two wives, was the mother of Tubal-Cain, the first metalworker, and Naamah.

Zillah.name: Zillah
Zillah.description: Woman living at the time before the Flood
Zillah.briefDescription: Manassite leader who joined David at Ziklag
Zillah.shortDescription: Zillethai, a leader from the tribe of Manasseh, was one of the men who joined David at Ziklag.

Zillethai.name: Zillethai
Zillethai.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Zillethai.briefDescription: Leah's maidservant; mother of Gad and Asher
Zillethai.shortDescription: Zilpah, Leah's maidservant, bore Jacob two sons, Gad and Asher.

Zilpah.name: Zilpah
Zilpah.description: Woman living at the time of the Patriarchs
Zilpah.briefDescription: Levite, son of Shimei, descendant of Gershon
Zilpah.shortDescription: Zimmah, a Levite and descendant of Gershon, was the son of Shimei.

Zimmah.name: Zimmah
Zimmah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Zimmah.shortDescription: Zimran was one of the sons born to Abraham and his wife Keturah after the death of Sarah.

Zimran.name: Zimran
Zimran.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Zimran.shortDescription: Zimri, a Benjamite, was the son of Jehoadah and is mentioned in the genealogies of Benjamin.

Zimri.name: Zimri
Zimri.briefDescription: A nation mentioned in Jeremiah's prophecy against nations
Zimri.shortDescription: Zimri was a nation mentioned in Jeremiah's prophecy of God's judgment against various nations .

Ziph.name: Ziph
Ziph.briefDescription: A town in Judah where David hid from Saul
Ziph.shortDescription: Ziph was a town in the hill country of Judah where David hid from Saul in the nearby wilderness .

Ziphah.name: Ziphah
Ziphah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ziphah.briefDescription: Son of Gad, progenitor of the Zephonites
Ziphah.shortDescription: Ziphion (or Zephon), a son of Gad, was the ancestor of the Zephonite clan.

Ziphion.name: Ziphion
Ziphion.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Ziphion.briefDescription: Father of Balak, king of Moab
Ziphion.shortDescription: Zippor was the father of Balak, the king of Moab who hired Balaam to curse Israel.

Zippor.name: Zippor
Zippor.description: Moabite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Zippor.briefDescription: Wife of Moses, daughter of Jethro
Zippor.shortDescription: Zipporah, the daughter of Jethro, was Moses' wife and the mother of his two sons, Gershom and Eliezer.

Zipporah.name: Zipporah
Zipporah.description: Woman living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Zipporah.shortDescription: Ziza, a descendant of Simeon, was the son of Shiphi.

Ziza.name: Ziza
Ziza.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Ziza.shortDescription: Zizah (or Zina), a Gershonite Levite, was the second son of Shimei.

Zizah.name: Zizah
Zizah.description: Levite living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Zizah.shortDescription: Zobebah was one of the sons of Koz, a descendant of Judah.

Zobebah.name: Zobebah
Zobebah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Zobebah.shortDescription: Zohar was the father of Ephron the Hittite, from whom Abraham purchased the cave of Machpelah as a burial site.

Zohar.name: Zohar
Zohar.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Zohar.shortDescription: Zoheth was a son of Ishi and a descendant of Judah.

Zoheth.name: Zoheth
Zoheth.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Zoheth.shortDescription: Zophah was a son of Hotham (or 'Helem') and a descendant of Asher.

Zophah.name: Zophah
Zophah.description: Man living at the time of Divided Monarchy
Zophah.briefDescription: One of Job's three friends, the Naamathite
Zophah.shortDescription: Zophar the Naamathite was one of Job's three friends who came to comfort him during his affliction.

Zophar.name: Zophar
Zophar.description: Man living at the time of the Patriarchs
Zophar.shortDescription: The Zorathites were a Judahite clan descended from Shobal.

Zorathites.name: Zorathites
Zorathites.briefDescription: Father of Nethanel, leader of Issachar
Zorathites.shortDescription: Zuar was the father of Nethanel, a leader of the tribe of Issachar during the wilderness wanderings.

Zuar.name: Zuar
Zuar.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Zuar.briefDescription: Levite ancestor of Samuel the prophet
Zuar.shortDescription: Zuph, also called Zophai, was an Ephraimite Levite, an ancestor of Samuel the prophet.

Zuph.name: Zuph
Zuph.briefDescription: A land where Saul searched for his father's donkeys
Zuph.shortDescription: The land of Zuph was a region in Ephraim where Saul and his servant searched for his father's lost donkeys .

Zur.name: Zur
Zur.description: King living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Zur.shortDescription: Zuriel was the leader of the Merarite Levites during the wilderness wanderings.

Zuriel.name: Zuriel
Zuriel.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Zuriel.briefDescription: Father of Shelumiel, leader of Simeon
Zuriel.shortDescription: Zurishaddai was the father of Shelumiel, a leader of the tribe of Simeon during the wilderness wanderings.

Zurishaddai.name: Zurishaddai
Zurishaddai.description: Man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness
Zurishaddai.shortDescription: The Zuzim were an ancient people defeated by Chedorlaomer and his allies during Abraham's time.

Zuzim.name: Zuzim
Zuzim.shortDescription: The Zuzim were an ancient people defeated by Chedorlaomer and his allies during Abraham's time.

Abana.name: Abana
Abana.briefDescription: A river of Damascus, preferred by Naaman over the Jordan.
Abana.shortDescription: Abana, a river near Damascus, which Naaman saw as superior to the Jordan River.

Abel-beth-maacah.name: Abel-beth-maacah
Abel-beth-maacah.briefDescription: A city in northern Israel, also called Abel-maim.
Abel-beth-maacah.shortDescription: Abel-beth-maacah, a fortified city in northern Israel, near Dan.

Abel-keramim.name: Abel-keramim
Abel-keramim.briefDescription: A place near Ammon, attacked by Jephthah.
Abel-keramim.shortDescription: Abel-keramim, a town near Ammon, destroyed by Jephthah during his battles against the Ammonites.

Abel-meholah.name: Abel-meholah
Abel-meholah.briefDescription: A town in Israel, home of Elisha.
Abel-meholah.shortDescription: Abel-meholah, a town in Israel, the birthplace of Elisha.

Abilene.name: Abilene
Abilene.briefDescription: A region ruled by Lysanias when John began preaching.
Abilene.shortDescription: Abilene, a region north of Damascus, ruled by the tetrarch Lysanias.

Abronah.name: Abronah
Abronah.briefDescription: A desert campsite during Israel's wilderness wanderings.
Abronah.shortDescription: Abronah, an Israelite campsite between Jotbathah and Ezion-geber during the wilderness wanderings.

Accad.name: Accad
Accad.briefDescription: An ancient city in Nimrod's kingdom, probably Akkad.
Accad.shortDescription: Accad, one of the cities in Nimrod's kingdom, likely the Akkadian city of Akkad.

Acco.name: Acco
Acco.briefDescription: A seaport in Asher, also called Ptolemais.
Acco.shortDescription: Acco, a port city in Asher, later known as Ptolemais.

Achaia.name: Achaia
Achaia.briefDescription: A Roman province in southern Greece, including Corinth.
Achaia.shortDescription: Achaia, a Roman province covering much of Greece, with Corinth as its capital.

Achor_Valley.name: Achor_Valley
Achor_Valley.briefDescription: A valley near Jericho, where Achan was stoned.
Achor_Valley.shortDescription: The Valley of Achor, where Achan was punished for his sin.

Achshaph.name: Achshaph
Achshaph.briefDescription: A Canaanite city-state conquered by Joshua.
Achshaph.shortDescription: Achshaph, a Canaanite city defeated by Joshua and allotted to Asher.

Achzib.name: Achzib
Achzib.briefDescription: A coastal town allotted to Asher.
Achzib.shortDescription: Achzib, a coastal city assigned to Asher but not conquered.

Adadah.name: Adadah
Adadah.briefDescription: A town in the Negev region of Judah.
Adadah.shortDescription: Adadah, a town in the southernmost part of Judah.

Adamah.name: Adamah
Adamah.briefDescription: A fortified city allotted to Naphtali.
Adamah.shortDescription: Adamah, a fortified city in the territory of Naphtali.

Adami-nekeb.name: Adami-nekeb
Adami-nekeb.briefDescription: A boundary marker for the territory of Naphtali.
Adami-nekeb.shortDescription: Adami-nekeb, a place marking the border of Naphtali's territory.

Addan.name: Addan
Addan.briefDescription: A place from which some returned exiles came.
Addan.shortDescription: Addan (Addon), a place from which some Jewish exiles returned to Judah.

Adithaim.name: Adithaim
Adithaim.briefDescription: A town in the Shephelah region of Judah.
Adithaim.shortDescription: Adithaim, a town allotted to Judah in the Shephelah.

Admah.name: Admah
Admah.briefDescription: One of the cities destroyed with Sodom and Gomorrah.
Admah.shortDescription: Admah, one of the "cities of the plain" destroyed along with Sodom and Gomorrah.

Adoraim.name: Adoraim
Adoraim.briefDescription: A fortified city built by Rehoboam in Judah.
Adoraim.shortDescription: Adoraim, one of the cities fortified by King Rehoboam in Judah.

Adramyttium.name: Adramyttium
Adramyttium.briefDescription: A seaport in Asia Minor, visited by Paul.
Adramyttium.shortDescription: Adramyttium, a port city in Asia Minor, where Paul boarded a ship.

Adriatic_Sea.name: Adriatic_Sea
Adriatic_Sea.briefDescription: The sea where Paul's ship drifted during a storm.
Adriatic_Sea.shortDescription: The Adriatic Sea, where Paul's ship was driven by a storm on his journey to Rome.

Adullam.name: Adullam
Adullam.briefDescription: A Canaanite city, later fortified by Judah.
Adullam.shortDescription: Adullam, a city in Judah, known for its cave where David hid.

Adummim.name: Adummim
Adummim.briefDescription: A pass on the border between Judah and Benjamin.
Adummim.shortDescription: Adummim, a mountain pass on the boundary between Judah and Benjamin.

Aenon.name: Aenon
Aenon.briefDescription: A place near Salim where John the Baptist baptized.
Aenon.shortDescription: Aenon, a site near Salim where John the Baptist conducted baptisms.

Ahava.name: Ahava
Ahava.briefDescription: A place where Ezra assembled the returning exiles.
Ahava.shortDescription: Ahava, a location near Babylon where Ezra gathered the exiles before their return to Jerusalem.

Ahlab.name: Ahlab
Ahlab.briefDescription: A Canaanite city allotted to Asher.
Ahlab.shortDescription: Ahlab (Mahalab), a city in Asher's territory, not conquered by the Israelites.

Ai.name: Ai
Ai.briefDescription: An Ammonite city, to be destroyed by Babylon.
Ai.shortDescription: Ai, an Ammonite city, mentioned in a prophecy against the Ammonites.

Aijalon.name: Aijalon
Aijalon.briefDescription: A Levitical city in Dan, site of Joshua's miracle.
Aijalon.shortDescription: Aijalon, a city allotted to Dan and the Levites, known for Joshua's miracle.

Ain.name: Ain
Ain.briefDescription: A place on the eastern boundary of the Promised Land.
Ain.shortDescription: Ain or Rimmon, a location marking the eastern border of Israel's territory.

Akeldama.name: Akeldama
Akeldama.briefDescription: Field purchased with Judas' betrayal money; burial place for strangers
Akeldama.shortDescription: Akeldama, also known as the Field of Blood or Potter's Field, was purchased with the money Judas received for betraying Jesus and used as a burial place for foreigners.

Akrabbim.name: Akrabbim
Akrabbim.briefDescription: A pass marking the southern border of Judah
Akrabbim.shortDescription: Akrabbim, a pass located in the Negev, marked the southern border of the tribe of Judah.

Alexandria.name: Alexandria
Alexandria.shortDescription: Alexandria, a significant port city in Egypt, played a role in Paul's journey to Rome.

Allammelech.name: Allammelech
Allammelech.shortDescription: Allammelech, a town allotted to the tribe of Asher.

Allon-bacuth.name: Allon-bacuth
Allon-bacuth.briefDescription: The burial place of Deborah, Rebekah's nurse.
Allon-bacuth.shortDescription: Allon-bacuth, meaning "oak of weeping," where Rebekah's nurse Deborah was buried.

Alush.name: Alush
Alush.briefDescription: A campsite during Israel's wilderness journey.
Alush.shortDescription: Alush, an Israelite encampment between Dophkah and Rephidim during the Exodus.

Amad.name: Amad
Amad.briefDescription: A border town in the territory of Asher.
Amad.shortDescription: Amad, a town on the boundary of Asher's territory.

Amam.name: Amam
Amam.briefDescription: A town allotted to the tribe of Judah.
Amam.shortDescription: Amam, a town in the Negev region of Judah.

Amana.name: Amana
Amana.briefDescription: A mountain range mentioned in the Song of Solomon.
Amana.shortDescription: Amana, a mountain range featured in a poetic description of the beloved's beauty.

Amaw.name: Amaw
Amaw.briefDescription: A place near the Euphrates River, associated with Balaam.
Amaw.shortDescription: Amaw, a place near the Euphrates, where Balaam son of Beor lived.

Ammah.name: Ammah
Ammah.briefDescription: A hill near Gibeon, where Joab pursued Abner.
Ammah.shortDescription: Ammah, a hill near Giah on the way to the wilderness of Gibeon.

Ammon.name: Ammon
Ammon.briefDescription: An ancient nation and its capital city, enemies of Israel.
Ammon.shortDescription: Ammon, a nation descended from Lot, and its capital Rabbah, often in conflict with Israel.

Amphipolis.name: Amphipolis
Amphipolis.briefDescription: A city visited by Paul on his missionary journey.
Amphipolis.shortDescription: Amphipolis, a city in Macedonia, passed through by Paul and Silas.

Anab.name: Anab
Anab.briefDescription: A city in the hill country of Judah.
Anab.shortDescription: Anab, a town in Judah's hill country, captured by Joshua.

Anaharath.name: Anaharath
Anaharath.briefDescription: A border town in the territory of Issachar.
Anaharath.shortDescription: Anaharath, a town on the border of Issachar's territory.

Angle.name: Angle
Angle.briefDescription: A tower built by King Uzziah in Jerusalem.
Angle.shortDescription: The Angle, a fortified tower constructed by Uzziah in Jerusalem.

Anim.name: Anim
Anim.briefDescription: A city in the hill country of Judah.
Anim.shortDescription: Anim, a town allotted to Judah in the hill country.

Antioch.name: Antioch
Antioch.briefDescription: A major city where the gospel spread to Gentiles.
Antioch.shortDescription: Antioch, a city in Syria where the gospel first reached Gentiles and the believers were first called Christians.

Antipatris.name: Antipatris
Antipatris.briefDescription: A city where Paul was taken during his transfer to Caesarea.
Antipatris.shortDescription: Antipatris, a city where Paul was brought by soldiers during his journey to Caesarea.

Aphek.name: Aphek
Aphek.briefDescription: A city in the territory of Asher.
Aphek.shortDescription: Aphek, a city allotted to the tribe of Asher.

Apollonia.name: Apollonia
Apollonia.briefDescription: A city in Macedonia visited by Paul.
Apollonia.shortDescription: Apollonia, a city in Macedonia through which Paul passed on his way to Thessalonica.

Ar.name: Ar
Ar.shortDescription: Ar, the capital of Moab, mentioned in the context of Israel's journey and prophecies.

Arab.name: Arab
Arab.briefDescription: A city allotted to the tribe of Judah.
Arab.shortDescription: Arab, a town in the hill country of Judah.

Arabah.name: Arabah
Arabah.briefDescription: A desert region in the Jordan Valley and southward.
Arabah.shortDescription: The Arabah, a desert plain extending from the Sea of Galilee to the Gulf of Aqaba.

Arabia.name: Arabia
Arabia.briefDescription: A region and its inhabitants, mentioned in various contexts.
Arabia.shortDescription: Arabia, a desert region southeast of Israel, known for trade and nomadic tribes.

Aram-maacah.name: Aram-maacah
Aram-maacah.shortDescription: Aram-maacah was an Aramean kingdom that allied with the Ammonites against David.

Aram-naharaim.name: Aram-naharaim
Aram-naharaim.briefDescription: A region mentioned in the title of Psalm 60.
Aram-naharaim.shortDescription: Aram-naharaim, a region David fought against, as referenced in Psalm 60's title.

Aram-zobah.name: Aram-zobah
Aram-zobah.briefDescription: A region mentioned in the title of Psalm 60.
Aram-zobah.shortDescription: Aram-zobah, a region David fought against, as referenced in Psalm 60's title.

Ararat.name: Ararat
Ararat.briefDescription: A mountain range where Noah's ark rested after the flood.
Ararat.shortDescription: Ararat, the mountain range where Noah's ark came to rest after the flood.

Areopagus.name: Areopagus
Areopagus.briefDescription: A hill in Athens where Paul preached the gospel.
Areopagus.shortDescription: The Areopagus, a hill in Athens where Paul addressed the philosophers and preached about the unknown God.

Arnon.name: Arnon
Arnon.briefDescription: A river and valley, serving as a boundary for Israel.
Arnon.shortDescription: The Arnon, a river and valley that marked the boundary between Moab and the Amorites, later serving as Israel's southern border.

Aroer.name: Aroer
Aroer.briefDescription: A city in Gad, built by the Gadites.
Aroer.shortDescription: Aroer, a city in the territory of Gad, built by the Gadites.

Arpad.name: Arpad
Arpad.shortDescription: Arpad was a city in northern Syria conquered by the Assyrians.

Arubboth.name: Arubboth
Arubboth.briefDescription: A district in Israel under Solomon's rule.
Arubboth.shortDescription: Arubboth, a district governed by one of Solomon's twelve officers.

Arumah.name: Arumah
Arumah.briefDescription: A city associated with Abimelech and King Jehoiakim's mother.
Arumah.shortDescription: Arumah or Rumah, a city mentioned in connection with Abimelech's reign and as the hometown of King Jehoiakim's mother.

Arvad.name: Arvad
Arvad.briefDescription: An island city, known for its skilled mariners and soldiers.
Arvad.shortDescription: Arvad, an island city whose inhabitants served as sailors and soldiers for Tyre.

Ashan.name: Ashan
Ashan.briefDescription: A city allotted to Judah and Simeon.
Ashan.shortDescription: Ashan, a city assigned to the tribes of Judah and Simeon, later given to the Levites.

Ashdod.name: Ashdod
Ashdod.briefDescription: A major Philistine city, known for Dagon worship.
Ashdod.shortDescription: Ashdod or Azotus, one of the five major Philistine cities, was a center of Dagon worship and a target of prophetic judgment.

Ashkelon.name: Ashkelon
Ashkelon.briefDescription: A major Philistine city, often a target of prophecy.
Ashkelon.shortDescription: Ashkelon, one of the five principal Philistine cities, was captured by Judah but later became a symbol of judgment.

Ashnah.name: Ashnah
Ashnah.briefDescription: Two cities in the Shephelah region of Judah.
Ashnah.shortDescription: Ashnah, two cities in the lowlands of Judah, mentioned in the tribal allotment.

Ashtaroth.name: Ashtaroth
Ashtaroth.description: A female deity in the time of the Old Testament
Ashtaroth.briefDescription: A Canaanite goddess of fertility and war
Ashtaroth.shortDescription: Ashtaroth was a Canaanite goddess of fertility and war whose worship was condemned in the Bible.

Ashteroth-karnaim.name: Ashteroth-karnaim
Ashteroth-karnaim.shortDescription: Ashteroth-karnaim was a city east of the Jordan River defeated by King Chedorlaomer.

Asia.name: Asia
Asia.briefDescription: A Roman province in western Asia Minor.
Asia.shortDescription: Asia, a Roman province in western Asia Minor, with Ephesus as its capital.

Assos.name: Assos
Assos.briefDescription: A seaport in Mysia, visited by Paul.
Assos.shortDescription: Assos, a seaport in Mysia where Paul met his companions during his third missionary journey.

Assyria.name: Assyria
Assyria.briefDescription: A powerful ancient empire, often in conflict with Israel.
Assyria.shortDescription: Assyria, a major Mesopotamian empire, frequently mentioned in the Old Testament as an adversary of Israel and Judah.

Atad.name: Atad
Atad.description: Atad between Jericho and the Jordan(?)
Atad.briefDescription: Threshing floor where Egyptians mourned Jacob
Atad.shortDescription: Atad was a threshing floor near the Jordan River where the Egyptians mourned Jacob's death.

Ataroth.name: Ataroth
Ataroth.briefDescription: Town in Gilead allotted to Gad
Ataroth.shortDescription: Ataroth was a town in the region of Gilead allotted to the tribe of Gad.

Ataroth-addar.name: Ataroth-addar
Ataroth-addar.briefDescription: A town on the border of Ephraim and Benjamin.
Ataroth-addar.shortDescription: Ataroth-addar, a border town between the territories of Ephraim and Benjamin.

Athach.name: Athach
Athach.briefDescription: A town that received spoils from David's victory.
Athach.shortDescription: Athach, a town in Judah that received a share of the spoils from David's victory over the Amalekites.

Atharim.name: Atharim
Atharim.briefDescription: A place where the Canaanite king of Arad fought Israel.
Atharim.shortDescription: Atharim, a place near the Negev where the Canaanite king of Arad attacked the Israelites during their journey.

Athens.name: Athens
Athens.briefDescription: A major city in Greece, visited by Paul.
Athens.shortDescription: Athens, the intellectual and cultural center of ancient Greece, where Paul preached the gospel and addressed the Areopagus.

Atroth-beth-joab.name: Atroth-beth-joab
Atroth-beth-joab.briefDescription: Town associated with Joab from the tribe of Judah.
Atroth-beth-joab.shortDescription: Atroth-beth-joab was a town linked to Joab in Judah. (1Ch.2.54)

Atroth-shophan.name: Atroth-shophan
Atroth-shophan.briefDescription: Town allotted to the tribe of Gad.
Atroth-shophan.shortDescription: Atroth-shophan was a town given to the tribe of Gad. (Numbers 32:35)

Attalia.name: Attalia
Attalia.briefDescription: Seaport in Pamphylia visited by Paul and Barnabas.
Attalia.shortDescription: Attalia, a seaport in Pamphylia, was visited by Paul and Barnabas. (Act.14.25)

Aven_Valley.name: Aven_Valley
Aven_Valley.briefDescription: Plain near Damascus, prophesied to be destroyed.
Aven_Valley.shortDescription: Aven Valley, a plain near Damascus, was prophesied to be destroyed. (Amo.1.5)

Avith.name: Avith
Avith.briefDescription: Capital city of Edomite king Hadad.
Avith.shortDescription: Avith was the capital city of Hadad, an early Edomite king.

Azal.name: Azal
Azal.briefDescription: Unidentified place near Jerusalem mentioned in Zechariah's prophecy.
Azal.shortDescription: Azal was an unidentified place near Jerusalem mentioned in Zechariah's prophecy. (Zechariah 14:5)

Azekah.name: Azekah
Azekah.briefDescription: Judean town, site of battles and sieges.
Azekah.shortDescription: Azekah, a Judean town, was the site of several battles and sieges.

Azmon.name: Azmon
Azmon.briefDescription: Place on the southern border of Judah.
Azmon.shortDescription: Azmon was a place on the southern border of Judah.

Aznoth-tabor.name: Aznoth-tabor
Aznoth-tabor.briefDescription: Border town of Naphtali near Mount Tabor.
Aznoth-tabor.shortDescription: Aznoth-tabor was a border town of Naphtali near Mount Tabor. (Joshua 19:34)

Baalah.name: Baalah
Baalah.briefDescription: Town allotted to the tribe of Simeon.
Baalah.shortDescription: Baalah was a town allotted to the tribe of Simeon. (Joshua 15:29)

Baalah_Mount.name: Baalah_Mount
Baalah_Mount.briefDescription: Mountain marking the northern boundary of Judah.
Baalah_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Baalah marked the northern boundary of Judah's territory. (Joshua 15:11)

Baalath.name: Baalath
Baalath.briefDescription: Town allotted to the tribe of Dan.
Baalath.shortDescription: Baalath was a town allotted to the tribe of Dan. (Joshua 19:44)

Baalath-beer.name: Baalath-beer
Baalath-beer.briefDescription: Town in the Negev, allotted to Simeon.
Baalath-beer.shortDescription: Baalath-beer (Bealoth, Baal, or Ramoth) was a town in the Negev, allotted to Simeon.

Baal-hazor.name: Baal-hazor
Baal-hazor.briefDescription: Place where Absalom sheared his sheep.
Baal-hazor.shortDescription: Baal-hazor was the place where Absalom sheared his sheep. (2Sa.13.23)

Baal-meon.name: Baal-meon
Baal-meon.briefDescription: Moabite town, allotted to Reuben, later retaken by Moab.
Baal-meon.shortDescription: Baal-meon (Beth-baal-meon, Beth-meon, or Beon) was a Moabite town, allotted to Reuben, but later retaken by Moab.

Baal-perazim.name: Baal-perazim
Baal-perazim.briefDescription: Place where David defeated the Philistines.
Baal-perazim.shortDescription: Baal-perazim was the place where David defeated the Philistines. (2Sa.5.20; 1Ch.14.11)

Baal-shalishah.name: Baal-shalishah
Baal-shalishah.briefDescription: Place where a man brought provisions to Elisha.
Baal-shalishah.shortDescription: Baal-shalishah was the place where a man brought provisions to Elisha. (2Ki.4.42)

Baal-tamar.name: Baal-tamar
Baal-tamar.briefDescription: Place near Gibeah where Israelites fought the Benjamites.
Baal-tamar.shortDescription: Baal-tamar was a place near Gibeah where the Israelites fought against the Benjamites. (Judges 20:33)

Baal-zephon.name: Baal-zephon
Baal-zephon.briefDescription: Egyptian location where the Israelites camped before crossing the Red Sea.
Baal-zephon.shortDescription: Baal-zephon was an Egyptian location where the Israelites camped before crossing the Red Sea.

Babylon.name: Babylon
Babylon.briefDescription: Ancient Mesopotamian city, capital of Babylonian Empire, site of the Tower of Babel.
Babylon.shortDescription: Babylon, also known as Babel, Sheshak, Merathaim, or Pekod, was an ancient Mesopotamian city that served as the capital of the Babylonian Empire. (Multiple references, see article for details)

Bahurim.name: Bahurim
Bahurim.briefDescription: Village near Jerusalem, home of Shimei, who cursed David.
Bahurim.shortDescription: Bahurim (or Baharum) was a village near Jerusalem, known as the home of Shimei, who cursed David. (2Sa.3.16; 16:5; 17:18; 19:16; 1Ki.2.8; 2Sa.23.31; 1Ch.11.33)

Bajith.name: Bajith

father.name: Word sometimes translated as a place
father.raw: Word sometimes translated as a place

Bajith.briefDescription: Moabite temple or high place mentioned in Isaiah's prophecy.
Bajith.shortDescription: Bajith (or "temple") was a Moabite temple or high place mentioned in Isaiah's prophecy against Moab. (Isaiah 15:2)

relations.father: Word sometimes translated as a place

Balah.name: Balah
Balah.briefDescription: Town allotted to the tribe of Simeon.
Balah.shortDescription: Balah was a town allotted to the tribe of Simeon. (Joshua 19:3)

Bamoth.name: Bamoth
Bamoth.briefDescription: Israelite encampment in Moab during the Exodus.
Bamoth.shortDescription: Bamoth was an Israelite encampment in Moab during the Exodus. (Numbers 21:19-20)

Bamoth-baal.name: Bamoth-baal
Bamoth-baal.briefDescription: Moabite high place where Balak took Balaam.
Bamoth-baal.shortDescription: Bamoth-baal was a Moabite high place where Balak took Balaam to curse Israel.

Bashan.name: Bashan
Bashan.briefDescription: Region east of the Jordan, known for its fertility.
Bashan.shortDescription: Bashan was a region east of the Jordan River, known for its fertility and allotted to the half-tribe of Manasseh. (Multiple references, see article for details)

Bath-rabbim.name: Bath-rabbim
Bath-rabbim.briefDescription: Gate in Heshbon, mentioned in Song of Solomon.
Bath-rabbim.shortDescription: Bath-rabbim was a gate in Heshbon, mentioned in the Song of Solomon. (Sng.7.4)

Beautiful_Gate.name: Beautiful_Gate
Beautiful_Gate.briefDescription: Gate of the Jerusalem temple, site of Peter's miracle.
Beautiful_Gate.shortDescription: The Beautiful Gate was a gate of the Jerusalem temple, where Peter healed a lame man. (Act.3.2, 10)

Beer.name: Beer
Beer.briefDescription: Site where God provided water for the Israelites.
Beer.shortDescription: Beer was a site where God provided water for the Israelites during their wilderness journey. (Numbers 21:16)

Beer-elim.name: Beer-elim
Beer-elim.briefDescription: Place mentioned in Isaiah's prophecy against Moab.
Beer-elim.shortDescription: Beer-elim was a place mentioned in Isaiah's prophecy against Moab. (Isaiah 15:8)

Beer-lahai-roi.name: Beer-lahai-roi
Beer-lahai-roi.briefDescription: Well where Hagar encountered the Angel of the Lord.
Beer-lahai-roi.shortDescription: Beer-lahai-roi was a well where Hagar encountered the Angel of the Lord, between Kadesh and Bered.

Beeroth.name: Beeroth
Beeroth.briefDescription: Hivite city, later allotted to Benjamin.
Beeroth.shortDescription: Beeroth (or Beer) was a Hivite city that later became part of the territory allotted to the tribe of Benjamin.

Beersheba.name: Beersheba
Beersheba.briefDescription: Important city in southern Israel, associated with the patriarchs.
Beersheba.shortDescription: Beersheba (Sheba or Shibah) was an important city in southern Israel, associated with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. (Multiple references, see article for details)

Bene-berak.name: Bene-berak
Bene-berak.briefDescription: Town allotted to the tribe of Dan.
Bene-berak.shortDescription: Bene-berak was a town allotted to the tribe of Dan. (Joshua 19:45)

Bene-jaakan.name: Bene-jaakan
Bene-jaakan.briefDescription: Israelite encampment during the wilderness wanderings.
Bene-jaakan.shortDescription: Bene-jaakan (Beeroth Bene-jaakan) was an Israelite encampment during the wilderness wanderings.

Benjamin_Gate.name: Benjamin_Gate
Benjamin_Gate.briefDescription: Gate in Jerusalem's wall, mentioned in Zechariah's prophecy.
Benjamin_Gate.shortDescription: The Benjamin Gate was a gate in Jerusalem's wall, mentioned in Zechariah's prophecy about the city's restoration. (Zechariah 14:10)

Beracah_Valley.name: Beracah_Valley
Beracah_Valley.briefDescription: Valley where Jehoshaphat and his army blessed the Lord.
Beracah_Valley.shortDescription: The Beracah Valley was where Jehoshaphat and his army blessed the Lord after a miraculous victory. (2Ch.20.26)

Berea.name: Berea
Berea.briefDescription: City in Macedonia where Paul preached; home of Sopater.
Berea.shortDescription: Berea was a city in Macedonia where Paul preached, and the home of Sopater, one of Paul's traveling companions.

Berothah.name: Berothah
Berothah.briefDescription: City mentioned in Ezekiel's vision and David's conquests.
Berothah.shortDescription: Berothah (Berothai or Cun) was a city mentioned in Ezekiel's vision of Israel's boundaries and in the account of David's conquests.

Besor.name: Besor
Besor.briefDescription: Brook where David's men pursued the Amalekites.
Besor.shortDescription: The Besor was a brook where David's men pursued the Amalekites who had raided Ziklag. (1Sa.30.9, 10, 21)

Betah.name: Betah
Betah.briefDescription: City from which David took bronze after defeating Hadadezer.
Betah.shortDescription: Betah was a city from which David took a large amount of bronze after defeating Hadadezer, king of Zobah. (2Sa.8.8)

Beten.name: Beten
Beten.briefDescription: Town allotted to the tribe of Asher.
Beten.shortDescription: Beten was a town allotted to the tribe of Asher in the division of the Promised Land. (Joshua 19:25)

Beth-anath.name: Beth-anath
Beth-anath.briefDescription: Canaanite town allotted to Naphtali, but not fully conquered.
Beth-anath.shortDescription: Beth-anath was a Canaanite town allotted to the tribe of Naphtali, but the Naphtalites did not drive out its inhabitants.

Beth-anoth.name: Beth-anoth
Beth-anoth.briefDescription: Town in the hill country of Judah.
Beth-anoth.shortDescription: Beth-anoth was a town in the hill country of Judah, mentioned in the list of cities and villages within Judah's inheritance. (Joshua 15:59)

Bethany.name: Bethany
Bethany.briefDescription: Village near Jerusalem, home of Mary, Martha, and Lazarus.
Bethany.shortDescription: Bethany was a village near Jerusalem, known as the home of Mary, Martha, and Lazarus, where Jesus often visited and performed miracles. (Multiple references, see article for details)

Beth-arabah.name: Beth-arabah
Beth-arabah.briefDescription: Town and wilderness area in Judah, also associated with Benjamin.
Beth-arabah.shortDescription: Beth-arabah was a town and wilderness area allotted to Judah and Benjamin, and the Arbathite was a descendant of Abi-albon.

Beth-arbel.name: Beth-arbel
Beth-arbel.briefDescription: Place destroyed by Shalman, mentioned in Hosea's prophecy.
Beth-arbel.shortDescription: Beth-arbel was a place destroyed by Shalman, mentioned in Hosea's prophecy as an example of destruction. (Hosea 10:14)

Beth-ashbea.name: Beth-ashbea
Beth-ashbea.briefDescription: Place associated with the families of the house of Ashbea.
Beth-ashbea.shortDescription: Beth-ashbea was a place associated with the families of the house of Ashbea, who were known for their fine linen work. (1Ch.4.21)

Beth-Ashtoreth.name: Beth-Ashtoreth
Beth-Ashtoreth.description: Temple of the female deity Ashoreth (H6252G) in the time of the Old Testament
Beth-Ashtoreth.briefDescription: Philistine city where Saul's armor was put in a temple.
Beth-Ashtoreth.shortDescription: Beth-Ashtoreth was a Philistine city where Saul's armor was put in the temple of Ashtoreth after his death. (1Sa.31.10)

Beth-aven.name: Beth-aven
Beth-aven.briefDescription: Town and wilderness near Ai and Bethel.
Beth-aven.shortDescription: Beth-aven was a town and wilderness near Ai and Bethel, associated with idolatry and Israel's defeat.

Beth-azmaveth.name: Beth-azmaveth
Beth-azmaveth.briefDescription: Town of Benjamin, home to some who returned from exile.
Beth-azmaveth.shortDescription: Beth-azmaveth (Azmaveth) was a town in Benjamin, home to some who returned from the Babylonian exile.

Beth-barah.name: Beth-barah
Beth-barah.briefDescription: Place where Gideon stationed men to intercept the Midianites.
Beth-barah.shortDescription: Beth-barah (Bethabara) was a place where Gideon stationed men to intercept the fleeing Midianites, and where John the Baptist baptized.

Beth-biri.name: Beth-biri
Beth-biri.briefDescription: Town in Simeon, formerly called Bethul.
Beth-biri.shortDescription: Beth-biri was a town allotted to the tribe of Simeon, formerly known as Bethul. (1Ch.4.31)

Beth-car.name: Beth-car
Beth-car.briefDescription: Place where the Israelites pursued the Philistines.
Beth-car.shortDescription: Beth-car was the place where the Israelites pursued the Philistines after the return of the Ark of the Covenant. (1Sa.7.11)

Beth-dagon.name: Beth-dagon
Beth-dagon.briefDescription: Town in Asher, named after the Philistine god Dagon.
Beth-dagon.shortDescription: Beth-dagon was a town in the territory of Asher, likely named after the Philistine god Dagon. (Joshua 19:27)

Beth-eden.name: Beth-eden
Beth-eden.briefDescription: Place associated with the rulers of Aram (Syria).
Beth-eden.shortDescription: Beth-eden was a place associated with the rulers of Aram (Syria), mentioned in Amos's prophecy against Damascus. (Amo.1.5)

Beth-eked.name: Beth-eked
Beth-eked.briefDescription: Place where Jehu killed the brothers of Ahaziah.
Beth-eked.shortDescription: Beth-eked was a place where Jehu killed the forty-two brothers of Ahaziah, king of Judah. (2Ki.10.12, 14)

Bethel.name: Bethel
Bethel.description: Bethel_2= same as Bethul (Bethul@Jos.19.4=H1329) or Bethuel Bethuel@1Ch.4.30=H1328B)
Bethel.briefDescription: Town in Judah that received spoils from David's victory.
Bethel.shortDescription: Bethel was a town in Judah that received spoils from David's victory over the Amalekites. (1Sa.30.27)

Beth-emek.name: Beth-emek
Beth-emek.briefDescription: Town in Asher's territory, near the border.
Beth-emek.shortDescription: Beth-emek was a town in the territory of Asher, mentioned in the description of Asher's border. (Joshua 19:27)

Bether_Mountain.name: Bether_Mountain

father.name: Word sometimes translated as a place
father.raw: Word sometimes translated as a place

Bether_Mountain.briefDescription: Mountain or mountains mentioned in Song of Solomon.
Bether_Mountain.shortDescription: Bether is a mountain or mountains mentioned in the Song of Solomon, possibly symbolizing separation between lovers. (Sng.2.17)

relations.father: Word sometimes translated as a place

Bethesda.name: Bethesda
Bethesda.briefDescription: Pool in Jerusalem where Jesus healed a paralyzed man.
Bethesda.shortDescription: Bethesda (Bethzatha) was a pool in Jerusalem with five roofed colonnades, where Jesus healed a man who had been an invalid for thirty-eight years. (Jhn.5.2)

Beth-ezel.name: Beth-ezel
Beth-ezel.briefDescription: Town mentioned in Micah's prophecy, possibly near Jerusalem.
Beth-ezel.shortDescription: Beth-ezel was a town mentioned in Micah's prophecy, where the inhabitants could not offer support or refuge during a time of judgment. (Micah 1:11)

Beth-gader.name: Beth-gader
Beth-gader.briefDescription: Town associated with the descendants of Hareph.
Beth-gader.shortDescription: Beth-gader was a town associated with the descendants of Hareph, mentioned in the genealogy of Judah. (1Ch.2.51)

Beth-gamul.name: Beth-gamul
Beth-gamul.briefDescription: Moabite town mentioned in Jeremiah's prophecy against Moab.
Beth-gamul.shortDescription: Beth-gamul was a Moabite town mentioned in Jeremiah's prophecy against Moab, likely destroyed or captured by the Babylonians. (Jeremiah 48:23)

Beth-haccherem.name: Beth-haccherem
