Beth-haccherem.briefDescription: Town in Judah, possibly fortified, mentioned by Jeremiah.
Beth-haccherem.shortDescription: Beth-haccherem was a town in Judah, possibly fortified, mentioned by Jeremiah as a place to raise a signal fire.

Beth-haggan.name: Beth-haggan
Beth-haggan.briefDescription: Place near Jezreel where Ahaziah fled from Jehu.
Beth-haggan.shortDescription: Beth-haggan was a place near Jezreel where Ahaziah, king of Judah, fled from Jehu before being killed. (2Ki.9.27)

Beth-haram.name: Beth-haram
Beth-haram.briefDescription: Town in Gad, east of the Jordan, fortified by Gad.
Beth-haram.shortDescription: Beth-haram (Beth-haran) was a town allotted to the tribe of Gad, east of the Jordan River, which they fortified.

Beth-hoglah.name: Beth-hoglah
Beth-hoglah.briefDescription: Town on the border between Judah and Benjamin.
Beth-hoglah.shortDescription: Beth-hoglah was a town on the border between Judah and Benjamin, near the northern end of the Dead Sea. (Joshua 15:6; 18:19, 21)

Beth-horon_Lower.name: Beth-horon_Lower
Beth-horon_Lower.briefDescription: Town in Ephraim and Dan, site of a famous battle.
Beth-horon_Lower.shortDescription: Lower Beth-horon was a town allotted to Ephraim and Dan, known for a battle where the sun stood still. (Multiple references, see article for details)

Beth-horon_Upper.name: Beth-horon_Upper
Beth-horon_Upper.briefDescription: Town in Ephraim, near Lower Beth-horon.
Beth-horon_Upper.shortDescription: Upper Beth-horon was a town in Ephraim, near Lower Beth-horon, fortified by Solomon.

Beth-jeshimoth.name: Beth-jeshimoth
Beth-jeshimoth.briefDescription: Town in Moab, allotted to Reuben, mentioned in prophecy.
Beth-jeshimoth.shortDescription: Beth-jeshimoth was a town in Moab, allotted to Reuben, near where the Israelites camped and mentioned in a prophecy against Moab.

Beth-lebaoth.name: Beth-lebaoth
Beth-lebaoth.briefDescription: Town in Simeon, also called Lebaoth.
Beth-lebaoth.shortDescription: Beth-lebaoth was a town allotted to the tribe of Simeon, also known as Lebaoth. (Joshua 19:6)

Bethlehem.name: Bethlehem
Bethlehem.briefDescription: Town in Zebulun, distinct from Bethlehem in Judah.
Bethlehem.shortDescription: Bethlehem was a town allotted to the tribe of Zebulun, distinct from the more well-known Bethlehem in Judah. (Joshua 19:15)

Beth-merchak.name: Beth-merchak
Beth-merchak.briefDescription: Place near Jerusalem where David stayed during Absalom's revolt.
Beth-merchak.shortDescription: Beth-merchak was a place near Jerusalem where David stayed with his household during Absalom's revolt. (2Sa.15.17)

Beth-millo.name: Beth-millo
Beth-millo.briefDescription: Fortified place in Shechem, associated with Abimelech's rule.
Beth-millo.shortDescription: Beth-millo was a fortified place in Shechem where the men of the city made Abimelech king, later becoming his enemies. (Judges 9:6, 20)

Beth-nimrah.name: Beth-nimrah
Beth-nimrah.briefDescription: Town in Gad, east of the Jordan River.
Beth-nimrah.shortDescription: Beth-nimrah (Nimrah) was a town allotted to the tribe of Gad, east of the Jordan River, which they built or fortified.

Beth-pazzez.name: Beth-pazzez
Beth-pazzez.shortDescription: Beth-pazzez, a town within the territory allotted to the tribe of Issachar.

Beth-pelet.name: Beth-pelet
Beth-pelet.briefDescription: Town in southern Judah, resettled after the Babylonian exile.
Beth-pelet.shortDescription: Beth-pelet was a town in southern Judah, allotted to Judah and later resettled by the people of Judah after the Babylonian exile.

Beth-peor.name: Beth-peor
Beth-peor.briefDescription: Place east of the Jordan, where Moses was buried.
Beth-peor.shortDescription: Beth-peor was a place east of the Jordan River, where the Israelites camped and Moses was buried in an unknown valley.

Bethphage.name: Bethphage
Bethphage.briefDescription: Village near Bethany, where Jesus began His triumphal entry.
Bethphage.shortDescription: Bethphage was a village near Bethany, where Jesus sent His disciples to find a donkey and a colt for His triumphal entry into Jerusalem.

Beth-rehob.name: Beth-rehob
Beth-rehob.briefDescription: Aramean city and region, allied with Ammon against David.
Beth-rehob.shortDescription: Beth-rehob (Rehob) was an Aramean city and region, allied with the Ammonites against David, located near the northern border of Israel.

Bethsaida.name: Bethsaida
Bethsaida.briefDescription: Town where Jesus performed miracles, home of several disciples
Bethsaida.shortDescription: Bethsaida was a fishing village on the Sea of Galilee, notable as the hometown of several of Jesus' disciples and a place where He performed miracles.

Beth-shean.name: Beth-shean
Beth-shean.briefDescription: City in Manasseh, where Saul's body was displayed.
Beth-shean.shortDescription: Beth-shean (Beth-shan) was a city allotted to Manasseh, where the Philistines fastened Saul's body to the wall after his death. (Multiple references, see article for details)

Beth-shemesh.name: Beth-shemesh
Beth-shemesh.briefDescription: Fortified city in Naphtali, not conquered by the tribe.
Beth-shemesh.shortDescription: Beth-shemesh was a fortified city in the territory of Naphtali, from which the Naphtalites did not drive out the Canaanites.

Beth-shittah.name: Beth-shittah
Beth-shittah.briefDescription: Beth-shittah, place near where Midianites fled from Gideon
Beth-shittah.shortDescription: Beth-shittah, a location near where the Midianites fled after being defeated by Gideon's army.

Beth-tappuah.name: Beth-tappuah
Beth-tappuah.briefDescription: Town in the hill country of Judah.
Beth-tappuah.shortDescription: Beth-tappuah was a town allotted to Judah, located in the hill country. (Joshua 15:53)

Beth-togarmah.name: Beth-togarmah
Beth-togarmah.briefDescription: Region or people group trading with Tyre, allied with Gog.
Beth-togarmah.shortDescription: Beth-togarmah was a region or people group that traded horses and mules with Tyre and was allied with Gog, a future enemy of Israel.

Beth-zur.name: Beth-zur
Beth-zur.briefDescription: Town in Judah, fortified by Rehoboam, repaired by Nehemiah.
Beth-zur.shortDescription: Beth-zur was a town in the hill country of Judah, fortified by Rehoboam, later repaired by Nehemiah, and associated with Maon.

Betonim.name: Betonim
Betonim.shortDescription: Betonim was a town allotted to the tribe of Gad in the region of Gilead.

Bezek.name: Bezek
Bezek.shortDescription: Bezek was a city where the tribe of Judah defeated the Canaanites and Perizzites.

Bithron.name: Bithron

father.name: Word sometimes translated as a place
father.raw: Word sometimes translated as a place

Bithron.briefDescription: A ravine or region crossed by Abner's men
Bithron.shortDescription: Bithron, a ravine or region traversed by Abner and his men after a battle with Joab's forces.

relations.father: Word sometimes translated as a place

Bithynia.name: Bithynia
Bithynia.shortDescription: Bithynia, a region in Asia Minor, where Paul attempted to go but was prevented by the Holy Spirit.

Biziothiah.name: Biziothiah
Biziothiah.briefDescription: A town in the Negev, allotted to Judah
Biziothiah.shortDescription: Biziothiah, a town in the Negev region, was allotted to the tribe of Judah.

Bor-ashan.name: Bor-ashan
Bor-ashan.shortDescription: Bor-ashan, a town in southern Judah, was one of the places where David sent spoils from his victory over the Amalekites.

Bozez.name: Bozez
Bozez.shortDescription: Bozez, one of the rocky crags that Jonathan and his armor-bearer crossed to attack the Philistine garrison.

Bozkath.name: Bozkath
Bozkath.briefDescription: Town in Judah, birthplace of Josiah's mother
Bozkath.shortDescription: Bozkath was a town in Judah and the birthplace of King Josiah's mother, Jedidah.

Bozrah.name: Bozrah
Bozrah.shortDescription: Bozrah was an important city in Edom, often mentioned in prophecies of judgment.

Broad_Wall.name: Broad_Wall
Broad_Wall.shortDescription: The Broad Wall was a section of Jerusalem's wall rebuilt during Nehemiah's time.

Brook_of_Egypt.name: Brook_of_Egypt
Brook_of_Egypt.briefDescription: Southwestern boundary of the Promised Land
Brook_of_Egypt.shortDescription: The Brook of Egypt (Shihor) marked the southwestern boundary of the Promised Land.

Brook_of_the_Arabah.name: Brook_of_the_Arabah
Brook_of_the_Arabah.description: Brook of the Arabah= near Hamath (Hamath@Num.13.21-Zec=H2574G)
Brook_of_the_Arabah.briefDescription: Eastern boundary of Jeroboam II's kingdom
Brook_of_the_Arabah.shortDescription: The Brook of the Arabah was the eastern boundary of King Jeroboam II's kingdom.

Brook_of_the_Willows.name: Brook_of_the_Willows
Brook_of_the_Willows.description: Brook of the Willows= in the Jordan Valley
Brook_of_the_Willows.shortDescription: The Brook of the Willows was a boundary of Moab's territory, possibly near the Wadi Zered.

Cabbon.name: Cabbon
Cabbon.shortDescription: Cabbon was a town in the lowlands of Judah.

Cabul.name: Cabul
Cabul.shortDescription: Cabul was a border town in the territory of Asher.

Caesarea.name: Caesarea
Caesarea.briefDescription: Coastal city, center of Roman rule in Judea
Caesarea.shortDescription: Caesarea was a coastal city in Judea that served as a center of Roman administration.

Caesarea_Philippi.name: Caesarea_Philippi
Caesarea_Philippi.briefDescription: City where Peter confessed Jesus as Messiah
Caesarea_Philippi.shortDescription: Caesarea Philippi was the location where Peter acknowledged Jesus as the Messiah and Son of God.

Calah.name: Calah
Calah.briefDescription: Ancient Assyrian city built by Nimrod
Calah.shortDescription: Calah was an ancient Assyrian city built by Nimrod, along with Nineveh and Resen.

Caleb_Ephrathah.name: Caleb_Ephrathah
Caleb_Ephrathah.shortDescription: Caleb Ephrathah was the place where Hezron, the son of Perez, died.

Calneh.name: Calneh
Calneh.briefDescription: Ancient Mesopotamian city, possibly Kullania or Kullanhu
Calneh.shortDescription: Calneh (Calno) was an ancient city in Mesopotamia, mentioned as one of Nimrod's cities and later as a trading partner of Tyre.

Capernaum.name: Capernaum
Capernaum.briefDescription: Town in Galilee; center of Jesus' ministry
Capernaum.shortDescription: Capernaum was a fishing village on the Sea of Galilee that served as a center for Jesus' ministry in the region.

Caphtor.name: Caphtor
Caphtor.briefDescription: Island home of the Philistines; visited by Paul
Caphtor.shortDescription: Caphtor, identified as the island of Crete, was the original homeland of the Philistines and a place visited by the apostle Paul.

Cappadocia.name: Cappadocia
Cappadocia.briefDescription: Region in Asia Minor; home to early Christian believers
Cappadocia.shortDescription: Cappadocia was a region in Asia Minor where some early Christian believers lived.

Carchemish.name: Carchemish
Carchemish.briefDescription: Ancient city; site of Babylonian victory over Egypt
Carchemish.shortDescription: Carchemish was an ancient city on the Euphrates River where the Babylonians decisively defeated the Egyptians.

Carmel.name: Carmel
Carmel.briefDescription: Town in Judah; home of Nabal and Abigail
Carmel.shortDescription: Carmel was a town in Judah, known as the home of Nabal and his wife Abigail, who later married David.

Carmel_Mount.name: Carmel_Mount
Carmel_Mount.briefDescription: Mountain range; site of Elijah's confrontation with prophets of Baal
Carmel_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Carmel was a mountain range in northern Israel where the prophet Elijah confronted the prophets of Baal.

Casiphia.name: Casiphia
Casiphia.briefDescription: Place where Levites were recruited for temple service
Casiphia.shortDescription: Casiphia was a place in Babylon where Ezra sent for Levites to join the returning exiles in Jerusalem.

Cauda.name: Cauda
Cauda.briefDescription: Island where Paul's ship sought shelter
Cauda.shortDescription: Cauda was an island where the ship carrying Paul sought shelter during a storm on his journey to Rome.

Cenchreae.name: Cenchreae
Cenchreae.briefDescription: Port of Corinth; home of Phoebe
Cenchreae.shortDescription: Cenchreae was the eastern port of Corinth, where Paul had his hair cut in connection with a vow, and was the home of Phoebe, a deaconess of the church.

Chaldea.name: Chaldea
Chaldea.briefDescription: Ancient Mesopotamian empire; known for astrology and wisdom
Chaldea.shortDescription: Chaldea was an ancient Mesopotamian empire, known for its astrologers and wise men, that played a significant role in the history of Israel, particularly during the Babylonian exile.

Chebar.name: Chebar
Chebar.briefDescription: River in Babylonia; location of Ezekiel's visions
Chebar.shortDescription: The Chebar River was a waterway in Babylonia near which the prophet Ezekiel received visions from God during the Babylonian exile.

Chephar-ammoni.name: Chephar-ammoni
Chephar-ammoni.shortDescription: Chephar-ammoni was a town allotted to the tribe of Benjamin.

Chephirah.name: Chephirah
Chephirah.briefDescription: Gibeonite town; later part of Benjamin and Judah
Chephirah.shortDescription: Chephirah was one of the Gibeonite towns that became part of the territories of Benjamin and Judah.

Cherith.name: Cherith
Cherith.briefDescription: Brook where Elijah hid during drought
Cherith.shortDescription: The Brook Cherith was where the prophet Elijah hid and was fed by ravens during a drought in Israel.

Cherub.name: Cherub
Cherub.briefDescription: Babylonian location; home of some returning exiles
Cherub.shortDescription: Cherub was a place in Babylonia from which some Jewish exiles returned to Jerusalem after the Babylonian captivity.

Chesalon.name: Chesalon
Chesalon.shortDescription: Chesalon, also known as Mount Jearim, was a landmark on the northern boundary of Judah, near Bethshemesh.

Chesil.name: Chesil
Chesil.shortDescription: Chesil was a town allotted to the tribe of Judah in the southern part of their territory.

Chesulloth.name: Chesulloth
Chesulloth.briefDescription: Border town of Issachar and Zebulun
Chesulloth.shortDescription: Chesulloth, also known as Chisloth-tabor or Tabor, was a town on the border between Issachar and Zebulun.

Chilmad.name: Chilmad
Chilmad.shortDescription: Chilmad was a place mentioned by Ezekiel as one of Tyre's trading partners.

Chinnereth.name: Chinnereth
Chinnereth.briefDescription: Freshwater lake in northern Israel; location of Jesus' ministry
Chinnereth.shortDescription: Chinnereth, also known as the Sea of Galilee or the Sea of Tiberias, is a freshwater lake in northern Israel where Jesus conducted much of his ministry.

Chinneroth.name: Chinneroth
Chinneroth.briefDescription: Region and city near the Sea of Galilee
Chinneroth.shortDescription: Chinneroth, also known as Chinnereth, was a region and a city near the Sea of Galilee, which was also called the Sea of Chinnereth.

Chios.name: Chios
Chios.briefDescription: Island visited by Paul on his journey
Chios.shortDescription: Chios was an island in the Aegean Sea where Paul's ship stopped during his journey to Jerusalem.

Chitlish.name: Chitlish
Chitlish.shortDescription: Chitlish was a town allotted to the tribe of Judah in the Shephelah region.

Chorazin.name: Chorazin
Chorazin.briefDescription: Town in Galilee; rebuked by Jesus for unbelief
Chorazin.shortDescription: Chorazin was a town in Galilee that Jesus rebuked for its lack of repentance despite the mighty works he had done there.

Cilicia.name: Cilicia
Cilicia.briefDescription: Province in Asia Minor; Paul's birthplace and ministry area
Cilicia.shortDescription: Cilicia, a province in southeast Asia Minor, was the birthplace of Paul and an area where he ministered to the early churches.

City_of_Salt.name: City_of_Salt
City_of_Salt.briefDescription: Town in the wilderness of Judah
City_of_Salt.shortDescription: The City of Salt was a town located in the wilderness of Judah, near the Dead Sea.

Cnidus.name: Cnidus
Cnidus.briefDescription: City on the coast of Asia Minor
Cnidus.shortDescription: Cnidus was a city on the southwest coast of Asia Minor, where Paul's ship stopped during his journey to Rome.

Colossae.name: Colossae
Colossae.briefDescription: City in Phrygia; recipient of Paul's epistle
Colossae.shortDescription: Colossae was a city in the region of Phrygia in Asia Minor, to which Paul addressed one of his epistles.

Corban.name: Corban
Corban.briefDescription: Offering dedicated to God; temple treasury
Corban.shortDescription: Corban refers to an offering dedicated to God, which the Pharisees used to avoid supporting their parents, and also to the temple treasury.

Corinth.name: Corinth
Corinth.briefDescription: Ancient Greek city; recipient of Paul's epistles
Corinth.shortDescription: Corinth, an important city in ancient Greece, was the location of a Christian church to which Paul wrote two epistles.

Corner_Gate.name: Corner_Gate
Corner_Gate.shortDescription: The Corner Gate was a fortified gate in Jerusalem's wall, mentioned in connection with repairs and measurements of the city.

Cos.name: Cos
Cos.briefDescription: Island visited by Paul on his journey
Cos.shortDescription: Cos was an island in the Aegean Sea where Paul's ship stopped during his return journey to Jerusalem.

Cushan.name: Cushan
Cushan.briefDescription: Unknown location, possibly related to Cush
Cushan.shortDescription: Cushan, a location mentioned in Habakkuk 3:7, is possibly related to Cush or another unidentified place.

Cuthah.name: Cuthah
Cuthah.briefDescription: Babylonian city; origin of Samaritan settlers
Cuthah.shortDescription: Cuthah was a Babylonian city from which the Assyrian king brought people to settle in Samaria after the deportation of the Israelites.

Cyprus.name: Cyprus
Cyprus.briefDescription: Island in the Mediterranean Sea; early Christian community
Cyprus.shortDescription: Cyprus, also referred to as Kittim and Elishah, was an island in the Mediterranean Sea that played a significant role in the spread of early Christianity.

Cyrene.name: Cyrene
Cyrene.briefDescription: City in North Africa; home of early Christians
Cyrene.shortDescription: Cyrene, a city in North Africa, was the hometown of Simon, who carried Jesus' cross, and several early Christians mentioned in Acts.

Dabbesheth.name: Dabbesheth
Dabbesheth.shortDescription: Dabbesheth was a town on the border of the territory allocated to the tribe of Zebulun.

Daberath.name: Daberath
Daberath.shortDescription: Daberath was a Levitical city located in the territory of Issachar, later given to the Levites of the Gershonite clan.

Dalmatia.name: Dalmatia
Dalmatia.briefDescription: Region on the eastern coast of the Adriatic Sea.
Dalmatia.shortDescription: Dalmatia, a region in Illyricum where Titus traveled (2Ti.4.10).

Damascus.name: Damascus
Damascus.briefDescription: Ancient city, capital of Syria, important in biblical history.
Damascus.shortDescription: Damascus, an ancient city in Syria, played a significant role in biblical history.

Dannah.name: Dannah
Dannah.briefDescription: A city in the hill country of Judah.
Dannah.shortDescription: Dannah, a city located in the hill country of Judah (Joshua 15:49).

Decapolis.name: Decapolis
Decapolis.briefDescription: A group of ten Hellenistic cities in ancient Palestine.
Decapolis.shortDescription: The Decapolis was a group of ten Hellenistic cities located in ancient Palestine, mentioned in the New Testament.

Derbe.name: Derbe
Derbe.briefDescription: City in Lycaonia visited by Paul on missionary journeys.
Derbe.shortDescription: Derbe, a city in Lycaonia, was visited by Paul during his missionary journeys.

Diblathaim.name: Diblathaim
Diblathaim.briefDescription: A place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness wanderings.
Diblathaim.shortDescription: Diblathaim, also known as Almon-diblathaim or Beth-diblathaim, was a place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness wanderings.

Dibon.name: Dibon
Dibon.briefDescription: Moabite city, allotted to Gad, later possessed by Moab.
Dibon.shortDescription: Dibon, also known as Dibon-gad, was a Moabite city allotted to the tribe of Gad, later repossessed by Moab.

Dilean.name: Dilean
Dilean.briefDescription: A city in the lowlands of Judah.
Dilean.shortDescription: Dilean, a city located in the lowlands of Judah (Joshua 15:38).

Dimnah.name: Dimnah
Dimnah.briefDescription: A Levitical city in the territory of Zebulun.
Dimnah.shortDescription: Dimnah, also known as Rimmon or Rimmono, was a Levitical city in the territory of Zebulun.

Dimonah.name: Dimonah
Dimonah.briefDescription: A city in the Negev region of Judah.
Dimonah.shortDescription: Dimonah, a city located in the Negev region of Judah (Joshua 15:22).

Dinhabah.name: Dinhabah
Dinhabah.briefDescription: The capital city of Edom during Bela's reign.
Dinhabah.shortDescription: Dinhabah, the capital city of Edom during the reign of King Bela (Genesis 36:32; 1Ch.1.43).

Diviners'_Oak.name: Diviners'_Oak
Diviners'_Oak.briefDescription: A tree near Shechem, associated with pagan divination practices.
Diviners'_Oak.shortDescription: The Diviners' Oak was a tree near Shechem, likely associated with pagan divination practices (Judges 9:37).

Dizahab.name: Dizahab
Dizahab.briefDescription: A place near where Moses delivered his final speeches.
Dizahab.shortDescription: Dizahab, a place near where Moses delivered his final speeches to the Israelites (Deu.1.1).

Dophkah.name: Dophkah
Dophkah.briefDescription: A place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness wanderings.
Dophkah.shortDescription: Dophkah, a place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness wanderings after leaving Egypt (Numbers 33:12-13).

Dor.name: Dor
Dor.briefDescription: A Canaanite city, later allotted to Manasseh but not conquered.
Dor.shortDescription: Dor, also known as Naphath-dor, was a Canaanite city allotted to Manasseh but not fully conquered (Joshua 12:23; 17:11; Judges 1:27).

Dothan.name: Dothan
Dothan.briefDescription: A place where Joseph found his brothers and was sold.
Dothan.shortDescription: Dothan, a place where Joseph found his brothers and was later sold into slavery (Genesis 37:17).

Dragon_Spring.name: Dragon_Spring
Dragon_Spring.briefDescription: A water source near Jerusalem, mentioned in Nehemiah's wall inspection.
Dragon_Spring.shortDescription: The Dragon Spring was a water source near Jerusalem, mentioned during Nehemiah's inspection of the city walls (Nehemiah 2:13).

Dung_Gate.name: Dung_Gate
Dung_Gate.briefDescription: A gate in Jerusalem's wall, possibly used for waste removal.
Dung_Gate.shortDescription: The Dung Gate was one of the gates in Jerusalem's wall, possibly used for removing waste from the city (Nehemiah 2:13; 3:13-14; 12:31).

Dura.name: Dura
Dura.briefDescription: A plain in Babylon where Nebuchadnezzar set up a statue.
Dura.shortDescription: Dura, a plain in the province of Babylon where King Nebuchadnezzar set up a golden statue (Dan.3.1).

East.name: East
East.briefDescription: A direction associated with invading armies and nomadic tribes.
East.shortDescription: In the Bible, the East is often associated with invading armies and nomadic tribes (Judges 6:3, 33; 7:12; 8:10; Ezk.25.4, 10).

East_Gate.name: East_Gate
East_Gate.briefDescription: A gate in Jerusalem's wall, near the house of Shemaiah.
East_Gate.shortDescription: The East Gate was a gate in Jerusalem's wall, located near the house of Shemaiah (Nehemiah 3:29).

Ebal_Mount.name: Ebal_Mount
Ebal_Mount.briefDescription: A mountain in Canaan, site of curses and blessings ceremony.
Ebal_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Ebal, also known as Mount Zalmon or Mount Ephraim, was a mountain in Canaan where the Israelites held a ceremony of curses and blessings (Deu.11.29; 27:4, 13; Joshua 8:30, 33; Judges 9:48; Psalm 68:14; Jeremiah 4:15).

Ebenezer.name: Ebenezer
Ebenezer.briefDescription: A stone memorial set up by Samuel to commemorate victory.
Ebenezer.shortDescription: Ebenezer was a stone memorial set up by Samuel to commemorate Israel's victory over the Philistines (1Sa.4.1; 5:1; 7:12).

Ebez.name: Ebez
Ebez.briefDescription: A town allotted to the tribe of Issachar.
Ebez.shortDescription: Ebez was a town allotted to the tribe of Issachar (Joshua 19:20).

Ecbatana.name: Ecbatana
Ecbatana.briefDescription: A city in Media where a significant scroll was found.
Ecbatana.shortDescription: Ecbatana was a city in Media where a scroll was found containing a decree by King Cyrus (Ezra 6:2).

Edom.name: Edom
Edom.briefDescription: Descendants of Esau; enemy nation southeast of Israel
Edom.shortDescription: Edom, a nation descended from Esau that was often in conflict with Israel.

Edrei.name: Edrei
Edrei.briefDescription: Amorite city defeated by Moses and the Israelites
Edrei.shortDescription: Edrei, an Amorite city east of the Jordan defeated by the Israelites under Moses.

Eglaim.name: Eglaim
Eglaim.briefDescription: Moabite city; its destruction prophesied by Isaiah
Eglaim.shortDescription: Eglaim, a Moabite city whose destruction was prophesied by Isaiah.

Eglath-shelishiyah.name: Eglath-shelishiyah
Eglath-shelishiyah.briefDescription: Moabite city; its destruction prophesied by Isaiah and Jeremiah
Eglath-shelishiyah.shortDescription: Eglath-shelishiyah, a Moabite city whose destruction was prophesied by Isaiah and Jeremiah.

Egypt_Sea.name: Egypt_Sea
Egypt_Sea.briefDescription: Likely the Gulf of Suez, part of the Red Sea
Egypt_Sea.shortDescription: The Egypt Sea, probably referring to the Gulf of Suez, an arm of the Red Sea.

Ekron.name: Ekron
Ekron.briefDescription: One of the five major Philistine city-states
Ekron.shortDescription: Ekron, one of the five principal Philistine cities, located in southwestern Canaan.

Elah_Valley.name: Elah_Valley
Elah_Valley.briefDescription: Site of David's victory over Goliath
Elah_Valley.shortDescription: The Elah Valley, where David defeated the Philistine giant Goliath.

Elath.name: Elath
Elath.briefDescription: Edomite port on the Red Sea; fortified by Judah
Elath.shortDescription: Elath, an Edomite port on the Red Sea, was fortified by Judah during the reigns of Solomon and Uzziah.

Elealeh.name: Elealeh
Elealeh.briefDescription: Moabite city assigned to Reuben; later prophecies of destruction
Elealeh.shortDescription: Elealeh, a Moabite city assigned to the tribe of Reuben, later featured in prophecies of Moab's destruction.

Elim.name: Elim
Elim.briefDescription: Desert oasis on Israel's journey from Egypt to Sinai
Elim.shortDescription: Elim, a desert oasis where the Israelites camped during their journey from Egypt to Mount Sinai.

Elkosh.name: Elkosh
Elkosh.shortDescription: Elkosh, the hometown of the prophet Nahum, probably located in Judah.

Ellasar.name: Ellasar
Ellasar.briefDescription: Mesopotamian city-state; allied with Elam against Sodom and Gomorrah
Ellasar.shortDescription: Ellasar, a Mesopotamian city-state whose king Arioch joined Chedorlaomer of Elam in battle against Sodom and Gomorrah.

Elonbeth-hanan.name: Elonbeth-hanan
Elonbeth-hanan.briefDescription: City under the jurisdiction of Solomon's governor Ben-Deker
Elonbeth-hanan.shortDescription: Elonbeth-hanan, a city under the jurisdiction of Ben-Deker, one of Solomon's twelve governors.

El-paran.name: El-paran
El-paran.briefDescription: Wilderness area where the Horites were defeated by Chedorlaomer
El-paran.shortDescription: El-paran, a wilderness region where Chedorlaomer and his allies defeated the Horites during their campaign.

Eltekeh.name: Eltekeh
Eltekeh.briefDescription: City in Dan's territory; later given to the Levites
Eltekeh.shortDescription: Eltekeh, a city in Dan's territory that was later given to the Levites.

Eltekon.name: Eltekon
Eltekon.briefDescription: City in the hill country of Judah
Eltekon.shortDescription: Eltekon, a city in the hill country of Judah, mentioned in the list of cities allotted to Judah.

Eltolad.name: Eltolad
Eltolad.briefDescription: A town allotted to Judah and Simeon
Eltolad.shortDescription: Eltolad, a town allotted to the tribes of Judah and Simeon in the Negev region.

Emek-keziz.name: Emek-keziz
Emek-keziz.shortDescription: Emek-keziz, a town within the territory allotted to the tribe of Benjamin.

Emmaus.name: Emmaus
Emmaus.briefDescription: Emmaus, village where Jesus appeared to two disciples after resurrection
Emmaus.shortDescription: Emmaus, a village approximately seven miles from Jerusalem, where Jesus appeared to two disciples after His resurrection.

Enaim.name: Enaim
Enaim.briefDescription: Enaim, place where Tamar posed as a prostitute
Enaim.shortDescription: Enaim, the place where Tamar sat and posed as a prostitute to deceive Judah.

Enam.name: Enam
Enam.briefDescription: Enam, town allotted to Judah in the Shephelah
Enam.shortDescription: Enam, a town in the Shephelah region, allotted to the tribe of Judah.

En-dor.name: En-dor
En-dor.briefDescription: Canaanite city; site of Saul's consultation with medium
En-dor.shortDescription: En-dor, a Canaanite city where Saul consulted a medium before his final battle with the Philistines.

Eneglaim.name: Eneglaim
Eneglaim.briefDescription: Place mentioned in Ezekiel's vision of restored Israel
Eneglaim.shortDescription: Eneglaim, a place mentioned in Ezekiel's vision of the restored land of Israel.

En-gannim.name: En-gannim
En-gannim.shortDescription: En-gannim (or Anem), a Levitical city in the territory of Issachar, given to the Gershonite Levites.

Engedi.name: Engedi
Engedi.briefDescription: Oasis in Judah; refuge for David; Chedorlaomer's victory
Engedi.shortDescription: Engedi (or Hazazon-tamar), an oasis in Judah where David sought refuge from Saul, also the site of Chedorlaomer's victory.

En-haddah.name: En-haddah
En-haddah.briefDescription: City allotted to the tribe of Issachar
En-haddah.shortDescription: En-haddah, a city allotted to the tribe of Issachar during the division of the Promised Land.

En-hakkore.name: En-hakkore
En-hakkore.briefDescription: Spring that appeared after Samson's victory over the Philistines
En-hakkore.shortDescription: En-hakkore, the spring that God provided for Samson after his victory over the Philistines at Lehi.

En-hazor.name: En-hazor
En-hazor.briefDescription: City allotted to the tribe of Naphtali
En-hazor.shortDescription: En-hazor, a city allotted to the tribe of Naphtali during the division of the Promised Land.

En-rogel.name: En-rogel
En-rogel.briefDescription: Spring near Jerusalem; associated with Adonijah's and David's kingships
En-rogel.shortDescription: En-rogel, a spring near Jerusalem, was the site of Adonijah's attempt to become king and later served as a meeting place for David's supporters.

En-shemesh.name: En-shemesh
En-shemesh.briefDescription: Spring on the border between Judah and Benjamin
En-shemesh.shortDescription: En-shemesh, a spring on the border between the territories of Judah and Benjamin, near Jerusalem.

Ephes-dammim.name: Ephes-dammim
Ephes-dammim.briefDescription: Place where the Philistines and Israelites fought; Goliath's challenge
Ephes-dammim.shortDescription: Ephes-dammim (or Pas-dammim), the place where the Philistines and Israelites faced each other in battle, and where Goliath challenged the Israelites.

Ephesus.name: Ephesus
Ephesus.briefDescription: Major city in Asia Minor; important center for early Christianity
Ephesus.shortDescription: Ephesus, a major city in Asia Minor, was an important center for early Christianity and the recipient of one of Paul's epistles.

Ephraim_Gate.name: Ephraim_Gate
Ephraim_Gate.briefDescription: Gate in Jerusalem's wall, rebuilt during Nehemiah's time
Ephraim_Gate.shortDescription: The Ephraim Gate, a gate in Jerusalem's wall that was rebuilt during Nehemiah's time and used for public gatherings and proce∟ssions.

Ephron_Mount.name: Ephron_Mount
Ephron_Mount.briefDescription: Mountain on the border between Judah and Benjamin
Ephron_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Ephron, a mountain on the border between the territories of Judah and Benjamin, near Kiriath-jearim.

Erech.name: Erech
Erech.briefDescription: Ancient Mesopotamian city; part of Nimrod's kingdom
Erech.shortDescription: Erech, an ancient Mesopotamian city that was part of Nimrod's kingdom and later inhabited by people who opposed the rebuilding of the Jerusalem Temple.

Esau_Mount.name: Esau_Mount
Esau_Mount.briefDescription: Mountainous region of Edom; judgment prophesied by Obadiah
Esau_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Esau, the mountainous region of Edom, against which the prophet Obadiah declared God's judgment for their hostility toward Israel.

Esek.name: Esek
Esek.briefDescription: Well dug by Isaac's servants; named after quarrel
Esek.shortDescription: Esek, a well dug by Isaac's servants, named after a quarrel with local herdsmen.

Eshan.name: Eshan
Eshan.briefDescription: City in the hill country of Judah
Eshan.shortDescription: Eshan, a city in the hill country of Judah, allotted to the tribe of Judah.

Eshcol_Valley.name: Eshcol_Valley
Eshcol_Valley.briefDescription: Valley where Israelite spies obtained a large cluster of grapes
Eshcol_Valley.shortDescription: The Eshcol Valley, where the Israelite spies cut down a large cluster of grapes during their reconnaissance mission in Canaan.

Eshtaol.name: Eshtaol
Eshtaol.briefDescription: City allotted to Judah and Dan; associated with Samson
Eshtaol.shortDescription: Eshtaol, a city allotted to Judah and Dan, was the hometown of Samson and the starting point of the Danite migration.

Eshtemoa.name: Eshtemoa
Eshtemoa.briefDescription: Levitical city in Judah; received spoils from David's victory
Eshtemoa.shortDescription: Eshtemoa (or Eshtemoh), a Levitical city in Judah, was the home of several individuals and received spoils from David's victory over the Amalekites.

Etam.name: Etam
Etam.briefDescription: Cliff where Samson hid after defeating the Philistines
Etam.shortDescription: Etam, the cliff where Samson hid after defeating the Philistines, located in Judah.

Etham.name: Etham
Etham.briefDescription: Wilderness area where Israelites camped during the Exodus
Etham.shortDescription: Etham, a wilderness area on the edge of the desert where the Israelites camped during their journey from Egypt to the Promised Land.

Ether.name: Ether
Ether.briefDescription: City allotted to Judah and Simeon
Ether.shortDescription: Ether, a city allotted to the tribes of Judah and Simeon in the Shephelah region.

Eth-kazin.name: Eth-kazin
Eth-kazin.briefDescription: Border town of Zebulun near Neah
Eth-kazin.shortDescription: Eth-kazin, a border town of Zebulun located near Neah.

Euphrates.name: Euphrates
Euphrates.briefDescription: Major river in ancient Near East; boundary of Promised Land
Euphrates.shortDescription: The Euphrates, a major river in the ancient Near East, often served as a boundary marker for the Promised Land and was the site of significant events in biblical history.

Ezel_Stone.name: Ezel_Stone
Ezel_Stone.briefDescription: Location where David and Jonathan planned to meet
Ezel_Stone.shortDescription: The Stone of Ezel, a location where David and Jonathan planned to meet and communicate secretly during David's flight from King Saul.

Ezem.name: Ezem
Ezem.briefDescription: City allotted to Judah and Simeon
Ezem.shortDescription: Ezem, a city allotted to the tribes of Judah and Simeon in the Negev region.

Ezion-geber.name: Ezion-geber
Ezion-geber.briefDescription: Israelite encampment; port city on Red Sea
Ezion-geber.shortDescription: Ezion-geber, an Israelite encampment during the Exodus and later a port city on the Red Sea, known for its maritime trade and shipbuilding.

Fair_Havens.name: Fair_Havens
Fair_Havens.briefDescription: Harbor on Crete; Paul's ship stopped during journey to Rome
Fair_Havens.shortDescription: Fair Havens, a harbor on the island of Crete where Paul's ship stopped during his journey to Rome.

Fish_Gate.name: Fish_Gate
Fish_Gate.briefDescription: Gate in Jerusalem's wall; rebuilt by Hassenaah during Nehemiah's time
Fish_Gate.shortDescription: The Fish Gate, a gate in Jerusalem's wall, was rebuilt by the sons of Hassenaah during Nehemiah's time and was a location of judgment and trade.

Forest_Ephraim.name: Forest_Ephraim
Forest_Ephraim.briefDescription: Location of the battle between David's forces and Absalom's army
Forest_Ephraim.shortDescription: The Forest of Ephraim, where the battle between David's forces and Absalom's army took place during Absalom's rebellion.

Forum_of_Appius.name: Forum_of_Appius
Forum_of_Appius.briefDescription: Marketplace on Appian Way; Christians met Paul on journey to Rome
Forum_of_Appius.shortDescription: The Forum of Appius, a marketplace on the Appian Way, where Christians from Rome met Paul during his journey to the city as a prisoner.

Foundation_Gate.name: Foundation_Gate
Foundation_Gate.description: Gate of the Foundation= in Jerusalem (Jerusalem@Gen.14.18-Rev=H3389)
Foundation_Gate.briefDescription: Gate in Jerusalem's wall; location of Levite singers during coronation
Foundation_Gate.shortDescription: The Foundation Gate, a gate in Jerusalem's wall, where a third of the Levite singers were positioned during the coronation of Joash.

Fountain_Gate.name: Fountain_Gate
Fountain_Gate.briefDescription: Gate in Jerusalem's wall; rebuilt by Shallun during Nehemiah's time
Fountain_Gate.shortDescription: The Fountain Gate, a gate in Jerusalem's wall, was rebuilt by Shallun during Nehemiah's time and was a location for processions and gatherings.

Gaash.name: Gaash
Gaash.briefDescription: Mountain in Ephraim; burial place of Joshua
Gaash.shortDescription: Gaash, a mountain in the region of Ephraim, was the burial place of Joshua and the homeland of two of David's mighty men.

Gabbatha.name: Gabbatha
Gabbatha.briefDescription: Raised stone platform in Jerusalem; location of Jesus' trial
Gabbatha.shortDescription: Gabbatha, also known as the Stone Pavement, was the location in Jerusalem where Pontius Pilate sat in judgment over Jesus.

Galatia.name: Galatia
Galatia.briefDescription: A region in Asia Minor, recipient of Paul's epistle.
Galatia.shortDescription: Galatia, a region in Asia Minor, was the recipient of Paul's epistle to the Galatians and was visited by Paul on his missionary journeys (Act.16.6; 18:23; 1Co.16.1; Galatians 1:2; 3:1; 6:18; 2Ti.4.10; 1Pe.1.1).

Galilee.name: Galilee
Galilee.briefDescription: Region in northern Israel; key location in Jesus' ministry
Galilee.shortDescription: Galilee, a region in northern Israel, was a significant location in Jesus' life and ministry, as well as the homeland of many of his disciples.

Gallim.name: Gallim
Gallim.briefDescription: Town in Benjamin; home of Michal's husband Paltiel
Gallim.shortDescription: Gallim, a town in Benjamin, was the home of Paltiel, Michal's husband, and is mentioned in a prophecy of Isaiah.

Gamad.name: Gamad
Gamad.briefDescription: People group; defenders of the towers of Tyre
Gamad.shortDescription: The Gamad were a people group mentioned in Ezekiel's prophecy as defenders of the towers of Tyre.

Gath.name: Gath
Gath.briefDescription: Philistine city; home of Goliath; refuge for David
Gath.shortDescription: Gath, one of the five major Philistine cities, was the hometown of Goliath and a place of refuge for David during his flight from Saul.

Gath-hepher.name: Gath-hepher
Gath-hepher.briefDescription: Town in Zebulun; hometown of prophet Jonah
Gath-hepher.shortDescription: Gath-hepher, a town in the territory of Zebulun, was the hometown of the prophet Jonah.

Gath-rimmon.name: Gath-rimmon
Gath-rimmon.briefDescription: Levitical city in Dan and Manasseh
Gath-rimmon.shortDescription: Gath-rimmon, a Levitical city, was allotted to the Kohathite Levites within the territories of Dan and Manasseh.

Gaza.name: Gaza
Gaza.briefDescription: Philistine city; Samson's exploits; Philip's ministry
Gaza.shortDescription: Gaza, one of the five major Philistine cities, was the site of Samson's exploits and a place visited by Philip the evangelist in the early church.

Geba.name: Geba
Geba.shortDescription: Geba, a Levitical city in the territory of Benjamin, was located on the northern border of the kingdom of Judah (Joshua 18:24; 1Ki.15.22).

Gebal.name: Gebal
Gebal.briefDescription: Phoenician city; skilled builders; allied against Israel
Gebal.shortDescription: Gebal, a Phoenician city known for its skilled builders, was allied against Israel and provided materials for Solomon's temple.

Gebim.name: Gebim
Gebim.briefDescription: Town in Benjamin; mentioned in Isaiah's prophecy
Gebim.shortDescription: Gebim, a town in Benjamin, is mentioned in Isaiah's prophecy describing the advance of the Assyrian army towards Jerusalem.

Gederah.name: Gederah
Gederah.briefDescription: Town in Judah; home of Baal-hanan and Jozabad
Gederah.shortDescription: Gederah, a town in Judah, was the home of Baal-hanan, one of David's overseers, and Jozabad, one of his mighty men.

Gederoth.name: Gederoth
Gederoth.briefDescription: Town in Judah; captured by Philistines during Ahaz's reign
Gederoth.shortDescription: Gederoth, a town in Judah, was captured by the Philistines during the reign of King Ahaz.

Gederothaim.name: Gederothaim
Gederothaim.briefDescription: Town in Judah; mentioned in list of allotted cities
Gederothaim.shortDescription: Gederothaim, a town in Judah, is mentioned in the list of cities allotted to the tribe of Judah.

Ge-harashim.name: Ge-harashim
Ge-harashim.briefDescription: Valley of craftsmen; inhabited by Benjamites after the exile
Ge-harashim.shortDescription: Ge-harashim, the Valley of Craftsmen, was inhabited by some Benjamites after the return from the Babylonian exile.

Gennesaret.name: Gennesaret
Gennesaret.briefDescription: Plain on the northwest shore of the Sea of Galilee
Gennesaret.shortDescription: Gennesaret, a fertile plain on the northwest shore of the Sea of Galilee, was the location of several events in Jesus' ministry.

Gerar.name: Gerar
Gerar.briefDescription: Philistine city; Abraham and Isaac's encounters with Abimelech
Gerar.shortDescription: Gerar, a Philistine city, was the location of Abraham and Isaac's encounters with Abimelech and the site of a battle during King Asa's reign.

Gerasa.name: Gerasa
Gerasa.briefDescription: Region where Jesus healed a demon-possessed man
Gerasa.shortDescription: Gerasa (or Gadara), a region southeast of the Sea of Galilee, was the location where Jesus healed a demon-possessed man.

Gerizim_Mount.name: Gerizim_Mount
Gerizim_Mount.briefDescription: Mountain in Samaria; site of blessings and Jotham's parable
Gerizim_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Gerizim, a mountain in Samaria, was designated as the site for pronouncing blessings and was the location of Jotham's parable to the men of Shechem.

Geruth_Chimham.name: Geruth_Chimham
Geruth_Chimham.briefDescription: Lodging place near Bethlehem; refuge for Johanan's group
Geruth_Chimham.shortDescription: Geruth Chimham, a lodging place near Bethlehem, served as a temporary refuge for Johanan and his group after the murder of Gedaliah.

Geshur.name: Geshur
Geshur.briefDescription: Aramean kingdom; Talmai's domain; refuge for Absalom
Geshur.shortDescription: Geshur, an Aramean kingdom ruled by Talmai, was the homeland of David's wife Maacah and a refuge for Absalom after he murdered his half-brother Amnon.

Gethsemane.name: Gethsemane
Gethsemane.briefDescription: Garden near Jerusalem; site of Jesus' prayer and arrest
Gethsemane.shortDescription: Gethsemane, a garden at the Mount of Olives, was the site where Jesus prayed and was arrested before his crucifixion.

Gezer.name: Gezer
Gezer.briefDescription: A Canaanite city, conquered by Joshua and later by David.
Gezer.shortDescription: Gezer, also known as Gob, was a Canaanite city conquered by Joshua and later by David, and it was fortified by Solomon (Joshua 10:33; 12:12; 16:3, 10; 21:21; Judges 1:29; 2Sa.5.25; 21:18-19; 1Ki.9.15-17; 1Ch.6.67; 7:28; 14:16; 20:4).

Giah.name: Giah
Giah.briefDescription: Location near Gibeon where Joab and Abishai pursued Abner.
Giah.shortDescription: Giah was a place near Gibeon where Joab and Abishai pursued Abner after the battle at the pool of Gibeon.

Gibbethon.name: Gibbethon
Gibbethon.briefDescription: A Levitical city in the territory of Dan.
Gibbethon.shortDescription: Gibbethon, a Levitical city in the territory of Dan, was the site of conflict during the reigns of Nadab and Baasha (Joshua 19:44; 21:23; 1Ki.15.27; 16:15, 17).

Gibeah.name: Gibeah
Gibeah.briefDescription: A city of Benjamin, associated with Saul and atrocities.
Gibeah.shortDescription: Gibeah, a city of Benjamin, was the hometown of Saul and the site of infamous atrocities during the time of the judges (Joshua 18:28; 24:33; Judges 19-20; 1Sa.10.5, 10, 26; 11:4; 13:2, 15; 14:2, 16; 15:34; 22:6; 23:19; 26:1; 2Sa.21.6; 23:29; 1Ch.11.31; 12:3; Isaiah 10:29; Hosea 5:8; 9:9; 10:9).

Gibeath-elohim.name: Gibeath-elohim
Gibeath-elohim.briefDescription: A place with a garrison of Philistines, near Gibeah.
Gibeath-elohim.shortDescription: Gibeath-elohim, meaning "Hill of God," was a place near Gibeah where a garrison of Philistines was located (1Sa.10.5).

Gibeon.name: Gibeon
Gibeon.briefDescription: Hivite city that tricked Joshua into a peace treaty.
Gibeon.shortDescription: Gibeon was a prominent Hivite city that deceived Joshua into making a peace treaty, and later became a Levitical city and site of worship.

Gidom.name: Gidom
Gidom.briefDescription: A place near Gibeah, associated with the Benjamite War.
Gidom.shortDescription: Gidom was a place near Gibeah where the Benjamites were pursued and defeated during the Benjamite War (Judges 20:45).

Gihon.name: Gihon
Gihon.briefDescription: A river of Eden; a spring near Jerusalem.
Gihon.shortDescription: Gihon refers to both a river of Eden (Genesis 2:13) and a spring near Jerusalem (1Ki.1.33, 38, 45; 2Ch.32.30; 33:14).

Gilboa.name: Gilboa
Gilboa.briefDescription: A mountain range where Saul and his sons died.
Gilboa.shortDescription: Mount Gilboa was the site of a battle where Saul and his sons were killed by the Philistines (1Sa.28.4; 2Sa.1.21; 21:12).

Gilboa_Mount.name: Gilboa_Mount
Gilboa_Mount.briefDescription: A mountain range where Saul and his sons died.
Gilboa_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Gilboa was the site where Saul and his sons were killed in battle against the Philistines (1Sa.31.1, 8; 2Sa.1.6; 1Ch.10.1, 8; Judges 7:3).

Gilgal.name: Gilgal
Gilgal.briefDescription: A significant site during Israel's early history in Canaan.
Gilgal.shortDescription: Gilgal, also known as Geliloth, Beth-gilgal, or Gibeath-haaraloth, was a significant site during Israel's early history in Canaan, serving as a base for Joshua and a place of worship (Deu.11.30; Joshua 4:19-20; 5:9-10; 9:6; 10:6-9, 15, 43; 14:6; 15:7; Judges 2:1; 3:19; 1Sa.7.16; 10:8; 11:14-15; 13:4-8, 12, 15; 15:12, 21, 33; 2Sa.19.15, 40; Hosea 4:15; 9:15; 12:11; Amo.4.4; 5:5; Micah 6:5; Joshua 18:17; Nehemiah 12:29; Joshua 5:3).

Giloh.name: Giloh
Giloh.briefDescription: A city in Judah; home of Ahithophel, David's counselor.
Giloh.shortDescription: Giloh, also known as Gilo, was a city in Judah and the hometown of Ahithophel, David's counselor who later conspired with Absalom (Joshua 15:51; 2Sa.15.12; 23:34).

Gimzo.name: Gimzo
Gimzo.briefDescription: A city in Judah captured by the Philistines.
Gimzo.shortDescription: Gimzo was a city in the plain of Judah that was captured by the Philistines during the reign of Ahaz (2Ch.28.18).

Gittaim.name: Gittaim
Gittaim.briefDescription: Town where Beerothites fled; later inhabited by Benjamites.
Gittaim.shortDescription: Gittaim was a town where the Beerothites fled and was later inhabited by the Benjamites after the Babylonian exile.

Goah.name: Goah
Goah.briefDescription: A place near Jerusalem, mentioned in a prophetic context.
Goah.shortDescription: Goah was a place near Jerusalem, mentioned in the context of a prophetic description of the city's future restoration (Jeremiah 31:39).

Golan.name: Golan
Golan.briefDescription: A city of refuge in Bashan, allotted to Manasseh.
Golan.shortDescription: Golan was a city of refuge in Bashan, allotted to the Levites of the family of Gershon and located in the territory of the half-tribe of Manasseh (Deu.4.43; Joshua 20:8; 21:27; 1Ch.6.71).

Golgotha.name: Golgotha
Golgotha.briefDescription: The place where Jesus was crucified, meaning "Place of a Skull."
Golgotha.shortDescription: Golgotha, also known as the Place of a Skull, was the site outside Jerusalem where Jesus was crucified (Matthew 27:33; Mrk.15.22; Luke 23:33; Jhn.19.17).

Gomorrah.name: Gomorrah
Gomorrah.briefDescription: Wicked city destroyed by God along with Sodom.
Gomorrah.shortDescription: Gomorrah was a wicked city in the plain of Jordan that God destroyed with sulfur and fire, along with Sodom, due to their grave sins.

Goshen.name: Goshen
Goshen.briefDescription: A town and region in the hill country of Judah.
Goshen.shortDescription: Goshen was a town and region in the hill country of Judah, mentioned in the accounts of Joshua's conquests (Joshua 10:41; 11:16; 15:51).

Gozan.name: Gozan
Gozan.briefDescription: A region in Mesopotamia where Israelite captives were deported.
Gozan.shortDescription: Gozan was a region in Mesopotamia where Israelite captives from the northern kingdom were deported by the Assyrians (2Ki.17.6; 18:11; 19:12; 1Ch.5.26; Isaiah 37:12).

Great_Sea.name: Great_Sea
Great_Sea.briefDescription: The Mediterranean Sea, the western boundary of the Promised Land.
Great_Sea.shortDescription: The Great Sea, also known as the Sea of the Philistines, refers to the Mediterranean Sea, which formed the western boundary of the Promised Land (Numbers 34:6-7; Joshua 1:4; 9:1; 15:12, 47; 23:4; Ezk.47.10, 15, 19-20; 48:28; Exodus 23:31).

Greece.name: Greece
Greece.briefDescription: A nation and culture, often in opposition to Israel
Greece.shortDescription: Greece (Javan), a nation and culture mentioned in both the Old and New Testaments.

Guard_Gate.name: Guard_Gate
Guard_Gate.description: Gate of the Guard= in Jerusalem (Jerusalem@Gen.14.18-Rev=H3389)
Guard_Gate.briefDescription: A gate in Jerusalem's wall, likely the Muster Gate.
Guard_Gate.shortDescription: The Guard Gate was a gate in Jerusalem's wall, mentioned in Nehemiah 12:39, likely the same as the Muster Gate near the Temple.

Gur.name: Gur
Gur.briefDescription: A place near Ibleam where Ahaziah was wounded.
Gur.shortDescription: Gur was a place near Ibleam where Ahaziah, king of Judah, was wounded by Jehu's men while fleeing (2Ki.9.27).

Gurbaal.name: Gurbaal
Gurbaal.briefDescription: A place inhabited by Arabians, attacked by Uzziah's army.
Gurbaal.shortDescription: Gurbaal was a place inhabited by Arabians, against whom Uzziah, king of Judah, waged war and prevailed (2Ch.26.7).

Habor.name: Habor
Habor.briefDescription: A river in Assyria where Israelite captives were settled.
Habor.shortDescription: Habor was a river in Assyria along which the Israelite captives from the northern kingdom were settled by the Assyrians (2Ki.17.6; 18:11; 1Ch.5.26).

Hachilah.name: Hachilah
Hachilah.briefDescription: A hill in the Wilderness of Ziph, near David's hideout.
Hachilah.shortDescription: Hachilah was a hill in the Wilderness of Ziph, where David hid from Saul and where the Ziphites revealed David's location to Saul (1Sa.23.19; 26:1, 3).

Hadad-rimmon.name: Hadad-rimmon
Hadad-rimmon.briefDescription: A place near Megiddo associated with mourning for Josiah.
Hadad-rimmon.shortDescription: Hadad-rimmon was a place near Megiddo where a great lamentation was held for King Josiah, who was mortally wounded there.

Hadashah.name: Hadashah
Hadashah.briefDescription: A town in the Shephelah region of Judah.
Hadashah.shortDescription: Hadashah was a town allotted to the tribe of Judah, located in the Shephelah region (Joshua 15:37).

Hadid.name: Hadid
Hadid.briefDescription: A town in Benjamin, resettled after the Babylonian exile.
Hadid.shortDescription: Hadid was a town in Benjamin, whose inhabitants returned from the Babylonian exile and resettled in the town (Ezra 2:33; Nehemiah 7:37; 11:34).

Hadrach.name: Hadrach
Hadrach.briefDescription: A region mentioned in a prophetic oracle of judgment.
Hadrach.shortDescription: Hadrach was a region mentioned in a prophetic oracle of judgment, possibly located in Syria (Zechariah 9:1).

Haeleph.name: Haeleph
Haeleph.briefDescription: A town allotted to the tribe of Benjamin.
Haeleph.shortDescription: Haeleph was a town allotted to the tribe of Benjamin (Joshua 18:28).

Halah.name: Halah
Halah.briefDescription: A place where Israelites were exiled by the Assyrians.
Halah.shortDescription: Halah was one of the places where the Assyrians settled the exiled Israelites after conquering the Northern Kingdom.

Halak_Mount.name: Halak_Mount
Halak_Mount.briefDescription: A mountain marking the southern boundary of Joshua's conquests.
Halak_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Halak was a mountain that marked the southern boundary of the territory conquered by Joshua (Joshua 11:17; 12:7).

Halhul.name: Halhul
Halhul.briefDescription: A town in the hill country of Judah.
Halhul.shortDescription: Halhul was a town allotted to the tribe of Judah, located in the hill country (Joshua 15:58).

Hali.name: Hali
Hali.briefDescription: A town allotted to the tribe of Asher.
Hali.shortDescription: Hali was a town allotted to the tribe of Asher (Joshua 19:25).

Hall_of_Judgment.name: Hall_of_Judgment
Hall_of_Judgment.briefDescription: A hall in Solomon's palace where he administered justice.
Hall_of_Judgment.shortDescription: The Hall of Judgment was a hall in Solomon's palace where he sat to judge and administer justice (1Ki.7.7).

Hall_of_Pillars.name: Hall_of_Pillars
Hall_of_Pillars.briefDescription: A porch with pillars in Solomon's palace.
Hall_of_Pillars.shortDescription: The Hall of Pillars was a porch in Solomon's palace, measuring fifty cubits long and thirty cubits wide, with a portico of pillars and a canopy.

Hall_of_the_Throne.name: Hall_of_the_Throne
Hall_of_the_Throne.description: Hall of the Throne= in Jerusalem (Jerusalem@Gen.14.18-Rev=H3389)
Hall_of_the_Throne.briefDescription: Another name for the Hall of Judgment in Solomon's palace.
Hall_of_the_Throne.shortDescription: The Hall of the Throne was another name for the Hall of Judgment in Solomon's palace, where he sat to administer justice (1Ki.7.7).

Hamath.name: Hamath
Hamath.briefDescription: A major city and kingdom in Syria, often mentioned in the Bible.
Hamath.shortDescription: Hamath, also known as Hamath Rabbah or Lebo-hamath, was a significant city and kingdom in Syria, frequently mentioned in the Bible as a reference point for the northern border of Israel and in the context of political and military events (Numbers 13:21; 34:8; Joshua 13:5; Judges 3:3; 2Sa.8.9; 1Ki.8.65; 2Ki.14.25, 28; 17:24, 30; 18:34; 19:13; 23:33; 25:21; 1Ch.13.5; 18:9; 2Ch.7.8; 8:4; Isaiah 10:9; 11:11; 36:19; 37:13; Jeremiah 39:5; 49:23; 52:9, 27; Ezk.47.15-17, 20; 48:1; Amo.6.2, 14; Zechariah 9:2).

Hamonah.name: Hamonah
Hamonah.briefDescription: A city in Ezekiel's vision, meaning "multitude" or "horde."
Hamonah.shortDescription: Hamonah is a city mentioned in Ezekiel's vision (Ezk.39.16), its name meaning "multitude" or "horde," likely referring to the burial place of Gog's army.

Hamon-gog_Valley.name: Hamon-gog_Valley
Hamon-gog_Valley.briefDescription: A valley where Gog and his army will be buried.
Hamon-gog_Valley.shortDescription: The Valley of Hamon-gog is the place where Gog and his army will be buried after their defeat, as prophesied by Ezekiel (Ezk.39.11, 15).

Hanes.name: Hanes
Hanes.briefDescription: An Egyptian city mentioned in a prophecy against Judah.
Hanes.shortDescription: Hanes was an Egyptian city to which Judah sent envoys, as mentioned in a prophecy by Isaiah (Isaiah 30:4).

Hannathon.name: Hannathon
Hannathon.briefDescription: A border town in the territory of Zebulun.
Hannathon.shortDescription: Hannathon was a border town in the territory allotted to the tribe of Zebulun (Joshua 19:14).

Hara.name: Hara
Hara.briefDescription: A place where Israelite tribes were exiled by Tiglath-pileser.
Hara.shortDescription: Hara was a place, possibly in Media, where the Reubenites, Gadites, and half-tribe of Manasseh were exiled by Tiglath-pileser, king of Assyria (1Ch.5.26).

Haradah.name: Haradah
Haradah.briefDescription: A desert campsite during Israel's journey from Egypt.
Haradah.shortDescription: Haradah was one of the places where the Israelites camped during their journey through the wilderness after leaving Egypt.

Harmon.name: Harmon
Harmon.briefDescription: A place mentioned in Amos's prophecy against Israel.
Harmon.shortDescription: Harmon is a place mentioned in Amos's prophecy, possibly referring to the destination of the exiled Israelites (Amo.4.3).

Harod.name: Harod
Harod.briefDescription: A spring near where Gideon gathered his army.
Harod.shortDescription: Harod was a spring near the hill of Moreh, where Gideon gathered his army before battling the Midianites (Judges 7:1).

Harosheth-hagoyim.name: Harosheth-hagoyim
Harosheth-hagoyim.shortDescription: Harosheth-hagoyim was a Canaanite city that served as the base for Sisera, the commander of King Jabin's army (Judg 4:2).

Hashmonah.name: Hashmonah
Hashmonah.briefDescription: A desert campsite during Israel's journey from Egypt.
Hashmonah.shortDescription: Hashmonah was one of the places where the Israelites camped during their wilderness wanderings after leaving Egypt.

Hauran.name: Hauran
Hauran.briefDescription: Region east of the Sea of Galilee
Hauran.shortDescription: Hauran was a fertile region located east of the Sea of Galilee, mentioned as part of Israel's future boundaries (Ezk.47.16,18).

Havvoth-jair.name: Havvoth-jair
Havvoth-jair.briefDescription: Villages in Gilead captured by Jair
Havvoth-jair.shortDescription: Havvoth-jair were villages in Gilead captured by Jair, a leader from the tribe of Manasseh, during the Israelite conquest of the region (Num.32.41; Deut 3:14; Judg 10:4; 1Ch.2.23).

Hazar-addar.name: Hazar-addar
Hazar-addar.briefDescription: A town on the southern border of Canaan.
Hazar-addar.shortDescription: Hazar-addar was a town on the southern border of Canaan, mentioned in the description of the land's boundaries.

Hazar-enan.name: Hazar-enan
Hazar-enan.briefDescription: Town on the northern border of Israel
Hazar-enan.shortDescription: Hazar-enan was a town located on the northern border of the Promised Land (Num.34.9-10) and the future inheritance of the tribe of Dan (Ezk.48.1).

Hazar-gaddah.name: Hazar-gaddah
Hazar-gaddah.briefDescription: Town allotted to the tribe of Judah
Hazar-gaddah.shortDescription: Hazar-gaddah was a town in the Negev region, allotted to the tribe of Judah during the division of the Promised Land (Josh 15:27).

Hazar-shual.name: Hazar-shual
Hazar-shual.briefDescription: Town allotted to Judah and Simeon
Hazar-shual.shortDescription: Hazar-shual was a town in the Negev allotted to Judah (Josh 15:28), later given to the tribe of Simeon (Josh 19:3; 1Ch.4.28), and inhabited by Jews after the Babylonian exile (Neh.11.27).

Hazar-susah.name: Hazar-susah
Hazar-susah.briefDescription: Town allotted to the tribe of Simeon
Hazar-susah.shortDescription: Hazar-susah was a town in the Negev region, allotted to the tribe of Simeon during the division of the Promised Land (Josh 19:5).

Hazar-susim.name: Hazar-susim
Hazar-susim.briefDescription: A town allotted to the tribe of Simeon.
Hazar-susim.shortDescription: Hazar-susim was a town allotted to the tribe of Simeon, mentioned in the list of their cities in 1 Chronicles.

Hazer-hatticon.name: Hazer-hatticon
Hazer-hatticon.briefDescription: Place on the ideal border of Israel
Hazer-hatticon.shortDescription: Hazer-hatticon, meaning "the middle village," was a place named on the ideal border of Israel in Ezekiel's vision (Ezk.47.16), likely identical to Hazar-enan.

Hazerim.name: Hazerim

father.name: Word sometimes translated as a place
father.raw: Word sometimes translated as a place

Hazerim.shortDescription: Hazerim, meaning "villages," refers to the settlements inhabited by the Avvim, a people group dispossessed by the Caphtorim (Deut 2:23).

relations.father: Word sometimes translated as a place

Hazeroth.name: Hazeroth
Hazeroth.briefDescription: Israelite encampment during the wilderness wanderings
Hazeroth.shortDescription: Hazeroth was one of the places where the Israelites camped during their wilderness wanderings, mentioned in the context of Moses' leadership challenges (Num.11.35; 12:16; 33:17-18; Deut 1:1).

Hazor.name: Hazor
Hazor.briefDescription: Town resettled by Benjamites after the Babylonian exile
Hazor.shortDescription: Hazor was a town resettled by the Benjamites after their return from the Babylonian exile (Neh.11.33).

Hazor-hadattah.name: Hazor-hadattah
Hazor-hadattah.briefDescription: Town in the Negev region of Judah
Hazor-hadattah.shortDescription: Hazor-hadattah, meaning "New Hazor," was a town in the Negev region, part of the territory allotted to the tribe of Judah (Josh 15:25).

Hebron_Valley.name: Hebron_Valley
Hebron_Valley.briefDescription: Valley near Hebron where Joseph sought his brothers
Hebron_Valley.shortDescription: The Hebron Valley was the location where Joseph was sent by his father, Jacob, to check on his brothers who were pasturing their flocks near Shechem (Gen.37.14).

Helam.name: Helam
Helam.briefDescription: Place where David defeated the Arameans
Helam.shortDescription: Helam was the location of a battle between David's forces and the Arameans, where David defeated Hadadezer, king of Zobah, and his allies (2Sa.10.16-17).

Helbah.name: Helbah
Helbah.briefDescription: Canaanite town not conquered by the tribe of Asher
Helbah.shortDescription: Helbah was a Canaanite town that the tribe of Asher failed to conquer during the period of the judges (Judg 1:31).

Helbon.name: Helbon
Helbon.briefDescription: City known for its fine wine
Helbon.shortDescription: Helbon was a city mentioned in Ezk.27.18 as a trading partner of Tyre, known for its fine wine.

Helech.name: Helech
Helech.briefDescription: A place or people mentioned in Ezekiel's prophecy.
Helech.shortDescription: Helech was either a place, possibly in Cilicia, or a term referring to the army of Tyre, mentioned in Ezekiel's prophecy against Tyre.

Heleph.name: Heleph
Heleph.shortDescription: Heleph was a town marking the border of the territory allocated to the tribe of Naphtali (Josh 19:33).

Helkath.name: Helkath
Helkath.briefDescription: Levitical city in the territory of Asher
Helkath.shortDescription: Helkath (or Hukok) was a city allocated to the Levites within the territory of the tribe of Asher (Josh 19:25; 21:31; 1Ch.6.75).

Helkath-hazzurim.name: Helkath-hazzurim
Helkath-hazzurim.briefDescription: Site of a battle between David's and Ish-bosheth's men
Helkath-hazzurim.shortDescription: Helkath-hazzurim, meaning "Field of Swords," was the site of a battle between David's men and those of Ish-bosheth, resulting in Abner's defeat (2Sa.2.16).

Hena.name: Hena
Hena.shortDescription: Hena was a Mesopotamian city conquered by the Assyrians before the time of Sennacherib, possibly located near Sippara (2Ki.18.34; 19:13; Isa.37.13).

Heres.name: Heres
Heres.briefDescription: Place near where Gideon defeated the Midianites
Heres.shortDescription: Heres was a place near the site where Gideon and his men defeated the Midianites (Judg 8:13).

Hereth.name: Hereth
Hereth.briefDescription: Forest where David hid from Saul
Hereth.shortDescription: The forest of Hereth was where David sought refuge when fleeing from King Saul (1Sa.22.5).

Hermon_Mount.name: Hermon_Mount
Hermon_Mount.briefDescription: Mountain on the northern border of Israel
Hermon_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Hermon, also known as Baal-hermon, Baal-hamon, Baal-gad, Senir, or Sirion, was a prominent mountain on the northern border of Israel (Deut 3:8-9; 4:48; Josh 11:3,17; 12:1,5,7; 13:5,11; 1Ch.5.23; Psa.29.6; 42:6; 89:12; 133:3; Sng.4.8; 8:11; Jer.18.14; Ezk.27.5).

Heshbon.name: Heshbon
Heshbon.briefDescription: Amorite city conquered by Moses, allotted to Reuben
Heshbon.shortDescription: Heshbon, an Amorite city conquered by Moses (Num.21.25-30), was allotted to Reuben (Num.32.37; Josh 13:17) but later given to the Levites (Josh 21:39); it was a significant city in the region (Isa.15.4; 16:8-9; Jer.48.2, 34, 45; 49:3).

Heshmon.name: Heshmon
Heshmon.briefDescription: A town in the southland of Judah.
Heshmon.shortDescription: Heshmon was a town allotted to the tribe of Judah in the Negev region.

Hethlon.name: Hethlon
Hethlon.briefDescription: Place on the northern border of Israel
Hethlon.shortDescription: Hethlon was a place on the northern border of Israel, mentioned in Ezekiel's vision of the restored land (Ezk.47.15; 48:1).

Hierapolis.name: Hierapolis
Hierapolis.briefDescription: City in Phrygia, home to a Christian community
Hierapolis.shortDescription: Hierapolis, a city in Phrygia, was home to a Christian community mentioned by Paul in his letter to the Colossians (Col.4.13).

Hobah.name: Hobah
Hobah.briefDescription: Place north of Damascus pursued by Abraham
Hobah.shortDescription: Hobah was a place north of Damascus where Abraham pursued the defeated army of Chedorlaomer after rescuing Lot (Gen.14.15).

Holon.name: Holon
Holon.briefDescription: Levitical city in the hill country of Judah
Holon.shortDescription: Holon, a Levitical city in Judah's hill country, also called Hilen.

Holy_Place.name: Holy_Place
Holy_Place.briefDescription: The inner sanctuary of the Tabernacle and Temple
Holy_Place.shortDescription: The Holy Place was the inner sanctuary of the Tabernacle and Temple.

Hor_Mount.name: Hor_Mount
Hor_Mount.briefDescription: A mountain marking Israel's northern boundary
Hor_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Hor, a mountain at the northern extent of Israel's inheritance.

Horem.name: Horem
Horem.shortDescription: Horem, a fortified city in the territory of Naphtali (Joshua 19:38).

Horesh.name: Horesh
Horesh.briefDescription: A wilderness area where David hid from Saul
Horesh.shortDescription: Horesh, a wilderness area in Judah where David hid from Saul.

Hor-haggidgad.name: Hor-haggidgad
Hor-haggidgad.briefDescription: An Israelite campsite during the wilderness wanderings
Hor-haggidgad.shortDescription: Hor-haggidgad (Gudgodah), an Israelite campsite between Bene-jaakan and Jotbathah.

Horonaim.name: Horonaim
Horonaim.briefDescription: A Moabite city mentioned in prophecies of judgment
Horonaim.shortDescription: Horonaim, a Moabite city featured in prophecies against Moab.

Horse_Gate.name: Horse_Gate
Horse_Gate.briefDescription: A gate in Jerusalem's wall near the temple
Horse_Gate.shortDescription: The Horse Gate, a gate in Jerusalem's wall near the temple.

House_of_the_Forest.name: House_of_the_Forest
House_of_the_Forest.description: House of the Forest of Lebanon= in Jerusalem (Jerusalem@Gen.14.18-Rev=H3389)
House_of_the_Forest.briefDescription: A building in Solomon's palace complex
House_of_the_Forest.shortDescription: The House of the Forest of Lebanon, a building in Solomon's palace.

Hukkok.name: Hukkok
Hukkok.briefDescription: A town on the border of Naphtali
Hukkok.shortDescription: Hukkok, a town on the border of Naphtali (Joshua 19:34).

Humtah.name: Humtah
Humtah.briefDescription: A town in the hill country of Judah
Humtah.shortDescription: Humtah, a town in the hill country of Judah (Joshua 15:54).

Ibleam.name: Ibleam
Ibleam.shortDescription: Ibleam (Bileam, Gath-rimmon), a Levitical city in Issachar.

Iconium.name: Iconium
Iconium.briefDescription: A city in Asia Minor visited by Paul
Iconium.shortDescription: Iconium, a city in Asia Minor where Paul preached the gospel.

Idalah.name: Idalah
Idalah.shortDescription: Idalah, a town in the territory of Zebulun (Joshua 19:15).

Idumea.name: Idumea
Idumea.shortDescription: Idumea, a region south of Judea, inhabited by Edomites.

Iim.name: Iim
Iim.briefDescription: A town in the Negev of Judah
Iim.shortDescription: Iim, a town in the Negev region of Judah (Joshua 15:29).

Ijon.name: Ijon
Ijon.briefDescription: A town in northern Israel conquered by Assyria
Ijon.shortDescription: Ijon (Jaan), a town in northern Israel conquered by the Assyrians.

Illyricum.name: Illyricum
Illyricum.briefDescription: A region where Paul preached the gospel
Illyricum.shortDescription: Illyricum, a region where Paul preached the gospel (Romans 15:19).

India.name: India
India.briefDescription: The eastern extent of Ahasuerus' Persian Empire
India.shortDescription: India, the eastern extent of Ahasuerus' Persian Empire (Esther 1:1; 8:9).

Iphtah.name: Iphtah
Iphtah.briefDescription: A town in the Shephelah of Judah
Iphtah.shortDescription: Iphtah, a town in the Shephelah region of Judah (Joshua 15:43).

Iphtahel_Valley.name: Iphtahel_Valley
Iphtahel_Valley.briefDescription: A valley on the border of Zebulun and Asher
Iphtahel_Valley.shortDescription: The Iphtahel Valley, on the border between Zebulun and Asher (Joshua 19:14, 27).

Ir-nahash.name: Ir-nahash
Ir-nahash.briefDescription: A town associated with the descendants of Eshton
Ir-nahash.shortDescription: Ir-nahash, a town associated with the descendants of Eshton (1Ch.4.12).

Irpeel.name: Irpeel
Irpeel.shortDescription: Irpeel, a town in the territory of Benjamin (Joshua 18:27).

Italy.name: Italy
Italy.briefDescription: A region in southern Europe, home to the Roman Empire
Italy.shortDescription: Italy, a region in southern Europe, home to the Roman Empire.

Ithlah.name: Ithlah
Ithlah.shortDescription: Ithlah, a town in the territory of Dan (Joshua 19:42).

Ithnan.name: Ithnan
Ithnan.briefDescription: A town in the Negev of Judah
Ithnan.shortDescription: Ithnan, a town in the Negev region of Judah (Joshua 15:23).

Ivvah.name: Ivvah
Ivvah.briefDescription: City conquered by Assyria, people resettled in Samaria.
Ivvah.shortDescription: Ivvah (or Avva) was a city conquered by Assyria, whose people were resettled in Samaria. (2Ki.18.34, 19:13; Isaiah 37:13; 2Ki.17.24, 31)

Iye-abarim.name: Iye-abarim
Iye-abarim.shortDescription: Iye-abarim, an Israelite campsite on the border of Moab (Numbers 21:11; 33:44).

Iyim.name: Iyim
Iyim.briefDescription: An Israelite campsite in the wilderness
Iyim.shortDescription: Iyim, an Israelite campsite during the wilderness journey (Numbers 33:45).

Jabbok.name: Jabbok
Jabbok.briefDescription: A river east of the Jordan, associated with Jacob
Jabbok.shortDescription: The Jabbok, a river east of the Jordan, where Jacob wrestled with God.

Jabneel.name: Jabneel
Jabneel.briefDescription: A town on the border of Naphtali
Jabneel.shortDescription: Jabneel, a town on the border of Naphtali (Joshua 19:33).

Jagur.name: Jagur
Jagur.briefDescription: A town in the Negev of Judah
Jagur.shortDescription: Jagur, a town in the Negev region of Judah (Joshua 15:21).

Jahaz.name: Jahaz
Jahaz.briefDescription: A town in Moab, site of Israelite victories
Jahaz.shortDescription: Jahaz (Jahzah), a Moabite town where the Israelites defeated Sihon and later allotted to Reuben.

Janen.name: Janen
Janen.briefDescription: A place mentioned in the Septuagint
Janen.shortDescription: Janen, a place mentioned in the Septuagint version of Numbers 21:19.

Janim.name: Janim
Janim.briefDescription: A town in the hill country of Judah
Janim.shortDescription: Janim, a town in the hill country of Judah (Joshua 15:53).

Janoah.name: Janoah
Janoah.briefDescription: A town on the border of Ephraim
Janoah.shortDescription: Janoah, a town on the border of Ephraim's territory (Joshua 16:6,7).

Jarmuth.name: Jarmuth
Jarmuth.shortDescription: Jarmuth, a Levitical city in the territory of Issachar (Joshua 21:29).

Jattir.name: Jattir
Jattir.briefDescription: A town in the hill country of Judah
Jattir.shortDescription: Jattir, a town in the hill country of Judah, later a Levitical city.

Jazer.name: Jazer
Jazer.briefDescription: A town in Gilead, allotted to Gad
Jazer.shortDescription: Jazer, a town in Gilead, allotted to Gad, known for its fertile land and vineyards.

Jazer_Sea.name: Jazer_Sea
Jazer_Sea.briefDescription: A poetic reference to the abundant vineyards of Jazer
Jazer_Sea.shortDescription: The Sea of Jazer, a poetic reference to the abundant vineyards of Jazer (Jeremiah 48:32).

Jehud.name: Jehud
Jehud.shortDescription: Jehud, a town allotted to the tribe of Dan (Joshua 19:45).

Jekabzeel.name: Jekabzeel
Jekabzeel.briefDescription: A town in Judah inhabited after the Babylonian exile
Jekabzeel.shortDescription: Jekabzeel, a town in Judah inhabited by the people of Judah after the Babylonian exile (Nehemiah 11:25).

Jemnath.name: Jemnath
Jemnath.briefDescription: A place mentioned in the Septuagint version of Joshua
Jemnath.shortDescription: Jemnath, a place mentioned in the Septuagint version of Joshua 15:46.

Jericho.name: Jericho
Jericho.briefDescription: An ancient city in Canaan, famously conquered by Israel
Jericho.shortDescription: Jericho, an ancient Canaanite city famously conquered by the Israelites under Joshua's leadership.

Jericho_Valley.name: Jericho_Valley
Jericho_Valley.briefDescription: The valley where the city of Jericho is located
Jericho_Valley.shortDescription: The Jericho Valley, the location of the ancient city of Jericho (Deu.34.3).

Jeruel.name: Jeruel
Jeruel.briefDescription: A wilderness where Jehoshaphat defeated his enemies
Jeruel.shortDescription: The Wilderness of Jeruel, where Jehoshaphat and Judah defeated their enemies (2Ch.20.16).

Jeshanah.name: Jeshanah
Jeshanah.briefDescription: A town captured by Abijah from Jeroboam
Jeshanah.shortDescription: Jeshanah, a town captured by King Abijah of Judah from Jeroboam of Israel (2Ch.13.19).

Jeshimon.name: Jeshimon
Jeshimon.briefDescription: A wilderness near Ziph, where David hid from Saul
Jeshimon.shortDescription: Jeshimon, a wilderness near Ziph where David hid from Saul (1Sa.23.19, 24; 26:1, 3).

Jezreel_Valley.name: Jezreel_Valley
Jezreel_Valley.briefDescription: A fertile valley in northern Israel, site of many battles
Jezreel_Valley.shortDescription: The Jezreel Valley, a strategically important and fertile valley in northern Israel, was the site of several significant battles in the Bible.

Jogbehah.name: Jogbehah
Jogbehah.briefDescription: A town in Gilead, fortified by the Gadites
Jogbehah.shortDescription: Jogbehah, a town in Gilead fortified by the Gadites (Numbers 32:35), was located near the route Gideon took when pursuing the Midianites (Judges 8:11).

Jokdeam.name: Jokdeam
Jokdeam.briefDescription: A town in the hill country of Judah
Jokdeam.shortDescription: Jokdeam, a town in the hill country of Judah (Joshua 15:56).

Jokneam.name: Jokneam
Jokneam.briefDescription: A Canaanite city, later a Levitical city in Zebulun
Jokneam.shortDescription: Jokneam (or Jokmeam), a Canaanite city conquered by Joshua, later allotted to the Levites within the territory of Zebulun.

Joktheel.name: Joktheel
Joktheel.briefDescription: A town in the Shephelah region of Judah
Joktheel.shortDescription: Joktheel, a town in the Shephelah (lowland) region of Judah (Joshua 15:38).

Joppa.name: Joppa
Joppa.briefDescription: An ancient seaport, the main port for Jerusalem
Joppa.shortDescription: Joppa, an ancient seaport on the Mediterranean coast, served as the main port for Jerusalem in biblical times.

Jordan.name: Jordan
Jordan.briefDescription: A river and region, significant in Israel's history
Jordan.shortDescription: The Jordan River and its surrounding region played a significant role in the history of Israel, from the time of the patriarchs to the New Testament era.

Jotbah.name: Jotbah
Jotbah.briefDescription: The hometown of Amon's mother, Meshullemeth
Jotbah.shortDescription: Jotbah, the hometown of Meshullemeth; mother of King Amon of Judah (2Ki.21.19).

Jotbathah.name: Jotbathah
Jotbathah.briefDescription: A place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness journey
Jotbathah.shortDescription: Jotbathah, a place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness journey, known for its streams of water (Numbers 33:33-34; Deu.10.7).

Judea.name: Judea
Judea.briefDescription: The southern region of Israel, centered around Jerusalem
Judea.shortDescription: Judea, the southern region of Israel, was the center of Jewish life and culture in biblical times, with Jerusalem as its capital.

Juttah.name: Juttah
Juttah.briefDescription: A town in the hill country of Judah, a Levitical city
Juttah.shortDescription: Juttah, a town in the hill country of Judah, was designated as a Levitical city (Joshua 15:55; 21:16).

Kabzeel.name: Kabzeel
Kabzeel.briefDescription: A town in the Negev, home of Benaiah
Kabzeel.shortDescription: Kabzeel, a town in the Negev, was the hometown of Benaiah, one of David's mighty men (Joshua 15:21; 2Sa.23.20; 1Ch.11.22).

Kadesh.name: Kadesh
Kadesh.briefDescription: An important oasis in the Wilderness of Zin
Kadesh.shortDescription: Kadesh (Kadesh-barnea, En-mishpat), an important oasis in the Wilderness of Zin, was a key location during the Israelites' wilderness wanderings.

Kain.name: Kain
Kain.briefDescription: A town in the hill country of Judah
Kain.shortDescription: Kain, a town in the hill country of Judah (Joshua 15:57), is possibly associated with the Kenites mentioned in Numbers 24:22.

Kamon.name: Kamon
Kamon.briefDescription: The burial place of Jair, a judge of Israel
Kamon.shortDescription: Kamon, a city in Gilead where Jair, a judge of Israel, was buried (Judges 10:5).

Kanah.name: Kanah
Kanah.briefDescription: Town in Asher's territory; village where Jesus performed miracles
Kanah.shortDescription: Kanah was a border town in Asher's territory, while Cana was the village in Galilee where Jesus performed his first miracle.

Karka.name: Karka
Karka.briefDescription: A place on the southern border of Judah
Karka.shortDescription: Karka, a location along the southern border of the tribe of Judah (Joshua 15:3).

Karkor.name: Karkor
Karkor.briefDescription: A place where Gideon defeated Zebah and Zalmunna
Karkor.shortDescription: Karkor, the place where Gideon defeated the Midianite kings Zebah and Zalmunna (Judges 8:10).

Kartan.name: Kartan
Kartan.briefDescription: A Levitical city in the territory of Naphtali
Kartan.shortDescription: Kartan, a city given to the Levites within the territory of Naphtali (Joshua 21:32).

Kedemoth.name: Kedemoth
Kedemoth.briefDescription: A town in Moab, later a Levitical city in Reuben
Kedemoth.shortDescription: Kedemoth, a town in Moab from which Moses sent messengers to Sihon, later became a Levitical city within the territory of Reuben (Deu.2.26; Joshua 13:18; 21:37; 1Ch.6.79).

Kedesh.name: Kedesh
Kedesh.briefDescription: A Canaanite city, later a Levitical city of refuge in Naphtali
Kedesh.shortDescription: Kedesh (Kedesh-naphtali), a Canaanite city conquered by Joshua, became a Levitical city and a city of refuge in the territory of Naphtali (Joshua 12:22; 19:37; 20:7; 21:32).

Kehelathah.name: Kehelathah
Kehelathah.briefDescription: A place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness journey
Kehelathah.shortDescription: Kehelathah, a place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness wanderings after leaving Mount Sinai (Numbers 33:22-23).

Keilah.name: Keilah
Keilah.briefDescription: A town in the Shephelah of Judah, rescued by David
Keilah.shortDescription: Keilah, a town in the Shephelah of Judah, was rescued by David from Philistine attack but later threatened to betray him to Saul (Joshua 15:44; 1 Samuel 23).

Kenath.name: Kenath
Kenath.briefDescription: A city in Gilead, captured by Nobah and renamed
Kenath.shortDescription: Kenath, a city in Gilead captured by Nobah and renamed after himself, was later recaptured by Geshur and Aram (Numbers 32:42; Judges 8:11; 1Ch.2.23).
