Kerioth-hezron.name: Kerioth-hezron
Kerioth-hezron.briefDescription: A town in the Negev region of Judah
Kerioth-hezron.shortDescription: Kerioth-hezron, also known as Hazor, was a town in the Negev region of Judah (Joshua 15:25).

Kibroth-hattaavah.name: Kibroth-hattaavah
Kibroth-hattaavah.briefDescription: A place where the Israelites complained and were punished
Kibroth-hattaavah.shortDescription: Kibroth-hattaavah, meaning "graves of craving," was a place where the Israelites complained about the lack of meat and were punished with a plague (Numbers 11:34-35; 33:16-17; Deu.9.22).

Kibzaim.name: Kibzaim
Kibzaim.briefDescription: A Levitical city in the territory of Ephraim
Kibzaim.shortDescription: Kibzaim, also known as Jokmeam, was a Levitical city within the territory of Ephraim (Joshua 21:22; 1Ch.6.68).

Kidron.name: Kidron
Kidron.briefDescription: A valley east of Jerusalem, site of significant events
Kidron.shortDescription: The Kidron Valley, also known as the Valley of Jehoshaphat, is located east of Jerusalem and was the site of several significant events in the Bible.

Kinah.name: Kinah
Kinah.briefDescription: A town in the Negev region of Judah
Kinah.shortDescription: Kinah, a town in the Negev region of Judah (Joshua 15:22).

King's_Highway.name: King's_Highway
King's_Highway.briefDescription: An ancient trade route through Edom and Moab
King's_Highway.shortDescription: The King's Highway, an ancient trade route through Edom and Moab, was the path the Israelites sought to use during their journey to Canaan (Numbers 20:17; 21:22).

King's_Valley.name: King's_Valley
King's_Valley.briefDescription: A valley near Salem (Jerusalem), site of significant events
King's_Valley.shortDescription: The King's Valley, located near Salem (Jerusalem), was the site where Melchizedek met Abraham and where Absalom erected a pillar for himself (Genesis 14:17; 2Sa.18.18).

Kir.name: Kir
Kir.briefDescription: A city or region in Mesopotamia, possibly Elam
Kir.shortDescription: Kir, a city or region in Mesopotamia, possibly Elam, was the place where the Arameans were exiled and from where they originally came (2Ki.16.9; Amo.9.7).

Kiriathaim.name: Kiriathaim
Kiriathaim.briefDescription: A Moabite city, later allotted to Reuben
Kiriathaim.shortDescription: Kiriathaim, also known as Kerioth, was a Moabite city later allotted to the tribe of Reuben (Numbers 32:37; Joshua 13:19; Jeremiah 48:1, 23).

Kiriath-huzoth.name: Kiriath-huzoth
Kiriath-huzoth.shortDescription: Kiriath-huzoth, a place where Balak took Balaam (Numbers 22:39).

Kiriath-jearim.name: Kiriath-jearim
Kiriath-jearim.briefDescription: A city where the ark resided
Kiriath-jearim.shortDescription: Kiriath-jearim, a city where the ark of the covenant resided for 20 years (1Sa.7.1-2).

Kishion.name: Kishion
Kishion.shortDescription: Kishion (Kedesh), a Levitical city in the territory of Issachar (Joshua 19:20, 21:28; 1Ch.6.72).

Kishon.name: Kishon
Kishon.shortDescription: Kishon, a river in northern Israel, where Deborah and Barak defeated Sisera's army (Judges 4:7, 13; 5:21).

Kitron.name: Kitron
Kitron.briefDescription: A city in Zebulun not conquered
Kitron.shortDescription: Kitron (Kartah or Kattath), a city in Zebulun that was not conquered by the tribe (Judges 1:30; Joshua 21:34, 19:15).

Koa.name: Koa
Koa.briefDescription: A people group mentioned by Ezekiel
Koa.shortDescription: Koa, a people group mentioned by Ezekiel (Ezk.23.23).

Kue.name: Kue
Kue.shortDescription: Kue, a place from which Solomon obtained horses (1Ki.10.28; 2Ch.1.16).

Lachish.name: Lachish
Lachish.shortDescription: Lachish, a fortified city in Judah, captured by Joshua and later by the Assyrians (Joshua 10; 2Ki.18.14, 19:8).

Lahmam.name: Lahmam
Lahmam.briefDescription: A city in the lowlands of Judah
Lahmam.shortDescription: Lahmam, a city in the lowlands of Judah (Joshua 15:40).

Laishah.name: Laishah
Laishah.briefDescription: A place near Anathoth and Gallim
Laishah.shortDescription: Laishah, a place near Anathoth and Gallim, mentioned in a prophecy of Isaiah (Isaiah 10:30).

Lakkum.name: Lakkum
Lakkum.shortDescription: Lakkum, a border town of the tribe of Naphtali (Joshua 19:33).

Laodicea.name: Laodicea
Laodicea.shortDescription: Laodicea, a city in Asia Minor, known for its lukewarm faith (Revelation 3:14-16).

Lasea.name: Lasea
Lasea.briefDescription: A city near Fair Havens, Crete
Lasea.shortDescription: Lasea, a city near Fair Havens on the island of Crete (Act.27.8).

Lasha.name: Lasha
Lasha.briefDescription: A place marking Canaanite territory; a stop in Israel's journey
Lasha.shortDescription: Lasha (Nahaliel), a place marking the extent of Canaanite territory (Genesis 10:19) and a stop in Israel's wilderness journey (Numbers 21:19).

Lasharon.name: Lasharon
Lasharon.briefDescription: A Canaanite city conquered by Joshua
Lasharon.shortDescription: Lasharon, a Canaanite city whose king was defeated by Joshua (Joshua 12:18).

Lebanon.name: Lebanon
Lebanon.briefDescription: A mountainous region known for its cedar trees
Lebanon.shortDescription: Lebanon, a mountainous region north of Israel, known for its cedar trees and as a symbol of strength and beauty (1Ki.4.33; Psalm 92:12).

Lebanon_Mount.name: Lebanon_Mount
Lebanon_Mount.briefDescription: A mountain range marking the northern border of Israel
Lebanon_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Lebanon, a mountain range marking the northern border of the Promised Land (Judges 3:3).

Lebanon_Valley.name: Lebanon_Valley
Lebanon_Valley.briefDescription: A valley in the Promised Land conquered by Joshua
Lebanon_Valley.shortDescription: The Valley of Lebanon, a valley in the Promised Land conquered by Joshua (Joshua 11:17; 12:7).

Lebaoth.name: Lebaoth
Lebaoth.briefDescription: A town in the Negev of Judah
Lebaoth.shortDescription: Lebaoth, a town in the Negev region of Judah (Joshua 15:32).

Leb-kamai.name: Leb-kamai
Leb-kamai.shortDescription: Leb-kamai, a cryptic name for Chaldea (Babylonia) in a prophecy of Jeremiah (Jeremiah 51:1).

Lebonah.name: Lebonah
Lebonah.briefDescription: A place near Shiloh in Ephraim
Lebonah.shortDescription: Lebonah, a place near Shiloh in the territory of Ephraim (Judges 21:19).

Lehi.name: Lehi
Lehi.briefDescription: A place where Samson fought the Philistines
Lehi.shortDescription: Lehi, a place where Samson fought against the Philistines and God provided him with water (Judges 15:9, 14, 19).

Libnah.name: Libnah
Libnah.briefDescription: A place where Israel camped in the wilderness
Libnah.shortDescription: Libnah, a place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness journey (Numbers 33:20-21).

Libya.name: Libya
Libya.shortDescription: Libya (Put or Pul), a country in North Africa mentioned in various Old Testament prophecies (Ezk.30.5; 38:5; Nam.3.9).

Lod.name: Lod
Lod.briefDescription: A city in Benjamin; home of Aeneas healed by Peter
Lod.shortDescription: Lod (Lydda), a city in the territory of Benjamin (1Ch.8.12), where Peter healed Aeneas (Act.9.32-35).

Lo-debar.name: Lo-debar
Lo-debar.briefDescription: A town in Gilead; home of Mephibosheth
Lo-debar.shortDescription: Lo-debar (Debir), a town in Gilead where Mephibosheth, Jonathan's son, lived (2Sa.9.4-5).

Luhith.name: Luhith
Luhith.shortDescription: Luhith, a place in Moab mentioned in prophecies of Isaiah and Jeremiah (Isaiah 15:5; Jeremiah 48:5).

Luz.name: Luz
Luz.briefDescription: A Canaanite city; a man built another city named Luz
Luz.shortDescription: Luz, a Canaanite city that was destroyed, and another city built by a man who had betrayed the first Luz (Judges 1:26).

Lycaonia.name: Lycaonia
Lycaonia.briefDescription: A region in Asia Minor; Paul and Barnabas preached there
Lycaonia.shortDescription: Lycaonia, a region in Asia Minor where Paul and Barnabas preached the gospel (Act.14.6, 11).

Lycia.name: Lycia
Lycia.briefDescription: A region on the southwest coast of Asia Minor
Lycia.shortDescription: Lycia, a region on the southwest coast of Asia Minor, passed by Paul on his journey to Rome (Act.27.5).

Lystra.name: Lystra
Lystra.briefDescription: A city in Lycaonia; Paul and Barnabas preached there
Lystra.shortDescription: Lystra, a city in Lycaonia where Paul and Barnabas preached and Paul healed a lame man (Act.14.6-21).

Maarath.name: Maarath
Maarath.briefDescription: A town in the hill country of Judah
Maarath.shortDescription: Maarath, a town in the hill country of Judah (Joshua 15:59).

Maareh-geba.name: Maareh-geba
Maareh-geba.briefDescription: A place near Gibeah where an ambush was set
Maareh-geba.shortDescription: Maareh-geba, a place near Gibeah where the Israelites set an ambush against the Benjamites (Judges 20:33).

Macedonia.name: Macedonia
Macedonia.briefDescription: A region in northern Greece; Paul preached there
Macedonia.shortDescription: Macedonia, a region in northern Greece where Paul preached the gospel and established churches (Act.16.9-12; 20:1-3).

Machpelah.name: Machpelah
Machpelah.briefDescription: A cave near Hebron; burial place of patriarchs
Machpelah.shortDescription: Machpelah, a cave near Hebron purchased by Abraham as a burial place for Sarah and later used for other patriarchs (Genesis 23:9, 17-19).

Madmen.name: Madmen
Madmen.briefDescription: A place in Moab threatened with destruction
Madmen.shortDescription: Madmen, a place in Moab against which Jeremiah prophesied destruction (Jeremiah 48:2).

Madmenah.name: Madmenah
Madmenah.shortDescription: Madmenah, a place near Jerusalem mentioned in Isaiah's prophecy (Isaiah 10:31).

Madon.name: Madon
Madon.briefDescription: A Canaanite city conquered by Joshua
Madon.shortDescription: Madon, a Canaanite city whose king joined a coalition against Joshua but was defeated (Joshua 11:1; 12:19).

Magadan.name: Magadan
Magadan.briefDescription: A place on the Sea of Galilee; home of Mary Magdalene
Magadan.shortDescription: Magadan (Dalmanutha), a place on the shore of the Sea of Galilee visited by Jesus (Matthew 15:39; Mrk.8.10), possibly the hometown of Mary Magdalene (Matthew 27:56, 61; 28:1; Mrk.15.40, 47; 16:1, 9; Luke 8:2; 24:10; Jhn.19.25; 20:1, 18).

Magbish.name: Magbish
Magbish.briefDescription: A place whose people returned from Babylonian exile
Magbish.shortDescription: Magbish, a place whose people are listed among those who returned to Judah from Babylonian exile (Ezra 2:30).

Mahanaim.name: Mahanaim
Mahanaim.briefDescription: A place east of the Jordan; David's refuge
Mahanaim.shortDescription: Mahanaim, a place east of the Jordan River where Jacob met angels and David found refuge during Absalom's rebellion (Genesis 32:2; 2Sa.17.24, 27).

Mahaneh-dan.name: Mahaneh-dan
Mahaneh-dan.briefDescription: A place between Zorah and Eshtaol; Samson's birthplace
Mahaneh-dan.shortDescription: Mahaneh-dan, a place between Zorah and Eshtaol where the Spirit of the Lord began to stir Samson (Judges 13:25; 18:12).

Makaz.name: Makaz
Makaz.briefDescription: A place in one of Solomon's administrative districts
Makaz.shortDescription: Makaz, a place in one of the twelve administrative districts established by Solomon (1Ki.4.9).

Makheloth.name: Makheloth
Makheloth.briefDescription: A place where the Israelites camped in the wilderness
Makheloth.shortDescription: Makheloth, a place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness journey (Numbers 33:25-26).

Makkedah.name: Makkedah
Makkedah.briefDescription: A Canaanite city conquered by Joshua
Makkedah.shortDescription: Makkedah, a Canaanite city where Joshua defeated and executed five Amorite kings who had hidden in a cave (Joshua 10:16-28).

Malta.name: Malta
Malta.briefDescription: An island where Paul was shipwrecked
Malta.shortDescription: Malta, an island in the Mediterranean Sea where Paul was shipwrecked on his journey to Rome (Act.28.1).

Marah.name: Marah
Marah.briefDescription: A place with bitter water on the Exodus route
Marah.shortDescription: Marah, a place where the Israelites found bitter water that Moses made drinkable by casting a tree into it (Exodus 15:23-25; Numbers 33:8-9).

Mareal.name: Mareal
Mareal.briefDescription: A place on the border of Zebulun
Mareal.shortDescription: Mareal, a place mentioned in describing the border of the territory of Zebulun (Joshua 19:11).

Maroth.name: Maroth
Maroth.shortDescription: Maroth, a town in Judah mentioned in Micah's prophecy (Micah 1:12).

Masrekah.name: Masrekah
Masrekah.briefDescription: The royal city of Samlah, an Edomite king
Masrekah.shortDescription: Masrekah, the royal city of Samlah, one of the ancient kings of Edom before any Israelite king reigned (Genesis 36:36; 1Ch.1.47).

Massah.name: Massah
Massah.briefDescription: A place where the Israelites tested God
Massah.shortDescription: Massah, a place in the wilderness where the Israelites tested the Lord by demanding water (Exodus 17:7; Deu.6.16; 9:22; 33:8; Psalm 95:8).

Mattanah.name: Mattanah
Mattanah.briefDescription: A place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness journey
Mattanah.shortDescription: Mattanah, a place where the Israelites camped during their journey through the wilderness (Numbers 21:18-19).

Mearah.name: Mearah
Mearah.shortDescription: Mearah, a place near Sidon, mentioned in the description of the land yet to be conquered by the Israelites (Joshua 13:4).

Meconah.name: Meconah
Meconah.briefDescription: A town inhabited by the people of Judah after the exile
Meconah.shortDescription: Meconah, a town inhabited by the people of Judah after their return from the Babylonian exile (Nehemiah 11:28).

Medeba.name: Medeba
Medeba.briefDescription: A Moabite city captured by the Israelites
Medeba.shortDescription: Medeba, a city in Moab captured by the Israelites (Numbers 21:30; Joshua 13:9, 16), later a site of battle between the Ammonites and David's army (1Ch.19.7).

Media.name: Media
Media.briefDescription: An ancient kingdom; part of the Persian Empire
Media.shortDescription: Media (Medes), an ancient kingdom in western Iran that, along with Persia, conquered Babylon and formed the Persian Empire (2Ki.17.6; 18:11; Esther 1:3, 14, 18; 10:2; Isaiah 21:2; Jeremiah 25:25; Dan.8.20).

Megiddo.name: Megiddo
Megiddo.briefDescription: A strategic city in the Jezreel Valley
Megiddo.shortDescription: Megiddo, a strategically important city in the Jezreel Valley (Joshua 12:21; 17:11; Judges 1:27; 5:19), fortified by Solomon (1Ki.9.15), and the site of King Josiah's death (2Ki.23.29-30; 2Ch.35.22).

Me-jarkon.name: Me-jarkon
Me-jarkon.briefDescription: A place in the territory of Dan
Me-jarkon.shortDescription: Me-jarkon, a place in the territory allotted to the tribe of Dan (Joshua 19:46).

Memphis.name: Memphis
Memphis.briefDescription: An important city in ancient Egypt
Memphis.shortDescription: Memphis, an ancient Egyptian city mentioned in prophecies of judgment against Egypt (Isaiah 19:13; Jeremiah 2:16; 44:1; 46:14, 19; Ezk.30.13, 16; Hosea 9:6).

Mephaath.name: Mephaath
Mephaath.briefDescription: A Levitical city in Reuben, later Moabite
Mephaath.shortDescription: Mephaath, a Levitical city in the territory of Reuben (Joshua 13:18; 21:37; 1Ch.6.79), later mentioned in a prophecy against Moab (Jeremiah 48:21).

Meribah.name: Meribah
Meribah.briefDescription: A place where Moses disobeyed God
Meribah.shortDescription: Meribah-kadesh, a place where Moses disobeyed God by striking the rock to bring forth water (Numbers 20:13, 24; 27:14; Deu.32.51; Psalm 81:7; 106:32; Ezk.47.19; 48:28).

Merom.name: Merom
Merom.briefDescription: A place where Joshua defeated Jabin's coalition
Merom.shortDescription: Merom, a place near the waters of Merom where Joshua defeated a coalition of Canaanite kings led by Jabin king of Hazor (Joshua 11:5, 7).

Meronothite.name: Meronothite
Meronothite.briefDescription: A term used to describe two individuals in the Bible
Meronothite.shortDescription: Meronothite, a term used to describe Jehdeiah, who was in charge of King David's donkeys (1Ch.27.30), and Jadon, who helped repair the wall of Jerusalem (Nehemiah 3:7).

Meroz.name: Meroz
Meroz.briefDescription: A place cursed for not helping against Sisera
Meroz.shortDescription: Meroz, a place cursed by the angel of the Lord for not coming to help fight against Sisera (Judges 5:23).

Meshech-Tubal.name: Meshech-Tubal
Meshech-Tubal.briefDescription: Two nations mentioned together; defeated by Babylon
Meshech-Tubal.shortDescription: Meshech-Tubal, two nations mentioned together as having been defeated by the Babylonians and lying in the grave with the uncircumcised (Ezk.32.26).

Mesopotamia.name: Mesopotamia
Mesopotamia.briefDescription: The region between the Tigris and Euphrates rivers
Mesopotamia.shortDescription: Mesopotamia, the ancient region between the Tigris and Euphrates rivers, where Abraham lived before being called to Canaan (Genesis 24:10; Act.7.2) and where the Israelites were oppressed by Cushan-rishathaim (Judges 3:8, 10).

Michmash.name: Michmash
Michmash.briefDescription: A town in Benjamin; site of Saul's camp and Jonathan's victory
Michmash.shortDescription: Michmash, a town in Benjamin where Saul camped (1Sa.13.2, 5, 11, 16, 23) and near which Jonathan defeated a Philistine garrison (1Sa.14.5, 31).

Michmethath.name: Michmethath
Michmethath.briefDescription: A place on the borders of Ephraim and Manasseh
Michmethath.shortDescription: Michmethath, a place on the border between the territories of Ephraim and Manasseh (Joshua 16:6; 17:7).

Middin.name: Middin
Middin.briefDescription: A town in the wilderness of Judah
Middin.shortDescription: Middin, a town in the wilderness of Judah (Joshua 15:61).

Migdal-el.name: Migdal-el
Migdal-el.shortDescription: Migdal-el, a fortified city in the territory of Naphtali (Joshua 19:38).

Migdal-gad.name: Migdal-gad
Migdal-gad.briefDescription: A town in the lowlands of Judah
Migdal-gad.shortDescription: Migdal-gad, a town in the lowlands of Judah (Joshua 15:37).

Migdol.name: Migdol
Migdol.briefDescription: A place near the Red Sea where the Israelites camped
Migdol.shortDescription: Migdol, a place near the Red Sea where the Israelites camped during the Exodus (Exodus 14:2; Numbers 33:7), and a city in Egypt mentioned in prophecies (Jeremiah 44:1; 46:14; Ezk.29.10; 30:6).

Migron.name: Migron
Migron.briefDescription: A place near Gibeah where Saul camped
Migron.shortDescription: Migron, a place near Gibeah where Saul camped with his troops (1Sa.14.2), and a town mentioned in Isaiah's prophecy about the Assyrian invasion (Isaiah 10:28).

Miletus.name: Miletus
Miletus.briefDescription: A city in Asia Minor visited by Paul
Miletus.shortDescription: Miletus, a city in Asia Minor where Paul met with the elders of the Ephesian church on his way to Jerusalem (Act.20.15, 17) and where Trophimus was left ill (2Ti.4.20).

Millo.name: Millo
Millo.briefDescription: A fortification in Jerusalem built by Solomon
Millo.shortDescription: Millo (Akra), a fortification in Jerusalem built by Solomon (1Ki.9.15, 24; 11:27) and repaired by Hezekiah (2Ch.32.5).

Minni.name: Minni
Minni.briefDescription: A kingdom allied with Ararat and Ashkenaz
Minni.shortDescription: Minni, a kingdom mentioned alongside Ararat and Ashkenaz, was called to battle against Babylon.

Minnith.name: Minnith
Minnith.briefDescription: An Ammonite town known for its wheat
Minnith.shortDescription: Minnith, an Ammonite town, was known for its wheat and was a place of defeat for the Ammonites by Jephthah.

Misgab.name: Misgab
Misgab.description: Word sometimes translated as a place name
Misgab.shortDescription: Misgab, meaning "fortress," was a stronghold in Moab that faced judgment according to Jeremiah's prophecy.

Mishal.name: Mishal
Mishal.shortDescription: Mishal (Mashal), a Levitical city in the territory of Asher (Joshua 19:26; 21:30; 1Ch.6.74).

Misrephoth-maim.name: Misrephoth-maim
Misrephoth-maim.briefDescription: A place near Sidon where the Canaanites fled
Misrephoth-maim.shortDescription: Misrephoth-maim, a location near Sidon, was where the Canaanites fled after being defeated by Joshua.

Mithkah.name: Mithkah
Mithkah.briefDescription: An Israelite campsite during the wilderness wanderings
Mithkah.shortDescription: Mithkah was a place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness journey, between Terah and Hashmonah.

Mitylene.name: Mitylene
Mitylene.briefDescription: A city on the island of Lesbos visited by Paul
Mitylene.shortDescription: Mitylene, a city on the island of Lesbos where Paul's ship stopped on his way to Jerusalem (Act.20.14).

Mizar_Mount.name: Mizar_Mount
Mizar_Mount.briefDescription: A mountain mentioned in a psalm of lament
Mizar_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Mizar, a mountain mentioned in a psalm of lament, possibly representing a place of remembrance or longing for God's presence (Psalm 42:6).

Mizpah.name: Mizpah
Mizpah.briefDescription: A city in Benjamin; a place of gathering and judgment
Mizpah.shortDescription: Mizpah (Mizpeh, Ramah, Ramathaim-zophim, Arimathea), a city in Benjamin where the Israelites gathered for judgment and prayer (Judges 20:1, 3; 21:1, 5, 8; 1Sa.7.5-7, 11-12, 16; 10:17), and the hometown of Samuel (1Sa.1.19; 2:11; 7:17; 8:4; 15:34; 16:13; 19:18-23; 20:1; 25:1; 28:3) and Joseph of Arimathea (Matthew 27:57; Mrk.15.43; Luke 23:50-51; Jhn.19.38).

Mizpeh.name: Mizpeh
Mizpeh.briefDescription: A town in the lowlands of Judah
Mizpeh.shortDescription: Mizpeh, a town in the lowlands (Shephelah) of Judah (Joshua 15:38).

Mizpeh_Valley.name: Mizpeh_Valley
Mizpeh_Valley.shortDescription: The Valley of Mizpeh (Mizpah), a region near Mount Hermon where Joshua defeated the kings of the northern Canaanite coalition (Joshua 11:3, 8).

Moab_Plains.name: Moab_Plains
Moab_Plains.briefDescription: Region east of the Jordan River, opposite Jericho
Moab_Plains.shortDescription: The plains of Moab, located east of the Jordan River and opposite Jericho, were where the Israelites camped before entering the Promised Land (Num.22.1; 26:3, 63; 31:12; 33:48-50; 35:1; 36:13; Deut 34:1, 8; Josh 13:32).

Moladah.name: Moladah
Moladah.briefDescription: Town in the Negev, allotted to Judah and Simeon
Moladah.shortDescription: Moladah was a town in the Negev region, allotted to the tribes of Judah and Simeon, and later inhabited by the people of Judah after the Babylonian exile (Josh 15:26; 19:2; 1Ch.4.28; Neh.11.26).

Moreh.name: Moreh
Moreh.briefDescription: Place near Shechem; hill where Gideon camped
Moreh.shortDescription: Moreh refers to a place near Shechem associated with Abraham (Gen.12.6) and a hill where Gideon camped before attacking the Midianites (Judg 7:1).

Moresheth.name: Moresheth
Moresheth.briefDescription: Hometown of the prophet Micah in Judah
Moresheth.shortDescription: Moresheth was the hometown of the prophet Micah, located in Judah.

Moriah_Mount.name: Moriah_Mount
Moriah_Mount.briefDescription: Site of Solomon's temple in Jerusalem
Moriah_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Moriah was the site where Solomon built the temple in Jerusalem.

Mortar.name: Mortar
Mortar.briefDescription: A district or valley in or near Jerusalem
Mortar.shortDescription: The Mortar was a district or valley in or near Jerusalem, mentioned in Zephaniah's prophecy.

Moserah.name: Moserah
Moserah.briefDescription: A place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness wanderings
Moserah.shortDescription: Moserah was a place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness wanderings, and where Aaron died and was buried.

Moseroth.name: Moseroth
Moseroth.briefDescription: A place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness wanderings
Moseroth.shortDescription: Moseroth was a place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness wanderings after leaving Egypt.

Most_Holy_Place.name: Most_Holy_Place
Most_Holy_Place.briefDescription: The innermost room of the tabernacle and temple
Most_Holy_Place.shortDescription: The Most Holy Place was the innermost room of the tabernacle and temple, where the Ark of the Covenant was kept.

Mozah.name: Mozah
Mozah.briefDescription: A town in the territory of Benjamin
Mozah.shortDescription: Mozah was a town allotted to the tribe of Benjamin.

Muster_Gate.name: Muster_Gate
Muster_Gate.briefDescription: A gate in Jerusalem mentioned in Nehemiah's time
Muster_Gate.shortDescription: The Muster Gate, also known as the Miphkad Gate, was one of the gates of Jerusalem mentioned in Nehemiah's time.

Myra.name: Myra
Myra.briefDescription: A city in Lycia where Paul's ship stopped
Myra.shortDescription: Myra was a city in Lycia, Asia Minor, where Paul's ship stopped on his journey to Rome as a prisoner.

Mysia.name: Mysia
Mysia.briefDescription: A region in northwest Asia Minor visited by Paul
Mysia.shortDescription: Mysia was a region in northwest Asia Minor (modern Turkey) that Paul and his companions passed through on his second missionary journey.

Naaran.name: Naaran
Naaran.briefDescription: A border town between Ephraim and Benjamin
Naaran.shortDescription: Naaran, also called Naarah, was a border town between the tribes of Ephraim and Benjamin.

Nahalol.name: Nahalol
Nahalol.shortDescription: Nahalol, also called Nahalal, was a Levitical city in the territory of Zebulun.

Nain.name: Nain
Nain.briefDescription: A town in Galilee where Jesus raised a widow's son
Nain.shortDescription: Nain was a town in Galilee where Jesus raised a widow's son from the dead.

Naioth.name: Naioth
Naioth.briefDescription: A place near Ramah where David fled from Saul
Naioth.shortDescription: Naioth was a place near Ramah where David fled to escape King Saul, and where Samuel and a group of prophets lived.

Naphath.name: Naphath
Naphath.briefDescription: A region in Issachar with Manassite towns
Naphath.shortDescription: Naphath was a region in Issachar that contained towns belonging to the tribe of Manasseh.

Nazareth.name: Nazareth
Nazareth.briefDescription: The hometown of Jesus in Galilee
Nazareth.shortDescription: Nazareth was a village in Galilee where Jesus grew up, giving rise to His title "Jesus of Nazareth."

Neah.name: Neah
Neah.briefDescription: A border town of Zebulun and Asher
Neah.shortDescription: Neah, also called Neiel, was a border town between the territories of Zebulun and Asher.

Neapolis.name: Neapolis
Neapolis.briefDescription: A port city in Macedonia visited by Paul
Neapolis.shortDescription: Neapolis was a port city in Macedonia where Paul landed on his first journey to Europe.

Neballat.name: Neballat
Neballat.briefDescription: A town in Benjamin inhabited by Benjamites after the exile
Neballat.shortDescription: Neballat was a town in the territory of Benjamin where some Benjamites settled after returning from the Babylonian exile.

Nebo.name: Nebo
Nebo.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Nebo.briefDescription: A Babylonian god and a mountain in Moab
Nebo.shortDescription: Nebo, mentioned in Isaiah 46:1, is a Babylonian god of wisdom and writing, and also the name of a mountain in Moab where Moses viewed the Promised Land before his death (Deu.32.49; 34:1).

Nebo_Mount.name: Nebo_Mount
Nebo_Mount.briefDescription: The mountain where Moses viewed the Promised Land
Nebo_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Nebo, also called Abarim, was the mountain from which Moses viewed the Promised Land before his death.

Negeb.name: Negeb
Negeb.briefDescription: The southern region of Israel, a semi-arid area
Negeb.shortDescription: The Negeb, meaning "south," refers to the semi-arid region in the southern part of Israel.

Nephtoah.name: Nephtoah
Nephtoah.briefDescription: A spring on the border of Judah and Benjamin
Nephtoah.shortDescription: Nephtoah was a spring that served as a boundary marker between the territories of Judah and Benjamin.

Netaim.name: Netaim
Netaim.briefDescription: A place associated with potters in Judah
Netaim.shortDescription: Netaim was a place in Judah where potters lived and worked.

Netophah.name: Netophah
Netophah.briefDescription: A town in Judah, home to several notable individuals
Netophah.shortDescription: Netophah was a town in Judah, known as the hometown of several notable individuals in the Bible.

New_Gate.name: New_Gate
New_Gate.briefDescription: A gate in Jerusalem during Jeremiah's time
New_Gate.shortDescription: The New Gate was a gate in Jerusalem during the time of the prophet Jeremiah.

Nezib.name: Nezib
Nezib.briefDescription: A town in the Judean lowlands
Nezib.shortDescription: Nezib was a town allotted to the tribe of Judah in the lowland region.

Nibshan.name: Nibshan
Nibshan.briefDescription: A town in the Judean wilderness
Nibshan.shortDescription: Nibshan was a town in the wilderness of Judah, mentioned in the list of towns allotted to the tribe of Judah.

Nicopolis.name: Nicopolis
Nicopolis.briefDescription: A city in Epirus where Paul planned to winter
Nicopolis.shortDescription: Nicopolis was a city in Epirus where Paul intended to spend the winter, as mentioned in his letter to Titus.

Nile.name: Nile
Nile.briefDescription: The major river of Egypt, mentioned in various contexts
Nile.shortDescription: The Nile, the major river of Egypt, is mentioned in various contexts throughout the Bible, often in relation to the history of Israel and God's judgment on Egypt.

Nimrim.name: Nimrim
Nimrim.briefDescription: A place in Moab known for its waters
Nimrim.shortDescription: Nimrim was a place in Moab known for its waters, which are mentioned in the context of God's judgment on Moab.

Nineveh.name: Nineveh
Nineveh.briefDescription: The capital of Assyria, known for its wickedness and repentance
Nineveh.shortDescription: Nineveh, the capital of Assyria, is known in the Bible for its wickedness and its remarkable repentance following Jonah's preaching.

Nob.name: Nob
Nob.briefDescription: A city of priests near Jerusalem
Nob.shortDescription: Nob was a city of priests near Jerusalem, known for a massacre ordered by King Saul and as a temporary location of the Ark of the Covenant.

Nod.name: Nod
Nod.briefDescription: The land where Cain settled after killing Abel
Nod.shortDescription: Nod was the land east of Eden where Cain settled after he was banished for killing his brother Abel.

Oboth.name: Oboth
Oboth.briefDescription: A place where the Israelites camped during the Exodus
Oboth.shortDescription: Oboth was one of the places where the Israelites camped during their journey through the wilderness after the Exodus from Egypt.

Olives_Mount.name: Olives_Mount
Olives_Mount.briefDescription: A mountain ridge east of Jerusalem
Olives_Mount.shortDescription: The Mount of Olives is a mountain ridge east of Jerusalem, known as the site of many significant events in the Bible, particularly in the life of Jesus.

Ono.name: Ono
Ono.briefDescription: A town in Benjamin, near Lod
Ono.shortDescription: Ono was a town in the territory of Benjamin, often mentioned together with Lod, and was the site of a proposed meeting between Nehemiah and his enemies.

Ophel.name: Ophel
Ophel.shortDescription: Ophel was a fortified area in Jerusalem, located near the temple, and was inhabited by temple servants during the time of Nehemiah.

Ophni.name: Ophni
Ophni.shortDescription: Ophni was a town allotted to the tribe of Benjamin during the division of the Promised Land.

Pacatiana.name: Pacatiana
Pacatiana.briefDescription: A place mentioned in some manuscripts of 1 Timothy
Pacatiana.shortDescription: Pacatiana is a place mentioned in some manuscripts of 1Ti.6.21, but its exact location and significance are unknown.

Paddan.name: Paddan
Paddan.briefDescription: The homeland of the patriarchs in Mesopotamia
Paddan.shortDescription: Paddan, also known as Paddan-aram or Aram, was the homeland of the patriarchs Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, located in Mesopotamia.

Pamphylia.name: Pamphylia
Pamphylia.briefDescription: A Roman province in Asia Minor
Pamphylia.shortDescription: Pamphylia was a Roman province on the southern coast of Asia Minor, visited by Paul and Barnabas during their missionary journeys.

Paphos.name: Paphos
Paphos.briefDescription: A city on the island of Cyprus
Paphos.shortDescription: Paphos was a city on the island of Cyprus, where Paul and Barnabas encountered a sorcerer and converted the proconsul to Christianity.

Parah.name: Parah
Parah.shortDescription: Parah was a city allotted to the tribe of Benjamin, located between Avvim and Ophrah.

Paran.name: Paran
Paran.briefDescription: A desert region in the Sinai Peninsula
Paran.shortDescription: Paran was a desert region in the Sinai Peninsula, associated with the Israelites' wanderings and the early life of Ishmael.

Paran_Mount.name: Paran_Mount
Paran_Mount.briefDescription: A mountain associated with God's appearance
Paran_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Paran is mentioned in poetic passages as a place from which God comes or shines forth.

Parbar.name: Parbar

father.name: Word sometimes translated as a place
father.raw: Word sometimes translated as a place

Parbar.briefDescription: A colonnade or structure in the temple complex
Parbar.shortDescription: Parbar was a colonnade or structure located on the west side of the temple complex in Jerusalem.

relations.father: Word sometimes translated as a place

Parvaim.name: Parvaim
Parvaim.briefDescription: A place from which Solomon obtained gold
Parvaim.shortDescription: Parvaim was a place or region from which Solomon obtained gold for decorating the temple in Jerusalem.

Patara.name: Patara
Patara.briefDescription: A city in Lycia visited by Paul
Patara.shortDescription: Patara was a coastal city in Lycia (modern-day Turkey) where Paul changed ships during his journey to Jerusalem.

Pathros.name: Pathros
Pathros.shortDescription: Pathros was a region in Upper Egypt (southern Egypt) where some Judeans settled after the Babylonian exile.

Patmos.name: Patmos
Patmos.briefDescription: An island where John received the Revelation
Patmos.shortDescription: Patmos is a Greek island in the Aegean Sea where the apostle John was exiled and received the visions recorded in the Book of Revelation.

Pau.name: Pau
Pau.shortDescription: Pau, also known as Pai, was a city in Edom mentioned in the genealogy of Edomite kings.

Pelusium.name: Pelusium
Pelusium.briefDescription: An Egyptian city, also known as Sin
Pelusium.shortDescription: Pelusium, also known as Sin, was an important Egyptian city mentioned in Ezekiel's prophecy against Egypt.

Peniel.name: Peniel
Peniel.briefDescription: A place where Jacob wrestled with God
Peniel.shortDescription: Peniel, also called Penuel, was the place where Jacob wrestled with God and had his name changed to Israel.

People's_Gate.name: People's_Gate
People's_Gate.briefDescription: A gate in Jerusalem mentioned by Jeremiah
People's_Gate.shortDescription: The People's Gate was one of the gates of Jerusalem, where the prophet Jeremiah was instructed to proclaim God's message.

Peor.name: Peor
Peor.briefDescription: A mountain in Moab associated with idolatry
Peor.shortDescription: Peor was a mountain in Moab where Balak brought Balaam to curse Israel, and where the Israelites later engaged in idolatry and immorality with the Moabites.

Perazim_Mount.name: Perazim_Mount
Perazim_Mount.description: Mount Perazim= near Valley of Rephaim (Rephaim_Valley@Jos.15.8-Isa=H7497G)
Perazim_Mount.briefDescription: A place where David defeated the Philistines
Perazim_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Perazim was the location where David, with God's help, defeated the Philistines, and it is used by Isaiah as an example of God's judgment.

Perez-uzza.name: Perez-uzza
Perez-uzza.briefDescription: A place named after Uzzah's death
Perez-uzza.shortDescription: Perez-uzza was the name given to the place where Uzzah died after touching the Ark of the Covenant during its transport to Jerusalem.

Perga.name: Perga
Perga.briefDescription: A city in Pamphylia visited by Paul
Perga.shortDescription: Perga was a city in Pamphylia (modern-day Turkey) where Paul and Barnabas preached the gospel during their first missionary journey.

Pergamum.name: Pergamum
Pergamum.briefDescription: A city in Asia Minor with a church
Pergamum.shortDescription: Pergamum was one of the seven churches of Asia addressed in the Book of Revelation, known for its faithfulness despite living in a city associated with Satan's throne.

Persia.name: Persia
Persia.briefDescription: An empire that ruled over the exiled Jews
Persia.shortDescription: Persia was the empire that conquered Babylon and allowed the exiled Jews to return to Jerusalem and rebuild the temple.

Pethor.name: Pethor
Pethor.briefDescription: The hometown of Balaam in Mesopotamia
Pethor.shortDescription: Pethor was the hometown of Balaam, the prophet hired by Balak to curse Israel, located near the Euphrates River in Mesopotamia.

Pharpar.name: Pharpar
Pharpar.shortDescription: Pharpar was one of the rivers of Damascus, mentioned by Naaman the leper when comparing them to the Jordan River in Israel.

Philadelphia.name: Philadelphia
Philadelphia.briefDescription: One of the seven churches in Asia Minor
Philadelphia.shortDescription: Philadelphia was one of the seven churches in Asia Minor addressed in the Book of Revelation, known for its faithfulness and endurance.

Philippi.name: Philippi
Philippi.briefDescription: A city in Macedonia visited by Paul
Philippi.shortDescription: Philippi was a city in Macedonia where Paul founded a church and experienced persecution, imprisonment, and a miraculous deliverance.

Philistia.name: Philistia
Philistia.briefDescription: An ancient people and region in Canaan
Philistia.shortDescription: The Philistines were a powerful people who inhabited the coastal plain of Canaan and were often in conflict with the Israelites during the period of the judges and early monarchy.

Phoenicia.name: Phoenicia
Phoenicia.briefDescription: A region and people on the Mediterranean coast
Phoenicia.shortDescription: Phoenicia was a region along the Mediterranean coast, north of Israel, known for its maritime trade and mentioned in the New Testament in connection with the spread of the gospel.

Phoenix.name: Phoenix
Phoenix.briefDescription: A harbor on the island of Crete
Phoenix.shortDescription: Phoenix was a harbor on the island of Crete where the ship carrying Paul and other prisoners attempted to winter during their journey to Rome.

Phrygia.name: Phrygia
Phrygia.briefDescription: A region in Asia Minor with early Christians
Phrygia.shortDescription: Phrygia was a region in Asia Minor where Paul traveled and preached the gospel, and some of its inhabitants were present in Jerusalem on the day of Pentecost.

Pi-beseth.name: Pi-beseth
Pi-beseth.briefDescription: An Egyptian city mentioned in Ezekiel's prophecy
Pi-beseth.shortDescription: Pi-beseth was an Egyptian city that Ezekiel prophesied would experience God's judgment along with other cities in Egypt.

Pi-hahiroth.name: Pi-hahiroth
Pi-hahiroth.briefDescription: A place near the Red Sea crossing
Pi-hahiroth.shortDescription: Pi-hahiroth was a location near the Red Sea where the Israelites camped before crossing the sea during the Exodus from Egypt.

Pirathon.name: Pirathon
Pirathon.briefDescription: A town in Ephraim, home of several notable Israelites
Pirathon.shortDescription: Pirathon was a town in the hill country of Ephraim, associated with the judge Abdon and two of David's mighty men, Benaiah and Hiddai.

Pisgah.name: Pisgah
Pisgah.briefDescription: A mountain range in Moab, east of the Jordan
Pisgah.shortDescription: Pisgah, or the slopes of Pisgah, was a mountain range in Moab where Moses viewed the Promised Land before his death.

Pishon.name: Pishon
Pishon.briefDescription: A river flowing from Eden, encircling Havilah
Pishon.shortDescription: Pishon was one of the four rivers that flowed from the river in the Garden of Eden, encircling the land of Havilah, which was known for its gold, bdellium, and onyx.

Pisidia.name: Pisidia
Pisidia.briefDescription: A region in Asia Minor visited by Paul
Pisidia.shortDescription: Pisidia was a mountainous region in Asia Minor where Paul and Barnabas preached the gospel during their first missionary journey.

Pithom.name: Pithom
Pithom.briefDescription: A store city built by the Israelites in Egypt
Pithom.shortDescription: Pithom was one of the store cities built by the Israelites during their enslavement in Egypt, along with Rameses.

Pontus.name: Pontus
Pontus.briefDescription: A region in Asia Minor with Jewish inhabitants
Pontus.shortDescription: Pontus was a region in Asia Minor along the Black Sea coast, home to Jewish inhabitants and early Christian converts.

Potsherd_Gate.name: Potsherd_Gate
Potsherd_Gate.briefDescription: A gate in Jerusalem mentioned by Jeremiah
Potsherd_Gate.shortDescription: The Potsherd Gate was a gate in Jerusalem near the Valley of Ben Hinnom, where Jeremiah was instructed to proclaim a message of judgment against Judah.

Punon.name: Punon
Punon.briefDescription: A stopping place for the Israelites in the wilderness
Punon.shortDescription: Punon was one of the places where the Israelites camped during their wilderness wanderings, located between Zalmonah and Oboth.

Puteoli.name: Puteoli
Puteoli.briefDescription: A port city in Italy visited by Paul
Puteoli.shortDescription: Puteoli was a major port city in Italy where Paul stayed for seven days on his journey to Rome as a prisoner.

Raamses.name: Raamses
Raamses.briefDescription: A store city built by the Israelites in Egypt
Raamses.shortDescription: Raamses was one of the store cities built by the Israelites during their enslavement in Egypt, along with Pithom.

Rabbith.name: Rabbith
Rabbith.briefDescription: A town allotted to the tribe of Issachar
Rabbith.shortDescription: Rabbith was a town in the territory of Issachar, mentioned in the list of cities and towns allocated to the tribe during the division of the Promised Land.

Rakkath.name: Rakkath
Rakkath.briefDescription: City on the western shore of the Sea of Galilee
Rakkath.shortDescription: Tiberias (Rakkath), a city on the western shore of the Sea of Galilee, near where Jesus fed the five thousand (Josh. 19:35; Jhn.6.23).

Rakkon.name: Rakkon
Rakkon.briefDescription: A town allotted to the tribe of Dan
Rakkon.shortDescription: Rakkon was a town mentioned in the description of the territory allocated to the tribe of Dan in the Promised Land.

Ramah.name: Ramah
Ramah.briefDescription: A fortified city in the territory of Naphtali
Ramah.shortDescription: Ramah was a fortified city located in the territory of Naphtali, mentioned in the list of cities allocated to the tribe during the division of the Promised Land.

Ramath-lehi.name: Ramath-lehi
Ramath-lehi.briefDescription: The place where Samson killed a thousand Philistines
Ramath-lehi.shortDescription: Ramath-lehi, meaning "hill of the jawbone," was the place where Samson killed a thousand Philistines with the jawbone of a donkey.

Rameses.name: Rameses
Rameses.briefDescription: Egyptian city where Israelites lived before the Exodus
Rameses.shortDescription: Rameses was a city in Egypt where the Israelites lived during their enslavement before the Exodus (Gen. 47:11; Exo. 12:37).

Ramoth.name: Ramoth
Ramoth.briefDescription: City of refuge east of the Jordan
Ramoth.shortDescription: Ramoth, also called Ramoth-gilead, was a city of refuge in Gad and a Levitical city (Deu. 4:43; Jos. 20:8; 21:38).

Recah.name: Recah
Recah.shortDescription: Recah was a descendant of Judah through Caleb (1 Ch. 4:12).

Red_Sea.name: Red_Sea
Red_Sea.briefDescription: Sea crossed by Israelites in the Exodus
Red_Sea.shortDescription: The Red Sea was the body of water miraculously crossed by the Israelites in their escape from Egypt (Exo. 13:18; 15:4).

Rehoboth.name: Rehoboth
Rehoboth.briefDescription: City associated with an Edomite king
Rehoboth.shortDescription: Rehoboth was a city associated with Shaul, an Edomite king (Gen. 36:37; 1 Ch. 1:48).

Remeth.name: Remeth
Remeth.shortDescription: Remeth, also called Ramoth, was a Levitical city allotted to the tribe of Issachar (Jos. 19:21; 1 Ch. 6:73).

Rephaim_Valley.name: Rephaim_Valley
Rephaim_Valley.briefDescription: Valley near Jerusalem, site of battles
Rephaim_Valley.shortDescription: The Valley of Rephaim was a valley near Jerusalem where David fought the Philistines (2 Sa. 5:18, 22; 23:13; 1 Ch. 11:15; 14:9).

Rephidim.name: Rephidim
Rephidim.shortDescription: Rephidim was a place where the Israelites camped during their journey from Egypt to Sinai (Exo. 17:1, 8; 19:2; Num. 33:14, 15).

Resen.name: Resen
Resen.briefDescription: Ancient Assyrian city built by Nimrod
Resen.shortDescription: Resen was an ancient Assyrian city built by Nimrod between Nineveh and Calah (Gen. 10:12).

Rezeph.name: Rezeph
Rezeph.shortDescription: Rezeph was a city conquered by the Assyrians, mentioned in a letter from the Assyrian king to Hezekiah (2 Ki. 19:12; Isa. 37:12).

Rhegium.name: Rhegium
Rhegium.shortDescription: Rhegium was a city in Italy where Paul's ship stopped on his journey to Rome (Act. 28:13).

Rhodes.name: Rhodes
Rhodes.briefDescription: Island visited by Paul on his journey
Rhodes.shortDescription: Rhodes was an island where Paul stopped during his journey to Jerusalem (Act. 21:1).

Riblah.name: Riblah
Riblah.briefDescription: Place on the eastern border of the Promised Land
Riblah.shortDescription: Riblah was a place mentioned in the description of the eastern border of the Promised Land (Num. 34:11).

Rimmon-perez.name: Rimmon-perez
Rimmon-perez.shortDescription: Rimmon-perez was a place where the Israelites camped during their journey from Egypt to Canaan (Num. 33:19-20).

Rissah.name: Rissah
Rissah.shortDescription: Rissah was a place where the Israelites camped during their journey from Egypt to Canaan (Num. 33:21-22).

Rithmah.name: Rithmah
Rithmah.shortDescription: Rithmah was a place where the Israelites camped during their journey from Egypt to Canaan (Num. 33:18-19).

Rock_of_Escape.name: Rock_of_Escape
Rock_of_Escape.briefDescription: Place where Saul stopped pursuing David
Rock_of_Escape.shortDescription: The Rock of Escape was a place where Saul stopped pursuing David after being called away to fight the Philistines (1 Sa. 23:28).

Rogelim.name: Rogelim
Rogelim.shortDescription: Rogelim was the hometown of Barzillai the Gileadite, who provided supplies for David during Absalom's rebellion (2 Sa. 17:27; 19:31).

Rome.name: Rome
Rome.briefDescription: Capital city of the Roman Empire
Rome.shortDescription: Rome was the capital of the Roman Empire, where Paul was imprisoned and wrote several epistles (Act.28.16; Rom. 1:7).

Sahar.name: Sahar
Sahar.briefDescription: Unknown location mentioned in Ezekiel's prophecy
Sahar.shortDescription: Sahar is a place mentioned in Ezekiel's prophecy, but its exact location is unknown (Ezk. 27:18).

Salamis.name: Salamis
Salamis.briefDescription: City on Cyprus visited by Paul and Barnabas
Salamis.shortDescription: Salamis was a city on the island of Cyprus where Paul and Barnabas preached the gospel in the Jewish synagogues (Act.13.5).

Salecah.name: Salecah
Salecah.briefDescription: City in Bashan, east of the Jordan River
Salecah.shortDescription: Salecah was a city in the region of Bashan, east of the Jordan River, associated with the kingdoms of Og and Sihon (Deut. 3:10; Josh. 12:5; 13:11; 1 Chr. 5:11).

Salim.name: Salim
Salim.briefDescription: Place near Aenon where John the Baptist baptized
Salim.shortDescription: Salim was a place near Aenon where John the Baptist was baptizing, because there was plenty of water there (Jhn.3.23).

Salmone.name: Salmone
Salmone.briefDescription: Promontory on the island of Crete
Salmone.shortDescription: Salmone was a promontory on the island of Crete that Paul's ship passed on his voyage to Rome (Act.27.7).

Salt_Sea.name: Salt_Sea
Salt_Sea.briefDescription: Another name for the Dead Sea
Salt_Sea.shortDescription: The Salt Sea, also known as the Sea of Arabah, is another name for the Dead Sea, located at the lowest point on Earth (Gen. 14:3; Num. 34:3, 12; Deut. 3:17; Josh. 3:16; 12:3; 15:2, 5; 18:19; Deut. 4:49; 2 Ki. 14:25).

Salt_Valley.name: Salt_Valley
Salt_Valley.briefDescription: Valley where David and his commanders defeated enemies
Salt_Valley.shortDescription: The Salt Valley was a location where David and his commanders defeated the Edomites and Arameans (2 Sa. 8:13; 2 Ki. 14:7; 1 Chr. 18:12; 2 Chr. 25:11; Psa. 60:1).

Samaria.name: Samaria
Samaria.briefDescription: Capital city of the northern kingdom of Israel; Samaritan inhabitants
Samaria.shortDescription: Samaria was the capital city of the northern kingdom of Israel, and its inhabitants were known as Samaritans (1 Ki. 16:24; 2 Ki. 17:24; Jhn. 4:9; Act.8.5).

Samos.name: Samos
Samos.briefDescription: Island visited by Paul during his travels
Samos.shortDescription: Samos is an island in the Aegean Sea where Paul stopped during his third missionary journey (Act.20.15).

Samothrace.name: Samothrace
Samothrace.briefDescription: Island visited by Paul during his second missionary journey
Samothrace.shortDescription: Samothrace is an island in the Aegean Sea where Paul and his companions landed during his second missionary journey (Act.16.11).

Sansannah.name: Sansannah
Sansannah.briefDescription: Town in the Negev region of Judah
Sansannah.shortDescription: Sansannah was a town allotted to the tribe of Judah in the Negev region (Josh. 15:31).

Sarid.name: Sarid
Sarid.shortDescription: Sarid was a border town in the territory allotted to the tribe of Zebulun (Josh. 19:10, 12).

Sebam.name: Sebam
Sebam.briefDescription: Town in the territory of Reuben
Sebam.shortDescription: Sebam was one of the towns in the territory of Reuben, east of the Jordan River (Num. 32:3).

Secacah.name: Secacah
Secacah.briefDescription: Town in the wilderness of Judah
Secacah.shortDescription: Secacah was a town located in the wilderness region of Judah (Josh. 15:61).

Second_Quarter.name: Second_Quarter
Second_Quarter.briefDescription: District in Jerusalem mentioned by Huldah and Zephaniah
Second_Quarter.shortDescription: The Second Quarter was a district in Jerusalem mentioned in connection with the prophetess Huldah (2 Ki. 22:14; 2 Chr. 34:22) and in Zephaniah's prophecy (Zeph. 1:10).

Secu.name: Secu
Secu.briefDescription: Place near Ramah visited by Saul
Secu.shortDescription: Secu was a place near Ramah where Saul went in search of David (1 Sam. 19:22).

Seir_Mount.name: Seir_Mount
Seir_Mount.briefDescription: Landmark on the boundary of Judah
Seir_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Seir was a landmark on the boundary of Judah, near Kiriath-jearim and Chesalon (Josh. 15:10).

Seirah.name: Seirah
Seirah.briefDescription: Place where Ehud escaped after killing Eglon
Seirah.shortDescription: Seirah was the place to which Ehud escaped after killing Eglon, the king of Moab (Judg. 3:26).

Sela.name: Sela
Sela.briefDescription: Edomite stronghold captured by King Amaziah of Judah
Sela.shortDescription: Sela, also known as rock or Joktheel, was an Edomite stronghold captured by King Amaziah of Judah (2 Ki. 14:7; 2 Chr. 25:12; Isa. 16:1; 42:11; Judg. 1:36).

Seleucia.name: Seleucia
Seleucia.briefDescription: Port city from which Paul and Barnabas sailed
Seleucia.shortDescription: Seleucia was the port city from which Paul and Barnabas sailed to Cyprus on their first missionary journey (Act.13.4).

Senaah.name: Senaah
Senaah.briefDescription: Town whose people returned from Babylonian exile
Senaah.shortDescription: Senaah was a town whose people are listed among those who returned to Judah from the Babylonian exile (Ezr. 2:35; Neh. 7:38).

Seneh.name: Seneh
Seneh.shortDescription: Seneh was one of two rocky crags between which Jonathan and his armor-bearer passed to attack the Philistine garrison at Michmash (1 Sam. 14:4).

Sephar.name: Sephar
Sephar.briefDescription: Boundary of the territory settled by Joktan's descendants
Sephar.shortDescription: Sephar was a mountain in the east that marked the boundary of the territory settled by the descendants of Joktan (Gen. 10:30).

Sepharad.name: Sepharad
Sepharad.briefDescription: City in Asia Minor; destination of exiled Jews
Sepharad.shortDescription: Sepharad, possibly referring to Sardis, was a destination of exiled Jews (Obad. 1:20) and one of the seven churches mentioned in Revelation (Rev. 1:11; 3:1, 4).

Sepharvaim.name: Sepharvaim
Sepharvaim.briefDescription: City of origin for people resettled in Samaria
Sepharvaim.shortDescription: Sepharvaim was one of the cities from which the Assyrians brought people to resettle in Samaria after the deportation of the Israelites (2 Ki. 17:24, 31; 18:34; 19:13; Isa. 36:19; 37:13).

Shaalbim.name: Shaalbim
Shaalbim.briefDescription: Town in Dan, home of David's mighty men
Shaalbim.shortDescription: Shaalbim, also called Shaalabbin, was a town allotted to the tribe of Dan (Josh. 19:42; Judg. 1:35) and the hometown of some of David's mighty men (2 Sam. 23:32; 1 Chr. 11:33). It was also a town under Solomon's administration (1 Ki. 4:9).

Shaalim.name: Shaalim
Shaalim.briefDescription: Region traversed by Saul in search of donkeys
Shaalim.shortDescription: Shaalim was a region through which Saul passed while searching for his father's lost donkeys (1 Sam. 9:4).

Shaaraim.name: Shaaraim
Shaaraim.briefDescription: Town in Judah; site of Philistine retreat
Shaaraim.shortDescription: Shaaraim was a town in the Shephelah of Judah (Josh. 15:36) and a place the Philistines passed in their retreat from Socoh (1 Sam. 17:52).

Shahazumah.name: Shahazumah
Shahazumah.shortDescription: Shahazumah was a town on the border of Issachar's territory (Josh. 19:22).

Shalishah.name: Shalishah
Shalishah.briefDescription: Region Saul passed through seeking donkeys
Shalishah.shortDescription: Shalishah was a region Saul and his servant passed through while searching for his father's lost donkeys (1 Sam. 9:4).

Shallecheth.name: Shallecheth
Shallecheth.briefDescription: Gate in Jerusalem assigned to Shuppim and Hosah
Shallecheth.shortDescription: Shallecheth was a gate in Jerusalem assigned to the Levites Shuppim and Hosah for guard duty (1 Chr. 26:16).

Shaphir.name: Shaphir
Shaphir.shortDescription: Shaphir was a town mentioned in Micah's prophecy, urged to pass on in nakedness and shame (Mic. 1:11).

Sharon.name: Sharon
Sharon.briefDescription: Region where Gadites pastured their flocks
Sharon.shortDescription: Sharon was a region east of the Jordan where the Gadites pastured their flocks (1 Chr. 5:16).

Sharuhen.name: Sharuhen
Sharuhen.briefDescription: Town allotted to Simeon within Judah's territory
Sharuhen.shortDescription: Sharuhen, also called Shaaraim or Shilhim, was a town allotted to the tribe of Simeon within Judah's territory (Josh. 19:6; 1 Chr. 4:31; Josh. 15:32).

Shaveh.name: Shaveh
Shaveh.briefDescription: Valley where Chedorlaomer defeated the Emites
Shaveh.shortDescription: Shaveh Kiriathaim was a valley where Chedorlaomer and his allies defeated the Emites before battling the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah (Gen. 14:5, 17).

Shebarim.name: Shebarim
Shebarim.briefDescription: Place near Ai where Israelites fled
Shebarim.shortDescription: Shebarim was a place near Ai where the Israelites fled after being defeated due to Achan's sin (Josh. 7:5).

Sheep_Gate.name: Sheep_Gate
Sheep_Gate.briefDescription: Gate in Jerusalem near the sheep market
Sheep_Gate.shortDescription: The Sheep Gate was a gate in Jerusalem's wall, likely near the sheep market, that was rebuilt during Nehemiah's time (Neh. 3:1, 32; 12:39; Jhn.5.2).

Shen.name: Shen
Shen.briefDescription: Place where Samuel set up the Ebenezer stone
Shen.shortDescription: Shen was the place where Samuel set up the Ebenezer stone to commemorate the Israelites' victory over the Philistines (1 Sam. 7:12).

Shepham.name: Shepham
Shepham.briefDescription: Landmark on the northeastern border of the Promised Land
Shepham.shortDescription: Shepham was a landmark on the northeastern border of the Promised Land, between Hazar-enan and Riblah (Num. 34:10-11).

Shephelah.name: Shephelah
Shephelah.briefDescription: Lowland region between Judean hills and coastal plain
Shephelah.shortDescription: The Shephelah was the lowland region between the Judean hills and the coastal plain, known for its fertile valleys and strategic importance (Deut. 1:7; Josh. 9:1; 10:40; 11:2, 16; 12:8; 15:33; Judg. 1:9; 1 Ki. 10:27; 1 Chr. 27:28; 2 Chr. 1:15; 9:27; 26:10; 28:18; Jer. 17:26; 32:44; 33:13; Obad. 1:19; Zech. 7:7).

Shepher_Mount.name: Shepher_Mount
Shepher_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Shepher was a place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness wanderings between Kehelathah and Haradah (Num. 33:23-24).

Shihor-libnath.name: Shihor-libnath
Shihor-libnath.shortDescription: Shihor-libnath was a place that marked the boundary of the territory allotted to the tribe of Asher (Josh. 19:26).

Shikkeron.name: Shikkeron
Shikkeron.briefDescription: Town on the border of Judah
Shikkeron.shortDescription: Shikkeron was a town on the northern border of Judah's territory, near Mount Baalah (Josh. 15:11).

Shinar.name: Shinar
Shinar.briefDescription: Ancient region in Mesopotamia, associated with Babylon
Shinar.shortDescription: Shinar was an ancient region in Mesopotamia, associated with the cities of Babylon and Uruk, and the site of the Tower of Babel (Gen. 10:10; 11:2; 14:1, 9; Josh. 7:21; Isa. 11:11; Dan. 1:2; Zech. 5:11).

Shion.name: Shion
Shion.briefDescription: Town in the territory of Issachar
Shion.shortDescription: Shion was a town allotted to the tribe of Issachar, mentioned along with Shunem, Hapharaim, and Anaharath (Josh. 19:19).

Shittim.name: Shittim
Shittim.briefDescription: The last Israelite campsite before crossing the Jordan.
Shittim.shortDescription: Shittim, also called Abel-shittim, the final Israelite encampment before entering Canaan.

Shittim_Valley.name: Shittim_Valley
Shittim_Valley.shortDescription: The Shittim Valley is mentioned in Joel's prophecy as a place where water will flow when God restores Judah's fortunes (Jol.3.18).

Shoa.name: Shoa
Shoa.briefDescription: People group mentioned in Ezekiel's prophecy
Shoa.shortDescription: Shoa was a people group mentioned along with the Babylonians, Chaldeans, and Assyrians in Ezekiel's prophecy against Jerusalem (Ezek. 23:23).

Shunem.name: Shunem
Shunem.briefDescription: Town in Issachar; home of the Shunammite woman
Shunem.shortDescription: Shunem was a town allotted to the tribe of Issachar (Josh. 19:18), known for being the home of the Shunammite woman who hosted Elisha (2 Ki. 4:8) and where the Philistines camped before battling Saul (1 Sam. 28:4).

Shur.name: Shur
Shur.briefDescription: Wilderness area between Canaan and Egypt
Shur.shortDescription: Shur was a wilderness area between Canaan and Egypt, mentioned in connection with Hagar (Gen. 16:7), Abraham (Gen. 20:1; 25:18), the Israelites' wanderings (Exod. 15:22), and Saul's battles (1 Sam. 15:7; 27:8).

Sibmah.name: Sibmah
Sibmah.briefDescription: Moabite town known for its vineyards
Sibmah.shortDescription: Sibmah was a Moabite town known for its vineyards, allotted to the tribe of Reuben (Num. 32:38; Josh. 13:19) and later mentioned in prophecies concerning Moab (Isa. 16:8-9; Jer. 48:32).

Sibraim.name: Sibraim
Sibraim.briefDescription: Landmark on the northeastern border of the Promised Land
Sibraim.shortDescription: Sibraim was a place mentioned by Ezekiel as a landmark on the northeastern border of the Promised Land, between the territory of Damascus and that of Hamath (Ezek. 47:16).

Siddim_Valley.name: Siddim_Valley
Siddim_Valley.briefDescription: Valley near the Dead Sea; site of battle
Siddim_Valley.shortDescription: The Valley of Siddim, located near the Dead Sea, was the site of a battle between the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah and the kings of Elam and their allies (Gen. 14:3, 8, 10).

Silla.name: Silla
Silla.briefDescription: Place where King Joash was assassinated
Silla.shortDescription: Silla was the place where King Joash of Judah was assassinated by his servants (2 Ki. 12:20).

Sin.name: Sin
Sin.briefDescription: Wilderness area between Elim and Sinai
Sin.shortDescription: Sin was a wilderness area between Elim and Sinai where the Israelites camped during their journey from Egypt to the Promised Land (Exod. 16:1; 17:1; Num. 33:11-12).

Sinai.name: Sinai
Sinai.briefDescription: Mountain where Moses received the Ten Commandments
Sinai.shortDescription: Sinai, also called Mount Sinai, Horeb, or Mount Horeb, was the mountain where God gave Moses the Ten Commandments and other laws (Exod. 19-40; Lev. 7:38; 25:1; 26:46; 27:34; Num. 3:1; 28:6; Deut. 33:2; Judg. 5:5; Ps. 68:8, 17; Neh. 9:13; Mal. 4:4; Act.7.30, 38; Gal. 4:24-25).

Sinai_Wilderness.name: Sinai_Wilderness
Sinai_Wilderness.briefDescription: Wilderness where Israel camped after leaving Egypt
Sinai_Wilderness.shortDescription: The Sinai Wilderness was the region where the Israelites camped after the Exodus and received the Law.

Siphmoth.name: Siphmoth
Siphmoth.briefDescription: Town in southern Judah that received spoils from David
Siphmoth.shortDescription: Siphmoth was one of the towns in southern Judah that received a share of the spoils from David after he defeated the Amalekites (1 Sam. 30:28).

Sirah.name: Sirah
Sirah.briefDescription: Place near Hebron where Abner was killed by Joab
Sirah.shortDescription: Sirah, also called the Cistern of Sirah, was a place near Hebron where Joab killed Abner in revenge for the death of his brother Asahel (2 Sam. 3:26).

Sitnah.name: Sitnah
Sitnah.briefDescription: Well dug by Isaac, disputed by Philistines
Sitnah.shortDescription: Sitnah was a well dug by Isaac in the Valley of Gerar, but the Philistines disputed its ownership (Gen. 26:21).

Smyrna.name: Smyrna
Smyrna.briefDescription: One of the seven churches of Asia Minor
Smyrna.shortDescription: Smyrna was one of the seven churches of Asia Minor addressed in the Book of Revelation, known for its persecution and poverty, yet faithful to Christ (Rev. 1:11; 2:8-11).

Socoh.name: Socoh
Socoh.briefDescription: A town in the hill country of Judah
Socoh.shortDescription: Socoh, a town allotted to the tribe of Judah in the hill country (Jos. 15:48).

Sodom.name: Sodom
Sodom.briefDescription: Wicked city destroyed by God for its sins
Sodom.shortDescription: Sodom, a notoriously wicked city destroyed by God along with Gomorrah for their grave sins (Gen. 19:24-25).

Solomon's_Portico.name: Solomon's_Portico
Solomon's_Portico.briefDescription: Colonnade in Jerusalem's temple where apostles taught
Solomon's_Portico.shortDescription: Solomon's Portico, a colonnade in the Jerusalem temple where the apostles gathered to teach (Act.3.11; 5:12).

Sorek_Valley.name: Sorek_Valley
Sorek_Valley.briefDescription: Valley where Delilah lived, Samson's downfall occurred
Sorek_Valley.shortDescription: Sorek Valley, the place where Delilah lived and where Samson's downfall began (Judg. 16:4).

Spain.name: Spain
Spain.briefDescription: Country Paul intended to visit to preach gospel
Spain.shortDescription: Spain, a country Paul planned to visit to preach the gospel (Rom. 15:24, 28).

Straight.name: Straight
Straight.briefDescription: Street in Damascus where Saul stayed after conversion
Straight.shortDescription: Straight, a street in Damascus where Saul stayed in the house of Judas after his conversion (Act.9.11).

Succoth.name: Succoth
Succoth.briefDescription: City in Canaan, location of Solomon's bronze works
Succoth.shortDescription: Succoth, a city in Canaan allocated to the tribe of Gad, later the site of Solomon's bronze works (Gen. 33:17; Josh. 13:27; 1 Kgs. 7:46).

Suph.name: Suph
Suph.briefDescription: Place near where Moses spoke to Israel
Suph.shortDescription: Suph, a place near the wilderness where Moses spoke to the Israelites (Deut. 1:1).

Suphah.name: Suphah
Suphah.briefDescription: Place mentioned in the Book of the Wars of the Lord
Suphah.shortDescription: Suphah, a place mentioned in the Book of the Wars of the Lord (Num. 21:14).

Sur.name: Sur
Sur.briefDescription: Gate of the temple in Jerusalem
Sur.shortDescription: Sur, a gate of the temple in Jerusalem, possibly referring to the Foundation Gate (2 Kgs. 11:6).

Susa.name: Susa
Susa.briefDescription: Capital city of the Persian Empire
Susa.shortDescription: Susa, the capital city of the Persian Empire, where Esther lived and the events of her book took place (Esth. 1:2; Dan. 8:2).

Sychar.name: Sychar
Sychar.shortDescription: Sychar, a Samaritan city near Jacob's well, where Jesus spoke with a Samaritan woman (Jhn.4.5).

Syene.name: Syene
Syene.briefDescription: Southern border of Egypt, modern-day Aswan
Syene.shortDescription: Syene, the southern border of Egypt, mentioned in prophecies about Egypt's destruction (Ezek. 29:10; 30:6).

Syracuse.name: Syracuse
Syracuse.briefDescription: City in Sicily where Paul's ship stopped
Syracuse.shortDescription: Syracuse, a city on the island of Sicily where Paul's ship stopped for three days on his journey to Rome (Act.28.12).

Syria.name: Syria
Syria.briefDescription: Ancient region north of Israel, often in conflict
Syria.shortDescription: Syria, an ancient region north of Israel, often engaged in conflicts with Israel and Judah (1 Kgs. 20:1; 2 Kgs. 13:3).

Syrtis.name: Syrtis
Syrtis.briefDescription: Dangerous shallow waters off the coast of Libya
Syrtis.shortDescription: Syrtis, a dangerous area of shallow waters off the coast of Libya, feared by sailors (Act.27.17).

Taanach.name: Taanach
Taanach.briefDescription: Canaanite city allotted to Manasseh, later a Levitical city
Taanach.shortDescription: Taanach, a Canaanite city allotted to the tribe of Manasseh, later designated as a Levitical city (Josh. 12:21; 17:11; 21:25).

Taanath-shiloh.name: Taanath-shiloh
Taanath-shiloh.briefDescription: Landmark on the border of Ephraim's territory
Taanath-shiloh.shortDescription: Taanath-shiloh, a landmark on the eastern border of the territory allotted to the tribe of Ephraim (Josh. 16:6).

Tabbath.name: Tabbath
Tabbath.briefDescription: Place near where Midianites fled from Gideon
Tabbath.shortDescription: Tabbath, a place near the Jordan River where the Midianites fled after being defeated by Gideon (Judg. 7:22).

Taberah.name: Taberah
Taberah.briefDescription: Israelite camp where God sent fire as judgment
Taberah.shortDescription: Taberah, a place where the Israelites camped and experienced God's judgment by fire for their complaints (Num. 11:3; Deut. 9:22).

Tabor.name: Tabor
Tabor.briefDescription: Oak tree near Bethel, mentioned in Samuel's prophecy
Tabor.shortDescription: Tabor, an oak tree near Bethel, mentioned in Samuel's prophetic message to Saul (1 Sam. 10:3).

Tabor_Mount.name: Tabor_Mount
Tabor_Mount.briefDescription: Mountain in Israel, site of Deborah and Barak's victory
Tabor_Mount.shortDescription: Mount Tabor, a mountain in Israel, known for the gathering of Deborah and Barak's forces and their victory over Sisera (Judg. 4:6, 12, 14).

Tadmor.name: Tadmor
Tadmor.briefDescription: City built by Solomon in the wilderness
Tadmor.shortDescription: Tadmor (or Tamar), a city built by Solomon in the wilderness, possibly the later site of Palmyra (1 Kgs. 9:18; 2 Chr. 8:4).

Tahpanhes.name: Tahpanhes
Tahpanhes.briefDescription: Egyptian city where Jewish refugees fled after Jerusalem's destruction
Tahpanhes.shortDescription: Tahpanhes (or Tehaphnehes), an Egyptian city where Jewish refugees, including Jeremiah, settled after the destruction of Jerusalem (Jer. 43:7-9; 44:1; 46:14; Ezek. 30:18).

Taralah.name: Taralah
Taralah.briefDescription: Town allotted to the tribe of Benjamin
Taralah.shortDescription: Taralah, a town allotted to the tribe of Benjamin (Josh. 18:27).

Tarsus.name: Tarsus
Tarsus.briefDescription: City in Cilicia, birthplace of the apostle Paul
Tarsus.shortDescription: Tarsus, a city in Cilicia, known as the birthplace and early residence of the apostle Paul (Act.9.11, 30; 21:39; 22:3).

Tekoa.name: Tekoa
Tekoa.briefDescription: Town in Judah, home of the prophet Amos
Tekoa.shortDescription: Tekoa, a town in Judah, known as the birthplace of the prophet Amos and the home of one of David's mighty men (2 Sam. 14:2; 1 Chr. 11:28; Amo.1.1).

Tel-abib.name: Tel-abib
Tel-abib.briefDescription: Settlement of exiled Jews in Babylonia
Tel-abib.shortDescription: Tel-abib, a settlement of exiled Jews by the Chebar River in Babylonia, where the prophet Ezekiel lived (Ezek. 3:15).

Telaim.name: Telaim
Telaim.briefDescription: Place where Saul gathered his army
Telaim.shortDescription: Telaim, a place where Saul gathered his army before attacking the Amalekites (1 Sam. 15:4).

Telassar.name: Telassar
Telassar.briefDescription: Mesopotamian city conquered by the Assyrians
Telassar.shortDescription: Telassar, a Mesopotamian city conquered by the Assyrians, mentioned in the context of Sennacherib's boasting (2 Kgs. 19:12; Isa. 37:12).

Tel-harsha.name: Tel-harsha
Tel-harsha.briefDescription: Babylonian city from which exiles returned
Tel-harsha.shortDescription: Tel-harsha, a Babylonian city from which some Jewish exiles returned to Jerusalem, but could not prove their genealogy (Ezra 2:59; Neh. 7:61).

Tel-melah.name: Tel-melah
Tel-melah.briefDescription: Babylonian city from which exiles returned
Tel-melah.shortDescription: Tel-melah, a Babylonian city from which some Jewish exiles returned to Jerusalem, but could not prove their genealogy (Ezra 2:59; Neh. 7:61).

Thebes.name: Thebes
Thebes.briefDescription: Ancient Egyptian city, known for its wealth and power
Thebes.shortDescription: Thebes, an ancient Egyptian city, known for its wealth and power, mentioned in prophecies of judgment against Egypt (Jer. 46:25; Ezek. 30:14-16; Nah. 3:8).

Thebez.name: Thebez
Thebez.briefDescription: Canaanite city where Abimelech was killed
Thebez.shortDescription: Thebez, a Canaanite city where Abimelech was fatally wounded by a millstone thrown by a woman (Judg. 9:50-54; 2 Sam. 11:21).

Thessalonica.name: Thessalonica
Thessalonica.briefDescription: Macedonian city, site of an early Christian church
Thessalonica.shortDescription: Thessalonica, a prominent city in Macedonia, where Paul established a church and wrote two epistles to the believers (Act.17.1-9; 1 Thess. 1:1; 2 Thess. 1:1).

Three_Taverns.name: Three_Taverns
Three_Taverns.briefDescription: Staging post on the Appian Way near Rome
Three_Taverns.shortDescription: Three Taverns, a staging post on the Appian Way, about 33 miles from Rome, where Paul was met by Roman Christians (Act.28.15).

Thyatira.name: Thyatira
Thyatira.briefDescription: City in Asia Minor, home of the prophet Lydia
Thyatira.shortDescription: Thyatira, a city in Asia Minor, known for its dyeing industry and the home of Lydia, a convert of Paul (Act.16.14; Rev. 1:11; 2:18, 24).

Tibhath.name: Tibhath
Tibhath.briefDescription: City from which David took bronze for the Temple
Tibhath.shortDescription: Tibhath, a city from which David took a large amount of bronze, used in the construction of the Temple (1 Chr. 18:8).

Tigris.name: Tigris
Tigris.briefDescription: River flowing from Eden, mentioned in Daniel's vision
Tigris.shortDescription: Tigris, one of the rivers flowing from the Garden of Eden (Gen. 2:14), and the location of Daniel's vision (Dan. 10:4).

Timnah.name: Timnah
Timnah.briefDescription: Town in the hill country of Judah
Timnah.shortDescription: Timnah, a town in the hill country of Judah (Josh. 15:57).

Timnath-heres.name: Timnath-heres
Timnath-heres.briefDescription: Town allotted to Joshua, where he was buried
Timnath-heres.shortDescription: Timnath-heres (or Timnath-serah), a town in the hill country of Ephraim, allotted to Joshua, where he was buried (Josh. 19:50; 24:30; Judg. 2:9).

Tiphsah.name: Tiphsah
Tiphsah.briefDescription: City attacked by King Menahem of Israel
Tiphsah.shortDescription: Tiphsah, a city attacked and brutally conquered by Menahem, king of Israel (2 Kgs. 15:16).

Tishbe.name: Tishbe
Tishbe.shortDescription: Tishbe (or Tishbite), the hometown of the prophet Elijah, located in Gilead (1 Kgs. 17:1; 21:17, 28; 2 Kgs. 1:3, 8; 9:36).

Tob.name: Tob
Tob.briefDescription: Region where Jephthah fled and recruited mercenaries
Tob.shortDescription: Tob (or Ish-tob), a region east of the Jordan River where Jephthah fled and later recruited mercenaries to fight the Ammonites (Judg. 11:3, 5; 2 Sam. 10:6, 8).

Tochen.name: Tochen
Tochen.briefDescription: Town in the territory of Simeon
Tochen.shortDescription: Tochen, a town allotted to the tribe of Simeon (1 Chr. 4:32).

Tolad.name: Tolad
Tolad.briefDescription: Town in the territory of Simeon
Tolad.shortDescription: Tolad, a town allotted to the tribe of Simeon (1 Chr. 4:29).

Tophel.name: Tophel
Tophel.briefDescription: A place near where Moses addressed the Israelites
Tophel.shortDescription: Tophel, a location mentioned in Moses' speech to the Israelites, possibly near modern-day et-Tafeleh, southeast of the Dead Sea (Deut. 1:1).

Topheth.name: Topheth
Topheth.briefDescription: Site of pagan child sacrifice, south of Jerusalem
Topheth.shortDescription: Topheth, located in the Valley of Hinnom (also called the Valley of Slaughter or Baca Valley) south of Jerusalem, was a site of pagan child sacrifice that was defiled by King Josiah (2Ki.23.10; Jer.7.31-32; 19:2, 6, 11-14; 32:35; Isa.30.33).

Tower_of_Eder.name: Tower_of_Eder
Tower_of_Eder.briefDescription: A tower near Bethlehem, associated with the Messiah
Tower_of_Eder.shortDescription: The Tower of Eder, a watchtower near Bethlehem, where Jacob pitched his tent and later associated with the coming of the Messiah (Gen. 35:21; Mic. 4:8).

Tower_of_Hananel.name: Tower_of_Hananel
Tower_of_Hananel.briefDescription: A tower in Jerusalem's wall, rebuilt by Nehemiah
Tower_of_Hananel.shortDescription: The Tower of Hananel, a tower in Jerusalem's wall, rebuilt by Nehemiah and mentioned in prophetic visions of the city's restoration (Neh. 3:1; 12:39; Jer. 31:38; Zech. 14:10).

Tower_of_the_Hundred.name: Tower_of_the_Hundred
Tower_of_the_Hundred.description: Tower of the Hundred= in Jerusalem (Jerusalem@Gen.14.18-Rev=H3389)
Tower_of_the_Hundred.briefDescription: A tower in Jerusalem's wall, rebuilt by Nehemiah
Tower_of_the_Hundred.shortDescription: The Tower of the Hundred, a tower in Jerusalem's wall, rebuilt during Nehemiah's restoration of the city (Neh. 3:1; 12:39).

Tower_of_the_Ovens.name: Tower_of_the_Ovens
Tower_of_the_Ovens.description: Tower of the Ovens= in Jerusalem (Jerusalem@Gen.14.18-Rev=H3389)
Tower_of_the_Ovens.briefDescription: A tower in Jerusalem's wall, rebuilt by Nehemiah
Tower_of_the_Ovens.shortDescription: The Tower of the Ovens, a tower in Jerusalem's wall, rebuilt during Nehemiah's restoration of the city (Neh. 3:11; 12:38).

Trachonitis.name: Trachonitis
Trachonitis.briefDescription: A rugged region northeast of the Jordan River
Trachonitis.shortDescription: Trachonitis, a rugged, rocky region northeast of the Jordan River, part of Philip's tetrarchy during the time of John the Baptist (Luke 3:1).

Travelers_Valley.name: Travelers_Valley
Travelers_Valley.description: Valley of the Travelers= near Dibon_1 (Diblathaim@Num.33.46-Jer=H5963)
Travelers_Valley.briefDescription: A valley where Gog's army will be buried
Travelers_Valley.shortDescription: The Valley of the Travelers, a location where the army of Gog will be buried after their defeat, blocking the path of travelers (Ezek. 39:11).

Troas.name: Troas
Troas.briefDescription: A city in Asia Minor, visited by Paul
Troas.shortDescription: Troas, a city in Asia Minor, where Paul had a vision of a man urging him to come to Macedonia, and where he later preached and raised Eutychus from the dead (Act.16.8-11; 20:5-12; 2 Cor. 2:12; 2 Tim. 4:13).

Trogyllium.name: Trogyllium
Trogyllium.briefDescription: A city near Ephesus, visited by Paul
Trogyllium.shortDescription: Trogyllium, a coastal town near Ephesus, where Paul's ship anchored during his journey to Jerusalem (Act.20.15).

Tyre.name: Tyre
Tyre.briefDescription: A major Phoenician port city, known for its trade
Tyre.shortDescription: Tyre, a prominent Phoenician port city, known for its wealth, trade, and craftsmanship, often mentioned in the context of judgment and prophecy (Josh. 19:29; 2 Sam. 5:11; 1 Kgs. 5:1; Isa. 23:1-17; Ezek. 26-28; Matt. 11:21-22; Act.12.20).

Ulai.name: Ulai
Ulai.briefDescription: A river in Elam, near where Daniel saw a vision
Ulai.shortDescription: Ulai, a river in Elam, near Susa, where Daniel received a vision of a ram and a goat (Dan. 8:2, 16).

Ummah.name: Ummah
Ummah.briefDescription: A town allotted to the tribe of Asher
Ummah.shortDescription: Ummah, a town allotted to the tribe of Asher in the division of the Promised Land (Josh. 19:30).

Uphaz.name: Uphaz
Uphaz.briefDescription: A place known for its fine gold
Uphaz.shortDescription: Uphaz, a place mentioned in the Bible, known for its high-quality gold (Jer. 10:9; Dan. 10:5).

Uzzen-sheerah.name: Uzzen-sheerah
Uzzen-sheerah.briefDescription: A town built by Sheerah, daughter of Ephraim
Uzzen-sheerah.shortDescription: Uzzen-sheerah, a town built by Sheerah, the daughter of Ephraim, along with the towns of Upper and Lower Beth-horon (1 Chr. 7:24).

Valley_Gate.name: Valley_Gate
Valley_Gate.briefDescription: A gate in Jerusalem's wall, rebuilt by Nehemiah
Valley_Gate.shortDescription: The Valley Gate, one of the gates in Jerusalem's wall, rebuilt by Nehemiah and mentioned in connection with King Uzziah's fortifications (2 Chr. 26:9; Neh. 2:13, 15; 3:13).

Vedan.name: Vedan
Vedan.briefDescription: A place mentioned in Ezekiel's prophecy
Vedan.shortDescription: Vedan, a place of uncertain location, mentioned in Ezekiel's prophecy as trading with Tyre (Ezek. 27:19).

Waheb.name: Waheb
Waheb.briefDescription: A place mentioned in the Book of Numbers
Waheb.shortDescription: Waheb, a place mentioned in the Book of Numbers, possibly located in Moab (Num. 21:14).

Washer's_Field.name: Washer's_Field
Washer's_Field.description: Washer's Field= near Valley of Hinnom (Hinnom_Valley@Jos.15.8-Jer=H8612)
Washer's_Field.briefDescription: A location near Jerusalem's wall, mentioned in prophecies
Washer's_Field.shortDescription: The Washer's Field, a location near Jerusalem's wall, where the Assyrian officials stood and delivered messages to King Hezekiah (2 Kgs. 18:17; Isa. 7:3; 36:2).

Water_Gate.name: Water_Gate
Water_Gate.briefDescription: A gate in Jerusalem's wall, rebuilt by Nehemiah
Water_Gate.shortDescription: The Water Gate, one of the gates in Jerusalem's wall, rebuilt by Nehemiah and associated with the reading of the Law and the celebration of the Feast of Tabernacles (Neh. 3:26; 8:1, 3, 16; 12:37).

Way_of_Holiness.name: Way_of_Holiness
Way_of_Holiness.briefDescription: A prophesied highway for the redeemed in Isaiah
Way_of_Holiness.shortDescription: The Way of Holiness, a prophesied highway in Isaiah, reserved for the redeemed and leading to Zion (Isa. 35:8).

Wildgoats_Rocks.name: Wildgoats_Rocks
Wildgoats_Rocks.briefDescription: A place near En Gedi where David hid from Saul
Wildgoats_Rocks.shortDescription: Wildgoats Rocks, a rugged area near En Gedi where David hid from Saul and had the opportunity to spare the king's life (1 Sam. 24:2).

Yeshanah_Gate.name: Yeshanah_Gate
Yeshanah_Gate.briefDescription: A gate in Jerusalem's wall, rebuilt by Nehemiah
Yeshanah_Gate.shortDescription: The Yeshanah Gate, also known as the Old Gate, one of the gates in Jerusalem's wall rebuilt by Nehemiah (Neh. 3:6; 12:39).

Yiron.name: Yiron
Yiron.briefDescription: A town allotted to the tribe of Naphtali
Yiron.shortDescription: Yiron, a town in the territory of Naphtali, mentioned in the list of cities allotted to the tribe during the division of the Promised Land (Josh. 19:38).

Zaanan.name: Zaanan
Zaanan.briefDescription: A town mentioned in Micah's prophecy
