Zaanan.shortDescription: Zaanan, a town mentioned in Micah's prophecy, possibly located in the Shephelah region of Judah (Mic. 1:11).

Zaanannim.name: Zaanannim
Zaanannim.briefDescription: A place near Kedesh in Naphtali
Zaanannim.shortDescription: Zaanannim, a place near Kedesh in Naphtali, associated with the Kenites and mentioned in connection with the story of Deborah and Barak (Josh. 19:33; Judg. 4:11).

Zalmonah.name: Zalmonah
Zalmonah.briefDescription: A place where the Israelites camped during their wilderness journey
Zalmonah.shortDescription: Zalmonah, a campsite of the Israelites during their wilderness journey, located between Mount Hor and Punon (Num. 33:41-42).

Zanoah.name: Zanoah
Zanoah.briefDescription: A town in the hill country of Judah
Zanoah.shortDescription: Zanoah, a town in the hill country of Judah, mentioned in the list of cities allotted to the tribe (Josh. 15:56).

Zaphon.name: Zaphon
Zaphon.briefDescription: A city in Gad and a term for the north
Zaphon.shortDescription: Zaphon, a city allotted to the tribe of Gad, also used as a term for the north or the heavenly dwelling place of God (Josh. 13:27; Judg. 12:1; Ps. 48:2; Isa. 14:13).

Zarephath.name: Zarephath
Zarephath.briefDescription: A Phoenician city where Elijah stayed with a widow
Zarephath.shortDescription: Zarephath, a Phoenician city between Tyre and Sidon, where Elijah stayed with a widow during a famine and performed miracles (1 Kgs. 17:9-10; Obad. 1:20; Luke 4:26).

Zarethan.name: Zarethan
Zarethan.briefDescription: A place near the Jordan River associated with Israel's crossing
Zarethan.shortDescription: Zarethan, a place near Adam and the Jordan River, mentioned in connection with Israel's crossing and the casting of bronze implements for Solomon's Temple (Josh. 3:16; 1 Kgs. 4:12; 7:46; 2 Chr. 4:17; Judg. 7:22).

Zeboiim.name: Zeboiim
Zeboiim.briefDescription: One of the cities of the plain destroyed with Sodom
Zeboiim.shortDescription: Zeboiim, one of the cities of the plain, along with Sodom and Gomorrah, that was destroyed by God due to its wickedness (Gen. 10:19; 14:2, 8; Deut. 29:23; Hos. 11:8).

Zeboim.name: Zeboim
Zeboim.briefDescription: A town resettled by the people of Judah after the exile
Zeboim.shortDescription: Zeboim, a town resettled by the people of Judah after the Babylonian exile (Neh. 11:34).

Zeboim_Valley.name: Zeboim_Valley
Zeboim_Valley.briefDescription: A valley mentioned in connection with Saul's military campaigns
Zeboim_Valley.shortDescription: The Valley of Zeboim, a location mentioned in the context of Saul's military campaigns against the Philistines (1 Sam. 13:18).

Zedad.name: Zedad
Zedad.briefDescription: A place on the northern border of the Promised Land
Zedad.shortDescription: Zedad, a place mentioned as part of the northern border of the Promised Land in both the Mosaic and Ezekiel's descriptions (Num. 34:8; Ezek. 47:15).

Zela.name: Zela
Zela.briefDescription: A city in Benjamin where Saul's family was buried
Zela.shortDescription: Zela was a city in Benjamin where Saul and his family were buried .

Zelzah.name: Zelzah
Zelzah.briefDescription: A place near Rachel's tomb in Benjamin
Zelzah.shortDescription: Zelzah was a place near Rachel's tomb in Benjamin that Samuel referenced when giving directions to Saul .

Zemaraim.name: Zemaraim
Zemaraim.briefDescription: A town in Benjamin; also a hill in Ephraim
Zemaraim.shortDescription: Zemaraim was a town allotted to Benjamin (Jos.18.22). Mount Zemaraim in Ephraim was where Abijah addressed Jeroboam's army .

Zenan.name: Zenan
Zenan.briefDescription: A town in the Judean lowlands
Zenan.shortDescription: Zenan was a town in the lowlands of Judah .

Zephath.name: Zephath
Zephath.briefDescription: A Canaanite city captured by Judah and Simeon
Zephath.shortDescription: Zephath, later called Hormah, was a Canaanite city that Israel failed to capture but was later defeated by Judah and Simeon .

Zephathah_Valley.name: Zephathah_Valley
Zephathah_Valley.briefDescription: A valley where Asa defeated the Cushite army
Zephathah_Valley.shortDescription: The Valley of Zephathah is where King Asa of Judah defeated Zerah the Cushite's large army .

Zer.name: Zer
Zer.shortDescription: Zer was a fortified city in the territory of Naphtali .

Zered.name: Zered
Zered.briefDescription: A brook marking the end of Israel's wilderness wanderings
Zered.shortDescription: The Zered Brook marked the end of Israel's 38 years of wilderness wanderings until they crossed it to enter Moab .

Zeredah.name: Zeredah
Zeredah.briefDescription: The hometown of Jeroboam, first king of Israel
Zeredah.shortDescription: Zeredah was the hometown of Jeroboam, who became the first king of the northern kingdom of Israel .

Zereth-shahar.name: Zereth-shahar
Zereth-shahar.briefDescription: A city in Reuben's territory by the Dead Sea
Zereth-shahar.shortDescription: Zereth-shahar was a city in the territory of Reuben, located on a mountain in the plain by the Dead Sea .

Ziddim.name: Ziddim
Ziddim.shortDescription: Ziddim was a fortified city in the territory of Naphtali .

Ziklag.name: Ziklag
Ziklag.briefDescription: A city given to David; originally Judah's, then Simeon's
Ziklag.shortDescription: Ziklag was a city allotted to Judah and Simeon that was later given to David by the Philistine king Achish .

Zin.name: Zin
Zin.briefDescription: A desert and wilderness region south of Judah
Zin.shortDescription: The Wilderness of Zin was a desert region south of Judah where the Israelites encamped during their wilderness wanderings .

Zior.name: Zior
Zior.briefDescription: A town in the hill country of Judah
Zior.shortDescription: Zior was a town in the hill country of Judah .

Ziphron.name: Ziphron
Ziphron.briefDescription: A place on the northern border of the Promised Land
Ziphron.shortDescription: Ziphron was a place on the northern border of the Promised Land .

Ziz.name: Ziz
Ziz.briefDescription: A pass where Jehoshaphat defeated Moab and Ammon
Ziz.shortDescription: The Ascent of Ziz was a pass where Jehoshaphat defeated the armies of Moab and Ammon .

Zoan.name: Zoan
Zoan.briefDescription: An ancient Egyptian city, probably Tanis
Zoan.shortDescription: Zoan was an ancient Egyptian city where God performed miracles through Moses; it is probably to be identified with Tanis .

Zoar.name: Zoar
Zoar.briefDescription: A city near Sodom, spared from destruction
Zoar.shortDescription: Zoar, also known as Bela, was a small city near Sodom where Lot fled when God destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah .

Zobah.name: Zobah
Zobah.briefDescription: An Aramean kingdom, enemy of Israel during David's reign
Zobah.shortDescription: Zobah was an Aramean kingdom northeast of Israel, conquered by David and later a source of opposition to Solomon .

Zophim.name: Zophim
Zophim.briefDescription: A place in Moab where Balaam blessed Israel
Zophim.shortDescription: The field of Zophim was a place on the top of Pisgah in Moab where Balaam blessed Israel .

Zorah.name: Zorah
Zorah.briefDescription: A town in the Shephelah, home of Samson
Zorah.shortDescription: Zorah was a town in the Shephelah region, originally allotted to Judah and later to Dan, and was the hometown of Samson .

Abaddon.name: Abaddon
Abaddon.briefDescription: A Hebrew term for the realm of the dead
Abaddon.shortDescription: Abaddon is a Hebrew term used in the Old Testament to refer to the realm of the dead or the place of destruction .

Abib.name: Abib
Abib.briefDescription: The first month of the Hebrew religious calendar
Abib.shortDescription: Abib, later known as Nisan, was the first month of the Hebrew religious calendar, in which the Passover was celebrated .

Adar.name: Adar
Adar.briefDescription: The twelfth month of the Hebrew religious calendar
Adar.shortDescription: Adar was the twelfth month of the Hebrew religious calendar, in which the feast of Purim was celebrated .

Alamoth.name: Alamoth
Alamoth.briefDescription: A musical term, possibly referring to a high pitch
Alamoth.shortDescription: Alamoth is a musical term used in the superscriptions of some Psalms, possibly referring to a high pitch or a musical instrument .

Alpha.name: Alpha
Alpha.briefDescription: A title of Jesus, signifying His eternal nature
Alpha.shortDescription: In Revelation 1:8, 21:6, and 22:13, Jesus is referred to as "the Alpha and the Omega," a title that emphasizes His eternal nature and His role as the beginning and the end of all things.

Al-taschith.name: Al-taschith
Al-taschith.briefDescription: Musical or literary term in Psalms, meaning "Do Not Destroy"
Al-taschith.shortDescription: Al-taschith, meaning "Do Not Destroy," appears in the titles of Psalms 57, 58, 59, and 75, possibly indicating a musical or literary term.

Apharasthchite.name: Apharasthchite
Apharasthchite.description: Word sometimes translated as a title
Apharasthchite.briefDescription: Apharsathchites: A group opposing the rebuilding of Jerusalem's temple
Apharasthchite.shortDescription: The Apharsathchites were a group that opposed the rebuilding of the temple in Jerusalem during the time of Ezra (Ezra 4:9; 5:6; 6:6).

Artemis.name: Artemis
Artemis.description: A female deity in the New Testament
Artemis.briefDescription: Greek goddess worshipped in Ephesus, associated with fertility
Artemis.shortDescription: Artemis, a Greek goddess worshipped in Ephesus, was the focus of a major controversy during Paul's ministry in the city (Act.19.24, 27, 28, 34, 35).

Asherah.name: Asherah
Asherah.description: A female deity in the Old Testament
Asherah.briefDescription: Canaanite goddess; associated with wooden poles, idolatry
Asherah.shortDescription: Asherah, a Canaanite goddess, was worshipped by the Israelites through wooden poles or images, often leading them into idolatry.

Ashima.name: Ashima
Ashima.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Ashima.briefDescription: Deity worshipped by the people of Hamath
Ashima.shortDescription: Ashima was a deity worshipped by the people of Hamath who were resettled in Samaria by the Assyrian king (2Ki.17.30).

Ashtoreth.name: Ashtoreth
Ashtoreth.description: A female deity in the Old Testament
Ashtoreth.briefDescription: A Syrian goddess of fertility and war
Ashtoreth.shortDescription: Ashtoreth, was a Sidonian goddess of fertility and war whose worship was condemned in the Bible.

Azazel.name: Azazel
Azazel.description: A male deity/angel in the Old Testament
Azazel.briefDescription: Mysterious figure connected to the Day of Atonement
Azazel.shortDescription: Azazel, mentioned in Leviticus 16, is a mysterious figure connected to the scapegoat ritual on the Day of Atonement.

Baal-berith.name: Baal-berith
Baal-berith.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Baal-berith.briefDescription: Canaanite god of fertility and weather
Baal-berith.shortDescription: Baal, a Canaanite god of fertility and weather, and Peor, a Moabite god, were worshipped by the Israelites, leading them into idolatry and disobedience to the Lord.

Bear.name: Bear
Bear.briefDescription: Constellation mentioned in God's discourse with Job
Bear.shortDescription: The Bear is a constellation referred to in God's speeches to Job, showcasing God's power and wisdom in creation.

Behemoth.name: Behemoth
Behemoth.briefDescription: A powerful, majestic creature described in the Bible
Behemoth.shortDescription: Behemoth, mentioned in Job.40.15, is a powerful and majestic creature that God uses to illustrate His creative power and sovereignty.

Bel.name: Bel
Bel.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Bel.shortDescription: Bel, a Babylonian god mentioned in Isaiah 46:1 and Jeremiah 50:2; 51:44, is associated with the god Marduk and is depicted as bowing down and being humiliated by the Lord.

Booths_Festival.name: Booths_Festival
Booths_Festival.briefDescription: Jewish festival commemorating the Israelites' wilderness wanderings
Booths_Festival.shortDescription: The Booths_Festival, also known as the Feast of Tabernacles or Sukkot, was a Jewish festival commemorating the Israelites' wilderness wanderings and God's provision (Jhn.7.2).

Bul.name: Bul
Bul.briefDescription: The eighth month in the ancient Hebrew calendar
Bul.shortDescription: Bul, mentioned in 1Ki.6.38, is the eighth month in the ancient Hebrew calendar, corresponding to October-November in the modern calendar.

Chemosh.name: Chemosh
Chemosh.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Chemosh.briefDescription: Moabite god, worshipped by Solomon and others
Chemosh.shortDescription: Chemosh, the god of the Moabites, was worshipped by Solomon and the Israelites, leading them into idolatry.

Chislev.name: Chislev
Chislev.briefDescription: The ninth month in the ancient Hebrew calendar
Chislev.shortDescription: Chislev, mentioned in Nehemiah 1:1 and Zechariah 7:1, is the ninth month in the ancient Hebrew calendar, corresponding to November-December in the modern calendar.

Chronicles.name: Chronicles
Chronicles.briefDescription: Historical records of the kings of Israel and Judah
Chronicles.shortDescription: The Chronicles, mentioned throughout the books of Kings and Chronicles, were historical records of the reigns of the kings of Israel and Judah.

Dagon.name: Dagon
Dagon.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Dagon.briefDescription: Philistine god whose temple was destroyed by Samson
Dagon.shortDescription: Dagon was the chief deity of the Philistines, with temples at Gaza and Ashdod.

Dedication_Festival.name: Dedication_Festival
Dedication_Festival.briefDescription: Jewish festival commemorating the rededication of the Temple
Dedication_Festival.shortDescription: The Dedication_Festival, also known as Hanukkah, was a Jewish festival that commemorated the rededication of the Temple in Jerusalem (Jhn.10.22).

Destiny.name: Destiny
Destiny.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Destiny.briefDescription: A pagan deity mentioned in the Book of Isaiah
Destiny.shortDescription: Destiny, mentioned in Isaiah 65:11, is a pagan deity associated with fate or fortune, which some Israelites worshipped, forsaking the Lord.

Elul.name: Elul
Elul.briefDescription: The sixth month in the ancient Hebrew calendar
Elul.shortDescription: Elul, mentioned in Nehemiah 6:15, is the sixth month in the ancient Hebrew calendar, corresponding to August-September in the modern calendar.

Epicurean.name: Epicurean
Epicurean.description: Philosophical group in the New Testament
Epicurean.briefDescription: A philosophical school in ancient Greece, mentioned in Acts
Epicurean.shortDescription: Epicureans, mentioned in Act.17.18, were adherents of a philosophical school in ancient Greece that emphasized the pursuit of pleasure and the avoidance of pain.

Ethanim.name: Ethanim
Ethanim.briefDescription: The seventh month in the ancient Hebrew calendar
Ethanim.shortDescription: Ethanim, mentioned in 1Ki.8.2, is the seventh month in the ancient Hebrew calendar, corresponding to September-October in the modern calendar.

Fortune.name: Fortune
Fortune.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Fortune.briefDescription: A pagan deity associated with luck and prosperity
Fortune.shortDescription: Fortune, mentioned in Isaiah 65:11, is a pagan deity associated with luck and prosperity, which some Israelites worshipped, forsaking the Lord.

Freedmen.name: Freedmen
Freedmen.description: Group of Jews in the New Testament
Freedmen.briefDescription: Former slaves who had been granted freedom
Freedmen.shortDescription: The Freedmen, mentioned in Act.6.9, were likely former Jewish slaves who had been granted freedom and had their own synagogue in Jerusalem.

Gabriel.name: Gabriel
Gabriel.briefDescription: An angel who delivers messages from God
Gabriel.shortDescription: Gabriel is an angel who appears in the Bible to deliver important messages from God, such as interpreting visions for Daniel (Dan.8.16; 9:21) and announcing the births of John the Baptist and Jesus (Luke 1:19, 26).

Gittith.name: Gittith
Gittith.briefDescription: A musical term in the superscriptions of Psalms
Gittith.shortDescription: Gittith, mentioned in the superscriptions of Psalms 8, 81, and 84, is a musical term of uncertain meaning, possibly referring to a musical instrument, a tune, or a style associated with the city of Gath.

Herodian.name: Herodian
Herodian.description: Political group in the New Testament
Herodian.briefDescription: A Jewish political party supporting the Herodian dynasty
Herodian.shortDescription: The Herodians, mentioned in Matthew 22:16, Mrk.3.6, and Mrk.12.13, were a Jewish political party that supported the rule of the Herodian dynasty and collaborated with the Pharisees in their opposition to Jesus.

Higgaion.name: Higgaion
Higgaion.briefDescription: A musical or literary term in the Psalms
Higgaion.shortDescription: Higgaion, mentioned in Psalm 9:16, is a musical or literary term of uncertain meaning, possibly indicating a pause for meditation or reflection.

Hosanna.name: Hosanna
Hosanna.briefDescription: A Hebrew expression meaning "save now" or "save, please"
Hosanna.shortDescription: Hosanna, a Hebrew expression meaning "save now" or "save, please," was shouted by the crowds during Jesus' triumphal entry into Jerusalem, acknowledging Him as the Messiah (Matthew 21:9, 15; Mrk.11.9-10; Jhn.12.13).

Jonath-elem-rechokim.name: Jonath-elem-rechokim
Jonath-elem-rechokim.briefDescription: A musical term in the superscription of Psalm 56
Jonath-elem-rechokim.shortDescription: Jonath-elem-rechokim, meaning "Dove on Far-off Terebinths," is a musical term or phrase found in the superscription of Psalm 56, possibly indicating the melody or musical setting to which the psalm was to be sung.

Judaism.name: Judaism
Judaism.briefDescription: The monotheistic religion of the Jewish people
Judaism.shortDescription: In Galatians 1:13-14, Paul refers to his former way of life in Judaism, emphasizing his zealous adherence to the traditions of his ancestors before his conversion to Christianity.

Kiyyun.name: Kiyyun
Kiyyun.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Kiyyun.briefDescription: Astral deity worshiped by Israelites, provoking God's judgment
Kiyyun.shortDescription: Kiyyun was an astral deity that the Israelites worshiped, leading to God's judgment and their exile.

Legion.name: Legion
Legion.briefDescription: Name of a demon or group of demons
Legion.shortDescription: In the New Testament, Legion is the name given to a demon or group of demons that possessed a man in the region of the Gerasenes (Mrk.5.9; Luke 8:30).

Leviathan.name: Leviathan
Leviathan.briefDescription: A large sea creature mentioned in the Bible
Leviathan.shortDescription: Leviathan is a large sea creature mentioned in several Old Testament passages, often associated with chaos, evil, or God's power over creation (Job.3.8; 41:1; Psalm 74:14; 104:26; Isaiah 27:1).

Lucifer.name: Lucifer
Lucifer.description: An angel in the Old Testament
Lucifer.briefDescription: Symbolic name referring to the king of Babylon's fall
Lucifer.shortDescription: Isaiah poetically describes the king of Babylon as Lucifer, portraying his fall from power.

Maranatha.name: Maranatha
Maranatha.briefDescription: An Aramaic expression meaning "Our Lord, come!"
Maranatha.shortDescription: Maranatha is an Aramaic expression that means "Our Lord, come!" and is used in 1Co.16.22 as a prayer for the Lord's return.

Maskil.name: Maskil
Maskil.briefDescription: A musical or literary term in the Psalms
Maskil.shortDescription: Maskil is a term found in the superscriptions of several psalms, possibly indicating a contemplative or instructive psalm or a specific musical style.

Mazzaroth.name: Mazzaroth
Mazzaroth.briefDescription: A term related to astronomy or constellations
Mazzaroth.shortDescription: Mazzaroth, mentioned in Job.38.32, is a term of uncertain meaning, possibly referring to constellations or the zodiac.

Merodach.name: Merodach
Merodach.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Merodach.shortDescription: Merodach, also known as Bel, was the chief god of the Babylonian pantheon.

Messiah.name: Messiah
Messiah.briefDescription: The anointed one, a title for Jesus Christ.
Messiah.shortDescription: Messiah, meaning "anointed one," is a title used for Jesus Christ.

Miktam.name: Miktam
Miktam.briefDescription: A musical or literary term used in Psalm titles.
Miktam.shortDescription: Miktam is a musical or literary term of uncertain meaning used in Psalm titles.

Molech.name: Molech
Molech.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Molech.briefDescription: Canaanite deity associated with child sacrifice.
Molech.shortDescription: Molech (or Milcom or Moloch) was a Canaanite deity associated with child sacrifice.

Muth-labben.name: Muth-labben
Muth-labben.briefDescription: Musical term or tune in a psalm title
Muth-labben.shortDescription: Muth-labben, a phrase of uncertain meaning, appears in the title of Psalm 9.

Nazirite.name: Nazirite
Nazirite.description: Religious category in the Old and New Testaments
Nazirite.briefDescription: Person who took a special vow of dedication
Nazirite.shortDescription: A Nazirite was an individual who took a special vow of dedication to God, abstaining from wine, cutting hair, and avoiding corpses.

Nehushtan.name: Nehushtan
Nehushtan.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Nehushtan.briefDescription: The bronze serpent made by Moses in the wilderness
Nehushtan.shortDescription: Nehushtan, mentioned in 2Ki.18.4, was the name given to the bronze serpent that Moses made in the wilderness (Numbers 21:8-9), which had become an object of idolatrous worship by the time of King Hezekiah.

Nergal.name: Nergal
Nergal.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Nergal.briefDescription: A Mesopotamian god worshipped by the Assyrians
Nergal.shortDescription: Nergal, mentioned in 2Ki.17.30, was a Mesopotamian god of war, pestilence, and the underworld, worshipped by the men of Cuth who were brought to Samaria by the Assyrians.

New_Moon.name: New_Moon
New_Moon.briefDescription: New Moon: A religious observance marking the beginning of months
New_Moon.shortDescription: The New Moon, mentioned in Col.2.16, was a religious observance marking the beginning of each month in the Hebrew calendar, often celebrated with special sacrifices and feasts.

Nibhaz.name: Nibhaz
Nibhaz.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Nibhaz.briefDescription: Idol god worshiped by the Avvites in Samaria
Nibhaz.shortDescription: Nibhaz was a deity worshiped by the Avvite people who settled in Samaria.

Nicolaitans.name: Nicolaitans
Nicolaitans.description: Religious group in the New Testament
Nicolaitans.briefDescription: A group whose practices were condemned by Jesus
Nicolaitans.shortDescription: The Nicolaitans, mentioned in Revelation 2:6 and 2:15, were a group whose practices were hated by Jesus and condemned by the early church.

Nisroch.name: Nisroch
Nisroch.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Nisroch.briefDescription: An Assyrian deity, possibly associated with agriculture
Nisroch.shortDescription: Nisroch, mentioned in 2Ki.19.37 and Isaiah 37:38, was an Assyrian deity in whose temple King Sennacherib was worshipping when he was assassinated by his sons.

Omega.name: Omega
Omega.briefDescription: A title of Jesus, signifying His eternal nature
Omega.shortDescription: In Revelation 1:8, 21:6, and 22:13, Jesus is referred to as "the Alpha and the Omega," a title that emphasizes His eternal nature and His role as the beginning and the end of all things.

Orion.name: Orion
Orion.briefDescription: A prominent constellation mentioned in the Bible
Orion.shortDescription: Orion, a prominent constellation, is mentioned in Job.9.9, 38:31, and Amo.5.8 as a display of God's power and creative work in the heavens.

Passover.name: Passover
Passover.briefDescription: An annual Jewish feast commemorating the Exodus
Passover.shortDescription: The Passover is an annual Jewish feast that commemorates the deliverance of the Israelites from slavery in Egypt, as described in Exodus 12, and is celebrated with specific rituals and offerings as prescribed in the Old Testament and observed in the New Testament.

Pharisee.name: Pharisee
Pharisee.description: Religious group in the New Testament
Pharisee.briefDescription: A strict Jewish sect known for legalism and hypocrisy
Pharisee.shortDescription: The Pharisees were a Jewish sect known for their strict observance of the law and oral traditions, often clashing with Jesus over matters of ritual purity, Sabbath regulations, and His claims to divine authority (Matthew 23; Luke 11:37-54).

Pleiades.name: Pleiades
Pleiades.briefDescription: A star cluster mentioned as a display of God's power
Pleiades.shortDescription: The Pleiades are a star cluster mentioned in Job.9.9 and 38:31 as a display of God's creative power and in Amo.5.8 as evidence of God's control over the natural world.

Preparation_Festival.name: Preparation_Festival
Preparation_Festival.briefDescription: The day before the Sabbath or Passover
Preparation_Festival.shortDescription: The day of Preparation was the day before the Sabbath (Mrk.15.42; Luke 23:54) or the day before the Passover (Jhn.19.14, 31, 42).

Purim.name: Purim
Purim.briefDescription: Jewish festival commemorating the events in the book of Esther
Purim.shortDescription: Purim is the Jewish festival celebrating the deliverance of the Jews from Haman's plot to destroy them, as recorded in the book of Esther (Esther 9:26-32).

Raca.name: Raca
Raca.briefDescription: An Aramaic term of contempt meaning "empty-headed"
Raca.shortDescription: Raca is an Aramaic term of contempt, meaning "empty-headed" or "foolish," which Jesus warned against using to insult others, equating it with murder (Matthew 5:22).

Rephan.name: Rephan
Rephan.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Rephan.briefDescription: A pagan deity worshipped by the Israelites in the wilderness
Rephan.shortDescription: Rephan was a pagan deity worshipped by the Israelites in the wilderness, for which God allowed them to be carried into exile (Act.7.43).

Sabbath.name: Sabbath
Sabbath.briefDescription: The seventh day of the week, set apart for rest and worship
Sabbath.shortDescription: The Sabbath, meaning "rest," was the seventh day of the week, set apart by God as a holy day for rest and worship (Exodus 20:8-11; Leviticus 23:3).

Sadducee.name: Sadducee
Sadducee.description: Religious group in the New Testament
Sadducee.briefDescription: A Jewish sect that denied the resurrection and spirits
Sadducee.shortDescription: The Sadducees were a Jewish sect that denied the resurrection, angels, and spirits, and often clashed with Jesus and the early church (Matthew 22:23; Act.23.6-8).

Satan.name: Satan
Satan.description: An angel in the Old Testament
Satan.briefDescription: The adversary, the devil, the tempter of humanity
Satan.shortDescription: Satan, meaning "adversary" or "accuser," is the devil, the chief enemy of God and tempter of humanity, who seeks to hinder God's purposes and lead people into sin (Job.1.6-12; Matthew 4:1-11; Revelation 12:9).

Selah.name: Selah
Selah.briefDescription: A musical or liturgical term of uncertain meaning
Selah.shortDescription: Selah is a term frequently used in the Psalms and Habakkuk, likely a musical or liturgical direction, though its precise meaning is uncertain (Psalm 3:2, 4, 8; Habakkuk 3:3, 9, 13).

Shebat.name: Shebat
Shebat.briefDescription: The eleventh month of the Jewish calendar
Shebat.shortDescription: Shebat is the eleventh month of the Jewish calendar, mentioned in Zechariah 1:7 as the date of one of the prophet's visions.

Sheminith.name: Sheminith
Sheminith.briefDescription: A musical term of uncertain meaning
Sheminith.shortDescription: Sheminith is a musical term that appears in the titles of Psalms 6 and 12, and in 1Ch.15.21, possibly referring to a musical instrument, a part in music, or a melody.

Shibboleth.name: Shibboleth
Shibboleth.briefDescription: A Hebrew word used to identify Ephraimites
Shibboleth.shortDescription: Shibboleth was a Hebrew word used by the Gileadites to identify Ephraimites, who could not pronounce it correctly, resulting in the death of 42,000 Ephraimites (Judges 12:6).

Shiggaion.name: Shiggaion
Shiggaion.briefDescription: A musical term in the title of Psalm 7
Shiggaion.shortDescription: Shiggaion is a musical term that appears in the title of Psalm 7, possibly indicating a specific style or genre of music.

Shigionoth.name: Shigionoth
Shigionoth.briefDescription: A musical term in the title of Habakkuk's prayer
Shigionoth.shortDescription: Shigionoth is a musical term that appears in the title of Habakkuk's prayer in Habakkuk 3:1, possibly indicating a specific style or genre of music.

Shimeathite.name: Shimeathite
Shimeathite.description: Scribal group in the Old Testament
Shimeathite.briefDescription: A Kenite clan skilled in scribal arts
Shimeathite.shortDescription: The Shimeathites were a Kenite clan known for their scribal skills, mentioned in 1Ch.2.55 among the descendants of Salma.

Shushan_Eduth.name: Shushan_Eduth
Shushan_Eduth.briefDescription: A musical term or tune in the title of Psalm 60
Shushan_Eduth.shortDescription: Shushan Eduth, meaning "Lily of the Testimony," is a musical term or tune mentioned in the title of Psalm 60, possibly referring to a specific melody or musical setting.

Sikkuth.name: Sikkuth
Sikkuth.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Sikkuth.briefDescription: A pagan deity worshipped by the Israelites
Sikkuth.shortDescription: Sikkuth, possibly an Assyrian deity, was one of the pagan gods worshipped by the Israelites, for which they were condemned to exile (Amo.5.26).

Sivan.name: Sivan
Sivan.briefDescription: The third month of the Jewish calendar
Sivan.shortDescription: Sivan is the third month of the Jewish calendar, mentioned in Esther 8:9 as the month in which Mordecai's decree was written and sent to the provinces.

Stoic.name: Stoic
Stoic.description: Philosophical group in the New Testament
Stoic.briefDescription: A Greek philosophical school encountered by Paul in Athens
Stoic.shortDescription: The Stoics were a Greek philosophical school that emphasized reason, self-control, and the acceptance of fate, some of whom debated with Paul in Athens (Act.17.18).

Sucathite.name: Sucathite
Sucathite.description: Scribal group in the Old Testament
Sucathite.briefDescription: A Kenite clan skilled in scribal arts
Sucathite.shortDescription: The Sucathites were a Kenite clan known for their scribal skills, mentioned in 1Ch.2.55 among the descendants of Salma.

Succoth-benoth.name: Succoth-benoth
Succoth-benoth.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Succoth-benoth.briefDescription: A Babylonian deity worshipped by the Sepharvites
Succoth-benoth.shortDescription: Succoth-benoth was a Babylonian deity worshipped by the Sepharvites, one of the peoples settled in Samaria by the Assyrian king (2Ki.17.30).

Tammuz.name: Tammuz
Tammuz.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Tammuz.briefDescription: A Mesopotamian deity mourned by women in Jerusalem
Tammuz.shortDescription: Tammuz, a Mesopotamian deity, was mourned by women at the north gate of the Jerusalem temple, a practice condemned by Ezekiel (Ezk.8.14).

Tarpelite.name: Tarpelite
Tarpelite.description: Word  sometimes translated as a title
Tarpelite.briefDescription: A group of officials in the Persian Empire
Tarpelite.shortDescription: The Tarpelites were a group of officials in the Persian Empire who opposed the rebuilding of Jerusalem during the time of Ezra (Ezra 4:9).

Tartak.name: Tartak
Tartak.description: A male deity in the Old Testament
Tartak.briefDescription: An Avvite deity worshipped in Samaria
Tartak.shortDescription: Tartak was a deity worshipped by the Avvites, one of the peoples settled in Samaria by the Assyrian king (2Ki.17.31).

Tebeth.name: Tebeth
Tebeth.briefDescription: The tenth month of the Jewish calendar
Tebeth.shortDescription: Tebeth is the tenth month of the Jewish calendar, mentioned in Esther 2:16 as the month in which Esther was taken to King Ahasuerus.

Thummim.name: Thummim
Thummim.briefDescription: Objects used with the Urim for divine guidance
Thummim.shortDescription: The Thummim, along with the Urim, were objects carried in the breastpiece of the high priest's ephod and used to seek divine guidance (Exodus 28:30; Numbers 27:21).

Tirathite.name: Tirathite
Tirathite.description: Scribal group in the Old Testament
Tirathite.briefDescription: A Kenite clan skilled in scribal arts
Tirathite.shortDescription: The Tirathites were a Kenite clan known for their scribal skills, mentioned in 1Ch.2.55 among the descendants of Salma.

Urim.name: Urim
Urim.briefDescription: Objects used with the Thummim for divine guidance
Urim.shortDescription: The Urim, along with the Thummim, were objects carried in the breastpiece of the high priest's ephod and used to seek divine guidance (Exodus 28:30; Numbers 27:21).

Weeks.name: Weeks
Weeks.briefDescription: A Jewish agricultural festival celebrated 50 days after Passover
Weeks.shortDescription: The Feast of Weeks, also known as Pentecost, was a Jewish agricultural festival celebrated fifty days after Passover, commemorating the wheat harvest and the giving of the Law at Sinai.

Zealot.name: Zealot
Zealot.description: Political group in the New Testament
Zealot.briefDescription: A member of a Jewish political faction
Zealot.shortDescription: The Zealots were a Jewish political faction known for their fierce opposition to Roman rule and their advocacy for armed resistance (Matthew 10:4; Mrk.3.18).

Zeus.name: Zeus
Zeus.description: A male deity in the New Testament
Zeus.briefDescription: The chief god in Greek mythology
Zeus.shortDescription: Zeus, the chief god in Greek mythology, was identified with the Roman god Jupiter and was worshipped in Lystra (Act.14.12-13).

Ziv.name: Ziv
Ziv.briefDescription: The second month in the ancient Hebrew calendar
Ziv.shortDescription: Ziv, the second month in the ancient Hebrew calendar, is mentioned in connection with the construction of Solomon's Temple (1Ki.6.1, 37).

Latin.name: Latin
Latin.briefDescription: Language used in the inscription on Jesus' cross
Latin.shortDescription: Latin, along with Greek and Hebrew, was one of the languages used in the inscription placed above Jesus' cross during His crucifixion (Luke 23:38; Jhn.19.20).

Aramaic.name: Aramaic
Aramaic.briefDescription: Semitic languages used in the Bible and ancient Near East
Aramaic.shortDescription: Aramaic and Hebrew are Semitic languages used in various passages of the Bible, with Hebrew being the primary language of the Old Testament and Aramaic appearing in some later texts.

Greek.name: Greek
Greek.briefDescription: A language used in the New Testament
Greek.shortDescription: Greek, one of the original languages of the New Testament, is mentioned in Luke 23:38, Jhn.19.20, Act.21.37, and Revelation 9:11, highlighting its widespread use in the Roman Empire during the time of Jesus and the early church.

Hebrew.name: Hebrew
Hebrew.shortDescription: Hebrew was the language spoken by the Jews, as evidenced by its use in communication and writings (2Ki.18.26; 2Ch.32.18; Neh.13.24; Isa.36.11, 13).

Gentile.name: Gentile
Gentile.description: A person not descended from Jews

Asiarch.name: Asiarch
Asiarch.briefDescription: High-ranking officials in the Roman province of Asia
Asiarch.shortDescription: The Asiarchs, mentioned in Act.19.31, were high-ranking officials in the Roman province of Asia who had a friendly relationship with Paul.

Candace.name: Candace
Candace.briefDescription: Queen of Ethiopia, her eunuch met Philip
Candace.shortDescription: Candacé, the queen of Ethiopia, is mentioned in Act.8.27 in connection with her eunuch, who was baptized by Philip.

Rabbi.name: Rabbi
Rabbi.briefDescription: A Jewish title of respect for a teacher
Rabbi.shortDescription: Rabbi, meaning "my teacher," was a title of respect used by Jews to address their religious teachers and leaders (Matthew 23:7-8; Jhn.1.38, 49).

Seraph.name: Seraph
Seraph.description: An angel in the Old Testament
Seraph.briefDescription: Angelic beings that attend to God's throne
Seraph.shortDescription: Seraphim are angelic beings that surround God's throne, each having six wings and praising His holiness (Isaiah 6:2-6).

Teraphim.name: Teraphim
Teraphim.description: Group of deities in the Old Testament
Teraphim.briefDescription: Household gods or idols used for divination and worship
Teraphim.shortDescription: Teraphim were household gods or idols used for divination and worship, often associated with pagan practices and condemned in the Bible (Genesis 31:19; 2Ki.23.24; Ezk.21.21).

Sheol.name: Sheol
Sheol.briefDescription: The underworld; the abode of the dead in the Bible
Sheol.shortDescription: Sheol, also referred to as the Pit, Gehenna, Tartarus, or Hades, is the biblical concept of the underworld or the abode of the dead, often associated with darkness, stillness, and separation from God.

Names are based on the most common form.id: Names are based on the most common form + the first ref where the individual occurs (where a different name may be used), eg Uzziah@2Ki.14.21-Mat even though Uzziah is called "Azariah" in that verse
Names are based on the most common form.name: Names are based on the most common form + the first ref where the individual occurs (where a different name may be used), eg Uzziah

When a deliberate difference exists that.id: When a deliberate difference exists that should be reflected in the translation, the alternate is prefixed, eg Abram|Abraham@Gen.11.26-1Pe, Israel (or 'Jacob')|Israel@Gen.25.26-Rev
When a deliberate difference exists that.name: When a deliberate difference exists that should be reflected in the translation, the alternate is prefixed, eg Abram / Abraham

The exhaustive lists of refs were create.id: The exhaustive lists of refs were created from these texts. For 2nd + 3rd occurrences of a name in the same verse, reference appended with "a", "b" etc.  - see e.g. Abel@Gen.4.2-Heb
